We compare two reasoning settings across nine non-English target languages: En-CoT, where models process target-language inputs, but reason in English; and Target-CoT, where models both process inputs and generate long CoTs in the target language. We find that scaling reasoning model size improves multilingual task performance in En-CoT, but Target-CoT performance lags behind. This gap widens for tasks requiring long, multi-step CoTs such as mathematical reasoning. Shifting to pretraining, we find that adding a specialized reasoning stage enhances En-CoT performance but degrades Target-CoT, whereas broad multilingual pretraining improves both modes simultaneously. Given the scarcity of high-quality reasoning traces in languages other than English, we explore synthetic data curation approaches for post-training. We demonstrate that fine-tuning on reasoning traces
automatically translated from gold English traces outperforms fine-tuning on target-language traces distilled from large reasoning models. Finally, we report disparities in inference efficiency between languages and uncover language-specific failure modes
in CoTs. We release models, datasets, and code to foster further research.
abstract_zh: 尽管大型推理模型已经展现出用英语生成长思维链（CoT）的显著能力，我们仍然缺乏对这些长形式推理能力如何迁移到世界上绝大多数语言的理解。在这项工作中，我们系统研究了模型开发的四个关键阶段——规模扩展、预训练、后训练和推理——以理解长 CoT 能力如何延伸到英语之外。我们比较了九种非英语目标语言中的两种推理设置：En-CoT，在这种设置下，模型处理目标语言输入，但用英语推理；以及 Target-CoT，在这种设置下，模型既处理输入，也用目标语言生成长 CoT。我们发现，扩大推理模型规模能够提升 En-CoT 中的多语言任务表现，但 Target-CoT 的表现仍然落后。对于需要长且多步 CoT 的任务，如数学推理，这一差距会进一步扩大。转向预训练阶段时，我们发现加入专门的推理阶段会增强 En-CoT 的表现，但会降低 Target-CoT 的表现；而广泛的多语言预训练则能同时提升这两种模式。鉴于除英语之外的语言中高质量推理轨迹较为稀缺，我们探索了用于后训练的合成数据整理方法。我们证明，在推理轨迹上进行微调
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Learning from A Single Markovian Trajectory: Optimality and Variance Reduction
title_zh: 从单条马尔可夫轨迹中学习：最优性与方差降低
abstract: In this paper, we consider the general stochastic non-convex optimization problem when the sampling process follows a Markov chain. This problem exhibits its significance in capturing many real-world applications, ranging from asynchronous distributed learning to reinforcement learning. In particular, we consider the worst case where one has no prior knowledge and control of the Markov chain, meaning multiple trajectories cannot be simulated but only a single trajectory is available for algorithm design. We first provide algorithm-independent lower bounds with $\Omega(\epsilon^{-3})$ (and $\Omega(\epsilon^{-4})$) samples, when objectives are (mean-squared) smooth, for any first-order methods accessing bounded variance gradient oracles to achieve $\epsilon$-approximate critical solutions of original problems. Then, we propose \textbf{Ma}rkov-\textbf{C}hain \textbf{SPIDER} (MaC-SPIDER), which leverages variance-reduced techniques, to achieve a $\mathcal{O}(\epsilon^{-3})$ upper bound for mean-squared smooth objective functions. To the best of our knowledge, MaC-SPIDER is the first to achieve $\mathcal{O}(\epsilon^{-3})$ complexity when sampling from a single Markovian trajectory. And our proposed lower bound concludes its (near) optimality.
abstract_zh: 本文考虑了采样过程遵循马尔可夫链时的一般随机非凸优化问题。该问题在刻画许多现实应用方面具有重要意义，范围涵盖从异步分布式学习到强化学习。特别地，我们考虑最坏情形，即对马尔可夫链既没有先验知识也无法加以控制，这意味着无法模拟多条轨迹，而算法设计时只能使用单条轨迹。我们首先给出了与算法无关的下界：当目标函数是（均方）光滑时，对于任何访问有界方差梯度预言机的一阶方法，要达到原问题的 ε-近似临界解，需要 Ω(ε^{-3})（以及 Ω(ε^{-4})）个样本。随后，我们提出了马尔可夫链 SPIDER（MaC-SPIDER），该方法利用方差缩减技术，在均方光滑目标函数情形下达到了 O(ε^{-3}) 的上界。据我们所知，MaC-SPIDER 是首个在从单条马尔可夫轨迹采样时实现 O(ε^{-3}) 复杂度的方法。而我们提出的下界表明其具有（近）最优性。
primary_research_object: 强化学习 / 序列决策
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Tokenizing Single-Channel EEG with Time-Frequency Motif Learning
title_zh: 带时频模态学习的单通道脑电信号分词
abstract:
Foundation models are reshaping EEG analysis, yet an important problem of EEG tokenization remains a challenge. 
This paper presents TFM-Tokenizer, a novel tokenization framework that learns a vocabulary of time-frequency motifs from *single-channel* EEG signals and encodes them into discrete tokens. 
We propose a dual-path architecture with time–frequency masking to capture robust motif representations, and it is model-agnostic, supporting both lightweight transformers and existing foundation models for downstream tasks. 
Our study demonstrates three key benefits:
*Accuracy:* Experiments on four diverse EEG benchmarks demonstrate consistent performance gains across both single- and multi-dataset pretraining settings, achieving up to 11% improvement in Cohen’s Kappa over strong baselines. 
*Generalization:* Moreover, as a plug-and-play component, it consistently boosts the performance of diverse foundation models, including BIOT and LaBraM. 
*Scalability:* By operating at the single-channel level rather than relying on the strict 10–20 EEG system, our method has the potential to be device-agnostic.
Experiments on ear-EEG sleep staging, which differs from the pretraining data in signal format, channel configuration, recording device, and task, show that our tokenizer outperforms baselines by 14%.
A comprehensive token analysis reveals strong class-discriminative, frequency-aware, and consistent structure, enabling improved representation quality and interpretability.
Code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/TFM-Token-FE33.
abstract_zh: TFM-Tokenizer 提出了一种从单通道脑电信号中学习时频模体词表并将其编码为离散令牌的新型标记化框架；我们设计了一个具备时频掩码的双路径结构以提取稳健模体表示，并确保该框架对下游任务的基础模型（从轻量级 Transformer 到现有大模型）通用。实验证明：在四个多样的脑电基准上，无论是单数据集还是多数据集预训练，均带来一致的 Cohen’s Kappa 提升（最多 11%）；作为即插即用组件，它也持续提升包括 BIOT 与 LaBraM 在内的多种基础模型；由于在单通道尺度上运行、无需依赖 10–20 体系，展示出设备无关的潜力。面对与预训练数据在信号格式、通道配置、采集设备及任务上均不同的耳式脑电睡眠分期，本方法仍领先基线 14%。全面的令牌分析进一步揭示了其具备强分类辨识能力、频率感知性以及结构一致性，从而提升表示质量与可解释性。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Lorentz Local Canonicalization: How to make any Network Lorentz-Equivariant
title_zh: 洛伦兹局部规范化：如何让任意网络具备洛伦兹等变性
abstract:
Lorentz-equivariant neural networks are becoming the leading architectures for  high-energy physics.
Current implementations rely on specialized layers, limiting architectural choices. We introduce Lorentz Local Canonicalization (LLoCa), a general framework that renders any backbone network exactly Lorentz-equivariant. Using equivariantly predicted local reference frames,
we construct LLoCa-transformers and graph networks.
We adapt a recent approach for geometric message passing to the non-compact Lorentz group, allowing propagation of space-time tensorial features.
Data augmentation emerges from LLoCa as a special choice of reference frame.
Our models achieve competitive and state-of-the-art accuracy on relevant particle physics tasks, while being $4\times$ faster and using $10\times$ fewer FLOPs.
abstract_zh:
用等变地预测的局部参考系将任意主干网络精确地转换为洛伦兹等变结构，构成了 Lorentz Local Canonicalization (LLoCa) 框架，并基于此构建了 LLoCa-Transformer 和图网络；
将最近的几何消息传递方案推广到非紧致洛伦兹群，从而支持时空张量特征的传播；
LLoCa 自然包含了一种特殊参考系选择，等效于数据增强；
在相关粒子物理任务上达到了有竞争力甚至领先的精度，同时推理速度快 4 倍、所需浮点操作数少 10 倍。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: When Reasoning Meets Compression: Understanding the Effects of LLMs Compression on Large Reasoning Models
title_zh: 当推理遇到压缩：理解大推理模型中 LLM 压缩的影响
abstract: Compression methods, including quantization, distillation, and pruning, improve the computational efficiency of large reasoning models (LRMs). However, existing studies either fail to sufficiently compare all three compression methods on LRMs or lack in-depth interpretation analysis. In this paper, we investigate how the reasoning capabilities of LRMs are compromised during compression, through performance benchmarking and mechanistic interpretation. To uncover the effects of compression on reasoning performance, we benchmark quantized, distilled, and pruned DeepSeek-R1 models on four reasoning datasets (AIME 2024, FOLIO, Temporal Sequences, and MuSiQue). To precisely locate compression effects on model weights, we adapt difference of means and attribution patching techniques, focusing on the activation of every linear component in compressed LRMs, to interpret fine-grained causal relationships between weights and various reasoning capabilities. This fine-grained interpretation addresses a fundamental question of compression: which weights are the most important for reasoning? Overall, we find dynamically quantized 2.51-bit R1 reaches close-to-R1 performance. With empirical verification, we present three main findings that generalize across both Llama and Qwen: (1) Weight count has a greater impact on LRMs' knowledge memorization than reasoning, highlighting the risks of pruning and distillation; (2) The MLP up projection in the final layer of distilled LRMs is one of the most important components, offering a new perspective on locating critical weights - a fundamental problem in model compression; and (3) Current quantization methods overly compress the final-layer modules and MLP gate projections, so protecting just 2% of all weights that are excessively compressed can raise average accuracy by 6.57%, greatly surpassing the state-of-the-art.
abstract_zh: 包括量化、蒸馏、剪枝在内的压缩方法可提升大型推理模型（LRM）的计算效率。然而现有研究要么未能充分在大型推理模型上对这三类压缩方法开展对比，要么缺乏深度解释性分析。本文通过性能基准测试与机制解释，探究大型推理模型的推理能力在压缩过程中是如何受损的。为揭示压缩对推理性能的影响，我们在四个推理数据集（2024年AIME、FOLIO、时间序列、MuSiQue）上对经过量化、蒸馏、剪枝处理的DeepSeek-R1模型开展基准测试。为精准定位压缩对模型权重的影响，我们调整了均值差与归因补丁技术，重点关注压缩后大型推理模型中每个线性组件的激活情况，以此解释权重与各类推理能力之间的细粒度因果关系。这种细粒度解释回应了压缩领域的一个核心问题：哪些权重对推理最为重要？总体而言，我们发现经过动态量化的2.51比特R1模型性能接近原始R1模型。经实证验证，我们提出了三条在Llama和Qwen上均具备普适性的核心结论：（1）权重数量对大型推理模型知识记忆能力的影响大于对其推理能力的影响，这凸显了剪枝与蒸馏的风险；（2）蒸馏后的大型推理模型最后一层的多层感知机（MLP）上投影是最重要的组件之一，这为模型压缩领域的核心问题——关键权重定位——提供了新视角；（3）现有量化方法过度压缩了最后一层模块和MLP门投影，因此仅保护所有被过度压缩权重中占总权重2%的部分，就能将平均准确率提升6.57%，性能大幅优于当前最优水平。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=22；命中=quantization, quantized, distillation, pruning, compression, compressed, llm, reasoning model

title: SemanticDraw: Towards Real-Time Interactive Content Creation from Image Diffusion Models
title_zh: SemanticDraw：迈向基于图像扩散模型的实时交互式内容创作
abstract: We introduce SemanticDraw, a new paradigm of interactive content creation where high-quality images are generated in near real-time from given multiple hand-drawn regions, each encoding prescribed semantic meaning. In order to maximize the productivity of content creators and to fully realize their artistic imagination, it requires both quick interactive interfaces and fine-grained regional controls in their tools. Despite astonishing generation quality from recent diffusion models, we find that existing approaches for regional controllability are very slow (52 seconds for 512 x 512 image) while not compatible with acceleration methods such as LCM, blocking their huge potential in interactive content creation. From this observation, we build our solution for interactive content creation in two steps: (1) we establish compatibility between region-based controls and acceleration techniques for diffusion models, maintaining high fidelity of multi-prompt image generation with x 10 reduced number of inference steps, (2) we increase the generation throughput with our new multi-prompt stream batch pipeline, enabling low-latency generation from multiple, region-based text prompts on a single RTX 2080 Ti GPU. Our proposed framework is generalizable to any existing diffusion models and acceleration schedulers, allowing sub-second (0.64 seconds) image content creation application upon well-established image diffusion models.
abstract_zh: 我们推出SemanticDraw，这是一种交互式内容创作的新范式，可根据给定的多个手绘区域近乎实时生成高质量图像，每个手绘区域均编码了指定的语义含义。为最大化内容创作者的生产力、充分实现其艺术想象，所使用的工具需要同时具备快速交互界面与细粒度区域控制能力。尽管近期的扩散模型已实现惊人的生成质量，但我们发现现有区域可控性方法运行速度极慢，生成512×512图像需52秒，且无法与LCM等加速方法兼容，阻碍了其在交互式内容创作领域的巨大潜力发挥。基于这一观察，我们分两步构建了交互式内容创作解决方案：第一，我们建立了基于区域的控制与扩散模型加速技术之间的兼容性，在推理步数减少10倍的情况下仍能保持多提示词图像生成的高保真度；第二，我们通过全新的多提示词流批处理流水线提升生成吞吐量，可在单张RTX 2080 Ti GPU上基于多个区域文本提示词实现低延迟生成。我们提出的框架可推广至所有现有扩散模型与加速调度器，基于成熟的图像扩散模型即可实现亚秒级的图像内容创作应用，耗时仅0.64秒。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Graph-of-Agents: A Graph-based Framework for Multi-Agent LLM Collaboration
title_zh: Graph-of-Agents：一种基于图的多智能体LLM协作框架
abstract: With an ever-growing zoo of LLMs and benchmarks, the need to orchestrate multiple models for improved task performance has never been more pressing. While frameworks like Mixture-of-Agents (MoA) attempt to coordinate LLMs, they often fall short in terms of (1) selecting relevant agents, (2) facilitating effective intra-agent communication, and (3) integrating responses efficiently. In this work, we propose Graph-of-Agents (GoA), a new graph-based framework for modeling multi-agent LLM communication. Our approach begins with node sampling, selecting only the most relevant agents by leveraging model cards that summarize each model’s domain, task specialization, and other characteristics. Next, we construct edges between the selected agents by evaluating their responses against one another to determine relevance ordering. Directed message passing is then performed from highly relevant agents to less relevant ones to enhance their responses, followed by reverse message passing to refine the original responses of the more relevant agents. Finally, the updated responses are aggregated via graph-based pooling (e.g., max or mean pooling) to produce a single, unified answer. We evaluate GoA on diverse multi-domain benchmarks (MMLU, MMLU-Pro, GPQA) and domain-specific benchmarks (MATH, HumanEval, MedMCQA), with an agent pool of 6 LLMs spanning multiple domains. Surprisingly, GoA achieves superior performance18 using only 3 selected agents, outperforming recent multi-agent LLM baselines that utilize all 6 agents simultaneously. By adopting a graph structure, GoA offers both scalability and effectiveness through structured message passing—positioning it as a strong candidate for navigating the challenges of the ever-growing LLM zoo.
abstract_zh: 随着大语言模型及其基准测试的种类不断增加，为了提升任务性能，对多个模型进行编排协调的需求变得前所未有地迫切。尽管像 Mixture-of-Agents（MoA）这样的框架试图协调大语言模型，但它们通常在以下方面存在不足：（1）选择相关智能体，（2）促进智能体内部的有效通信，以及（3）高效整合响应。在这项工作中，我们提出了 Graph-of-Agents（GoA），一种用于建模多智能体大语言模型通信的全新图结构框架。我们的方法首先从节点采样开始，通过利用模型卡片来仅选择最相关的智能体，模型卡片总结了每个模型的领域、任务专长及其他特征。接下来，我们通过相互评估所选智能体的响应来确定相关性排序，并据此在它们之间构建边。随后，从高度相关的智能体向相关性较低的智能体执行有向消息传递，以增强后者的响应；之后再进行反向消息传递，以细化高相关性智能体的原始响应。最后，通过基于图的池化（例如最大池化或均值池化）对更新后的响应进行聚合，从而生成单一统一的答案。我们在多种跨领域基准（MMLU、MMLU-Pro、GPQA）和特定领域基准（MATH、HumanEval、MedMCQA）上对 GoA 进行了评估，所使用的智能体池由跨越多个领域的 6 个大语言模型组成。令人惊讶的是，GoA 仅使用 3 个选定智能体就实现了更优的性能18，优于同时使用全部 6 个智能体的最新多智能体大语言模型基线方法。通过采用图结构，GoA 通过结构化消息传递同时具备可扩展性和有效性，使其成为应对不断扩张的大语言模型生态所带来挑战的有力候选方案。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Transductive Conformal Inference for Full Ranking
title_zh: 全排序的传递式保形推断
abstract: We introduce a method based on Conformal Prediction (CP) to quantify the uncertainty of full ranking algorithms. We focus on a specific scenario where $n+m$ items are to be ranked by some ``black box'' algorithm. It is assumed that the relative (ground truth) ranking of $n$ of them is known.  The objective is then to quantify the error made by the algorithm on the ranks of the $m$ new items among the total $(n+m)$. In such a setting, the true ranks of the $n$ original items in the total $(n+m)$ depend on the (unknown) true ranks of the $m$ new ones. Consequently, we have no direct access to a calibration set to apply a classical CP method. To address this challenge, we propose to construct distribution-free bounds of the unknown conformity scores using recent results on the distribution of conformal p-values. Using these scores upper bounds, we provide valid prediction sets for the rank of any item. We also control the false coverage proportion, a crucial quantity when dealing with multiple prediction sets. Finally, we empirically show on both synthetic and real data the efficiency of our CP method for state-of-the-art ranking algorithms such as RankNet or LambdaMart.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种基于保形预测（CP）的方法，用于量化全排序算法的不确定性。我们关注这样一种特定情形：有 $n+m$ 个项目需要由某个“黑盒”算法进行排序。假设其中 $n$ 个项目的相对（真实）排序已知。此时的目标是量化该算法对 $m$ 个新项目在总计 $(n+m)$ 个项目中的排名所产生的误差。在这种设定下，这 $n$ 个原始项目在总计 $(n+m)$ 个项目中的真实排名，取决于那 $m$ 个新项目的（未知）真实排名。因此，我们无法直接获得一个校准集来应用经典的 CP 方法。为应对这一挑战，我们利用关于保形 p 值分布的最新结果，提出构造未知一致性分数的分布无关界。利用这些一致性分数的上界，我们为任意项目的排名给出有效的预测集。我们还控制了错误覆盖比例，这在处理多个预测集时是一个关键量。最后，我们在合成数据和真实数据上通过实验表明，对于 RankNet 或 LambdaMart 等最先进的排序算法，我们的 CP 方法是有效的。
primary_research_object: 强化学习 / 序列决策
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: A$^2$Search: Ambiguity-Aware Question Answering with Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: A$^2$Search：结合强化学习的歧义感知问答
abstract:
Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) and Reinforcement Learning (RL) have led to strong performance in open-domain question answering (QA). However, existing models still struggle with questions that admit multiple valid answers. Standard QA benchmarks, which typically assume a single gold answer, overlook this reality and thus produce inappropriate training signals. Existing attempts to handle ambiguity often rely on costly manual annotation, which is difficult to scale to multi-hop datasets such as HotpotQA and MuSiQue.
In this paper, we present A$^2$Search, an annotation-free, end-to-end training framework to recognize and handle ambiguity. At its core is an automated pipeline that detects ambiguous questions and gathers alternative answers via trajectory sampling and evidence verification. The model is then optimized with RL using a carefully designed $\mathrm{AnsF1}$ reward, which naturally accommodates multiple answers.
Experiments on eight open-domain QA benchmarks demonstrate that A$^2$Search achieves new state-of-the-art performance. With only a single rollout, A$^2$Search-7B yields an average $\mathrm{AnsF1}@1$ score of $48.4$% across four multi-hop benchmarks, outperforming all strong baselines, including the substantially larger ReSearch-32B ($46.2$%). Extensive analyses further show that A$^2$Search resolves ambiguity and generalizes across benchmarks, highlighting that embracing ambiguity is essential for building more reliable QA systems.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）和强化学习（RL）的最新进展使开放域问答（QA）取得了强劲性能。然而，现有模型在处理存在多个有效答案的问题时仍然面临困难。标准QA基准通常假设只有一个黄金答案，忽视了这一现实，因此会产生不恰当的训练信号。现有处理歧义的尝试往往依赖成本高昂的人工标注，而这很难扩展到诸如 HotpotQA 和 MuSiQue 这样的多跳数据集。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Replicable Online pricing
title_zh: 可复制的在线定价
abstract:
We explore the concept of replicability, which ensures algorithmic consistency despite input data variations, for online pricing problems, specifically prophet inequalities and delegation. Given the crucial role of replicability in enhancing transparency in economic decision-making, we present a replicable and nearly optimal pricing strategy for prophet inequalities, achieving a sample complexity of 
$\textnormal{poly}(\log^* |\mathcal{X}|)$, where $\mathcal{X}$ is the ground set of distributions. Furthermore, we extend these findings to the delegation problem and establish lower bound that proves the necessity of the $\log^*|\mathcal{X}|$ dependence. En route to obtaining these results, we develop a number of technical contributions which are of independent interest. Most notably, we propose a new algorithm for a variant of the heavy hitter problem, which has a nearly linear dependence on the inverse of the heavy hitter parameter, significantly improving upon existing results which have a cubic dependence.
abstract_zh: 我们探讨了可复制性这一概念，它确保在输入数据发生变化时算法仍保持一致性，并将其应用于在线定价问题，特别是先知不等式和委托问题。鉴于可复制性在提升经济决策透明度方面的关键作用，我们提出了一种适用于先知不等式的可复制且近乎最优的定价策略，其样本复杂度达到
primary_research_object: 强化学习 / 序列决策
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: An Intelligent Agentic System for Complex Image Restoration Problems
title_zh: 一种面向复杂图像复原问题的智能代理系统
abstract: Real-world image restoration (IR) is inherently complex and often requires combining multiple specialized models to address diverse degradations. Inspired by human problem-solving, we propose AgenticIR, an agentic system that mimics the human approach to image processing by following five key stages: Perception, Scheduling, Execution, Reflection, and Rescheduling. AgenticIR leverages large language models (LLMs) and vision-language models (VLMs) that interact via text generation to dynamically operate a toolbox of IR models. We fine-tune VLMs for image quality analysis and employ LLMs for reasoning, guiding the system step by step. To compensate for LLMs' lack of specific IR knowledge and experience, we introduce a self-exploration method, allowing the LLM to observe and summarize restoration results into referenceable documents. Experiments demonstrate AgenticIR's potential in handling complex IR tasks, representing a promising path toward achieving general intelligence in visual processing.
abstract_zh: 真实场景图像复原（IR）本质上具有复杂性，通常需要结合多个专用模型来应对各类退化问题。受人类问题解决过程的启发，我们提出了AgenticIR这一智能体系统，它通过遵循感知、调度、执行、反思、重调度五个核心阶段，模拟人类处理图像的方式。AgenticIR利用大语言模型（LLMs）和视觉语言模型（VLMs），二者通过文本生成实现交互，从而动态调度图像复原模型工具箱。我们对视觉语言模型进行微调以实现图像质量分析，同时利用大语言模型完成推理，逐步引导系统运行。为弥补大语言模型缺乏特定图像复原知识与经验的不足，我们引入了一种自探索方法，使大语言模型能够观察复原结果并将其总结为可参考的文档。实验表明，AgenticIR在处理复杂图像复原任务方面具备潜力，为实现视觉处理领域的通用智能提供了一条前景可观的发展路径。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: TranSUN: A Preemptive Paradigm to Eradicate Retransformation Bias Intrinsically from Regression Models in Recommender Systems
title_zh: TranSUN：一种先发制人的范式，从回归模型中固有消除推荐系统的重复转换偏差
abstract: Regression models are crucial in recommender systems. However, retransformation bias problem has been conspicuously neglected within the community. While many works in other fields have devised effective bias correction methods, all of them are post-hoc cures externally to the model, facing practical challenges when applied to real-world recommender systems. Hence, we propose a preemptive paradigm to eradicate the bias intrinsically from the models via minor model refinement. Specifically, a novel TranSUN method is proposed with a joint bias learning manner to offer theoretically guaranteed unbiasedness under empirical superior convergence. It is further generalized into a novel generic regression model family, termed Generalized TranSUN (GTS), which not only offers more theoretical insights but also serves as a generic framework for flexibly developing various bias-free models. Comprehensive experimental results demonstrate the superiority of our methods across data from various domains, which have been successfully deployed in two real-world industrial recommendation scenarios, i.e. product and short video recommendation scenarios in Guess What You Like business domain in the homepage of Taobao App (a leading e-commerce platform with DAU > 300M), to serve the major online traffic.
abstract_zh: 回归模型在推荐系统中至关重要。然而，重变换偏差问题在该领域内却一直被明显忽视。尽管其他领域的许多工作已经设计出有效的偏差校正方法，但它们全部都是模型外部的事后补救方案，在应用于真实世界推荐系统时面临实际挑战。因此，我们提出了一种预防性范式，通过对模型进行轻微改进，从模型内部根除这种偏差。具体而言，我们提出了一种新的 TranSUN 方法，采用联合偏差学习方式，在经验上具有更优收敛性的同时，提供了理论保证的无偏性。我们进一步将其推广为一个新的通用回归模型家族，称为 Generalized TranSUN（GTS），它不仅提供了更多理论见解，还可作为一个通用框架，用于灵活开发各种无偏模型。全面的实验结果表明，我们的方法在来自多个领域的数据上均表现出优越性，并已成功部署于两个真实工业推荐场景中，即淘宝 App 首页“猜你喜欢”业务域中的商品推荐和短视频推荐场景（该平台是日活跃用户超过 3 亿的领先电商平台），以服务主要在线流量。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: TetraGT: Tetrahedral Geometry-Driven Explicit Token Interactions with Graph Transformer for Molecular Representation Learning
title_zh: TetraGT：基于四面体几何驱动的显式令牌交互图变换器用于分子表征学习
abstract:
Molecular representations that fully capture geometric parameters such as bond angles and torsion angles are crucial for accurately predicting important molecular properties including enzyme catalytic activity, drug bioactivity, and molecular spectral characteristics, as demonstrated by extensive studies.
However, current molecular graph representation learning approaches represent molecular geometric parameters only indirectly through combinations of atoms and bonds, neglecting the spatial relationships and interactions between these higher-order geometric structures.
In this paper, we propose \textbf{TetraGT} (\textbf{Tetra}hedral \textbf{G}eometry-Driven Explicit \textbf{T}oken Interactions with Graph Transformer), a novel architecture that directly models molecular geometric parameters.
Based on the spatial solid geometry theory of face angle and dihedral angle inequality, TetraGT explicitly represents bond angles and torsion angles as structured tokens for the first time, directly reflecting their intrinsic role in determining the molecular conformational stability and properties. 
Through our designed spatial tetrahedral attention mechanism, TetraGT achieves highly selective direct communication between structural tokens.
Experimental results demonstrate that TetraGT achieves superior performance on the PCQM4Mv2 and OC20 IS2RE benchmarks. 
We also apply our pre-trained TetraGT model to downstream tasks including QM9, PDBBind, Peptides and LIT-PCBA, demonstrating that TetraGT delivers excellent results in transfer learning scenarios and shows scalability with increasing molecular size.
abstract_zh: TetraGT通过直接建模分子几何参数，首次将键角与扭转角显式地作为结构化记号，基于面角与二面角不等式空间立体几何原理反映它们在构象稳定性与性质中的核心作用；设计的空间四面体注意力机制令这些结构记号之间实现高选择性的直接通信，从而完整捕捉键角与扭转角等高阶几何信息；在PCQM4Mv2与OC20 IS2RE基准上取得更优结果，迁移到QM9、PDBBind、Peptides与LIT-PCBA等下游任务也表现出色，并随分子尺寸扩展展现良好可扩展性。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Compress then Serve: Serving Thousands of LoRA Adapters with Little Overhead
title_zh: 先压缩再服务：以极少开销部署数千个 LoRA 适配器
abstract: Fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) with low-rank adaptations (LoRAs) has become common practice, often yielding numerous copies of the same LLM differing only in their LoRA updates. This paradigm presents challenges for systems that serve real-time responses to queries that each involve a different LoRA. Prior works optimize the design of such systems but still require continuous loading and offloading of LoRAs, as it is infeasible to store thousands of LoRAs in GPU memory. To mitigate this issue, we investigate the efficacy of compression when serving LoRAs. We propose a method for the joint compression of LoRAs into a shared basis paired with LoRA-specific scaling matrices. We extend our algorithm to learn clusters of LoRAs that are amenable to joint compression, allowing it to scale gracefully to large LoRA collections. Our experiments with up to 1000 LoRAs demonstrate that compressed LoRAs preserve performance while offering major throughput gains in realistic serving scenarios with over a thousand LoRAs, maintaining 80\% of the throughput of serving a single LoRA.
abstract_zh: 使用低秩适配（LoRA）微调大语言模型（LLM）已成为普遍做法，这通常会生成同一大语言模型的大量副本，不同副本之间仅LoRA更新部分存在差异。这种范式为需对各自关联不同LoRA的查询返回实时响应的服务系统带来了挑战。现有研究已针对此类系统的设计进行了优化，但仍需对LoRA进行持续加载与卸载，因为将数千个LoRA全部存储在GPU内存中并不现实。为缓解这一问题，我们研究了压缩技术在LoRA服务场景中的有效性。我们提出一种LoRA联合压缩方法，可将多个LoRA压缩至共享基，并搭配LoRA专属的缩放矩阵。我们进一步扩展了算法，使其能够学习适合联合压缩的LoRA集群，从而可以平滑适配大规模LoRA集合的场景。我们针对最多1000个LoRA开展的实验表明，压缩后的LoRA保留了原有性能，同时在包含上千个LoRA的真实服务场景中实现了吞吐量的大幅提升，可维持单个LoRA服务吞吐量的80%。
notes: 仲裁采纳：codex

title: On Speeding Up Language Model Evaluation
title_zh: 论加速语言模型评估
abstract: Developing prompt-based methods with Large Language Models (LLMs) requires making numerous decisions, which give rise to a combinatorial search problem over hyper-parameters. This exhaustive evaluation can be time-consuming and costly. In this paper, we propose an \textit{adaptive} approach to explore this space. We are exploiting the fact that often only few samples are needed to identify clearly superior or inferior settings, and that many evaluation tests are highly correlated. We lean on multi-armed bandits to sequentially identify the next (method, validation sample)-pair to evaluate and utilize low-rank matrix factorization to fill in missing evaluations. We carefully assess the efficacy of our approach on several competitive benchmark problems and show that it can identify the top-performing method using only 5-15% of the typical resources---resulting in 85-95% LLM cost savings. Our code is available at https://github.com/kilian-group/banditeval.
abstract_zh: 开发基于提示的大语言模型（LLM）方法需要做出大量决策，由此会产生超参数层面的组合搜索问题。这种穷举式评估往往耗时久、成本高。本文提出一种自适应方法来探索该搜索空间。我们利用了两个普遍存在的特征：通常仅需少量样本即可识别出明显更优或更劣的设置，且多数评估测试之间高度相关。我们依托多臂老虎机依次确定下一组待评估的（方法、验证样本）对，同时利用低秩矩阵分解补全缺失的评估结果。我们在多个主流基准问题上对所提方法的有效性进行了严谨测试，结果表明，该方法仅需使用常规所需资源的5%~15%即可识别出性能最优的方法，能够节省85%~95%的大语言模型使用成本。我们的代码可从https://github.com/kilian-group/banditeval获取。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Predicting LLM Output Length via Entropy-Guided Representations
title_zh: 基于熵引导表征的大语言模型输出长度预测
abstract: The long-tailed distribution of sequence lengths in LLM serving and reinforcement learning (RL) sampling causes significant computational waste due to excessive padding in batched inference. Existing methods rely on auxiliary models for static length prediction, but they incur high overhead, generalize poorly, and fail in stochastic "one-to-many" sampling scenarios. We introduce a lightweight framework that reuses the main model's internal hidden states for efficient length prediction. Our framework features two core components: 1) Entropy-Guided Token Pooling (EGTP), which uses on-the-fly activations and token entropy for highly accurate static prediction with negligible cost, and 2) Progressive Length Prediction (PLP), which dynamically estimates the remaining length at each decoding step to handle stochastic generation. To validate our approach, we build and release ForeLen, a comprehensive benchmark with long-sequence, Chain-of-Thought, and RL data. On ForeLen, EGTP achieves state-of-the-art accuracy, reducing MAE by 29.16\% over the best baseline. Integrating our methods with a length-aware scheduler yields significant end-to-end throughput gains. Our work provides a new technical and evaluation baseline for efficient LLM inference.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型服务与强化学习（RL）采样中的序列长度长尾分布，会因批推理过程中的过度填充导致严重的计算浪费。现有方法依赖辅助模型进行静态长度预测，但这类方法开销高、泛化性差，且无法适用于随机“一对多”采样场景。我们提出了一种轻量级框架，通过复用主模型的内部隐状态实现高效长度预测。该框架包含两个核心组件：1）熵引导令牌池化（EGTP），利用实时激活值与令牌熵实现高精度静态预测，且产生的开销可忽略不计；2）渐进式长度预测（PLP），在每个解码步骤动态估计剩余生成长度，以适配随机生成场景。为验证所提方法的有效性，我们构建并发布了综合基准ForeLen，该基准涵盖长序列、思维链及强化学习三类数据。在ForeLen基准上，EGTP取得了当前最优的预测精度，相较于最优基线方法的平均绝对误差（MAE）降低了29.16%。将我们的方法与长度感知调度器集成后，可实现显著的端到端吞吐量提升。本工作为高效大语言模型推理提供了新的技术基线与评估基线。
notes: 调度专项得分=4；命中组=llm_serving；augment_batch=20260402-scheduling；augment_score=4

title: Effects of Dropout on Performance in Long-range Graph Learning Tasks
title_zh: 在长距离图学习任务中 dropout 对性能的影响
abstract: Message Passing Neural Networks (MPNNs) are a class of Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) that propagate information across the graph via local neighborhoods. The scheme gives rise to two key challenges: over-smoothing and over-squashing. While several Dropout-style algorithms, such as DropEdge and DropMessage, have successfully addressed over-smoothing, their impact on over-squashing remains largely unexplored. This represents a critical gap in the literature, as failure to mitigate over-squashing would make these methods unsuitable for long-range tasks – the intended use case of deep MPNNs. In this work, we study the aforementioned algorithms, and closely related edge-dropping algorithms – DropNode, DropAgg and DropGNN – in the context of over-squashing. We present theoretical results showing that DropEdge-variants reduce sensitivity between distant nodes, limiting their suitability for long-range tasks. To address this, we introduce DropSens, a sensitivity-aware variant of DropEdge, which is developed following the message-passing scheme of GCN. DropSens explicitly controls the proportion of information lost due to edge-dropping, thereby increasing sensitivity to distant nodes despite dropping the same number of edges. Our experiments on long-range synthetic and real-world datasets confirm the predicted limitations of existing edge-dropping and feature-dropping methods. Moreover, DropSens with GCN consistently outperforms graph rewiring techniques designed to mitigate over-squashing, suggesting that simple, targeted modifications can substantially improve a model’s ability to capture long-range interactions. Our conclusions highlight the need to re-evaluate and re-design existing methods for training deep GNNs, with a renewed focus on modelling long-range interactions. The code for reproducing the results in our this work is available at https://github.com/ignasa007/Dropout-Effects-GNNs.
abstract_zh: 消息传递神经网络（MPNN）是一类图神经网络（GNN），通过局部邻域在图上传播信息。这种方案带来了两个关键挑战：过平滑和过压缩。尽管若干 Dropout 风格的算法，如 DropEdge 和 DropMessage，已成功应对过平滑问题，但它们对过压缩的影响仍在很大程度上尚未被探索。这在文献中构成了一个关键空白，因为若无法缓解过压缩，这些方法将不适用于长程任务，而长程任务正是深层 MPNN 的预期应用场景。在这项工作中，我们在过压缩的背景下研究了上述算法，以及与之密切相关的丢边算法 DropNode、DropAgg 和 DropGNN。我们给出了理论结果，表明 DropEdge 的各类变体会降低远距离节点之间的敏感性，从而限制其在长程任务中的适用性。为解决这一问题，我们提出了 DropSens，一种具有敏感性感知能力的 DropEdge 变体，并按照 GCN 的消息传递方案进行设计。DropSens 显式控制由于丢边而丢失的信息比例，因此即使丢弃相同数量的边，也能提高对远距离节点的敏感性。我们在长程合成数据集和真实世界数据集上的实验验证了现有丢边和丢特征方法的预期局限性。此外，结合 GCN 的 DropSens 持续优于旨在缓解过压缩的图重连技术，这表明简单而有针对性的修改可以显著提升模型捕捉长程交互的能力。我们的结论强调，需要重新评估并重新设计现有的深层 GNN 训练方法，将建模长程交互重新置于关注重点。用于复现本文结果的代码可在 https://github.com/ignasa007/Dropout-Effects-GNNs 获取。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Forward-Learned Discrete Diffusion: Learning how to noise to denoise faster
title_zh: 前向学习的离散扩散：学习如何加噪以加速去噪
abstract: Discrete diffusion models are a powerful class of generative models that demonstrate strong performance across many domains. However, for efficiency, discrete diffusion typically parameterizes the generative (reverse) process with factorized distributions, which makes it difficult for the model to learn a target process in a small number of steps and necessitates a long, computationally expensive sampling procedure. To reduce the gap between the target and model distributions and enable few-step generation, we introduce a learnable noising (forward) process for discrete diffusion. Instead of fixing a Markovian forward chain, we adopt a non-Markovian formulation and introduce learnable marginal and posterior distributions. This allows the generative process to remain factorized while matching the target defined by the noising process. We train all parameters end-to-end under the standard variational objective.
abstract_zh: 离散扩散模型是一类强大的生成模型，在许多领域都表现出优异性能。然而，出于效率考虑，离散扩散通常使用因子化分布对生成（反向）过程进行参数化，这使得模型难以在少量步数内学习目标过程，并且需要一个漫长且计算代价高昂的采样过程。为缩小目标分布与模型分布之间的差距并实现少步生成，我们为离散扩散引入了一个可学习的加噪（前向）过程。我们不再固定一个马尔可夫前向链，而是采用一种非马尔可夫形式，并引入可学习的边缘分布和后验分布。这使得生成过程在保持因子化的同时，能够匹配由加噪过程定义的目标分布。我们在标准变分目标下对所有参数进行端到端训练。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: ReMoE: Fully Differentiable Mixture-of-Experts with ReLU Routing
title_zh: ReMoE：采用ReLU路由的全可微分混合专家模型
abstract:
Sparsely activated Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models are widely adopted to scale up model capacity without increasing the computation budget. However, vanilla TopK routers are trained in a discontinuous, non-differentiable way, limiting their performance and scalability. 
To address this issue, we propose ReMoE, a fully differentiable MoE architecture that offers a simple yet effective drop-in replacement for the conventional TopK+Softmax routing, utilizing ReLU as the router instead.  We further propose methods to regulate the router's sparsity while balancing the load among experts. ReMoE’s continuous nature enables efficient dynamic allocation of computation across tokens and layers, while also exhibiting domain specialization. Our experiments demonstrate that ReMoE consistently outperforms vanilla TopK-routed MoE across various model sizes, expert counts, and levels of granularity. Furthermore, ReMoE exhibits superior scalability with respect to the number of experts, surpassing traditional MoE architectures. The implementation based on Megatron-LM is available at https://github.com/thu-ml/ReMoE.
abstract_zh:
稀疏激活混合专家（MoE）模型被广泛用于在不增加计算预算的前提下扩大模型容量。然而，常规TopK路由器采用不连续、不可微的方式训练，限制了其性能与可扩展性。
为解决这一问题，我们提出ReMoE，这是一种完全可微的MoE架构，使用ReLU作为路由器，可作为传统TopK+Softmax路由的简单且有效的即插即用替代方案。我们进一步提出了在调节路由器稀疏性的同时实现专家间负载均衡的方法。ReMoE的连续特性支持对跨令牌和跨层的计算资源进行高效动态分配，同时还具备领域专业化特性。我们的实验表明，在不同模型规模、专家数量和粒度水平下，ReMoE的性能始终优于采用常规TopK路由的MoE。此外，ReMoE在专家数量维度上表现出更优异的可扩展性，优于传统MoE架构。基于Megatron-LM的实现可从https://github.com/thu-ml/ReMoE获取。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Beyond Text-Only: Towards Multimodal Table Retrieval in Open-World
title_zh: 超越纯文本：面向开放世界的多模态表格检索
abstract: Open-domain table retrieval aims to retrieve semantically relevant structured tables from a large-scale corpus in response to natural language queries. Unlike unstructured text, tables store information not only through their textual or numerical content but also through their structural properties, including hierarchical relationships between headers and cells, as well as complex spatial arrangements within the table layout. Existing methods predominantly treat table retrieval as a variant of text retrieval. They struggle to accurately preserve the rich structural semantics of diverse table formats during text serialization. Existing methods typically flatten tables into linear text sequences through row-wise or column-wise serialization, inadvertently discarding structural information. The problem becomes particularly acute when processing complex table layouts containing merged cells or irregular alignments, ultimately compromising retrieval performance. Moreover, existing methods struggle to handle embedded images within table cells. Notably, visual representations inherently preserve both structural and content information while being format-agnostic. This insight motivates our exploration of image-based table retrieval, as it can naturally overcome the challenges faced by existing methods. In this paper, we introduce TaR-ViR (Table Retrieval via Visual Representations), a new benchmark that reformulates table retrieval as a multimodal task by treating tables as images. Experiments on TaR-ViR show that this paradigm shift achieved more effective and efficient retrieval performance. Crucially, it eliminates the need for error-prone text conversion, enabling scalable collection and utilization of open-world tables. Our data are available at \url{https://anonymous.4open.science/r/Anonymization-B115}.
abstract_zh: 开放域表格检索旨在从大规模语料库中，根据自然语言查询检索语义相关的结构化表格。与非结构化文本不同，表格不仅通过其文本或数值内容存储信息，还通过其结构属性存储信息，包括表头与单元格之间的层级关系，以及表格布局中的复杂空间排列。现有方法主要将表格检索视为文本检索的一种变体。它们难以在文本序列化过程中准确保留多样化表格格式中丰富的结构语义。现有方法通常通过按行或按列序列化将表格展平成线性文本序列，从而无意中丢弃了结构信息。在处理包含合并单元格或不规则对齐的复杂表格布局时，这一问题尤为突出，最终损害检索性能。此外，现有方法也难以处理表格单元格中嵌入的图像。值得注意的是，视觉表示天然能够同时保留结构信息和内容信息，并且与具体格式无关。这一认识促使我们探索基于图像的表格检索，因为它能够自然地克服现有方法面临的挑战。在本文中，我们提出了 TaR-ViR（通过视觉表示进行表格检索），这是一个新的基准，它通过将表格视为图像，把表格检索重新表述为一项多模态任务。在 TaR-ViR 上的实验表明，这一范式转变实现了更有效且更高效的检索性能。关键的是，它消除了对易出错文本转换的需求，从而能够对开放世界表格进行可扩展的收集与利用。我们的数据可在 \url{https://anonymous.4open.science/r/Anonymization-B115} 获取。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: SpecEdge: Scalable Edge-Assisted Serving Framework for Interactive LLMs
title_zh: SpecEdge：用于交互式大语言模型的可扩展边缘辅助服务框架
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) power many modern applications, but serving them at scale remains costly and resource-intensive. Current server-centric systems overlook consumer-grade GPUs at the edge. We introduce SpecEdge, an edge-assisted inference framework that splits LLM workloads between edge and server GPUs using a speculative decoding scheme, exchanging only token outputs over the network. SpecEdge employs proactive edge drafting to overlap edge token creation with server verification and pipeline-aware scheduling that interleaves multiple user requests to increase server-side throughput. Experiments show SpecEdge enhances overall cost efficiency by **1.91×** through achieving **2.22×** server throughput, and reduces inter token latency by **11.24\%** compared to a server-only baseline, introducing a scalable, cost-effective paradigm for LLM serving. The code is available at https://github.com/kaist-ina/specedge
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）为诸多现代应用提供支撑，但大规模部署这类模型的成本仍然较高，且资源消耗量大。当前以服务器为核心的系统未关注边缘侧的消费级GPU。我们推出SpecEdge，这是一种边缘辅助推理框架，通过投机解码机制在边缘GPU与服务器GPU之间拆分大语言模型工作负载，仅在网络中传输令牌输出。SpecEdge采用主动边缘草稿生成策略，使边缘令牌生成过程与服务器验证过程重叠执行，同时采用流水线感知调度机制交错处理多个用户请求，以提高服务器端吞吐量。实验表明，与仅使用服务器的基线方案相比，SpecEdge实现了2.22倍的服务器吞吐量，将整体成本效率提升了1.91倍，且令牌间延迟降低了11.24%，为大语言模型部署提供了一种可扩展、高性价比的范式。代码可从https://github.com/kaist-ina/specedge获取。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=22；命中=speculative decoding, throughput, latency, serving, inference, decoding, language model, large language model

title: InterMT: Multi-Turn Interleaved Preference Alignment with Human Feedback
title_zh: InterMT：结合人工反馈的多轮交错偏好对齐
abstract:
As multimodal large models (MLLMs) continue to advance across challenging tasks, a key question emerges: \textbf{\textit{What essential capabilities are still missing? }}
A critical aspect of human learning is continuous interaction with the environment -- not limited to language, but also involving multimodal understanding and generation.
To move closer to human-level intelligence, models must similarly support \textbf{multi-turn}, \textbf{multimodal interaction}. In particular, they should comprehend interleaved multimodal contexts and respond coherently in ongoing exchanges.
In this work, we present \textbf{an initial exploration} through the \textsc{InterMT} -- \textbf{the first preference dataset for \textit{multi-turn} multimodal interaction}, grounded in real human feedback. In this exploration, we particularly emphasize the importance of human oversight, introducing expert annotations to guide the process, motivated by the fact that current MLLMs lack such complex interactive capabilities. \textsc{InterMT} captures human preferences at both global and local levels into nine sub-dimensions, consists of 15.6k prompts, 52.6k multi-turn dialogue instances, and 32.4k human-labeled preference pairs. 
To compensate for the lack of capability for multi-modal understanding and generation, we introduce an agentic workflow that leverages tool-augmented MLLMs to construct multi-turn QA instances.
To further this goal, we introduce \textsc{InterMT-Bench} to assess the ability of
MLLMs in assisting judges with multi-turn, multimodal tasks.
We demonstrate the utility of \textsc{InterMT} through applications such as judge moderation and further reveal the \textit{multi-turn scaling law} of judge model.
We hope the open-source of our data can help facilitate further research on aligning current MLLMs to the next step.
abstract_zh: 随着多模态大模型在各种挑战任务上不断突破，一个关键问题浮现：究竟还缺乏哪些关键能力？人类学习的核心在于不断与环境互动，这种互动不仅是语言的，还涵盖多模态的理解与生成。要更接近人类智能，模型也必须支持多轮、多模态的交互，尤其需要理解交错的多模态语境并在持续交流中提供连贯回应。本文通过 InterMT 提出首次面向多轮多模态交互的偏好数据集，该数据集源于真实人类反馈，并在构建过程中注重人类监督，加入专家标注以引导任务，原因是现有多模态大模型尚不具备这类复杂交互能力。InterMT 在整体与局部层面捕获人类偏好、划分九个子维度，包含 1.56 万个提示、5.26 万条多轮对话实例以及 3.24 万对人工标注的偏好对。为弥补模型在多模态理解与生成上的不足，我们引入了由工具增强的多模态大模型构建多轮问答实例的 agentic 工作流。为进一步推进这项工作，我们发布 InterMT-Bench，用于评估多模态大模型在辅助人类评审多轮任务中的能力。通过裁判调度等应用演示 InterMT 的价值，并发现裁判模型的“多轮扩展规律”。我们希望开源数据能促进将现有多模态大模型对齐到下一阶段的研究。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Exploring Tradeoffs through Mode Connectivity for Multi-Task Learning
title_zh: 探索多任务学习中通过模式连通性权衡的方式
abstract: Nowadays deep models are required to be versatile due to the increasing realistic needs. Multi-task learning (MTL) offers an efficient way for this purpose to learn multiple tasks simultaneously with a single model. However, prior MTL solutions often focus on resolving conflicts and imbalances during optimization, which may not outperform simple linear scalarization strategies~\citep{xin2022current}. Instead of altering the optimization trajectory, this paper leverages mode connectivity to efficiently approach the Pareto front and identify the desired trade-off point. Unlike Pareto Front Learning (PFL), which aims to align with the entire Pareto front, we focus on effectively and efficiently exploring optimal trade-offs. However, three challenges persist: (1) the low-loss path can neither fully traverse trade-offs nor align with user preference due to its randomness, (2) commonly adopted Bézier curves in mode connectivity are ill-suited to navigating the complex loss landscapes of deep models, and (3) poor scalability to large-scale task scenarios. To address these challenges, we adopt non-uniform rational B-Splines (NURBS) to model mode connectivity, allowing for more flexible and precise curve optimization. Additionally, we introduce an order-aware objective to explore task loss trade-offs and employ a task grouping strategy to enhance scalability under massive task scenarios. Extensive experiments on key MTL datasets demonstrate that our proposed method, *EXTRA* (EXplore TRAde-offs), effectively identifies the desired point on the Pareto front and achieves state-of-the-art performance. *EXTRA* is also validated as a plug-and-play solution for mainstream MTL approaches.
abstract_zh: 如今，随着现实需求的不断增长，深度模型需要具备更强的通用性。多任务学习（MTL）为此提供了一种高效方式，使单个模型能够同时学习多个任务。然而，以往的多任务学习方法通常侧重于解决优化过程中的冲突与不平衡问题，而这未必优于简单的线性标量化策略~\citep{xin2022current}。本文不改变优化轨迹，而是利用模态连通性高效逼近帕累托前沿，并识别所需的权衡点。不同于旨在贴合整个帕累托前沿的帕累托前沿学习（PFL），我们关注的是高效且有效地探索最优权衡。然而，仍存在三个挑战：（1）低损失路径由于其随机性，既无法完整遍历各种权衡，也难以与用户偏好对齐；（2）模态连通性中常用的贝塞尔曲线并不适合在深度模型复杂的损失景观中进行导航；（3）在大规模任务场景下可扩展性较差。为应对这些挑战，我们采用非均匀有理B样条（NURBS）对模态连通性进行建模，从而实现更灵活且更精确的曲线优化。此外，我们引入了一个顺序感知目标来探索任务损失之间的权衡，并采用任务分组策略以提升在海量任务场景下的可扩展性。在关键多任务学习数据集上的大量实验表明，我们提出的方法 *EXTRA*（EXplore TRAde-offs）能够有效识别帕累托前沿上的目标点，并取得最先进的性能。*EXTRA* 还被验证为适用于主流多任务学习方法的即插即用解决方案。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: SLiM: One-shot Quantization and Sparsity with Low-rank Approximation for LLM Weight Compression
title_zh: SLiM：一种用于大模型权重压缩的低秩近似一次性量化与稀疏方法
abstract:
Conventional model compression techniques for LLMs address high memory consumption and slow inference challenges but typically require computationally expensive retraining to preserve accuracy. In contrast, one-shot compression methods eliminate retraining cost, but struggle to achieve accuracy comparable to dense models. This paper presents SLIM, a new one-shot compression framework that holistically integrates hardware-friendly quantization, sparsity, and low-rank approximation into a unified process. First, we formulate the quantization process using a probabilistic approach (SLIM-Quant) that enables us to apply uniform quantization. Then, we use an existing one-shot pruning method to apply semi-structured sparsity on top of the quantized weights. Finally, to compensate for the introduced aggregated quantization and sparsity error, we use a novel saliency function with unique invertible and additive features that enables us to
mathematically compute the value of low-rank adapters. SLIM improves model accuracy by up to 5.66% (LLaMA-2-7B) for 2:4 sparsity with 4-bit weight quantization, outperforming prior methods. Models compressed with SLIM achieve up to 4.3× and 3.8× on Nvidia RTX3060 and A100 GPUs, respectively. Additionally, they achieve up to 0.23× end-to-end memory reduction in comparison to their dense counterparts. We also propose an optional PEFT recipe that further improves accuracy
by up to 1.66% (LLaMA-2-13B) compared to SLIM without fine-tuning.
abstract_zh: 针对大语言模型的传统模型压缩技术可解决高内存占用与推理速度慢的难题，但通常需要计算成本高昂的重训练过程来维持精度。与之相反，单次压缩方法无需重训练成本，但难以达到与稠密模型相当的精度。本文提出SLIM，这是一种全新的单次压缩框架，将硬件友好型量化、稀疏化与低秩近似全面整合到统一流程中。首先，我们采用概率方法（SLIM-Quant）对量化过程进行公式化表达，从而可实现均匀量化。随后，我们采用现有的单次剪枝方法在量化后的权重上施加半结构化稀疏性。最后，为了补偿量化与稀疏化引入的累积误差，我们使用一种具备独特可逆性与可加性特征的新型显著性函数，可通过数学计算得到低秩适配器的取值。针对采用4位权重量化的2:4稀疏模型，SLIM将LLaMA-2-7B的模型精度最高提升了5.66%，性能优于现有方法。经SLIM压缩的模型在英伟达RTX3060和A100 GPU上的推理速度分别最高可达原有模型的4.3倍和3.8倍。此外，与对应的稠密模型相比，此类压缩模型的端到端内存占用最多可缩减为原来的23%。我们还提出了一种可选的参数高效微调（PEFT）方案，与未经过微调的SLIM模型相比，该方案可将LLaMA-2-13B的精度进一步提升最高1.66%。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=21；命中=quantization, quantized, 4-bit, pruning, sparsity, compression, compressed, inference

title: Brain-tuning Improves Generalizability and Efficiency of Brain Alignment in Speech Models
title_zh: Brain-tuning 提升语音模型脑对齐的泛化能力与效率
abstract: Pretrained language models are remarkably effective in aligning with human brain responses elicited by natural language stimuli, positioning them as promising model organisms for studying language processing in the brain. However, existing approaches for both estimating and improving this brain alignment are participant-dependent and highly affected by the amount of data available per participant, hindering both generalization to new participants and population-level analyses. In this work, we address these limitations by introducing a scalable, generalizable brain-tuning method, in which we fine-tune pretrained speech language models to jointly predict fMRI responses from multiple participants. We demonstrate that the resulting brain-tuned models exhibit strong individual brain alignment while generalizing across participants. Specifically, our method leads to 1) a 5-fold decrease in the amount of fMRI data needed to predict brain data from new participants,  2) up to a 50\% increase in the overall brain alignment, and 3) strong generalization to new unseen datasets. Furthermore, this multi-participant brain-tuning additionally improves downstream performance on semantic tasks, suggesting that training using brain data from multiple participants leads to more generalizable semantic representations. Taken together, these findings demonstrate a bidirectional benefit between neuroscience and AI, helping bridge the gap between the two fields. We make our code and models publicly available at https://github.com/bridge-ai-neuro/multi-brain-tuning.
abstract_zh: 预训练语言模型在与自然语言刺激诱发的人类脑反应对齐方面表现出显著效果，这使其成为研究大脑语言处理机制的有前景的模型有机体。然而，现有用于估计和改进这种脑对齐的方法都依赖于参与者个体，并且高度受每位参与者可用数据量的影响，从而阻碍了对新参与者的泛化以及群体层面的分析。在这项工作中，我们通过引入一种可扩展、可泛化的脑调优方法来解决这些限制，其中我们对预训练语音语言模型进行微调，使其能够联合预测来自多个参与者的 fMRI 反应。我们证明，由此得到的脑调优模型在表现出强个体脑对齐的同时，还能跨参与者泛化。具体而言，我们的方法实现了：1）将预测新参与者脑数据所需的 fMRI 数据量减少了 5 倍；2）整体脑对齐最高提升了 50%；3）对新的未见数据集具有很强的泛化能力。此外，这种多参与者脑调优还提升了语义任务上的下游性能，这表明利用多个参与者的脑数据进行训练能够得到更具泛化性的语义表征。总的来说，这些发现表明神经科学与人工智能之间存在双向收益，有助于弥合这两个领域之间的鸿沟。我们已将代码和模型公开发布于 https://github.com/bridge-ai-neuro/multi-brain-tuning。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: MIND: Material Interface Generation from UDFs for Non-Manifold Surface Reconstruction
title_zh: MIND：基于UDF生成材料界面以进行非流形表面重建
abstract: Unsigned distance fields (UDFs) are widely used in 3D deep learning due to their ability to represent shapes with arbitrary topology. While prior work has largely focused on learning UDFs from point clouds or multi-view images, extracting meshes from UDFs remains challenging, as the learned fields rarely attain exact zero distances. A common workaround is to reconstruct signed distance fields (SDFs) locally from UDFs to enable surface extraction via Marching Cubes. However, this often introduces topological artifacts such as holes or spurious components. Moreover, local SDFs are inherently incapable of representing non-manifold geometry, leading to complete failure in such cases. To address this gap, we propose MIND ($\underline{M}aterial$ $\underline{I}nterface$ $from$ $\underline{N}on$-$manifold$ $\underline{D}istance$ $fields$), a novel algorithm for generating material interfaces directly from UDFs, enabling non-manifold mesh extraction from a global perspective. The core of our method lies in deriving a meaningful spatial partitioning from the UDF, where the target surface emerges as the interface between distinct regions. We begin by computing a two-signed local field to distinguish the two sides of manifold patches, and then extend this to a multi-labeled global field capable of separating all sides of a non-manifold structure. By combining this multi-labeled field with the input UDF, we construct material interfaces that support non-manifold mesh extraction via a multi-labeled Marching Cubes algorithm. Extensive experiments on UDFs generated from diverse data sources, including point cloud reconstruction, multi-view reconstruction, and medial axis transforms, demonstrate that our approach robustly handles complex non-manifold surfaces and significantly outperforms existing methods. The source code is available at https://github.com/jjjkkyz/MIND.
abstract_zh: 无符号距离场（UDF）因其能够表示具有任意拓扑的形状而在三维深度学习中被广泛使用。尽管先前工作主要集中于从点云或多视图图像中学习 UDF，但从 UDF 中提取网格仍然具有挑战性，因为学习得到的场很少能达到精确的零距离。一个常见的变通方法是从 UDF 局部重建有符号距离场（SDF），以便通过 Marching Cubes 提取表面。然而，这往往会引入诸如孔洞或伪连通分量之类的拓扑伪影。此外，局部 SDF 本质上无法表示非流形几何，因此在这类情况下会完全失效。为了解决这一空白，我们提出了 MIND（从非流形距离场生成材料界面），这是一种新颖的算法，可直接从 UDF 生成材料界面，从而以全局视角实现非流形网格提取。我们方法的核心在于从 UDF 中导出一种有意义的空间划分，其中目标表面作为不同区域之间的界面出现。我们首先计算一个双符号局部场，以区分流形片两侧；随后将其扩展为一个多标签全局场，从而能够分离非流形结构的所有侧面。通过将这个多标签场与输入 UDF 结合，我们构建出材料界面，并借助多标签 Marching Cubes 算法支持非流形网格提取。在由多种数据源生成的 UDF 上进行的大量实验，包括点云重建、多视图重建和中轴变换，表明我们的方法能够稳健处理复杂的非流形表面，并显著优于现有方法。源代码可在 https://github.com/jjjkkyz/MIND 获取。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: ResQ: Mixed-Precision Quantization of Large Language Models with Low-Rank Residuals
title_zh: ResQ：带低秩残差的大型语言模型混合精度量化
abstract: Post-training quantization (PTQ) of large language models (LLMs) holds the promise in reducing the prohibitive computational cost at inference time. Quantization of all weight, activation and key-value (KV) cache tensors to 4-bit without significantly degrading generalizability is challenging, due to the high quantization error caused by extreme outliers in activations. To tackle this problem, we propose ResQ, a PTQ method that pushes further the state-of-the-art. By means of principal component analysis (PCA), it identifies a low-rank subspace (in practice 1/8 of the hidden dimension) in which activation variances are highest, and keep the coefficients within this subspace in high precision, e.g.~8-bit, while quantizing the rest to 4-bit. Within each subspace, invariant random rotation is applied to further suppress outliers.  We show that this is a provably optimal mixed precision quantization scheme that minimizes error. With the Llama and Qwen2.5 families of models, we demonstrate that ResQ outperforms recent uniform and mixed precision PTQ methods on a variety of benchmarks, achieving up to 33\% lower perplexity on Wikitext than the next best method SpinQuant, and upto 3X speedup over 16-bit baseline. Anonymous code repository available  at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/project-resq-2142.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型的训练后量化（PTQ）有望降低推理阶段极高的计算成本。将所有权重、激活值以及键值（KV）缓存张量量化至4比特同时不显著降低泛化能力是一项颇具挑战的任务，原因在于激活值中的极端离群点会带来很高的量化误差。为解决这一问题，我们提出ResQ，这是一种进一步提升当前最优水平的训练后量化方法。该方法通过主成分分析（PCA）识别出激活值方差最高的低秩子空间（实际为隐藏维度的1/8），将该子空间内的系数保持在高精度（例如8比特），同时将其余部分量化至4比特。在每个子空间内，我们应用不变随机旋转以进一步抑制离群点。我们证明这是一种可证最优的混合精度量化方案，能够最小化误差。我们在Llama和Qwen2.5系列模型上开展实验，结果表明ResQ在各类基准测试上的表现优于近年提出的均匀及混合精度训练后量化方法：在Wikitext数据集上，ResQ的困惑度较次优方法SpinQuant最高可降低33%，同时相比16比特基线最高可实现3倍的加速。匿名代码仓库可在https://anonymous.4open.science/r/project-resq-2142获取。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=23；命中=kv cache, cache compression, quantization, 4-bit, compression, mixed-precision, inference, language model

title: How Particle System Theory Enhances Hypergraph Message Passing
title_zh: 粒子系统理论如何增强超图消息传递
abstract: Hypergraphs effectively model higher-order relationships in natural phenomena, capturing complex interactions beyond pairwise connections. We introduce a novel hypergraph message passing framework inspired by interacting particle systems, where hyperedges act as fields inducing shared node dynamics. By incorporating attraction, repulsion, and Allen-Cahn forcing terms, particles of varying classes and features achieve class-dependent equilibrium, enabling separability through the particle-driven message passing. We investigate both first-order and second-order particle system equations for modeling these dynamics, which mitigate over-smoothing and heterophily thus can capture complete interactions. The more stable second-order system permits deeper message passing. Furthermore, we enhance deterministic message passing with stochastic element to account for interaction uncertainties. We prove theoretically that our approach mitigates over-smoothing by maintaining a positive lower bound on the hypergraph Dirichlet energy during propagation and thus to enable hypergraph message passing to go deep. Empirically, our models demonstrate competitive performance on diverse real-world hypergraph node classification tasks, excelling on both homophilic and heterophilic datasets. Source code is available at \href{https://github.com/Xuan-Elfin/HAMP}{the link}.
abstract_zh: 超图能够有效建模自然现象中的高阶关系，捕捉超越成对连接的复杂交互。我们受相互作用粒子系统启发，引入了一种新颖的超图消息传递框架，其中超边充当诱导共享节点动力学的场。通过引入吸引、排斥和 Allen-Cahn 强迫项，不同类别和特征的粒子能够达到依赖类别的平衡，从而通过粒子驱动的消息传递实现可分离性。我们研究了一阶和二阶粒子系统方程来建模这些动力学，这些方程能够缓解过平滑和异配性，因此可以捕捉完整的交互。更稳定的二阶系统允许更深层的消息传递。此外，我们还为确定性消息传递引入随机元素，以考虑交互中的不确定性。我们从理论上证明了，该方法通过在传播过程中保持超图狄利克雷能量的正下界来缓解过平滑，从而使超图消息传递能够进行深层传播。实验上，我们的模型在多样化的真实世界超图节点分类任务上展现出有竞争力的性能，并在同配和异配数据集上都表现出色。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Incentivizing Truthful Language Models via Peer Elicitation Games
title_zh: 通过同行引导博弈激励语言模型讲真话
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong generative capabilities but remain prone to inconsistencies and hallucinations. We introduce Peer Elicitation Games (PEG), a training-free, game-theoretic framework for aligning LLMs through a peer elicitation mechanism involving a generator and multiple discriminators instantiated from distinct base models. Discriminators interact in a peer evaluation setting, where utilities are computed using a determinant-based mutual information score that provably incentivizes truthful reporting without requiring ground-truth labels. We establish theoretical guarantees showing that each agent, via online learning, achieves sublinear regret in the sense their cumulative performance approaches that of the best fixed truthful strategy in hindsight. Moreover, we prove last-iterate convergence to a truthful Nash equilibrium, ensuring that the actual policies used by agents converge to stable and truthful behavior over time. Empirical evaluations across multiple benchmarks demonstrate significant improvements in factual accuracy. These results position PEG as a practical approach for eliciting truthful behavior from LLMs without supervision or fine-tuning.
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）已展现出强大的生成能力，但仍然容易出现不一致性和幻觉。我们提出了同行诱导博弈（PEG），这是一种无需训练、基于博弈论的框架，通过一种涉及一个生成器和多个由不同基础模型实例化的判别器的同行诱导机制来对齐大型语言模型。判别器在同行评估环境中进行交互，其中效用通过一种基于行列式的互信息分数来计算。该分数在理论上能够激励真实报告，且无需真实标签。我们建立了理论保证，表明每个智能体通过在线学习都能实现次线性遗憾，即其累积表现会逼近事后看来最佳的固定真实策略。此外，我们证明了末次迭代收敛到一个真实的纳什均衡，从而确保智能体实际使用的策略会随着时间推移收敛到稳定且真实的行为。在多个基准上的实证评估表明，事实准确性得到了显著提升。这些结果表明，PEG 是一种无需监督或微调即可诱导大型语言模型表现出真实行为的实用方法。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Towards Personalized Deep Research: Benchmarks and Evaluations
title_zh: 迈向个性化深度研究：基准与评估
abstract: Deep Research Agents (DRAs) can autonomously conduct complex investigations and generate comprehensive reports, demonstrating strong real-world potential. However, existing evaluations mostly rely on close-ended benchmarks, while open-ended deep research benchmarks remain scarce and typically neglect personalized scenarios. To bridge this gap, we introduce Personalized Deep Research Bench, the first benchmark for evaluating personalization in DRAs. It pairs 50 diverse research tasks across 10 domains with 25 authentic user profiles that combine structured persona attributes with dynamic real-world contexts, yielding 250 realistic user-task queries. To assess system performance, we propose the PQR Evaluation Framework, which jointly measures (P) Personalization Alignment, (Q) Content Quality, and (R) Factual Reliability. Our experiments on a range of systems highlight current capabilities and limitations in handling personalized deep research. This work establishes a rigorous foundation for developing and evaluating the next generation of truly personalized AI research assistants.
abstract_zh: 深度研究智能体（DRA）能够自主开展复杂调查并生成全面报告，展现出强大的现实世界潜力。然而，现有评测大多依赖封闭式基准，而开放式深度研究基准仍然稀缺，并且通常忽视个性化场景。为弥补这一空白，我们引入了 Personalized Deep Research Bench，这是首个用于评估 DRA 中个性化能力的基准。它将涵盖 10 个领域的 50 个多样化研究任务，与 25 个真实用户画像相配对；这些用户画像将结构化的人设属性与动态的现实世界情境相结合，从而生成 250 个真实的用户任务查询。为评估系统性能，我们提出了 PQR 评测框架，联合衡量（P）个性化对齐、（Q）内容质量和（R）事实可靠性。我们在一系列系统上的实验突出了当前系统在处理个性化深度研究方面的能力与局限性。这项工作为开发和评估下一代真正个性化的人工智能研究助手奠定了严格的基础。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: VideoMind: A Chain-of-LoRA Agent for Temporal-Grounded Video Reasoning
title_zh: VideoMind：用于时间锚定视频推理的链式 LoRA 智能体
abstract: Videos, with their unique temporal dimension, demand precise grounded understanding, where answers are directly linked to visual, interpretable evidence. Despite significant breakthroughs in text-based reasoning with large language models, multi-modal reasoning - especially for videos - remains limited. In this work, we fill this gap by introducing VideoMind, a novel video-language agent for temporal-grounded video reasoning. Our method involves two key innovations: (1) We identify four essential capabilities for grounded video reasoning and propose a role-based agentic workflow, comprising a planner to coordinate roles, a grounder for temporal event localization, a verifier to assess event candidates, and an answerer for question answering. (2) To efficiently integrate these roles during inference, we propose a novel Chain-of-LoRA mechanism, where a unified base model with multiple LoRA adapters is leveraged to enable seamless role switching, balancing efficiency and flexibility. Extensive experiments on 15 benchmarks across Grounded VideoQA, Video Temporal Grounding, and General VideoQA tasks demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed scheme in advancing video agent, test-time scaling, and long-form video reasoning. Code, models, datasets, and demos are available at https://videomind.github.io/.
abstract_zh: 视频由于其独特的时间维度，要求精确的有依据理解，即答案必须与可解释的视觉证据直接对应。尽管大型语言模型在基于文本的推理方面取得了显著突破，多模态推理，尤其是面向视频的推理，仍然受到限制。在这项工作中，我们通过提出 VideoMind 来填补这一空白。VideoMind 是一种新颖的视频语言智能体，用于时间定位的视频推理。我们的方法包含两项关键创新：（1）我们识别出有依据的视频推理所需的四种核心能力，并提出了一种基于角色的智能体工作流，其中包括用于协调各角色的规划器、用于时间事件定位的定位器、用于评估事件候选的验证器，以及用于问答的回答器。（2）为了在推理过程中高效整合这些角色，我们提出了一种新颖的 Chain-of-LoRA 机制，其中利用带有多个 LoRA 适配器的统一基础模型来实现无缝角色切换，在效率与灵活性之间取得平衡。在 Grounded VideoQA、视频时间定位和通用 VideoQA 任务上的 15 个基准数据集的大量实验表明，所提出方案在推动视频智能体、测试时扩展以及长视频推理方面具有有效性。代码、模型、数据集和演示可见于 https://videomind.github.io/。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: OrthoRank: Token Selection via Sink Token Orthogonality for Efficient LLM inference
title_zh: OrthoRank通过Sink Token正交性进行标记选择以实现高效LLM推理
abstract: Attention mechanisms are central to the success of large language models (LLMs), enabling them to capture intricate token dependencies and implicitly assign importance to each token. Recent studies have revealed the sink token, which receives disproportionately high attention despite their limited semantic role. In this paper, we first expand the relationship between the sink token and other tokens, moving beyond attention to explore their similarity in hidden states, considering the layer depth. We observe that as the layers get deeper, the cosine similarity between the normalized hidden states of the sink token and those of other tokens increases, and that the normalized hidden states of the sink token exhibit negligible changes. These imply that other tokens consistently are directed toward the sink token throughout the layers. Next, we propose a dynamic token selection method, called OrthoRank, using these findings to select important tokens. Specifically, in a certain layer, we define token importance by the speed at which the token moves toward the sink token. This is converted into orthogonality with the sink token, meaning that tokens that are more orthogonal to the sink token are assigned greater importance. Finally, through extensive experiments, we demonstrated that our method results in lower perplexity and higher zero-shot accuracy compared to layer pruning methods at the same sparsity ratio with comparable throughput, while also achieving superior performance on LongBench.
abstract_zh: 注意力机制是大语言模型（LLM）取得成功的核心，它能够让大语言模型捕捉复杂的令牌依赖关系，同时隐式地为每个令牌分配重要性。近期研究发现了汇令牌，这类令牌的语义作用有限，却获得了不成比例的高注意力权重。本文首先拓展了汇令牌与其他令牌之间的关系，跳出注意力层面，结合层深度探究二者在隐状态上的相似性。我们观察到，随着层深度增加，汇令牌的归一化隐状态与其他令牌的归一化隐状态之间的余弦相似度会上升，且汇令牌的归一化隐状态的变化可忽略不计。这说明在所有层的计算过程中，其他令牌始终向汇令牌的方向趋近。接下来，我们基于上述发现提出了名为OrthoRank的动态令牌选择方法，用于筛选重要令牌。具体而言，在某一层中，我们通过令牌向汇令牌趋近的速度来定义其重要性，该指标可转化为令牌与汇令牌的正交性，即与汇令牌正交性越高的令牌，被分配的重要性越高。最后，通过大量实验我们证明，在相同稀疏度、吞吐量相当的条件下，与层剪枝方法相比，我们的方法实现了更低的困惑度和更高的零样本准确率，同时在LongBench基准测试上也取得了更优的性能。
notes: 论文核心是面向LLM推理阶段的动态token选择，通过层内重要性排序减少计算，属于模型推理过程中的结构侧优化，而非系统调度或参数压缩。

title: RD-HRL: Generating Reliable Sub-Goals for Long-Horizon Sparse-Reward Tasks
title_zh: RD-HRL：为长周期稀疏奖励任务生成可靠子目标
abstract: Long-horizon sparse-reward tasks, such as goal-conditioned or robot manipulation tasks, remain challenging in offline reinforcement learning due to the credit assignment problem. Hierarchical methods have been proposed to tackle this problem by introducing sub-goal planning guided by value functions, which in principle can shorten the effective planning horizon for both high-level and low-level planners, and thereby avoiding  the credit assignment problem. However, we demonstrate that the sub-goal selection mechanism is unreliable, as it relies on value functions suffering from generalization noise, which misguides value estimation and thus leads to sub-optimal sub-goals. In this work, to provide more reliable sub-goals, we novelly introduce a reliability-driven decision mechanism, and propose Reliability-Driven HRL (RD-HRL) as the solution. The reliability-driven decision mechanism provide decision-level targets from transition regions, thereby providing noise-immune decision spaces for high-level policy, ensuring the reliability of sub-goals (which are termed as action-level targets in this paper). Comprehensive experimental results demonstrate that our approach RD-HRL outperforms baseline methods across multiple benchmarks, highlighting the competitive advantages of RD-HRL. Our code is anonymously available at \url{https://anonymous.4open.science/r/RD-HRL-243D }.
abstract_zh: 长时程稀疏奖励任务，例如目标条件任务或机器人操作任务，由于信用分配问题，在离线强化学习中仍然具有挑战性。为了解决这一问题，已有研究提出了层次化方法，通过由价值函数引导的子目标规划，从原理上能够缩短高层和低层规划器的有效规划时域，从而避免信用分配问题。然而，我们证明了子目标选择机制是不可靠的，因为它依赖于受到泛化噪声影响的价值函数，这会误导价值估计，进而导致次优的子目标。在这项工作中，为了提供更可靠的子目标，我们创新性地引入了一种可靠性驱动的决策机制，并提出了可靠性驱动层次强化学习（RD-HRL）作为解决方案。该可靠性驱动决策机制从转移区域中提供决策级目标，从而为高层策略提供抗噪声的决策空间，确保子目标（在本文中称为动作级目标）的可靠性。全面的实验结果表明，我们的方法 RD-HRL 在多个基准测试中优于基线方法，凸显了 RD-HRL 的竞争优势。我们的代码已匿名公开于 https://anonymous.4open.science/r/RD-HRL-243D。
primary_research_object: 强化学习 / 序列决策
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Unlocking the Power of Multi-Agent LLM for Reasoning: From Lazy Agents to Deliberation
title_zh: 解锁多智能体大语言模型推理能力：从懒惰智能体到深思
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) trained with reinforcement learning and verifiable rewards have achieved strong results on complex reasoning tasks. Recent work extends this paradigm to a multi-agent setting, where a meta-thinking agent proposes plans and monitors progress while a reasoning agent executes subtasks through sequential conversational turns. Despite promising performance, we identify a critical limitation: lazy agent behavior, in which one agent dominates while the other contributes little, undermining collaboration and collapsing the setup to an ineffective single agent.  In this paper, we first provide a theoretical analysis showing why lazy behavior naturally arises in multi-agent reasoning. We then introduce a stable and efficient method for measuring causal influence, helping mitigate this issue. Finally, as collaboration intensifies, the reasoning agent risks getting lost in multi-turn interactions and trapped by previous noisy responses. To counter this, we propose a verifiable reward mechanism that encourages deliberation by allowing the reasoning agent to discard noisy outputs, consolidate instructions, and restart its reasoning process when necessary. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our framework alleviates lazy agent behavior and unlocks the full potential of multi-agent framework for complex reasoning tasks.
abstract_zh:
通过强化学习和可验证奖励训练的大语言模型（LLM）在复杂推理任务上已取得出色成果。近期工作将这一范式扩展到多智能体设定中，其中一个元思考智能体负责提出计划并监控进展，而另一个推理智能体则通过顺序的对话轮次执行子任务。尽管性能前景可观，我们发现了一个关键局限：惰性智能体行为，即一个智能体占据主导而另一个几乎没有贡献，这会破坏协作，并使该设定退化为低效的单智能体。
在本文中，我们首先给出理论分析，说明为何惰性行为会在多智能体推理中自然出现。随后，我们引入一种稳定且高效的因果影响测量方法，以帮助缓解这一问题。最后，随着协作程度加深，推理智能体面临在多轮交互中迷失并受困于先前噪声响应的风险。为应对这一问题，我们提出一种可验证的奖励机制，通过允许推理智能体丢弃噪声输出、整合指令，并在必要时重新启动其推理过程，来鼓励审慎推理。大量实验表明，我们的框架缓解了惰性智能体行为，并释放了多智能体框架在复杂推理任务中的全部潜力。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Multipole Attention for Efficient Long Context Reasoning
title_zh: Multipole Attention 用于高效长上下文推理
abstract:
Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) have shown promising accuracy improvements on complex problem-solving tasks. While these models have attained high accuracy by leveraging additional computation at test time, they need to generate long chain-of-thought reasoning in order to think before answering, which requires generating thousands of tokens.
While sparse attention methods can help reduce the KV cache pressure induced by this long autoregressive reasoning, these methods can introduce errors which disrupt the reasoning process.
Our work addresses these challenges by introducing Multipole Attention, which accelerates autoregressive reasoning by only computing exact attention for the most important tokens, while maintaining approximate representations for the remaining tokens. 
Our method first performs clustering to group together semantically similar key vectors, and then uses the cluster centroids both to identify important key vectors and to approximate the remaining key vectors in order to retain high accuracy.
Additionally, in order to accelerate long generation tasks, we design a fast cluster update process to quickly re-cluster the input and previously generated tokens, thereby allowing for  accelerating attention to the previous output tokens.
We evaluate our method using emerging LRMs such as Qwen-8B and Deepseek-R1-Distil-Qwen2.5-14B, demonstrating that our approach can maintain accuracy on complex reasoning tasks even with aggressive attention sparsity settings.
We also provide kernel implementations to demonstrate the practical efficiency gains from our method, achieving up to 4.5$\times$ speedup for attention in long-context reasoning applications.
abstract_zh:
大型推理模型（LRMs）在复杂问题求解任务上已展现出可观的准确率提升前景。这类模型通过在测试阶段利用额外计算资源实现了高准确率，但为了在作答前完成思考，它们需要生成冗长的思维链推理过程，这需要生成数千个词元。
稀疏注意力方法虽可缓解这类长自回归推理带来的KV缓存压力，但此类方法可能引入错误，干扰推理过程。
本研究通过提出多极注意力应对上述挑战，该方法仅对最重要的词元计算精确注意力，同时为其余词元保留近似表示，以此加速自回归推理。
本方法首先执行聚类，将语义相似的键向量分组，随后利用聚类质心识别重要键向量，同时对其余键向量做近似处理，从而维持高准确率。
此外，为加速长生成任务，我们设计了快速聚类更新流程，可对输入和先前已生成的词元快速重新聚类，从而加快针对先前输出词元的注意力计算。
我们采用Qwen-8B、Deepseek-R1-Distil-Qwen2.5-14B等新兴大型推理模型对所提方法进行评估，结果表明，即使采用极高的注意力稀疏度设置，我们的方法仍能在复杂推理任务上维持准确率。
我们还提供了内核实现，验证了该方法可带来实际效率提升，在长上下文推理应用中，注意力计算的加速比最高可达4.5倍。
notes: 标题与摘要片段直接指向长上下文推理效率问题，最匹配“上下文与缓存优化”。未看到足够证据支持其他偏好标签。

title: Jackpot: Align Actor-Policy Distribution for scalable and stable RL for LLM
title_zh: Jackpot：对齐行动者-策略分布以实现可扩展且稳定的大语言模型强化学习
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become an increasingly important paradigm for improving large language models (LLMs) on alignment, reasoning, and coding tasks, yet it remains extremely costly. The majority of training time is spent on rollouts. Allowing actor and policy distributions to differ could unlock substantial scalability and efficiency benefits, such as supporting large-batch or asynchronous training, and even enabling a lightweight rollout model. However, existing importance sampling–based corrections for distribution mismatch suffer from an inherent trade-off between stability and training performance. To tackle this problem, we propose Jackpot, which leverages Optimal Budget Rejection Sampling to directly reduce the gap between actor and policy distributions. For efficiency and stability in practical training, We introduce an efficient probability estimation strategy based on Top-$K$ logits with batch bias correction, and designs a stabilized Jackpot-PPO loss that jointly accounts for both the importance sampling ratio and the trust-region constraint in PPO. Empirically, our method achieves stable improvements in large-batch and asynchronous training, and in extreme off-policy training it substantially delays the onset of collapse and delivers competitive performance. Specifically, we achieve 20\% improvement on AMC benchmarks and ~8\% AIME benchmarks over the off-policy baseline under 128$\times$ actor-policy update ratio for Qwen3-4B-Base and 64$\times$ for Qwen3-8B-Base, while achieving greater stability and better performance than prior off-policy RL methods under extreme settings.
abstract_zh: 强化学习（RL）已成为提升大语言模型（LLM）在对齐、推理和代码任务上表现的一个日益重要的范式，但其成本仍然极高。绝大多数训练时间都消耗在 rollout 上。允许 actor 分布与 policy 分布不同，可能带来显著的可扩展性和效率收益，例如支持大批量或异步训练，甚至能够启用轻量级 rollout 模型。然而，现有基于重要性采样的分布失配校正方法，存在稳定性与训练性能之间的内在权衡。为了解决这一问题，我们提出了 Jackpot，该方法利用最优预算拒绝采样直接缩小 actor 分布与 policy 分布之间的差距。为了在实际训练中兼顾效率与稳定性，我们引入了一种基于 Top-$K$ logits 并结合批偏差校正的高效概率估计策略，并设计了一种稳定化的 Jackpot-PPO 损失，同时联合考虑 PPO 中的重要性采样比率和信赖域约束。实验结果表明，我们的方法在大批量和异步训练中实现了稳定提升；在极端 off-policy 训练中，它显著延缓了崩溃的发生，并取得了有竞争力的性能。具体而言，在 Qwen3-4B-Base 的 128$\times$ actor-policy 更新比率以及 Qwen3-8B-Base 的 64$\times$ 更新比率下，相较于 off-policy 基线，我们在 AMC 基准上取得了 20\% 的提升，在 AIME 基准上取得了约 8\% 的提升；同时，在极端设置下，相比先前的 off-policy RL 方法实现了更高的稳定性和更好的性能。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: You Only Prune Once: Designing Calibration-Free Model Compression With Policy Learning
title_zh: 一次剪枝即可：通过策略学习设计免校准的模型压缩
abstract: The ever-increasing size of large language models (LLMs) presents significant challenges for deployment due to their heavy computational and memory requirements. Current model pruning techniques attempt to alleviate these issues by relying heavily on external calibration datasets to determine which parameters to prune or compress, thus limiting their flexibility and scalability across different compression ratios. Moreover, these methods often cause severe performance degradation, particularly in downstream tasks, when subjected to higher compression rates. In this paper, we propose *PruneNet*, a novel model compression method that addresses these limitations by reformulating model pruning as a policy learning process. PruneNet decouples the pruning process from the model architecture, eliminating the need for calibration datasets. It learns a stochastic pruning policy to assess parameter importance solely based on intrinsic model properties while preserving the spectral structure to minimize information loss. PruneNet can compress the LLaMA-2-7B model in just 15 minutes, achieving over 80\% retention of its zero-shot performance with a 30\% compression ratio, outperforming existing methods that retain only 75\% performance. Furthermore, on complex multitask language understanding tasks, PruneNet demonstrates its robustness by preserving up to 80\% performance of the original model, proving itself a superior alternative to conventional structured compression techniques.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型的规模不断增大，其极高的计算与内存需求给部署带来了严峻挑战。现有模型剪枝技术试图缓解这类问题，但该类技术高度依赖外部校准数据集来确定需要剪枝或压缩的参数，因此限制了其在不同压缩率下的灵活性与可扩展性。此外，在采用更高压缩率时，这些方法往往会造成严重的性能下降，下游任务中的性能下降尤为明显。本文提出一种新型模型压缩方法PruneNet，该方法将模型剪枝重构为策略学习过程，从而解决上述局限性。PruneNet将剪枝过程与模型架构解耦，无需使用校准数据集。它学习一种随机剪枝策略，仅基于模型固有属性评估参数重要性，同时保留频谱结构以最大限度减少信息损失。PruneNet仅需15分钟即可完成LLaMA-2-7B模型的压缩，在30%压缩率下零样本性能保留率超过80%，优于仅能保留75%性能的现有方法。此外，在复杂多任务语言理解任务中，PruneNet最多可保留原始模型80%的性能，展现出鲁棒性，证明其是优于传统结构化压缩技术的可选方案。
notes: 核心贡献是面向 LLM 的校准集无关结构化剪枝/压缩方法，将剪枝建模为策略学习；与上下文缓存或系统调度无直接关系。

title: SciTS: Scientific Time Series Understanding and Generation with LLMs
title_zh: SciTS：基于大语言模型的科学时间序列理解与生成
abstract: The scientific reasoning ability of large language models (LLMs) has recently attracted significant attention. Time series, as a fundamental modality in scientific data, presents unique challenges that are often overlooked in current multimodal LLMs, which either encode numerical sequences as text or convert them into images. Such approaches may be insufficient for comprehensive scientific time series understanding and generation. Existing unified time series models typically specialise in either forecasting or analysis, and their effectiveness on non-periodic, heterogeneous scientific signals remains unclear. To address these gaps, we introduce SciTS, a benchmark spanning 12 scientific domains and 43 tasks, with over 50k+ instances, both univariate and multivariate signals ranging from $10^0$ to $10^7$ in length and up to 10~MHz in frequency. We benchmark 17 models, including text-only LLMs, multimodal LLMs, and unified time series models, and find that general-purpose LLMs exhibit stronger generalisability than specialised time series models, while representing time series as text or images limits their performance due to excessively long sequences and loss of numerical precision, respectively. We then introduce TimeOmni, a working example to explore insights into how LLMs can be extended to handle scientific time series while remaining compatible with general-purpose LLM training. This work fills a gap in both dedicated benchmarks and illustrative frameworks for scientific time series, paving the way for LLMs to understand and generate complex temporal scientific data.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）的科学推理能力近来受到广泛关注。时间序列作为科学数据中的一种基础模态，带来了当前多模态 LLM 常常忽视的独特挑战；这些模型通常要么将数值序列编码为文本，要么将其转换为图像。这类方法可能不足以实现对科学时间序列的全面理解与生成。现有统一时间序列模型通常只专注于预测或分析中的一类任务，而它们在非周期性、异构科学信号上的有效性仍不明确。为弥补这些空白，我们提出了 SciTS，一个覆盖 12 个科学领域和 43 项任务的基准，包含超过 5 万个实例，涵盖单变量与多变量信号，长度范围从 $10^0$ 到 $10^7$，频率最高可达 10~MHz。我们对 17 个模型进行了基准测试，其中包括纯文本 LLM、多模态 LLM 以及统一时间序列模型，并发现通用 LLM 比专门的时间序列模型表现出更强的泛化能力；同时，将时间序列表示为文本或图像会分别因序列过长和数值精度损失而限制其性能。随后，我们提出了 TimeOmni，作为一个可运行示例，用于探索如何在保持与通用 LLM 训练兼容的同时，将 LLM 扩展到处理科学时间序列。该工作填补了科学时间序列在专用基准和示例性框架两方面的空白，为 LLM 理解和生成复杂时序科学数据铺平了道路。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: R-WoM: Retrieval-augmented World Model For Computer-use Agents
title_zh: R-WoM：面向计算机使用代理的检索增强世界模型
abstract:
Large Language Models (LLMs) can serve as world models to enhance agent decision-making in digital environments by simulating future states and predicting action outcomes, potentially eliminating costly trial-and-error exploration. However, this capability is fundamentally limited by LLM’s tendency to hallucination and their reliance on static training knowledge, which could lead to compounding errors that inhibit long-horizon simulations. To systematically investigate whether LLMs are appropriate for world modeling, we probe two core capabilities of world models – future state prediction and reward estimation – through three tasks: next-state identification, full-procedure planning alignment, and milestone transition recognition. Our analysis shows that while LLMs effectively capture immediate next states and identify meaningful state transitions, their performance rapidly degrades in full-procedure planning. This highlights LLMs’ limitations in reliably modeling environment dynamics over long horizons. To address these limitations, we propose the Retrieval-augmented World Model (R-WoM), which grounds LLM simulations by incorporating factual, up-to-date knowledge retrieved from external tutorials. Experiments show that R-WoM achieves substantial improvements of up to 25.3% (OSWorld) and 18.1% (WebArena) com-
pared to baselines, with particular advantage in longer-horizon simulations.
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）可以充当世界模型，通过模拟未来状态并预测动作结果来增强智能体在数字环境中的决策能力，从而有可能消除代价高昂的试错式探索。然而，这种能力从根本上受限于 LLM 易产生幻觉以及依赖静态训练知识的倾向，这可能导致误差不断累积，从而阻碍长时程模拟。为了系统研究 LLM 是否适合作为世界模型，我们通过三个任务来探查世界模型的两项核心能力，即未来状态预测和奖励估计：下一状态识别、完整过程规划对齐以及里程碑状态转移识别。我们的分析表明，尽管 LLM 能有效捕捉紧接着的下一状态并识别有意义的状态转移，但其在完整过程规划中的性能会迅速下降。这凸显了 LLM 在长时程范围内可靠建模环境动态方面的局限性。为解决这些局限，我们提出了检索增强世界模型（R-WoM），通过引入从外部教程中检索到的事实性、最新知识，为 LLM 的模拟提供依据。实验表明，R-WoM 在 OSWorld 上最高实现了 25.3% 的显著提升，在 WebArena 上最高实现了 18.1% 的显著提升。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Puzzles: Unbounded Video-Depth Augmentation for Scalable End-to-End 3D Reconstruction
title_zh: Puzzles：用于可扩展端到端 3D 重建的无限视频深度增强
abstract: Multi-view 3D reconstruction remains a core challenge in computer vision. Recent methods, such as DUSt3R and its successors, directly regress pointmaps from image pairs without relying on known scene geometry or camera parameters. However, the performance of these models is constrained by the diversity and scale of available training data. In this work, we introduce Puzzles, a data augmentation strategy that synthesizes an unbounded volume of high-quality, posed video-depth data from just a single image or video clip. By simulating diverse camera trajectories and realistic scene geometry through targeted image transformations, Puzzles significantly enhances data variety. Extensive experiments show that integrating Puzzles into existing video‑based 3D reconstruction pipelines consistently boosts performance, all without modifying the underlying network architecture. Notably, models trained on only 10% of the original data, augmented with Puzzles, achieve accuracy comparable to those trained on the full dataset.
abstract_zh: 多视角三维重建仍然是计算机视觉中的核心挑战。近年来的方法，如 DUSt3R 及其后续工作，能够直接从图像对回归点图，而无需依赖已知的场景几何或相机参数。然而，这些模型的性能受限于可用训练数据的多样性和规模。在这项工作中，我们提出了 Puzzles，这是一种数据增强策略，仅凭单张图像或单个视频片段即可合成数量无界的高质量、带位姿的视频-深度数据。通过有针对性的图像变换来模拟多样的相机轨迹和真实的场景几何，Puzzles 显著提升了数据多样性。大量实验表明，将 Puzzles 集成到现有的基于视频的三维重建流程中，能够在不修改底层网络架构的情况下持续提升性能。值得注意的是，仅使用原始数据集 10% 的数据并结合 Puzzles 增强训练的模型，其精度即可达到与使用完整数据集训练的模型相当的水平。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。
