Code-switching, especially between Arabic and English, is common in the Arab world and is widely used in digital communication. This linguistic mixing poses extra challenges for deepfake detection, as it can confuse models trained mostly on monolingual data. To address this, we introduce ArEnAV, the first large-scale Arabic-English audio-visual deepfake dataset featuring intra-utterance code-switching, dialectal variation, and monolingual Arabic content. It contains 387k videos and over 765 hours of real and fake videos. Our dataset is generated using a novel pipeline integrating four Text-To-Speech and two lip-sync models, enabling comprehensive analysis of multilingual multimodal deepfake detection. We benchmark our dataset against existing monolingual and multilingual datasets, state-of-the-art deepfake detection models, and a human evaluation, highlighting its potential to advance deepfake research. The dataset is [public](https://huggingface.co/datasets/kartik060702/ArEnAV-Full).
abstract_zh: 深度伪造生成方法正在快速演进，使得伪造媒体更难检测，并引发了严重的社会担忧。大多数深度伪造检测和数据集构建研究聚焦于单语内容，往往忽视了多语和语码转换语音中的挑战，即在同一段话语中混合多种语言。语码转换，尤其是阿拉伯语和英语之间的转换，在阿拉伯世界十分常见，并被广泛用于数字通信。这种语言混合给深度伪造检测带来了额外挑战，因为它可能会干扰主要基于单语数据训练的模型。为此，我们提出了 ArEnAV，这是首个大规模阿拉伯语-英语音视频深度伪造数据集，具有话语内语码转换、方言变体以及单语阿拉伯语内容。该数据集包含 38.7 万个视频，以及超过 765 小时的真实和伪造视频。我们的数据集通过一种新颖的流程生成，该流程集成了四种文本转语音模型和两种唇形同步模型，从而能够对多语多模态深度伪造检测进行全面分析。我们将该数据集与现有单语和多语数据集、最先进的深度伪造检测模型以及人工评估进行了基准比较，凸显了其推动深度伪造研究发展的潜力。该数据集已公开。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: PMark: Towards Robust and Distortion-free Semantic-level Watermarking with Channel Constraints
title_zh: PMark：面向通道约束的鲁棒且无失真语义级水印
abstract: Semantic-level watermarking (SWM) for large language models (LLMs) enhances watermarking robustness against text modifications and paraphrasing attacks by treating the sentence as the fundamental unit. However, existing methods still lack strong theoretical guarantees of robustness, and reject-sampling–based generation often introduces significant distribution distortions compared with unwatermarked outputs. In this work, we introduce a new theoretical framework on SWM through the concept of proxy functions (PFs) -- functions that map sentences to scalar values. Building on this framework, we propose **PMark**, a simple yet powerful SWM method that estimates the PF median for the next sentence dynamically through sampling while enforcing multiple PF constraints (which we call channels) to strengthen watermark evidence. Equipped with solid theoretical guarantees, **PMark** achieves the desired distortion-free property and improves the robustness against paraphrasing-style attacks. We also provide an empirically optimized version that further removes the requirement for dynamical median estimation for better sampling efficiency. Experimental results show that **PMark** consistently outperforms existing SWM baselines in both text quality and robustness, offering a more effective paradigm for detecting machine-generated text. The source code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/PMark.
abstract_zh: 面向大型语言模型（LLM）的语义层水印（SWM）将句子视为基本单位，从而增强了水印对文本修改和释义攻击的鲁棒性。然而，现有方法仍然缺乏关于鲁棒性的强理论保证，而且基于拒绝采样的生成方式常常会相较于无水印输出引入显著的分布失真。在这项工作中，我们通过代理函数（PF）的概念为 SWM 引入了一个新的理论框架。代理函数是将句子映射为标量值的函数。基于这一框架，我们提出了PMark，这是一种简单但强大的 SWM 方法：它通过采样动态估计下一个句子的 PF 中位数，同时施加多个 PF 约束（我们称之为通道）以增强水印证据。在坚实的理论保证支持下，PMark 实现了所需的无失真性质，并提升了对释义类攻击的鲁棒性。我们还提供了一个经过经验优化的版本，进一步去除了动态中位数估计的需求，以获得更高的采样效率。实验结果表明，PMark 在文本质量和鲁棒性方面均持续优于现有的 SWM 基线方法，为检测机器生成文本提供了一种更有效的范式。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: TidalDecode: Fast and Accurate LLM Decoding with Position Persistent Sparse Attention
title_zh: TidalDecode：具有位置持久稀疏注意力的快速且准确的大语言模型解码
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have driven significant advancements across diverse NLP tasks, with long-context models gaining prominence for handling extended inputs. However, the expanding key-value (KV) cache size required by Transformer architectures intensifies the memory constraints, particularly during the decoding phase, creating a significant bottleneck. Existing sparse attention mechanisms designed to address this bottleneck have two limitations: (1) they often fail to reliably identify the most relevant tokens for attention, and (2) they overlook the spatial coherence of token selection across consecutive Transformer layers, which can lead to performance degradation and substantial overhead in token selection. This paper introduces TidalDecode, a simple yet effective algorithm and system for fast and accurate LLM decoding through position persistent sparse attention. TidalDecode leverages the spatial coherence of tokens selected by existing sparse attention methods and introduces a few token selection layers that perform full attention to identify the tokens with the highest attention scores, while all other layers perform sparse attention with the pre-selected tokens. This design enables TidalDecode to substantially reduce the overhead of token selection for sparse attention without sacrificing the quality of the generated results. Evaluation on a diverse set of LLMs and tasks shows that TidalDecode closely matches the generative performance of full attention methods while reducing the LLM decoding latency by up to $2.1\times$.
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）已推动各类自然语言处理（NLP）任务取得显著进展，其中长上下文模型因可处理超长输入日益受到关注。然而，Transformer架构所需的键值（KV）缓存规模不断扩大，加剧了内存限制，该问题在解码阶段尤为突出，已形成严重瓶颈。现有用于解决该瓶颈的稀疏注意力机制存在两点局限：一是这类机制通常无法可靠识别与注意力最相关的令牌；二是它们忽略了连续Transformer层之间令牌选择的空间一致性，这会导致性能下降，同时带来极高的令牌选择开销。本文提出TidalDecode，这是一种通过位置持久稀疏注意力实现快速精准LLM解码的简单却有效的算法与系统。TidalDecode利用现有稀疏注意力方法所选令牌的空间一致性，引入少量令牌选择层执行全注意力以识别注意力得分最高的令牌，其余所有层则基于预先选定的令牌执行稀疏注意力。该设计使TidalDecode能够大幅降低稀疏注意力的令牌选择开销，同时不会牺牲生成结果的质量。在不同LLM和任务上的评估结果表明，TidalDecode的生成性能与全注意力方法十分接近，同时可将LLM解码延迟最高降低2.1倍。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: HashAttention: Semantic Sparsity for Faster Inference
title_zh: HashAttention：为更快推理带来语义稀疏性
abstract:
Leveraging long contexts is crucial for advanced AI systems, but attention computation poses a scalability challenge. While scaled dot-product attention (SDPA) exhibits token sparsity, i.e. only a few pivotal tokens significantly contribute to output, exploiting this sparsity remains challenging. Existing methods either suffer from quality degradation or require substantial additional resources. We show that identifying pivotal tokens is a Maximum Inner Product Search (MIPS) problem. However, existing MIPS solutions are not well-suited for SDPA, as they are not GPU-friendly and often underperform due to the separated query and key distributions. This paper introduces HashAttention, framing pivotal token identification as a recommendation problem. Given a query, HashAttention encodes keys and queries in Hamming space, capturing the required semantic similarity, using learned mapping functions. HashAttention efficiently identifies pivotal tokens for a given query using bitwise operations and computes attention using only these tokens, improving the overall attention efficiency. Trained on generic data, HashAttention reduces tokens used by up to $16\times$ with minimal quality loss, requiring only 32 bits of auxiliary memory per token. Sparsity can be further improved to $32\times$ through task-specific fine-tuning. 
On A100 GPU, at $32\times$ sparsity, incorporating HashAttention reduces attention latency by up to $4.3\times$ in GPT-FAST and $2.54\times$ in FlashDecode, and achieves up to $3.12\times$ higher throughput for GPT-FAST.
abstract_zh: 利用长上下文对先进AI系统至关重要，但注意力计算带来了可扩展性挑战。尽管缩放点积注意力（SDPA）存在令牌稀疏性，即仅有少数关键令牌对输出有显著贡献，但利用这种稀疏性仍面临挑战。现有方法要么存在质量下降问题，要么需要大量额外资源。我们证明，识别关键令牌属于最大内积搜索（MIPS）问题。但现有MIPS解决方案并不适配缩放点积注意力，因为它们对GPU不友好，且往往会因查询和键分布分离而表现不佳。本文提出HashAttention，将关键令牌识别转化为推荐问题。针对给定查询，HashAttention使用学习到的映射函数将键和查询编码到汉明空间，捕获所需的语义相似度。HashAttention通过位运算高效识别给定查询对应的关键令牌，仅使用这些令牌计算注意力，从而提升整体注意力效率。在通用数据上训练的HashAttention最多可将使用的令牌数减少16倍，且质量损失极小，每个令牌仅需32位辅助内存。通过特定任务微调，稀疏度可进一步提升至32倍。在A100 GPU上，稀疏度为32倍时，集成HashAttention可使GPT-FAST的注意力延迟最多降低4.3倍，FlashDecode的注意力延迟最多降低2.54倍，且GPT-FAST的吞吐量最多可提升3.12倍。
primary_research_object: AI 系统 / 基础设施
notes: 工作核心是通过改造注意力检索与计算路径来减少推理时参与 attention 的 token 数，属于长上下文推理优化；未聚焦解码策略、调度、内核或压缩。

title: Regret-Guided Search Control for Efficient Learning in AlphaZero
title_zh: 遗憾引导搜索控制以提升 AlphaZero 中的高效学习
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) agents achieve remarkable performance but remain far less learning efficient than humans. While RL agents require extensive self-play games to extract useful signals, humans often need only a few games, improving rapidly by repeatedly revisiting states where mistakes occurred. This idea, known as search control, aims to restart from valuable states rather than always from the initial state. In AlphaZero, prior work Go-Exploit applies this idea by sampling past states from self-play or search trees, but it treats all states equally, regardless of their learning potential. We propose Regret-Guided Search Control (RGSC), which extends AlphaZero with a regret network that learns to identify high-regret states, where the agent's evaluation diverges most from the actual outcome. These states are collected from both self-play trajectories and MCTS nodes, stored in a prioritized regret buffer, and reused as new starting positions. Across 9×9 Go, 10×10 Othello, and 11×11 Hex, RGSC outperforms AlphaZero and Go-Exploit by an average of 77 and 89 Elo, respectively. When training on a well-trained 9×9 Go model, RGSC further improves the win rate against KataGo from 69.3% to 78.2%, while both baselines show no improvement. These results demonstrate that RGSC provides an effective mechanism for search control, improving both efficiency and robustness of AlphaZero training.
abstract_zh: 强化学习（RL）智能体取得了显著的性能，但其学习效率仍远低于人类。RL 智能体需要进行大量自我博弈才能提取有用信号，而人类通常只需少量对局，并通过反复回顾出错状态而迅速提升。这一思想被称为搜索控制，其目标是从有价值的状态重新开始，而不是总是从初始状态开始。在 AlphaZero 中，先前工作 Go-Exploit 通过从自我博弈轨迹或搜索树中采样过去状态来应用这一思想，但它不区分状态的学习潜力，对所有状态一视同仁。我们提出了后悔引导的搜索控制（RGSC），通过引入一个后悔网络来扩展 AlphaZero，该网络学习识别高后悔状态，即智能体评估与实际结果偏差最大的状态。这些状态从自我博弈轨迹和 MCTS 节点中收集，存储于一个按优先级排列的后悔缓冲区中，并被复用为新的起始位置。在 9×9 围棋、10×10 黑白棋和 11×11 Hex 上，RGSC 相较于 AlphaZero 和 Go-Exploit 分别平均提升了 77 和 89 Elo。当在一个训练良好的 9×9 围棋模型上继续训练时，RGSC 还将对 KataGo 的胜率从 69.3% 提升至 78.2%，而两个基线方法都没有带来提升。这些结果表明，RGSC 为搜索控制提供了一种有效机制，提升了 AlphaZero 训练的效率和鲁棒性。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Agent-Oriented Planning in Multi-Agent Systems
title_zh: 多智能体系统中的面向智能体规划
abstract: Through the collaboration of multiple LLM-empowered agents possessing diverse expertise and tools, multi-agent systems achieve impressive progress in solving real-world problems. Given the user queries, the meta-agents, serving as the brain within multi-agent systems, are required to decompose the queries into multiple sub-tasks that can be allocated to suitable agents capable of solving them, so-called agent-oriented planning. In this study, we identify three critical design principles of agent-oriented planning, including solvability, completeness, and non-redundancy, to ensure that each sub-task can be effectively resolved, resulting in satisfactory responses to user queries. These principles further inspire us to propose AOP, a novel framework for agent-oriented planning in multi-agent systems, leveraging a fast task decomposition and allocation process followed by an effective and efficient evaluation via a reward model. According to the evaluation results, the meta-agent is also responsible for promptly making necessary adjustments to sub-tasks and scheduling. Besides, we integrate a feedback loop into AOP to further enhance the effectiveness and robustness of such a problem-solving process. Extensive experiments demonstrate the advancement of AOP in solving real-world problems compared to both single-agent systems and existing planning strategies for multi-agent systems. The source code is available at https://github.com/lalaliat/Agent-Oriented-Planning
abstract_zh: 通过多个具备不同专业知识与工具、受大语言模型赋能的智能体开展协作，多智能体系统在解决现实世界问题领域取得了令人瞩目的进展。针对用户查询，作为多智能体系统中承担“大脑”职能的元智能体需要将查询拆解为多个子任务，分配给有能力解决对应子任务的合适智能体，这一过程即为面向智能体的规划。本研究明确了面向智能体规划的三项关键设计原则，即可解性、完备性与非冗余性，以确保每个子任务都能得到有效解决，最终生成令用户满意的查询响应。受这些原则启发，我们提出了AOP，一种面向多智能体系统的新型面向智能体规划框架，该框架首先执行快速任务拆解与分配流程，随后通过奖励模型开展有效且高效的评估。元智能体还需根据评估结果及时对子任务和调度方案做出必要调整。此外，我们在AOP中集成了反馈回路，以进一步提升该问题解决流程的有效性与鲁棒性。大量实验表明，与单智能体系统以及现有的多智能体系统规划策略相比，AOP在解决现实世界问题方面具备明显优势。源代码可在https://github.com/lalaliat/Agent-Oriented-Planning获取。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: ResearchRubrics: A Benchmark of Prompts and Rubrics For Deep Research Agents
title_zh: ResearchRubrics：用于深度研究代理的提示与评分标准基准
abstract:
Deep Research (DR) is an emerging agent application that leverages large language models (LLMs) to address open-ended queries. It requires the integration of several capabilities, including multi-step reasoning, cross-document synthesis, and the generation of evidence-backed, long-form answers. Evaluating DR remains challenging because responses are lengthy and diverse, admit many valid solutions, and often depend on dynamic information sources. We introduce ResearchRubrics, a standardized benchmark for DR built with over 2,800+ hours of human labor that pairs realistic, domain-diverse prompts with 2,500+ expert-written, fine-grained rubrics to assess factual grounding, reasoning soundness, and clarity. We also propose a new complexity framework for categorizing DR tasks along three axes: conceptual breadth, logical nesting, and exploration. In addition, we develop human and model-based evaluation protocols that measure rubric adherence for DR agents. We evaluate several state-of-the-art DR systems and find that even leading agents like Gemini’s DR and OpenAI’s DR achieve under 68% average compliance with our rubrics, primarily due to missed implicit context and inadequate reasoning about retrieved information. Our results highlight the need for robust, scalable assessment of deep research capabilities, to which end we release ResearchRubrics (including all prompts, rubrics, and evaluation code) to facilitate
progress toward well-justified research assistants.
abstract_zh: 深度研究（DR）是一种新兴的智能体应用，它利用大语言模型（LLM）来处理开放式查询。它需要整合多种能力，包括多步推理、跨文档综合，以及生成有证据支撑的长篇回答。由于回答篇幅长且形式多样、存在许多有效解答，并且往往依赖动态信息源，对 DR 进行评估仍然具有挑战性。我们提出了 ResearchRubrics，这是一个用于 DR 的标准化基准，由超过 2800 小时的人力劳动构建而成，将真实、领域多样的提示与 2500 多份由专家撰写的细粒度评分量表配对，用于评估事实依据、推理可靠性和表述清晰度。我们还提出了一个新的复杂度框架，从三个维度对 DR 任务进行分类：概念广度、逻辑嵌套和探索性。此外，我们开发了基于人工和模型的评估协议，用于衡量 DR 智能体对评分量表的遵循情况。我们评估了若干最先进的 DR 系统，发现即使是 Gemini 的 DR 和 OpenAI 的 DR 这样的领先智能体，其对我们评分量表的平均符合率也低于 68%，主要原因在于遗漏隐含上下文，以及对检索到的信息推理不足。我们的结果凸显了对深度研究能力进行稳健、可扩展评估的必要性；为此，我们发布了 ResearchRubrics（包括所有提示、评分量表和评估代码），以促进这一目标的实现。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: OligoGym: Curated Datasets and Benchmarks for Oligonucleotide Drug Discovery
title_zh: OligoGym：为寡核苷酸药物发现准备的精选数据集与基准
abstract: Oligonucleotide therapeutics offer great potential to address previously undruggable targets and enable personalized medicine. However, their progress is often hindered by insufficient safety and efficacy profiles. Predictive modeling and machine learning could significantly accelerate oligonucleotide drug discovery by identifying suboptimal compounds early on, but their application in this area lags behind other modalities. A key obstacle to the adoption of machine learning in the field is the scarcity of readily accessible and standardized datasets for model development, as data are often scattered across diverse experiments with inconsistent molecular representations. To overcome this challenge, we introduce OligoGym, a curated collection of standardized, machine learning-ready datasets encompassing various oligonucleotide therapeutic modalities and endpoints. We used OligoGym to benchmark diverse classical and deep learning methods, establishing performance baselines for each dataset across different featurization techniques, model configurations, and splitting strategies. Our work represents a crucial first step in creating a more unified framework for oligonucleotide therapeutic dataset generation and model training.
abstract_zh: 寡核苷酸疗法在应对以往难以成药的靶点和实现个体化医疗方面具有巨大潜力。然而，其发展常常受限于安全性和有效性特征不足。预测建模和机器学习有望通过及早识别次优化合物来显著加速寡核苷酸药物发现，但它们在这一领域的应用仍落后于其他治疗模态。机器学习在该领域推广应用的一个关键障碍，是缺乏便于获取且标准化的数据集用于模型开发，因为相关数据通常分散于各种实验之中，且分子表示方式并不一致。为克服这一挑战，我们提出了 OligoGym，这是一个经过整理的标准化、适用于机器学习的数据集集合，涵盖多种寡核苷酸治疗模态和终点。我们利用 OligoGym 对多种经典方法和深度学习方法进行了基准测试，为每个数据集在不同特征化技术、模型配置和划分策略下建立了性能基线。我们的工作是朝着为寡核苷酸治疗数据集生成和模型训练构建更统一框架迈出的关键第一步。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: UniOD: A Universal Model for Outlier Detection across Diverse Domains
title_zh: UniOD：跨多样领域的通用异常检测模型
abstract:
Outlier detection (OD), distinguishing inliers and outliers in completely unlabeled datasets, plays a vital role in science and engineering. Although there have been many insightful OD methods, most of them require troublesome hyperparameter tuning (a challenge in unsupervised learning) and costly model training for every task or dataset. In this work, we propose UniOD, a universal OD framework that leverages labeled datasets to train a single model capable of detecting outliers of datasets with different feature dimensions and heterogeneous feature spaces from diverse domains. Specifically, UniOD extracts uniform and comparable features across different datasets by constructing and factorizing multi-scale point-wise similarity matrices. It then employs graph neural networks to capture comprehensive within-dataset and between-dataset information simultaneously, and formulates outlier detection tasks as node classification tasks. 
As a result, once the training is complete, UniOD can identify outliers in datasets from diverse domains without any further model/hyperparameter selection and parameter optimization, which greatly improves convenience and accuracy in real applications. More importantly, we provide theoretical guarantees for the effectiveness of UniOD, consistent with our numerical results. We evaluate UniOD on 30 benchmark OD datasets against 17 baselines, demonstrating its effectiveness and superiority.
abstract_zh: 异常值检测（OD）是在完全无标签的数据集中区分正常样本和异常样本，在科学与工程中发挥着重要作用。尽管已经提出了许多富有洞见的OD方法，但其中大多数都需要繁琐的超参数调优（这是无监督学习中的一项挑战），并且针对每个任务或数据集都需要进行高成本的模型训练。在这项工作中，我们提出了UniOD，一个通用的OD框架，它利用有标签数据集训练单个模型，使其能够检测来自不同领域、具有不同特征维度和异构特征空间的数据集中的异常值。具体而言，UniOD通过构建并分解多尺度逐点相似度矩阵，从不同数据集中提取统一且可比较的特征。随后，它采用图神经网络同时捕获数据集内和数据集间的全面信息，并将异常值检测任务表述为节点分类任务。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Sampling from multi-modal distributions with polynomial query complexity in fixed dimension via reverse diffusion
title_zh: 通过逆向扩散在固定维度下以多项式查询复杂度从多模态分布采样
abstract: Even in low dimensions, sampling from multi-modal distributions is challenging. We provide the first sampling algorithm for a broad class of distributions --- including all Gaussian mixtures --- with a query complexity that is polynomial in the parameters governing multi-modality, assuming fixed dimension. Our sampling algorithm simulates a time-reversed diffusion process, using a self-normalized Monte Carlo estimator of the intermediate score functions. Unlike previous works, it avoids metastability, requires no prior knowledge of the mode locations, and relaxes the well-known log-smoothness assumption which excluded general Gaussian mixtures so far.
abstract_zh: 即使在低维情况下，从多峰分布中采样也是具有挑战性的。我们为一大类分布给出了首个采样算法，在假设维度固定的情况下，其查询复杂度关于控制多峰性的参数是多项式级的，这类分布包括所有高斯混合分布。我们的采样算法通过使用中间得分函数的自归一化蒙特卡洛估计量，来模拟一个时间反向的扩散过程。不同于以往工作，它避免了亚稳性，不需要预先知道各个模态的位置，并放宽了著名的对数平滑性假设，而该假设此前一直将一般的高斯混合分布排除在外。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: EMOS: Embodiment-aware Heterogeneous Multi-robot Operating System with LLM Agents
title_zh: EMOS：搭载大语言模型智能体的具身感知异构多机器人操作系统
abstract:
Heterogeneous multi-robot systems (HMRS) have emerged as a powerful ap-
proach for tackling complex tasks that single robots cannot manage alone. Current
large-language-model-based multi-agent systems (LLM-based MAS) have shown
success in areas like software development and operating systems, but applying
these systems to robot control presents unique challenges. In particular, the ca-
pabilities of each agent in a multi-robot system are inherently tied to the physical
composition of the robots, rather than predefined roles. To address this issue,
we introduce a novel multi-agent framework designed to enable effective collab-
oration among heterogeneous robots with varying embodiments and capabilities,
along with a new benchmark named Habitat-MAS. One of our key designs is
Robot Resume: Instead of adopting human-designed role play, we propose a self-
prompted approach, where agents comprehend robot URDF files and call robot
kinematics tools to generate descriptions of their physics capabilities to guide
their behavior in task planning and action execution. The Habitat-MAS bench-
mark is designed to assess how a multi-agent framework handles tasks that require
embodiment-aware reasoning, which includes 1) manipulation, 2) perception, 3)
navigation, and 4) comprehensive multi-floor object rearrangement. The experi-
mental results indicate that the robot’s resume and the hierarchical design of our
multi-agent system are essential for the effective operation of the heterogeneous
multi-robot system within this intricate problem context.
abstract_zh: 异质多机器人系统（HMRS）已成为解决单机器人无法独立完成的复杂任务的有效方法。当前基于大语言模型的多智能体系统（LLM-based MAS）已在软件开发、操作系统等领域取得成功，但将这类系统应用于机器人控制面临独特的挑战。具体而言，多机器人系统中每个智能体的能力本质上与机器人的物理构成绑定，而非由预定义角色决定。为解决这一问题，我们提出了一种全新的多智能体框架，旨在支持具备不同具身形态与能力的异质机器人开展高效协作，同时我们还推出了名为Habitat-MAS的全新基准测试集。我们的核心设计之一是机器人简历（Robot Resume）：我们并未采用人工设计的角色扮演机制，而是提出了一种自提示方法，智能体可通过解读机器人URDF文件、调用机器人运动学工具，生成自身物理能力的相关描述，以此指导其在任务规划与动作执行过程中的行为。Habitat-MAS基准测试集旨在评估多智能体框架处理需要具身感知推理的任务的能力，这类任务包括1）操作、2）感知、3）导航、4）综合性多层物体重排列。实验结果表明，机器人简历与我们所提出的多智能体系统的分层设计，是异质多机器人系统在这类复杂问题场景下有效运行的核心要素。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Spectral Perturbation Bounds for Low-Rank Approximation with Applications to Privacy
title_zh: 低秩近似的谱扰动界及其在隐私方面的应用
abstract: A central challenge in machine learning is to understand how noise or measurement errors affect low-rank approximations, particularly in the spectral norm. This question is especially important in differentially private low-rank approximation, where one aims to preserve the top-$p$ structure of a data-derived matrix while ensuring privacy. Prior work often analyzes Frobenius norm error or changes in reconstruction quality, but these metrics can over- or under-estimate true subspace distortion. The spectral norm, by contrast, captures worst-case directional error and provides the strongest utility guarantees. We establish new high-probability spectral-norm perturbation bounds for symmetric matrices that refine the classical Eckart--Young--Mirsky theorem and explicitly capture interactions between a matrix $A \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times n}$ and an arbitrary symmetric perturbation $E$. Under mild eigengap and norm conditions, our bounds yield sharp estimates for $\| (A + E)_p - A_p \|$, where $A_p$ is the best rank-$p$ approximation of $A$, with improvements of up to a factor of $\sqrt{n}$. As an application, we derive improved utility guarantees for differentially private PCA, resolving an open problem in the literature. Our analysis relies on a novel contour bootstrapping method from complex analysis and extends it to a broad class of spectral functionals, including polynomials and matrix exponentials. Empirical results on real-world datasets confirm that our bounds closely track the actual spectral error under diverse perturbation regimes.
abstract_zh: 机器学习中的一个核心挑战是理解噪声或测量误差如何影响低秩近似，尤其是在谱范数意义下。这一问题在差分隐私低秩近似中尤为重要，其目标是在保证隐私的同时保留由数据导出的矩阵的前$p$阶结构。既有工作通常分析 Frobenius 范数误差或重建质量的变化，但这些度量可能高估或低估真实的子空间扰动。相比之下，谱范数能够刻画最坏情况下的方向性误差，并提供最强的效用保证。我们针对对称矩阵建立了新的高概率谱范数扰动界，对经典的 Eckart--Young--Mirsky 定理进行了改进，并显式刻画了矩阵 $A \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times n}$ 与任意对称扰动 $E$ 之间的相互作用。在温和的特征值间隙和范数条件下，我们的界对 $\| (A + E)_p - A_p \|$ 给出了精确估计，其中 $A_p$ 是 $A$ 的最佳秩-$p$ 近似，改进幅度最高可达 $\sqrt{n}$ 倍。作为应用，我们为差分隐私 PCA 推导出了改进的效用保证，解决了该领域中的一个公开问题。我们的分析依赖于一种来自复分析的新型轮廓自举方法，并将其扩展到一大类谱泛函，包括多项式和矩阵指数。真实数据集上的实验结果表明，在多种扰动机制下，我们的界都能紧密跟踪实际的谱误差。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Scaling Off-Policy Reinforcement Learning with Batch and Weight Normalization
title_zh: 利用批量与权重归一化扩展离策略强化学习
abstract:
Reinforcement learning has achieved significant milestones, but sample efficiency remains a bottleneck for real-world applications.
Recently, CrossQ has demonstrated state-of-the-art sample efficiency with a low update-to-data (UTD) ratio of 1.
In this work, we explore CrossQ's scaling behavior with higher UTD ratios.
We identify challenges in the training dynamics, which are emphasized by higher UTD ratios.
To address these, we integrate weight normalization into the CrossQ framework, a solution that stabilizes training, has been shown to prevent potential loss of plasticity, and keeps the effective learning rate constant.
Our proposed approach reliably scales with increasing UTD ratios, achieving competitive performance across 25 challenging continuous control tasks on the DeepMind Control Suite and Myosuite benchmarks, notably the complex dog and humanoid environments.
This work eliminates the need for drastic interventions, such as network resets, and offers a simple yet robust pathway for improving sample efficiency and scalability in model-free reinforcement learning.
abstract_zh: 在强化学习取得诸多里程碑的同时，样本效率仍是现实应用中的瓶颈。近期 CrossQ 在低更新与数据比（UTD=1）下展现了最先进的样本效率，本工作进一步探索 CrossQ 在更高 UTD 下的扩展能力。我们分析发现，训练动态在 UTD 增高时更易失稳，针对该问题将权重归一化引入 CrossQ 框架，从而稳定训练、避免可塑性丧失，并保持有效学习率恒定。所提方法能够可信地随 UTD 提升而扩展，在 DeepMind Control Suite 与 Myosuite 的 25 项高难度连续控制任务（包括复杂的狗形和类人环境）中取得有竞争力的表现。该工作无需通过重置网络等剧烈干预，即可为无模型强化学习提供一条简洁而稳健的样本效率与可扩展性提升路径。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Improving Decision Trees through the Lens of Parameterized Local Search
title_zh: 通过参数化局部搜索视角提升决策树
abstract: Algorithms for learning decision trees often include heuristic local-search operations such as (1) adjusting the threshold of a cut or (2) also exchanging the feature of that cut. We study minimizing the number of classification errors by performing a fixed number of a single type of these operations. Although we discover that the corresponding problems are NP-complete in general, we provide a comprehensive parameterized-complexity analysis with the aim of determining those properties of the problems that explain the hardness and those that make the problems tractable. For instance, we show that the problems remain hard for a small number $d$ of features or small domain size $D$ but the combination of both yields fixed-parameter tractability. That is, the problems are solvable in $(D + 1)^{2d} \cdot |\mathcal{I}|^{O(1)}$ time, where $|\mathcal{I}|$ is the size of the input. We also provide a proof-of-concept implementation of this algorithm and report on empirical results.
abstract_zh: 用于学习决策树的算法通常包含启发式局部搜索操作，例如：（1）调整一次切分的阈值，或（2）同时交换该切分所使用的特征。我们研究通过执行固定次数的某一种此类操作来最小化分类错误数量的问题。尽管我们发现相应问题在一般情形下是 NP 完全的，但我们仍给出了一个全面的参数化复杂性分析，旨在确定究竟是问题的哪些性质导致了困难性，以及哪些性质使问题变得可处理。例如，我们证明了当特征数 $d$ 很小或定义域大小 $D$ 很小时，这些问题仍然是困难的；但二者结合起来则可得到固定参数可解性。也就是说，这些问题可以在 $(D + 1)^{2d} \cdot |\mathcal{I}|^{O(1)}$ 时间内求解，其中 $|\mathcal{I}|$ 是输入规模。我们还提供了该算法的一个概念验证实现，并报告了实验结果。
primary_research_object: 强化学习 / 序列决策
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Faithfulness Under the Distribution: A New Look at Attribution Evaluation
title_zh: 在分布下的忠实性：重新审视归因评估
abstract: Evaluating the faithfulness of attribution methods remains an open challenge. Standard metrics such as Insertion and Deletion Scores rely on heuristic input perturbations (e.g., zeroing pixels), which often push samples out of the data distribution (OOD). This can distort model behavior and lead to unreliable evaluations. We propose FUD, a novel evaluation framework that reconstructs masked regions using score-based diffusion models to produce in-distribution, semantically coherent inputs. This distribution-aware approach avoids the common pitfalls of existing Attribution Evaluation Methods (AEMs) and yields assessments that more accurately reflect attribution faithfulness. Experiments across models show that FUD produces significantly different—and more reliable—judgments than prior approaches. Our implementation is available at: https://anonymous.4open.science/r/FUD-CCD5/
abstract_zh: 评估归因方法的忠实性仍然是一个尚未解决的挑战。标准指标，如插入分数和删除分数，依赖于启发式的输入扰动（例如将像素置零），而这常常会使样本偏离数据分布（OOD）。这会扭曲模型行为，并导致评估结果不可靠。我们提出了 FUD，这是一种新的评估框架，它利用基于分数的扩散模型重建被遮蔽区域，从而生成分布内且语义连贯的输入。这种分布感知的方法避免了现有归因评估方法（AEMs）的常见缺陷，并能给出更准确反映归因忠实性的评估。在跨模型实验中，FUD 产生了与先前方法显著不同且更可靠的判断。我们的实现可见：https://anonymous.4open.science/r/FUD-CCD5/
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Not All Heads Matter: A Head-Level KV Cache Compression Method with Integrated Retrieval and Reasoning
title_zh: 并非所有注意力头都重要：一种集成检索与推理的头级键值缓存压缩方法
abstract: Key-Value (KV) caching is a common technique to enhance the computational efficiency of Large Language Models (LLMs), but its memory overhead grows rapidly with input length. Prior work has shown that not all tokens are equally important for text generation, proposing layer-level KV cache compression to selectively retain key information. Recognizing the distinct roles of attention heads in generation, we propose HeadKV, a head-level KV cache compression method, and HeadKV-R2, which leverages a novel contextual reasoning ability estimation for compression. Our approach operates at the level of individual heads, estimating their importance for contextual QA tasks that require both retrieval and reasoning capabilities. Extensive experiments across diverse benchmarks (LongBench, LooGLE), model architectures (e.g., Llama-3-8B-Instruct, Mistral-7B-Instruct), and long-context abilities tests demonstrate that our head-level KV cache compression significantly outperforms strong baselines, particularly in low-resource settings (KV size = 64 & 128). Notably, our method retains just 1.5% of the KV cache while achieving 97% of the performance of the full KV cache on the contextual question answering benchmark.
abstract_zh: 键值（KV）缓存是提升大语言模型（LLM）计算效率的常用技术，但其内存开销会随输入长度快速增长。已有研究表明，对文本生成而言并非所有令牌的重要性都相同，因此相关工作提出了层级KV缓存压缩方法来选择性保留关键信息。我们注意到注意力头在生成过程中承担的角色存在差异，因此提出头层级KV缓存压缩方法HeadKV，以及利用新型上下文推理能力评估机制实现压缩的HeadKV-R2。我们的方法以单个注意力头为操作粒度，评估各头对同时需要检索与推理能力的上下文问答任务的重要性。我们在各类基准测试（LongBench、LooGLE）、不同模型架构（如Llama-3-8B-Instruct、Mistral-7B-Instruct）以及长上下文能力测试中开展了大量实验，结果表明我们提出的头层级KV缓存压缩方法性能显著优于强基线方法，在低资源场景（KV缓存大小为64与128）下的优势尤为突出。值得注意的是，在上下文问答基准测试中，我们的方法仅保留1.5%的KV缓存，即可达到完整KV缓存97%的性能。
notes: 论文聚焦 LLM 推理阶段的 KV Cache 压缩，在 head-level 上进行重要性估计，并结合 retrieval 与 reasoning 场景评估，明显属于上下文与缓存优化。

title: Do Not Let Low-Probability Tokens Over-Dominate in RL for LLMs
title_zh: 不要让低概率标记在大模型强化学习中占据主导
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a cornerstone for enhancing the reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs), with recent innovations such as Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) demonstrating exceptional effectiveness. In this study, we identify a critical yet underexplored issue in RL training: low-probability tokens disproportionately influence model updates due to their large gradient magnitudes. This dominance hinders the effective learning of high-probability tokens, whose gradients are essential for LLMs' performance but are substantially suppressed. To mitigate this interference, we propose two novel methods: Advantage Reweighting and Low-Probability Token Isolation (Lopti), both of which effectively attenuate gradients from low-probability tokens while emphasizing parameter updates driven by high-probability tokens. Our approaches promote balanced updates across tokens with varying probabilities, thereby enhancing the efficiency of RL training. Experimental results demonstrate that they substantially improve the performance of GRPO-trained LLMs, achieving up to a 46.2% improvement in K&K Logic Puzzle reasoning tasks.
abstract_zh: 强化学习（RL）已成为提升大语言模型（LLM）推理能力的核心方法，近期如组相对策略优化（GRPO）等创新方法已展现出卓越效果。在本研究中，我们识别出强化学习训练中一个关键但尚未得到充分探索的问题：低概率词元由于具有较大的梯度幅度，会对模型更新产生不成比例的影响。这种主导作用阻碍了高概率词元的有效学习，而高概率词元的梯度对大语言模型的性能至关重要，却被显著抑制。为减轻这种干扰，我们提出了两种新方法：优势重加权和低概率词元隔离（Lopti）。这两种方法都能有效削弱来自低概率词元的梯度，同时强化由高概率词元驱动的参数更新。我们的方法促进了不同概率词元之间更为均衡的更新，从而提升了强化学习训练的效率。实验结果表明，这些方法显著提升了经 GRPO 训练的大语言模型的性能，在 K&K 逻辑谜题推理任务上最高可实现 46.2% 的性能提升。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Inference Scaling for Long-Context Retrieval Augmented Generation
title_zh: 面向长上下文检索增强生成的推理缩放
abstract: The scaling of inference computation has unlocked the potential of long-context large language models (LLMs) across diverse settings. For knowledge-intensive tasks, the increased compute is often allocated to incorporate more external knowledge.  However, without effectively utilizing such knowledge, solely expanding context does not always enhance performance. In this work, we investigate inference scaling for retrieval augmented generation (RAG), exploring the combination of multiple strategies beyond simply increasing the quantity of knowledge, including in-context learning and iterative prompting. These strategies provide additional flexibility to scale test-time computation (e.g., by increasing retrieved documents or generation steps), thereby enhancing LLMs’ ability to effectively acquire and utilize contextual information. We address two key questions: (1) How does RAG performance benefit from the scaling of inference computation when optimally configured? (2) Can we predict the optimal test-time compute allocation for a given budget by modeling the relationship between RAG performance and inference parameters? Our observations reveal that increasing inference computation leads to nearly linear gains in RAG performance when optimally allocated, a relationship we describe as the inference scaling laws for RAG. Building on this,  we further develop the computation allocation model to estimate RAG performance across different inference configurations.  The model predicts optimal inference parameters under various computation constraints, which align closely with the experimental results. By applying these optimal configurations, we demonstrate that scaling inference compute on long-context LLMs achieves up to 58.9% gains on benchmark datasets compared to standard RAG.
abstract_zh: 推理计算的扩展释放了长上下文大语言模型（LLM）在各类场景下的潜力。对于知识密集型任务，增加的计算资源通常被用于纳入更多外部知识。然而，若无法有效利用这类知识，仅扩大上下文范围并不总能提升性能。本研究针对检索增强生成（RAG）的推理扩展问题展开研究，探索了除单纯增加知识量之外的多种策略组合，包括上下文学习与迭代提示。这些策略为扩展测试阶段计算量提供了额外灵活性，例如通过增加检索文档数量或生成步骤，进而提升大语言模型有效获取、利用上下文信息的能力。我们围绕两个核心问题展开研究：（1）经过最优配置时，检索增强生成的性能如何从推理计算的扩展中获益？（2）我们能否通过建模检索增强生成性能与推理参数之间的关系，预测给定预算下的最优测试阶段计算资源分配？我们的观察结果显示，当资源分配最优时，增加推理计算量可使检索增强生成性能实现近似线性提升，我们将这一关系称为检索增强生成的推理缩放定律。在此基础上，我们进一步开发了计算分配模型，用于估算不同推理配置下的检索增强生成性能。该模型可预测各类计算约束下的最优推理参数，预测结果与实验结果高度吻合。通过应用这些最优配置，我们证实对长上下文大语言模型扩展推理计算量后，其在基准数据集上的性能较标准检索增强生成最高可提升58.9%。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Beyond the Average: Distributional Causal Inference under Imperfect Compliance
title_zh: 超越平均水平：在不完全遵从下的分布式因果推断
abstract: We study the estimation of distributional treatment effects in randomized experiments with imperfect compliance. When participants do not adhere to their assigned treatments, we leverage treatment assignment as an instrumental variable to identify the local distributional treatment effect—the difference in outcome distributions between treatment and control groups for the subpopulation of compliers. We propose a regression-adjusted estimator based on a distribution regression framework with Neyman-orthogonal moment conditions, enabling robustness and flexibility with high-dimensional covariates. Our approach accommodates continuous, discrete, and mixed discrete-continuous outcomes, and applies under a broad class of covariate-adaptive randomization schemes, including stratified block designs and simple random sampling. We derive the estimator’s asymptotic distribution and show that it achieves the semiparametric efficiency bound. Simulation results demonstrate favorable finite-sample performance, and we demonstrate the method’s practical relevance in an application to the Oregon Health Insurance Experiment.
abstract_zh: 我们研究了在依从性不完全的随机实验中分布处理效应的估计问题。当参与者未遵从其被分配的处理时，我们利用处理分配作为工具变量来识别局部分布处理效应，即对于服从者这一子总体，处理组与对照组之间结果分布的差异。我们提出了一种基于分布回归框架并结合 Neyman 正交矩条件的回归调整估计量，从而在高维协变量下实现稳健性与灵活性。我们的方法能够处理连续、离散以及离散-连续混合型结果变量，并适用于一大类协变量自适应随机化方案，包括分层区组设计和简单随机抽样。我们推导了该估计量的渐近分布，并证明其达到了半参数效率界。模拟结果表明该方法在有限样本下具有良好表现，我们还通过其在俄勒冈医疗保险实验中的应用展示了该方法的实际相关性。
primary_research_object: 强化学习 / 序列决策
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Win Fast or Lose Slow: Balancing Speed and Accuracy in Latency-Sensitive Decisions of LLMs
title_zh: 速胜或缓败：平衡大语言模型时延敏感决策中的速度与准确性
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have shown remarkable performance across diverse reasoning and generation tasks, and are increasingly deployed as agents in dynamic environments such as code generation and recommendation systems. However, many real-world applications, such as high-frequency trading and real-time competitive gaming, require decisions under strict latency constraints, where faster responses directly translate into higher rewards. Despite the importance of this latency–quality trade-off, it remains underexplored in the context of LLM-based agents. In this work, we present the first systematic study of this trade-off in real-time decision-making tasks. To support our investigation, we introduce two new benchmarks: HFTBench, a high-frequency trading simulation, and StreetFighter, a competitive gaming platform. Our analysis reveals that optimal latency–quality balance varies by task, and that sacrificing quality for lower latency can significantly enhance downstream performance. To address this, we propose FPX, an adaptive framework that dynamically selects model size and quantization level based on real-time demands. Our method achieves the best performance on both benchmarks, improving win rate by up to 80% in Street Fighter and boosting daily yield by up to 26.52% in trading,  underscoring the need for latency-aware evaluation and deployment strategies for LLM-based agents. These results demonstrate the critical importance of latency-aware evaluation and deployment strategies for real-world LLM-based agents.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型在各类推理与生成任务中均表现出优异性能，且正越来越多地作为智能体部署在代码生成、推荐系统等动态环境中。然而，高频交易、实时竞技游戏等诸多实际应用要求在严格的延迟约束下制定决策，此时更快的响应直接对应更高的收益。尽管这种延迟-质量权衡十分重要，但在基于大语言模型的智能体研究领域仍未得到充分探索。本研究首次针对实时决策任务中的这一权衡问题开展系统性研究。为支撑研究工作，我们引入了两个全新基准：高频交易模拟基准HFTBench与竞技游戏平台StreetFighter。我们的分析表明，最优延迟-质量平衡随任务变化而有所不同，为降低延迟适当牺牲质量可显著提升下游性能。为此我们提出自适应框架FPX，该框架可根据实时需求动态选择模型大小与量化等级。我们的方法在两个基准上均取得了最优性能，在《街头霸王》任务中胜率最高提升80%，在交易任务中日收益率最高提升26.52%，凸显了为基于大语言模型的智能体设计感知延迟的评估与部署策略的必要性。这些结果表明，面向实际应用的大语言模型智能体采用感知延迟的评估与部署策略至关重要。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Adaptive Algorithms with Sharp Convergence Rates for Stochastic Hierarchical Optimization
title_zh: 具有明确收敛率的随机分层优化自适应算法
abstract: Hierarchical optimization refers to problems with interdependent decision variables and objectives, such as minimax and bilevel formulations. While various algorithms have been proposed, existing methods and analyses lack adaptivity in stochastic optimization settings: they cannot achieve optimal convergence rates across a wide spectrum of gradient noise levels without prior knowledge of the noise magnitude. In this paper, we propose novel adaptive algorithms for two important classes of stochastic hierarchical optimization problems: nonconvex-strongly-concave minimax optimization and nonconvex-strongly-convex bilevel optimization. Our algorithms achieve sharp convergence rates of $\widetilde{O}(1/\sqrt{T} + \sqrt{\bar{\sigma}}/T^{1/4})$ in $T$ iterations for the gradient norm, where $\bar{\sigma}$ is an upper bound on the stochastic gradient noise. Notably, these rates are obtained without prior knowledge of the noise level, thereby enabling automatic adaptivity in both low and high-noise regimes. To our knowledge, this work provides the first adaptive and sharp convergence guarantees for stochastic hierarchical optimization. Our algorithm design combines the momentum normalization technique with novel adaptive parameter choices. Extensive experiments on synthetic and deep learning tasks demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed algorithms.
abstract_zh: 分层优化是指具有相互依赖的决策变量和目标的问题，例如极小极大和双层形式。尽管已经提出了各种算法，但现有方法及其分析在随机优化设置下缺乏自适应性：在事先不知道噪声幅度的情况下，它们无法在广泛的梯度噪声水平范围内实现最优收敛率。本文针对两类重要的随机分层优化问题提出了新的自适应算法：非凸-强凹极小极大优化和非凸-强凸双层优化。我们的算法在进行 \(T\) 次迭代后，对梯度范数实现了 \(\widetilde{O}(1/\sqrt{T} + \sqrt{\bar{\sigma}}/T^{1/4})\) 的尖锐收敛率，其中 \(\bar{\sigma}\) 是随机梯度噪声的上界。值得注意的是，这些收敛率是在无需事先知道噪声水平的情况下获得的，因此能够在低噪声和高噪声情形下实现自动自适应。据我们所知，这项工作首次为随机分层优化提供了自适应且尖锐的收敛保证。我们的算法设计将动量归一化技术与新的自适应参数选择相结合。在合成任务和深度学习任务上的大量实验表明了所提算法的有效性。
primary_research_object: 强化学习 / 序列决策
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: CogVLA: Cognition-Aligned Vision-Language-Action Models via Instruction-Driven Routing & Sparsification
title_zh: CogVLA：通过指令驱动路由与稀疏化实现认知对齐的视觉-语言-动作模型
abstract:
Recent Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models built on pre-trained Vision-Language Models (VLMs) require extensive post-training, resulting in high computational overhead that limits scalability and deployment. Existing sparsification strategies—such as Mixture-of-Depths, layer skipping, and early exit—fall short by neglecting the semantic coupling across vision-language-action modalities, and focusing narrowly on intra-LLM computation while overlooking end-to-end coherence from perception to control. To address these challenges, we propose **CogVLA**, a Cognition-Aligned Vision-Language-Action framework that leverages instruction-driven routing and sparsification to improve both efficiency and performance. CogVLA draws inspiration from human multimodal coordination and introduces a 3-stage progressive architecture. 1) **Encoder-FiLM based Aggregation Routing (EFA-Routing)** injects instruction information into the vision encoder to selectively aggregate and compress dual-stream visual tokens, forming a instruction-aware latent representation. 2) Building upon this compact visual encoding, **LLM-FiLM based Pruning Routing (LFP-Routing)** introduces action intent into the language model by pruning instruction-irrelevant visually grounded tokens, thereby achieving token-level sparsity. 3) To ensure that compressed perception inputs can still support accurate and coherent action generation, we introduce **V‑L‑A Coupled Attention (CAtten)**, which combines causal vision-language attention with bidirectional action parallel decoding.
Extensive experiments on the LIBERO benchmark and real-world robotic tasks demonstrate that CogVLA achieves state-of-the-art performance with success rates of 97.4\% and 70.0\%, respectively, while reducing training costs by 2.5$\times$ and decreasing inference latency by 2.8$\times$ compared to OpenVLA.
abstract_zh: 当前基于预训练视觉语言模型（VLM）构建的视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型需要开展大量后训练，由此产生的极高计算开销限制了模型的可扩展性与部署落地。现有稀疏化策略如深度混合、层跳过、早退出等存在明显不足：这类策略忽略了视觉-语言-动作多模态之间的语义耦合，仅局限于关注大语言模型内部的计算过程，忽视了从感知到控制的端到端一致性。为解决上述挑战，我们提出CogVLA，一种认知对齐的视觉-语言-动作框架，借助指令驱动的路由与稀疏化机制同时提升效率与性能。CogVLA受人类多模态协调机制启发，采用三阶段渐进式架构：1）基于编码器FiLM的聚合路由（EFA-Routing）将指令信息注入视觉编码器，选择性聚合并压缩双流视觉令牌，生成具备指令感知能力的隐层表示。2）基于上述紧凑视觉编码结果，基于大语言模型FiLM的剪枝路由（LFP-Routing）通过剪除与指令无关的视觉接地令牌，将动作意图引入语言模型，从而实现令牌级稀疏化。3）为确保压缩后的感知输入仍可支持准确且一致的动作生成，我们提出视觉-语言-动作耦合注意力（CAtten），该模块将因果视觉-语言注意力与双向动作并行解码相结合。在LIBERO基准测试与真实世界机器人任务上开展的大量实验表明，CogVLA取得了当前最优性能，两类任务的成功率分别达到97.4%与70.0%，同时与OpenVLA相比，训练成本降低2.5倍，推理延迟降低2.8倍。
notes: 论文核心是通过指令驱动路由、token 压缩与剪枝来降低 VLA/VLM 推理开销，并用耦合注意力维持动作生成质量，属于明显的推理结构优化，并伴随压缩/稀疏化特征。

title: NeuroH-TGL: Neuro-Heterogeneity Guided Temporal Graph Learning Strategy for Brain Disease Diagnosis
title_zh: NeuroH-TGL：用于脑部疾病诊断的神经异质性引导时序图学习策略
abstract: Dynamic functional brain networks (DFBNs) are powerful tools in neuroscience research. Recent studies reveal that DFBNs contain heterogeneous neural nodes with more extensive connections and more drastic temporal changes, which play pivotal roles in coordinating the reorganization of the brain. Moreover, the spatio-temporal patterns of these nodes are modulated by the brain's historical states. However, existing methods not only ignore the spatio-temporal heterogeneity of neural nodes, but also fail to effectively encode the temporal propagation mechanism of heterogeneous activities. These limitations hinder the deep exploration of spatio-temporal relationships within DFBNs, preventing the capture of abnormal neural heterogeneity caused by brain diseases. To address these challenges, this paper propose a neuro-heterogeneity guided temporal graph learning strategy (NeuroH-TGL).  Specifically, we first develop a spatio-temporal pattern decoupling module to disentangle DFBNs into topological consistency networks and temporal trend networks that align with the brain's operational mechanisms. Then, we introduce a heterogeneity mining module to identify pivotal heterogeneity nodes that drive brain reorganization from the two decoupled networks. Finally, we design temporal propagation graph convolution to simulate the influence of the historical states of heterogeneity nodes on the current topology, thereby flexibly extracting heterogeneous spatio-temporal information from the brain. Experiments show that our method surpasses several state-of-the-art methods, and can identify abnormal heterogeneous nodes caused by brain diseases.
abstract_zh: 动态功能性脑网络（DFBN）是神经科学研究中的有力工具。近期研究表明，DFBN中存在异质性神经节点，它们具有更广泛的连接和更剧烈的时间变化，并在协调大脑重组中发挥关键作用。此外，这些节点的时空模式会受到大脑历史状态的调制。然而，现有方法不仅忽视了神经节点的时空异质性，也未能有效编码异质活动的时间传播机制。这些局限阻碍了对DFBN内时空关系的深入探索，无法捕捉由脑疾病引起的异常神经异质性。为应对这些挑战，本文提出了一种神经异质性引导的时间图学习策略（NeuroH-TGL）。具体而言，我们首先开发了一个时空模式解耦模块，将DFBN解耦为符合大脑运行机制的拓扑一致性网络和时间趋势网络。然后，我们引入异质性挖掘模块，从这两个解耦网络中识别驱动大脑重组的关键异质性节点。最后，我们设计了时间传播图卷积，以模拟异质性节点历史状态对当前拓扑的影响，从而灵活提取来自大脑的异质性时空信息。实验表明，我们的方法优于多种最先进方法，并且能够识别由脑疾病引起的异常异质性节点。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Beyond Sequential Reranking: Reranker-Guided Search Improves Reasoning Intensive Retrieval
title_zh: 超越顺序重排序：重排序器引导搜索提升推理密集型检索
abstract: The widely used retrieve-and-rerank pipeline faces two critical limitations: they are constrained by the initial retrieval quality of the top-k documents, and the growing computational demands of LLM-based rerankers restrict the number of documents that can be effectively processed. We introduce Reranker-Guided-Search (RGS), a novel approach that bypasses these limitations by directly retrieving documents according to reranker preferences rather than following the traditional sequential reranking method. Our method uses a greedy search on proximity graphs generated by approximate nearest neighbor algorithms, strategically prioritizing promising documents for reranking based on document similarity. Experimental results demonstrate substantial performance improvements across multiple benchmarks: 3.5 points on BRIGHT, 2.9 on FollowIR, and 5.1 on M-BEIR, all within a constrained reranker budget of 100 documents. Our analysis suggests that, given a fixed pair of embedding and reranker models, strategically selecting documents to rerank can significantly improve retrieval accuracy under limited reranker budget.
abstract_zh: 广泛使用的检索后重排流水线面临两个关键局限：其一，它们受限于初始检索得到的 top-k 文档质量；其二，基于大语言模型的重排器不断增长的计算需求限制了能够被有效处理的文档数量。我们提出了重排器引导搜索（RGS），这是一种新方法，通过直接依据重排器偏好来检索文档，而不是遵循传统的顺序重排方式，从而绕过这些限制。我们的方法在由近似最近邻算法生成的邻近图上采用贪心搜索，并基于文档相似性，有策略地优先选择有前景的文档进行重排。实验结果表明，在多个基准上性能均有显著提升：在 BRIGHT 上提升 3.5 分，在 FollowIR 上提升 2.9 分，在 M-BEIR 上提升 5.1 分，且这些结果都在重排器预算限制为 100 篇文档的条件下取得。我们的分析表明，在嵌入模型和重排器模型固定的情况下，有策略地选择进行重排的文档，能够在有限的重排器预算下显著提高检索准确率。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: A Gradient Guided Diffusion Framework for Chance Constrained Programming
title_zh: 面向概率约束规划的梯度引导扩散框架
abstract: Chance constrained programming (CCP) is a powerful framework for addressing optimization problems under uncertainty. In this paper, we introduce a novel Gradient-Guided Diffusion-based Optimization framework, termed GGDOpt, which tackles CCP through three key innovations. First, GGDOpt accommodates a broad class of CCP problems without requiring the knowledge of the exact distribution of uncertainty—relying solely on a set of samples. Second, to address the nonconvexity of the chance constraints, it reformulates the CCP as a sampling problem over the product of two distributions: an unknown data distribution supported on a nonconvex set and a Boltzmann distribution defined by the objective function, which fully leverages both first- and second-order gradient information. Third, GGDOpt has theoretical convergence guarantees and provides practical error bounds under mild assumptions. By progressively injecting noise during the forward diffusion process to convexify the nonconvex feasible region, GGDOpt enables guided reverse sampling to generate asymptotically optimal solutions. Experimental results on synthetic datasets and a waveform design task in wireless communications demonstrate that GGDOpt outperforms existing methods in both solution quality and stability with nearly 80\% overhead reduction.
abstract_zh: 机会约束规划（CCP）是处理不确定性下优化问题的强大框架。本文提出了一种新颖的梯度引导扩散优化框架，称为 GGDOpt，它通过三项关键创新来求解 CCP。首先，GGDOpt 能够适用于一大类 CCP 问题，而无需已知不确定性的精确分布，仅依赖于一组样本。其次，为了解决机会约束的非凸性问题，它将 CCP 重构为在两个分布乘积上的采样问题：一个支撑于非凸集合上的未知数据分布，以及一个由目标函数定义的玻尔兹曼分布，从而充分利用一阶和二阶梯度信息。第三，GGDOpt 在温和假设下具有理论收敛保证，并给出实用的误差界。通过在前向扩散过程中逐步注入噪声以凸化非凸可行域，GGDOpt 能够进行引导式反向采样，从而生成渐近最优解。在合成数据集和无线通信中的波形设计任务上的实验结果表明，GGDOpt 在解的质量和稳定性方面均优于现有方法，同时将开销降低了近 80%。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: EmbedLLM: Learning Compact Representations of Large Language Models
title_zh: EmbedLLM：大型语言模型紧凑表示学习
abstract: With hundreds of thousands of language models available on Huggingface today, efficiently evaluating and utilizing these models across various downstream tasks has become increasingly critical. Many existing methods repeatedly learn task-specific representations of Large Language Models (LLMs), which leads to inefficiencies in both time and computational resources. To address this, we propose EmbedLLM, a framework designed to learn compact vector representations of LLMs that facilitate downstream applications involving many models, such as model routing. We introduce an encoder-decoder approach for learning such embedding, along with a systematic framework to evaluate their effectiveness. Empirical results show that EmbedLLM outperforms prior methods in model routing. Additionally, we demonstrate that our method can forecast a model's performance on multiple benchmarks, without incurring additional inference cost. Extensive probing experiments validate that the learned embeddings capture key model characteristics, e.g. whether the model is specialized for coding tasks, even without being explicitly trained on them. We open source our dataset, code and embedder to facilitate further research and application.
abstract_zh: 如今Huggingface平台上已有数十万款语言模型，如何在各类下游任务中高效评估并使用这些模型已变得愈发关键。现有诸多方法会反复学习大语言模型（LLM）的任务专属表示，导致时间与计算资源的利用效率均较低。为解决这一问题，我们提出EmbedLLM框架，该框架旨在学习大语言模型的紧凑向量表示，为涉及多模型的下游应用（如模型路由）提供便利。我们提出了一种用于学习此类嵌入的编解码方法，同时搭建了一套系统框架来评估这些嵌入的有效性。实验结果表明，EmbedLLM在模型路由任务上的表现优于现有方法。此外，我们证明所提方法无需额外推理成本，即可预测模型在多个基准测试上的性能。大量探测实验验证，即使未针对关键模型特征进行显式训练，学习到的嵌入也能捕获这些特征，例如模型是否专门适配代码任务。我们已开源相关数据集、代码与嵌入器，以推动后续研究与应用。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: SimWorld: An Open-ended Simulator for Agents in Physical and Social Worlds
title_zh: SimWorld：面向物理与社交世界代理的开放式模拟器
abstract: While LLM/VLM-powered AI agents have advanced rapidly in math, coding, and computer use, their applications in complex physical and social environments remain challenging. Building agents that can survive and thrive in the real world (e.g., by autonomously earning income) requires massive-scale interaction, reasoning, training, and evaluation across diverse scenarios. However, existing world simulators for such development fall short: they often rely on limited hand-crafted environments, simulate simplified game-like physics and social rules, and lack native support for LLM/VLM agents. We introduce SimWorld, a new simulator built on Unreal Engine 5, designed for developing and evaluating LLM/VLM agents in rich, real-world-like settings. SimWorld offers three core capabilities: (1) realistic, open-ended world simulation, including accurate physical and social dynamics and language-driven procedural environment generation; (2) rich interface for LLM/VLM agents, with multi-modal world inputs/feedback and open-vocabulary action outputs at varying levels of abstraction; and (3) diverse physical and social reasoning scenarios that are easily customizable by users. We demonstrate SimWorld by deploying frontier LLM agents (e.g., Gemini-2.5-Flash, Claude-3.5, GPT-4o, and DeepSeek-Prover-V2) on both short-horizon navigation tasks requiring grounded re-planning, and long-horizon multi-agent food delivery tasks involving strategic cooperation and competition. The results reveal distinct reasoning patterns and limitations across models. We open-source SimWorld and hope it becomes a foundational platform for advancing real-world agent intelligence across disciplines. Please refer to the project website for the most up-to-date information: http://simworld.org/.
abstract_zh: 尽管由 LLM/VLM 驱动的 AI 智能体在数学、编程和计算机使用方面已迅速取得进展，但它们在复杂物理与社会环境中的应用仍然充满挑战。构建能够在真实世界中生存并繁荣发展的智能体（例如通过自主赚取收入）需要在多样化场景中进行大规模交互、推理、训练和评估。然而，现有用于此类开发的世界模拟器仍存在不足：它们往往依赖有限的手工构建环境，模拟的是简化的、类游戏式的物理和社会规则，并且缺乏对 LLM/VLM 智能体的原生支持。我们提出了 SimWorld，这是一种基于 Unreal Engine 5 构建的新型模拟器，旨在用于在丰富、类真实世界的环境中开发和评估 LLM/VLM 智能体。SimWorld 提供三项核心能力：（1）真实、开放式的世界模拟，包括准确的物理与社会动态，以及由语言驱动的程序化环境生成；（2）面向 LLM/VLM 智能体的丰富接口，具备多模态世界输入/反馈，以及在不同抽象层级上的开放词汇动作输出；（3）多样的物理与社会推理场景，且用户可轻松自定义。我们通过将前沿 LLM 智能体（例如 Gemini-2.5-Flash、Claude-3.5、GPT-4o 和 DeepSeek-Prover-V2）部署到两类任务中来展示 SimWorld：一类是需要基于现实进行重规划的短时域导航任务，另一类是涉及战略性合作与竞争的长时域多智能体送餐任务。结果揭示了不同模型之间截然不同的推理模式与局限性。我们已将 SimWorld 开源，并希望它能成为推动跨学科真实世界智能体智能发展的基础平台。有关最新信息，请参见项目网站：http://simworld.org/。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: SLMRec: Distilling Large Language Models into Small for Sequential Recommendation
title_zh: SLMRec：将大型语言模型蒸馏为用于序列推荐的小模型
abstract:
Sequential Recommendation (SR) task involves predicting the next item a user is likely to interact with, given their past interactions. 
The SR models examine the sequence of a user's actions to discern more complex behavioral patterns and temporal dynamics. 
Recent research demonstrates the great impact of LLMs on sequential recommendation systems, either viewing sequential recommendation as language modeling or serving as the backbone for user representation. Although these methods deliver outstanding performance, there is scant evidence of the necessity of a large language model and how large the language model is needed, especially in the sequential recommendation scene. Meanwhile, due to the huge size of LLMs, it is inefficient and impractical to apply a LLM-based model in real-world platforms that often need to process billions of traffic logs daily. In this paper, we explore the influence of LLMs' depth by conducting extensive experiments on large-scale industry datasets. Surprisingly, our motivational experiments reveal that most intermediate layers of LLMs are redundant, indicating that pruning the remaining layers can still maintain strong performance.
Motivated by this insight, we empower small language models for SR, namely SLMRec, which adopt a simple yet effective knowledge distillation method. Moreover, SLMRec is orthogonal to other post-training efficiency techniques, such as quantization and pruning, so that they can be leveraged in combination. Comprehensive experimental results illustrate that the proposed SLMRec model attains the best performance using only 13\% of the parameters found in LLM-based recommendation models while simultaneously achieving up to 6.6x and 8.0x speedups in training and inference time costs, respectively. Besides, we provide a theoretical justification for why small language models can perform comparably to large language models in SR.
abstract_zh: 序列推荐（SR）任务旨在根据用户的历史交互记录，预测用户接下来可能交互的物品。序列推荐模型会分析用户的行为序列，以识别更复杂的行为模式和时间动态。近年研究表明大语言模型（LLM）对序列推荐系统有显著作用，相关研究要么将序列推荐视为语言建模任务，要么将大语言模型作为生成用户表征的主干网络。尽管这些方法表现优异，但鲜有研究验证大语言模型的必要性，以及究竟需要多大规模的语言模型，尤其是在序列推荐场景中。同时，由于大语言模型体量庞大，将基于大语言模型的模型部署到通常需要每日处理数十亿流量日志的实际平台中，效率极低且不具备实用性。本文通过在大规模行业数据集上开展大量实验，探究大语言模型深度带来的影响。令人意外的是，我们的动机实验显示大语言模型的大多数中间层都是冗余的，这意味着剪除这些冗余层后模型仍能保持较强的性能。受这一发现启发，我们提出了面向序列推荐任务的小语言模型SLMRec，该模型采用了一种简单却有效的知识蒸馏方法。此外，SLMRec与量化、剪枝等其他训练后效率提升技术正交，因此可以结合使用这些技术。综合实验结果表明，所提出的SLMRec模型仅使用基于大语言模型的推荐模型13%的参数就实现了最优性能，同时训练和推理的时间成本分别最高可实现6.6倍和8.0倍的加速。此外，我们还从理论上论证了小语言模型为何能在序列推荐任务中取得与大语言模型相当的表现。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Private Continual Counting of Unbounded Streams
title_zh: 面向无限流的私密持续计数
abstract: We study the problem of differentially private continual counting in the unbounded setting where the input size $n$ is not known in advance. Current state-of-the-art algorithms based on optimal instantiations of the matrix mechanism cannot be directly applied here because their privacy guarantees only hold when key parameters are tuned to $n$. Using the common `doubling trick' avoids knowledge of $n$ but leads to suboptimal and non-smooth error. We solve this problem by introducing novel matrix factorizations based on logarithmic perturbations of the function $\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-z}}$ studied in prior works, which may be of independent interest. The resulting algorithm has smooth error, and for any $\alpha > 0$ and $t\leq n$ it is able to privately estimate the sum of the first $t$ data points with $O(\log^{2+2\alpha}(t))$ variance. It requires $O(t)$ space and amortized $O(\log t)$ time per round, compared to $O(\log(n)\log(t))$ variance, $O(n)$ space and $O(n \log n)$ pre-processing time for the nearly-optimal bounded-input algorithm of Henzinger et al. Empirically, we find that our algorithm's performance is also comparable to theirs in absolute terms: our variance is less than $1.5\times$ theirs for $t$ as large as $2^{24}$.
abstract_zh: 我们研究了无界设定下差分隐私持续计数问题，其中输入规模 $n$ 事先未知。当前基于矩阵机制最优实例化的最先进算法无法直接应用于这一情形，因为它们的隐私保证仅在关键参数针对 $n$ 调优时才成立。使用常见的“加倍技巧”可以避免预先知道 $n$，但会导致次优且不平滑的误差。我们通过引入新的矩阵分解方法解决了这一问题，该方法基于先前工作中研究的函数 $\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-z}}$ 的对数扰动，并且这一方法本身可能具有独立意义。所得算法具有平滑误差，并且对于任意 $\alpha > 0$ 和 $t\leq n$，都能够以 $O(\log^{2+2\alpha}(t))$ 的方差对前 $t$ 个数据点之和进行私有估计。与 Henzinger 等人提出的近最优有界输入算法相比，该算法每轮需要 $O(t)$ 空间和均摊 $O(\log t)$ 时间，而后者的方差为 $O(\log(n)\log(t))$、空间为 $O(n)$、预处理时间为 $O(n \log n)$。实验上，我们发现该算法的绝对性能也与他们的方法相当：当 $t$ 大至 $2^{24}$ 时，我们的方差仍不超过他们的 $1.5\times$。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Collaborative Gym: A Framework for Enabling and Evaluating Human-Agent Collaboration
title_zh: 协作健身：用于支持与评估人类与智能体协作的框架
abstract: While the advancement of large language models has spurred the development of AI agents to automate tasks, numerous use cases inherently require agents to collaborate with humans due to humans' latent preferences, domain expertise, or the need for control. To facilitate the study of human-agent collaboration, we introduce Collaborative Gym (Co-Gym), an open framework for developing and evaluating collaborative agents that engage in bidirectional communication with humans while interacting with task environments. We describe how the framework enables the implementation of new task environments and coordination between humans and agents through a flexible, non-turn-taking interaction paradigm, along with an evaluation suite that assesses both collaboration outcomes and processes. Our framework provides both a simulated condition with a reliable user simulator and a real-world condition with an interactive web application. Initial benchmark experiments across three representative tasks-creating travel plans, writing related work sections, and analyzing tabular data-demonstrate the benefits of human-agent collaboration: The best-performing collaborative agents consistently outperform their fully autonomous counterparts in task performance, achieving win rates of 86% in Travel Planning, 74% in Tabular Analysis, and 66% in Related Work when evaluated by real users. Despite these improvements, our evaluation reveals persistent limitations in current language models and agents, with communication and situational awareness failures observed in 65% and 40% of cases in the real condition, respectively. Released under the permissive MIT license, Co-Gym supports the addition of new task environments and can be used to develop collaborative agent applications, while its evaluation suite enables assessment and improvement of collaborative agents.
abstract_zh: 尽管大语言模型的发展推动了用于任务自动化的 AI 智能体的开发，但由于人类存在隐性偏好、具备领域专业知识，或出于控制需求，许多应用场景本质上要求智能体与人类协作。为了促进对人机协作的研究，我们提出了 Collaborative Gym（Co-Gym），这是一个用于开发和评估协作型智能体的开放框架，这些智能体在与任务环境交互的同时，能够与人类进行双向通信。我们描述了该框架如何通过一种灵活的、非轮流式的交互范式，支持新任务环境的实现以及人类与智能体之间的协调，并配备了一个同时评估协作结果与协作过程的评测套件。我们的框架既提供了带有可靠用户模拟器的模拟条件，也提供了带有交互式 Web 应用程序的真实世界条件。针对三个具有代表性的任务，即制定旅行计划、撰写相关工作章节以及分析表格数据，初步基准实验展示了人机协作的优势：在由真实用户评估时，表现最佳的协作型智能体在任务性能上始终优于其完全自主的对应系统，在旅行规划、表格分析和相关工作三个任务中分别取得了 86%、74% 和 66% 的胜率。尽管取得了这些改进，我们的评估仍揭示了当前语言模型和智能体的持续性局限：在真实条件下，分别有 65% 和 40% 的案例观察到了通信失败和情境感知失败。Co-Gym 在宽松的 MIT 许可下发布，支持添加新的任务环境，可用于开发协作型智能体应用，而其评测套件则支持对协作型智能体进行评估和改进。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: FlowRefiner: A Robust Traffic Classification Framework against Label Noise
title_zh: FlowRefiner：一个针对标签噪声的鲁棒流量分类框架
abstract: Network traffic classification is essential for network management and security. In recent years, deep learning (DL) algorithms have emerged as essential tools for classifying complex traffic. However, they rely heavily on high-quality labeled training data.  In practice, traffic data is often noisy due to human error or inaccurate automated labeling, which could render classification unreliable and lead to severe consequences. Although some studies have alleviated the label noise issue in specific scenarios, they are difficult to generalize to general traffic classification tasks due to the inherent semantic complexity of traffic data. In this paper, we propose FlowRefiner, a robust and general traffic classification framework against label noise. FlowRefiner consists of three core components: a traffic semantics-driven noise detector, a confidence-guided label correction mechanism, and a cross-granularity robust classifier. First, the noise detector utilizes traffic semantics extracted from a pre-trained encoder to identify mislabeled flows. Next, the confidence-guided label correction module fine-tunes a label predictor to correct noisy labels and construct refined flows. Finally, the cross-granularity robust classifier learns generalized patterns of both flow-level and packet-level, improving classification robustness against noisy labels. We evaluate our method on four traffic datasets with various classification scenarios across varying noise ratios. Experimental results demonstrate that FlowRefiner mitigates the impact of label noise and consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines by a large margin. The code is available at https://github.com/NSSL-SJTU/FlowRefiner.
abstract_zh: 网络流量分类对于网络管理和安全至关重要。近年来，深度学习（DL）算法已成为对复杂流量进行分类的重要工具。然而，它们严重依赖高质量的带标注训练数据。在实际中，由于人工错误或自动标注不准确，流量数据往往带有噪声，这可能使分类结果不可靠并导致严重后果。尽管一些研究在特定场景下缓解了标签噪声问题，但由于流量数据固有的语义复杂性，这些方法难以推广到通用流量分类任务中。本文提出了 FlowRefiner，一个针对标签噪声的鲁棒且通用的流量分类框架。FlowRefiner 由三个核心组件构成：流量语义驱动的噪声检测器、置信度引导的标签纠正机制，以及跨粒度鲁棒分类器。首先，噪声检测器利用从预训练编码器中提取的流量语义来识别错误标注的流。接着，置信度引导的标签纠正模块通过微调标签预测器来纠正噪声标签并构建精炼流。最后，跨粒度鲁棒分类器学习流级和分组级的广义模式，从而提升针对噪声标签的分类鲁棒性。我们在四个流量数据集上、针对不同噪声比例下的多种分类场景对该方法进行了评估。实验结果表明，FlowRefiner 能够缓解标签噪声的影响，并且始终以较大优势优于最先进的基线方法。代码已发布于 https://github.com/NSSL-SJTU/FlowRefiner。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Shortcut-connected Expert Parallelism for Accelerating Mixture of Experts
title_zh: 用于加速混合专家模型的捷径连接式专家并行方法
abstract:
Expert parallelism has emerged as a key strategy for distributing the computational workload of sparsely-gated mixture-of-experts (MoE) models across multiple devices, enabling the processing of increasingly large-scale models. However, the All-to-All communication inherent to expert parallelism poses a significant bottleneck, limiting the efficiency of MoE models. Although existing optimization methods partially mitigate this issue, they remain constrained by the sequential dependency between communication and computation operations. 
To address this challenge, we propose ScMoE, a novel shortcut-connected MoE architecture integrated with an overlapping parallelization strategy. ScMoE decouples communication from its conventional sequential ordering, enabling up to 100\% overlap with computation.
Compared to the prevalent top-2 MoE baseline, ScMoE achieves speedups of $1.49\times$ in training and $1.82\times$ in inference.
Moreover, our experiments and analyses indicate that ScMoE not only achieves comparable but in some instances surpasses the model quality of existing approaches.
abstract_zh: 专家并行已成为将稀疏门控混合专家（MoE）模型的计算负载分配至多台设备的关键策略，可支撑对规模持续扩大的模型的处理。然而，专家并行固有的全对全通信是一项显著瓶颈，限制了MoE模型的效率。现有优化方法虽能部分缓解这一问题，但仍受通信与计算操作之间的串行依赖约束。为解决该挑战，我们提出ScMoE，这是一种集成了重叠并行策略的新型捷径连接MoE架构。ScMoE将通信从传统的串行顺序中解耦，最高可实现与计算操作100%的重叠。与当前主流的top-2 MoE基线相比，ScMoE的训练加速比达1.49倍，推理加速比达1.82倍。此外，我们的实验与分析表明，ScMoE的模型质量不仅与现有方法相当，在部分场景下甚至优于现有方法。
notes: 调度专项得分=6；命中组=moe_scheduling；augment_batch=20260402-scheduling；augment_score=6

title: LoRA-Mixer: Coordinate Modular LoRA Experts Through Serial Attention Routing
title_zh: LoRA-Mixer：通过串行注意力路由协调模块化LoRA专家
abstract: Recent attempts to combine low-rank adaptation (LoRA) with mixture-of-experts (MoE) for multi-task adaptation of Large Language Models (LLMs) often replace whole attention/FFN layers with switch experts or append parallel expert branches, undermining parameter efficiency and limiting task specialization.  We introduce **LoRA-Mixer**, a modular MoE framework that routes task-specific LoRA experts into the core projection matrices of the attention module (input/output linear layers), rather than primarily targeting FFN blocks. The design delivers fine-grained token-level specialization by fully exploiting the attention mechanism, while remaining drop-in compatible with Transformers and state-space models (SSMs) as the linear projection layers are ubiquitous. To train robust routers from limited data while promoting stable, selective decisions and high expert reuse, **LoRA-Mixer** employs an adaptive **Routing Specialization Loss (RSL)** that jointly enforces global load balance and input-aware specialization via an entropy-shaping objective. The framework supports two regimes: (i) joint optimization of adapters and router with a differentiable hard–soft top-k routing scheme, and (ii) plug-and-play routing over frozen, pre-trained LoRA modules sourced from public repositories.  Across 15 benchmarks—including MedQA, GSM8K, HumanEval, and GLUE—RSL-optimized LoRA-Mixer outperforms state-of-the-art routing and LoRA-MoE baselines while using 48% of their trainable parameters, with gains of +3.79%, +2.90%, and +3.95% on GSM8K, CoLA, and ARC-C, respectively. Cross-model transfer and adapter reuse experiments further demonstrate the approach’s versatility and data efficiency.
abstract_zh: 最近将低秩适配（LoRA）与专家混合（MoE）相结合用于大语言模型（LLM）多任务适配的研究，通常会用开关专家替换整个注意力/前馈网络（FFN）层，或是附加并行专家分支，这会损害参数效率，同时限制任务专化能力。我们提出LoRA-Mixer，这是一种模块化MoE框架，可将任务专属的LoRA专家路由至注意力模块的核心投影矩阵（输入/输出线性层），而非主要针对FFN模块进行设计。该设计充分利用注意力机制实现细粒度的令牌级专化，同时由于线性投影层广泛存在，该框架可直接兼容Transformer与状态空间模型（SSM）。为了在有限数据下训练出鲁棒的路由器，同时实现稳定、有选择性的路由决策及高专家复用率，LoRA-Mixer采用自适应路由专化损失（RSL），该损失通过熵塑形目标，同时实现全局负载均衡和输入感知的专化能力。该框架支持两种模式：（i）通过可微分硬-软前k路由机制联合优化适配器和路由器；（ii）对来自公共仓库的已冻结预训练LoRA模块实现即插即用路由。在包含MedQA、GSM8K、HumanEval和GLUE在内的15个基准测试中，经RSL优化的LoRA-Mixer仅使用现有最优路由方法和LoRA-MoE基线模型48%的可训练参数，性能却优于上述基线，其中在GSM8K、CoLA和ARC-C数据集上分别实现了+3.79%、+2.90%和+3.95%的性能提升。跨模型迁移和适配器复用实验进一步证明了该方法的通用性和数据效率。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Mesh Interpolation Graph Network for Dynamic and Spatially Irregular Global Weather Forecasting
title_zh: 用于动态与空间不规则全球天气预报的网格插值图网络
abstract:
Graph neural networks have shown promising results in weather forecasting, which is critical for human activity such as agriculture planning and extreme weather preparation. However, most studies focus on finite and local areas for training, overlooking the influence of broader areas and limiting their ability to generalize effectively. Thus, in this work, we study global weather forecasting that is irregularly distributed and dynamically varying in practice, requiring the model to generalize to unobserved locations.
To address such challenges, we propose a general Mesh Interpolation Graph Network (MIGN) that models the irregular weather station forecasting, consisting of two key designs: (1) learning spatially irregular data with regular mesh interpolation network to align the data; (2) leveraging parametric spherical harmonics location embedding to further enhance spatial generalization ability. Extensive experiments on an up-to-date observation dataset show that MIGN significantly outperforms existing data-driven models. Besides, we show that MIGN has spatial generalization ability, and is capable of generalizing to previously unseen stations.
abstract_zh: 图神经网络在天气预报中已展现出良好前景，而天气预报对于农业规划和极端天气准备等人类活动至关重要。然而，大多数研究都聚焦于有限的局部区域进行训练，忽视了更广泛区域的影响，并限制了其有效泛化的能力。因此，在这项工作中，我们研究了实践中呈现不规则分布且动态变化的全球天气预报问题，这要求模型能够泛化到未观测的位置。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Fine-Grained Iterative Adversarial Attacks with Limited Computation Budget
title_zh: 有限计算预算下的细粒度迭代对抗攻击
abstract: This work tackles a critical challenge in AI safety research under limited compute: given a fixed computation budget, how can one maximize the strength of iterative adversarial attacks? Coarsely reducing the number of attack iterations lowers cost but substantially weakens effectiveness. To fulfill the attainable attack efficacy within a constrained budget, we propose a fine-grained control mechanism that selectively recomputes layer activations across both iteration-wise and layer-wise levels. Extensive experiments show that our method consistently outperforms existing baselines at equal cost. Moreover, when integrated into adversarial training, it attains comparable  performance with only 30\% of the original budget.
abstract_zh: 本工作针对有限计算资源下人工智能安全研究中的一个关键挑战：在给定固定计算预算的情况下，如何最大化迭代式对抗攻击的强度？粗略地减少攻击迭代次数虽然能降低成本，但会显著削弱攻击效果。为在受限预算内实现尽可能高的攻击效能，我们提出了一种细粒度控制机制，在迭代级和层级两个层面有选择地重新计算各层激活。大量实验表明，在相同成本下，我们的方法始终优于现有基线方法。此外，当将其集成到对抗训练中时，仅使用原始预算的30\%即可获得相当的性能。
primary_research_object: 强化学习 / 序列决策
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Semantic-Enhanced Time-Series Forecasting via Large Language Models
title_zh: 基于大型语言模型的语义增强时间序列预测
abstract: Time series forecasting plays a significant role in finance, energy, meteorology, and IoT applications. Recent studies have leveraged the generalization capabilities of large language models (LLMs) to adapt to time series forecasting, achieving promising performance. However, existing studies focus on token-level modal alignment, instead of bridging the intrinsic modality gap between linguistic knowledge structures and time series data patterns, greatly limiting the semantic representation. To address this issue, we propose a novel Semantic-Enhanced LLM (SE-LLM) that explores the inherent periodicity and anomalous characteristics of time series to embed into the semantic space to enhance the token embedding. This process enhances the interpretability of tokens for LLMs, thereby activating the potential of LLMs for temporal sequence analysis. Moreover, existing Transformer-based LLMs excel at capturing long-range dependencies but are weak at modeling short-term anomalies in time-series data. Hence, we propose a plugin module embedded within self-attention that models long-term and short-term dependencies to effectively adapt LLMs to time-series analysis. Our approach freezes the LLM and reduces the sequence dimensionality of tokens, greatly reducing computational consumption. Experiments demonstrate the superiority performance of our SE-LLM against the state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods.
abstract_zh: 时间序列预测在金融、能源、气象和物联网应用中发挥着重要作用。近期研究利用大语言模型（LLM）的泛化能力来适配时间序列预测，并取得了有前景的性能。然而，现有研究侧重于词元级别的模态对齐，而非弥合语言知识结构与时间序列数据模式之间内在的模态鸿沟，这极大限制了语义表示。为解决这一问题，我们提出了一种新颖的语义增强大语言模型（SE-LLM），该模型挖掘时间序列的固有周期性和异常特征，并将其嵌入语义空间以增强词元嵌入。这一过程增强了词元对LLM的可解释性，从而激发了LLM在时间序列分析中的潜力。此外，现有基于Transformer的LLM擅长捕捉长程依赖，但在建模时间序列数据中的短期异常方面较弱。因此，我们提出了一种嵌入自注意力内部的插件模块，用于同时建模长期和短期依赖，从而使LLM能够有效适应时间序列分析。我们的方法冻结LLM，并降低词元的序列维度，从而大幅减少计算消耗。实验表明，与最先进（SOTA）方法相比，我们的SE-LLM具有更优越的性能。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: SIU3R: Simultaneous Scene Understanding and 3D Reconstruction Beyond Feature Alignment
title_zh: SIU3R：超越特征对齐的同步场景理解与三维重建
abstract:
Simultaneous understanding and 3D reconstruction plays an important role in developing end-to-end embodied intelligent systems.
To achieve this, recent approaches resort to 2D-to-3D feature alignment paradigm, which leads to limited 3D understanding capability and potential semantic information loss.
In light of this, we propose SIU3R, the first alignment-free framework for generalizable simultaneous understanding and 3D reconstruction from unposed images.
Specifically, SIU3R bridges reconstruction and understanding tasks via pixel-aligned 3D representation, and unifies multiple understanding tasks into a set of unified learnable queries, enabling native 3D understanding without the need of alignment with 2D models.
To encourage collaboration between the two tasks with shared representation, we further conduct in-depth analyses of their mutual benefits, and propose two lightweight modules to facilitate their interaction.
Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance not only on the individual tasks of 3D reconstruction and understanding, but also on the task of simultaneous understanding and 3D reconstruction, highlighting the advantages of our alignment-free framework and the effectiveness of the mutual benefit designs.
abstract_zh: SIU3R 提出了一种首个无需特征对齐的框架，用于从未经定位的图像中同时实现通用化的场景理解与三维重建。该方法通过像素对齐的三维表示将重建与理解任务连接，并采用一组统一的可学习查询将多个理解任务融合，进而在不依赖二维模型对齐的情况下实现原生三维理解。为促进两类任务在共享表示下的协同，作者深入分析了它们的互补效应，并设计了两个轻量级模块以强化交互。实验证明，SIU3R 不仅在单独的三维重建与理解任务上取得了最先进的表现，还在同时执行理解与三维重建任务时展现了明显优势，凸显了无对齐框架与互惠设计的有效性。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Synergizing Understanding and Generation with Interleaved Analyzing-Drafting Thinking
title_zh: 在交错分析—撰写思维中实现理解与生成的协同
abstract: Unified Vision–Language Models (UVLMs) aim to advance multimodal learning by supporting both understanding and generation within a single framework. However, existing approaches largely focus on architectural unification while overlooking the need for explicit interaction between the two capabilities during task solving. As a result, current models treat understanding and generation as parallel skills rather than synergistic processes. To achieve real synergy, we introduce the interleaved Analyzing–Drafting problem-solving loop (AD-Loop), a new think paradigm that dynamically alternates between analytic and drafting operations. By interleaving textual thoughts with visual thoughts, AD-Loop enables models to iteratively refine both comprehension and outputs, fostering genuine synergy. To train this mechanism, we design a two-stage strategy: supervised learning on interleaved thought data to initialize alternation, followed by reinforcement learning to promote adaptive and autonomous control. Extensive experiments demonstrate that AD-Loop consistently improves performance across standard benchmarks for both understanding and generation, with strong transferability to various UVLMs architectures. Visual analyses further validate the effectiveness of implicit visual thoughts. These results highlight AD-Loop as a principled and broadly applicable strategy for synergizing comprehension and creation.
abstract_zh: 统一视觉-语言模型（UVLM）旨在通过在单一框架内同时支持理解与生成来推进多模态学习。然而，现有方法大多侧重于架构统一，却忽视了在任务求解过程中这两种能力之间进行显式交互的必要性。因此，当前模型将理解与生成视为并行技能，而非协同过程。为实现真正的协同，我们引入了交错式分析-起草问题求解循环（AD-Loop），这是一种新的思维范式，能够在分析操作与起草操作之间动态交替。通过交错文本思维与视觉思维，AD-Loop 使模型能够迭代式地同时改进理解与输出，从而促进真正的协同。为训练这一机制，我们设计了两阶段策略：首先在交错思维数据上进行监督学习以初始化交替过程，随后通过强化学习促进自适应且自主的控制。大量实验表明，AD-Loop 在理解与生成的标准基准测试上都能持续提升性能，并且对多种 UVLM 架构具有很强的可迁移性。视觉分析进一步验证了隐式视觉思维的有效性。这些结果表明，AD-Loop 是一种用于协同理解与创造的有原则且具有广泛适用性的策略。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: AttentionPredictor: Temporal Patterns Matter for KV Cache Compression
title_zh: AttentionPredictor：时间模式对 KV 缓存压缩至关重要
abstract:
With the development of large language models (LLMs), efficient inference through Key-Value (KV) cache compression has attracted considerable attention, especially for long-context generation.
To compress the KV cache, recent methods identify critical KV tokens through static modeling of attention scores. However, these methods often struggle to accurately determine critical tokens as they neglect the *temporal patterns* in attention scores, resulting in a noticeable degradation in LLM performance. 
To address this challenge, we propose **AttentionPredictor**, which is the **first learning-based method to directly predict attention patterns for KV cache compression and critical token identification**. 
Specifically, AttentionPredictor learns a lightweight, unified convolution model to dynamically capture spatiotemporal patterns and predict the next-token attention scores. An appealing feature of AttentionPredictor is that it accurately predicts the attention score and shares the unified prediction model, which consumes negligible memory, among all transformer layers. Moreover, we propose a cross-token critical cache prefetching framework that hides the token estimation time overhead to accelerate the decoding stage. By retaining most of the attention information, AttentionPredictor achieves **13$\times$** KV cache compression and **5.6$\times$** speedup in a cache offloading scenario with comparable LLM performance, significantly outperforming the state-of-the-arts. The code is available at https://github.com/MIRALab-USTC/LLM-AttentionPredictor.
abstract_zh: 随着大语言模型（LLM）的发展，通过键值（KV）缓存压缩实现高效推理受到了广泛关注，尤其在长上下文生成场景中。为压缩KV缓存，现有方法通过对注意力分数进行静态建模来识别关键KV令牌，但这类方法由于忽略了注意力分数中的时间模式，往往难以准确判定关键令牌，导致大语言模型的性能出现明显下降。为解决这一挑战，我们提出AttentionPredictor，这是首个基于学习的可直接预测注意力模式，用于KV缓存压缩与关键令牌识别的方法。具体而言，AttentionPredictor学习一个轻量级的统一卷积模型，动态捕获时空模式并预测下一个令牌的注意力分数。AttentionPredictor的一个突出优势是它能够准确预测注意力分数，且该统一预测模型可在所有Transformer层之间共享，仅消耗可忽略不计的内存。此外，我们提出了跨令牌关键缓存预取框架，该框架可隐藏令牌估计的时间开销，从而加快解码阶段的速度。通过保留大部分注意力信息，在缓存卸载场景下，AttentionPredictor实现了13倍的KV缓存压缩比与5.6倍的速度提升，同时大语言模型性能与压缩前相当，表现显著优于现有最优方法。代码可从https://github.com/MIRALab-USTC/LLM-AttentionPredictor获取。
notes: 论文聚焦 LLM 推理阶段的 KV cache compression，并强调 long-context generation 场景下的效率优化，明显属于上下文与缓存优化方向。

title: Improving Time Series Forecasting via Instance-aware Post-hoc Revision
title_zh: 通过面向实例的事后修正提升时间序列预测
abstract: Time series forecasting plays a pivotal role in various real-world applications and has attracted significant attention in recent decades. While recent methods have achieved remarkable accuracy by incorporating advanced inductive biases and training strategies, we observe that instance-level variations remain a significant challenge. These variations—stemming from distribution shifts, missing data, and long-tail patterns—often lead to suboptimal forecasts for specific instances, even when overall performance appears strong. To address this issue, we propose a model-agnostic framework, PIR, designed to enhance forecasting performance through Post-forecasting Identification and Revision. Specifically, PIR first identifies biased forecast instances by estimating their predictive accuracy. Based on this, the framework revises the forecasts using contextual information, including covariates and historical time series, from both local and global perspectives in a post-processing fashion. Extensive experiments on real-world datasets with mainstream forecasting models demonstrate that PIR effectively mitigates instance-level errors and significantly improves forecasting reliability.
abstract_zh: 时间序列预测在各种现实应用中发挥着关键作用，并在近几十年中受到了广泛关注。尽管近期方法通过引入先进的归纳偏置和训练策略取得了显著精度，我们观察到，实例级差异仍然是一个重要挑战。这些差异源于分布偏移、数据缺失和长尾模式，往往会导致某些特定实例的预测结果欠佳，即使整体性能看起来很强。为了解决这一问题，我们提出了一个与模型无关的框架PIR，旨在通过预测后识别与修正来提升预测性能。具体而言，PIR首先通过估计预测准确性来识别存在偏差的预测实例。在此基础上，该框架以一种后处理方式，结合局部和全局视角下的上下文信息，包括协变量和历史时间序列，对预测结果进行修正。在真实世界数据集以及主流预测模型上的大量实验表明，PIR能够有效缓解实例级误差，并显著提升预测的可靠性。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Generalized Linear Bandits: Almost Optimal Regret with One-Pass Update
title_zh: 广义线性多臂赌博机：一次遍历更新下的近乎最优懊悔
abstract: We study the generalized linear bandit (GLB) problem, a contextual multi-armed bandit framework that extends the classical linear model by incorporating a non-linear link function, thereby modeling a broad class of reward distributions such as Bernoulli and Poisson. While GLBs are widely applicable to real-world scenarios, their non-linear nature introduces significant challenges in achieving both computational and statistical efficiency. Existing methods typically trade off between two objectives, either incurring high per-round costs for optimal regret guarantees or compromising statistical efficiency to enable constant-time updates.
