Evaluations of state-of-the-art models reveal significant limitations: the strongest model (GPT-5) achieves $86.2$\% and $85.5$\% accuracy on recognition and editing tasks, which are intuitively simple for humans, and performs even worse on the generation task, reaching only $43.0$\% accuracy. These results highlight the shortcomings of current AI systems in handling even preliminary molecular recognition and manipulation tasks. We hope MolLangBench will catalyze further research toward more effective and reliable AI systems for chemical applications. The dataset and code can be accessed at https://huggingface.co/datasets/ChemFM/MolLangBench and https://github.com/TheLuoFengLab/MolLangBench, respectively.
abstract_zh: 对分子进行精确识别、编辑和生成，是化学家以及处理各类化学任务的人工智能系统所必需的前提。我们提出了 MolLangBench，这是一个综合性基准，旨在评估基础的分子-语言接口任务：语言提示下的分子结构识别、编辑和生成。为确保输出具有高质量、无歧义和确定性，我们使用自动化化学信息学工具构建识别任务，并通过严格的专家标注与验证整理编辑和生成任务。MolLangBench 支持评估能够将语言与不同分子表示形式进行交互的模型，包括线性字符串、分子图像和分子图。对最先进模型的评测揭示了显著局限性：最强模型（GPT-5）在识别和编辑任务上的准确率分别达到 86.2% 和 85.5%，而这些任务对人类而言直观上较为简单；在生成任务上的表现甚至更差，准确率仅为 43.0%。这些结果凸显了当前人工智能系统在处理哪怕是初步的分子识别与操作任务时也存在不足。我们希望 MolLangBench 能推动进一步研究，朝着更高效、更可靠的化学应用人工智能系统迈进。该数据集和代码分别可在 https://huggingface.co/datasets/ChemFM/MolLangBench 和 https://github.com/TheLuoFengLab/MolLangBench 获取。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: OmniSegmentor: A Flexible Multi-Modal Learning Framework for Semantic Segmentation
title_zh: OmniSegmentor：用于语义分割的灵活多模态学习框架
abstract: Recent research on representation learning has proved the merits of multi-modal clues for robust semantic segmentation. Nevertheless, a flexible pretrain-and-finetune pipeline for multiple visual modalities remains unexplored. In this paper, we propose a novel multi-modal learning framework, termed OmniSegmentor. It has two key innovations: 1) Based on ImageNet, we assemble a large-scale dataset for multi-modal pretraining, called OmniSegmentor, which contains five popular visual modalities; 2) We provide an efficient pretraining manner to endow the model with the capacity to encode different modality information in the OmniSegmentor. For the first time, we introduce a universal multi-modal pretraining framework that consistently amplifies the model's perceptual capabilities across various scenarios, regardless of the arbitrary combination of the involved modalities. Remarkably, our OmniSegmentor achieves new state-of-the-art records on a wide range of multi-modal semantic segmentation datasets, including NYU Depthv2, EventScape, MFNet, DeLiVER, SUNRGBD, and KITTI-360. Data, model checkpoints, and source code will be made publicly available: https://github.com/VCIP-RGBD/DFormer.
abstract_zh: 近期关于表征学习的研究已经证明，多模态线索对于实现鲁棒的语义分割具有优势。然而，针对多种视觉模态的灵活预训练与微调流程仍有待探索。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的多模态学习框架，称为 OmniSegmentor。它有两个关键创新点：1）基于 ImageNet，我们构建了一个用于多模态预训练的大规模数据集，称为 OmniSegmentor，包含五种流行的视觉模态；2）我们提供了一种高效的预训练方式，使模型具备在 OmniSegmentor 中编码不同模态信息的能力。我们首次引入了一种通用的多模态预训练框架，无论所涉及模态的任意组合如何，都能在各种场景中持续增强模型的感知能力。值得注意的是，我们的 OmniSegmentor 在多种多模态语义分割数据集上取得了新的最先进结果，包括 NYU Depthv2、EventScape、MFNet、DeLiVER、SUNRGBD 和 KITTI-360。数据、模型检查点和源代码将公开提供：https://github.com/VCIP-RGBD/DFormer。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Rethinking Pareto Frontier: On the Optimal Trade-offs in Fair Classification
title_zh: 重新思考帕累托前沿：公平分类中最优权衡
abstract: Fairness has become an arising concern in machine learning with its prevalence in decision-making processes, and the trade-offs between various fairness notions and between fairness and accuracy has been empirically observed. However, the inheritance of such trade-offs, as well as the quantification of the best achievable trade-offs, i.e., the Pareto optimal trade-offs, under varied constraints on fairness notions has been rarely and improperly discussed. Owing to the sub-optimality of fairness interventions, existing work fails to provide informative characterization regarding these trade-offs. In light of existing limitations, in this work, we propose a reformulation of the model-specific (MS) Pareto optimal trade-off, where we frame it as convex optimization problems involving fairness notions and accuracy w.r.t. the confusion vector. Our formulation provides an efficient approximation of the best achievable accuracy under dynamic fairness constraints, and yields systematical analysis regarding the fairness-accuracy trade-off. Going beyond the discussion on fairness-accuracy trade-offs, we extend the discussion to the trade-off between fairness notions, which characterizes the impact of accuracy on the compatibility between fairness notions. Inspired by our reformulation, we propose a last-layer retraining framework with group-dependent bias, and we prove theoretically the superiority of our method over existing baselines. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our method in achieving better fairness-accuracy trade-off, and that our MS Pareto frontiers sufficiently quantify the two trade-offs.
abstract_zh: 随着机器学习在决策过程中的广泛应用，公平性已成为一个日益受到关注的问题，而且不同公平性概念之间以及公平性与准确性之间的权衡关系已被经验性地观察到。然而，这类权衡关系的内在传承性，以及在公平性概念受到不同约束时对最佳可实现权衡，即帕累托最优权衡的量化，却很少被讨论，而且现有讨论也并不恰当。由于公平性干预的次优性，现有工作无法对这些权衡关系给出有信息量的刻画。针对现有局限，在本文中，我们提出了对模型特定的（MS）帕累托最优权衡的重新表述，将其构建为关于混淆向量下公平性概念与准确性的凸优化问题。我们的表述能够在动态公平性约束下高效逼近最佳可实现准确性，并对公平性与准确性之间的权衡给出系统化分析。除了对公平性与准确性权衡的讨论之外，我们还将讨论扩展到公平性概念之间的权衡，从而刻画准确性对公平性概念之间兼容性的影响。受这一重新表述的启发，我们提出了一个带有群体依赖偏置的最后一层重训练框架，并从理论上证明了我们的方法相较于现有基线方法的优越性。实验结果表明，我们的方法在实现更优的公平性与准确性权衡方面是有效的，而且我们的模型特定帕累托前沿能够充分量化这两类权衡。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Prepacking: A Simple Method for Fast Prefilling and Increased Throughput in Large Language Models
title_zh: 预打包：一种实现大语言模型快速预填充与吞吐量提升的简易方法
abstract: During inference for transformer-based large language models (LLM), prefilling is the computation of the key-value (KV) cache for input tokens in the prompt prior to autoregressive generation. For longer input prompt lengths, prefilling will incur a significant overhead on decoding time. In this work, we highlight the following pitfall of prefilling: for batches containing high-varying prompt lengths, significant computation is wasted by the standard practice of padding sequences to the maximum length. As LLMs increasingly support longer context lengths, potentially up to 10 million tokens, variations in prompt lengths within a batch become more pronounced. To address this, we propose prepacking, a simple yet effective method to optimize prefilling computation. To avoid redundant computation on pad tokens, prepacking combines prompts of varying lengths into a sequence and packs multiple sequences into a compact batch using a bin-packing algorithm. It then modifies the attention mask and positional encoding to compute multiple prefilled KV-caches for multiple prompts within a single sequence. On standard curated dataset containing prompts with varying lengths, we obtain a significant speed and memory efficiency improvements as compared to the default padding-based prefilling computation within Huggingface across a range of base model configurations and inference serving scenarios.
abstract_zh: 在基于Transformer的大语言模型（LLM）推理过程中，预填充是指在自回归生成之前，为提示中的输入令牌计算键值（KV）缓存的操作。当输入提示长度较长时，预填充会给解码时间带来显著开销。本研究指出了预填充存在的如下缺陷：当批次内的提示长度差异较大时，将序列填充至最大长度的标准做法会造成大量计算资源浪费。随着大语言模型支持的上下文长度不断提升，最高可达1000万令牌，批次内的提示长度差异会变得更加突出。为解决这一问题，我们提出预打包方法，这是一种简单高效的预填充计算优化方法。为避免在填充令牌上产生冗余计算，预打包将长度各异的提示合并为一个序列，并通过装箱算法将多个序列打包为紧凑的批次。该方法随后修改注意力掩码与位置编码，从而为单个序列内的多个提示计算多个预填充KV缓存。在包含不同长度提示的标准整理数据集上，相较于Huggingface中默认的基于填充的预填充计算方法，我们的方法在各类基础模型配置与推理服务场景下均实现了速度与内存效率的显著提升。
notes: 调度专项得分=7；命中组=llm_serving；augment_batch=20260407-scheduling；augment_score=7

title: VPO: Reasoning Preferences Optimization Based on $\mathcal{V}$-Usable Information
title_zh: VPO：基于𝒱可用信息的推理偏好优化
abstract:
Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) is a widely used preference optimization algorithm in large language model (LLM) alignment, which reparameterizes the reward function in reinforcement learning with human feedback (RLHF) without requiring a separate reward model. However, during the DPO training process, when a large negative gradient is applied to low-confidence samples, LLMs with a softmax output head tend to squeeze the confidence in the model's output distribution towards the highest-confidence sentence, which may lead to a decrease in the confidence of both preference and non-preference samples, while increasing the confidence of unrelated tokens.
This phenomenon becomes more complex in reasoning tasks. 
In this work, focusing on reasoning tasks, we propose VPO, a negative gradient constraint method for human non-preference samples based on $\mathcal{V}$-usable information.
By using $\mathcal{V}$-usable information to measure the similarity between preference pairs and selectively constrain the negative gradient, VPO can alleviate the squeezing effect of DPO, enhance alignment with the generation objective, and maintain the model's ability to distinguish between preference and non-preference samples.
We compare VPO with DPO and its latest variants on mathematical reasoning tasks using the LLama 3.1 and Qwen 2.5 series, including both Base and Instruct models.
Our results demonstrate that VPO consistently and significantly outperforms existing methods. Specifically, on Qwen2.5-7B-Base, VPO achieves 7.80\% and 13.25\% improvement over DPO on MATH500 and AMC23, respectively. We also conduct ablation experiments and in-depth analysis on VPO to explain its effectiveness and rationale.
abstract_zh: 直接偏好优化（DPO）是一种在大语言模型（LLM）对齐中广泛使用的偏好优化算法，它在不需要单独奖励模型的情况下，对基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF）中的奖励函数进行了重新参数化。然而，在 DPO 训练过程中，当对低置信度样本施加较大的负梯度时，具有 softmax 输出头的 LLM 往往会将模型输出分布中的置信度压缩到最高置信度的句子上，这可能导致偏好样本和非偏好样本的置信度同时下降，同时提高无关词元的置信度。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Nearly Space-Optimal Graph and Hypergraph Sparsification in Insertion-Only Data Streams
title_zh: 插入式数据流中几乎空间最优的图与超图稀疏化
abstract: We study the problem of graph and hypergraph sparsification in insertion-only data streams. The input is a hypergraph $H=(V, E, w)$ with $n$ nodes, $m$ hyperedges, and rank $r$, and the goal is to compute a hypergraph $\widehat{H}$ that preserves the energy of each vector $x \in \mathbb{R}^n$ in $H$, up to a small multiplicative error. In this paper, we give a streaming algorithm that achieves a $(1+\varepsilon)$-approximation, using $\mathcal{O}\left(\frac{rn}{\varepsilon^2} \log^2 n \log r\right) \cdot$ poly $(\log \log m)$ bits of space, matching the sample complexity of the best known offline algorithm up to poly $(\log \log m)$ factors. Our approach also provides a streaming algorithm for graph sparsification that achieves a $(1+\varepsilon)$-approximation, using $\mathcal{O}\left(\frac{n}{\varepsilon^2} \log n\right)\cdot\text{poly}(\log\log n)$ bits of space, improving the current bound by $\log n$ factors. Furthermore, we give a space-efficient streaming algorithm for min-cut approximation. Along the way, we present an online algorithm for $(1+\varepsilon)$-hypergraph sparsification, which is optimal up to poly-logarithmic factors. Hence, we achieve $(1+\varepsilon)$-hypergraph sparsification in the sliding window model, with space optimal up to poly-logarithmic factors. Lastly, we give an adversarially robust algorithm for hypergraph sparsification using $\frac{n}{\varepsilon^2} \cdot $ poly $(r, \log n, \log r, \log \log m)$ bits of space.
abstract_zh: 我们研究仅插入数据流中的图与超图稀疏化问题。输入是一个超图 $H=(V,E,w)$，其具有 $n$ 个节点、$m$ 条超边以及秩 $r$；目标是计算一个超图 $\widehat{H}$，使其在 $H$ 中对每个向量 $x\in\mathbb{R}^n$ 的能量都能在一个很小的乘法误差内得到保持。本文给出了一种流式算法，能够达到 $(1+\varepsilon)$-近似，所用空间为 $\mathcal{O}\left(\frac{rn}{\varepsilon^2}\log^2 n \log r\right)\cdot \mathrm{poly}(\log\log m)$ 比特，除 $\mathrm{poly}(\log\log m)$ 因子外，与当前已知最佳离线算法的样本复杂度相匹配。我们的方法还给出了一种用于图稀疏化的流式算法，能够达到 $(1+\varepsilon)$-近似，所用空间为 $\mathcal{O}\left(\frac{n}{\varepsilon^2}\log n\right)\cdot \mathrm{poly}(\log\log n)$ 比特，相比当前界改进了 $\log n$ 因子。此外，我们还给出了一种用于最小割近似的空间高效流式算法。在此过程中，我们提出了一种用于 $(1+\varepsilon)$-超图稀疏化的在线算法，除多对数因子外是最优的。因此，我们在滑动窗口模型中实现了 $(1+\varepsilon)$-超图稀疏化，其空间复杂度除多对数因子外是最优的。最后，我们给出了一种对对抗性鲁棒的超图稀疏化算法，使用 $\frac{n}{\varepsilon^2}\cdot \mathrm{poly}(r,\log n,\log r,\log\log m)$ 比特空间。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: ArtUV: Artist-style UV Unwrapping
title_zh: ArtUV：艺术家风格的 UV 展开
abstract:
UV unwrapping is an essential task in computer graphics, enabling various visual editing operations in rendering pipelines. However, existing UV unwrapping methods struggle with time-consuming, fragmentation, lack of semanticity, and irregular UV islands, limiting their practical use. An artist-style UV map must not only satisfy fundamental criteria, such as overlap-free mapping and minimal distortion, but also uphold higher-level standards, including clean boundaries, efficient space utilization, and semantic coherence.
We introduce ArtUV, a fully automated, end-to-end method for generating artist-style UV unwrapping. We simulates the professional UV mapping process by dividing it into two stages: surface seam prediction and artist-style UV parameterization. In the seam prediction stage, SeamGPT is used to generate semantically meaningful cutting seams. Then, in the parameterization stage, a rough UV obtained from an optimization-based method, along with the mesh, is fed into an Auto-Encoder, which refines it into an artist-style UV map. Our method ensures semantic consistency and preserves topological structure, making the UV map ready for 2D editing. We evaluate ArtUV across multiple benchmarks and show that it serves as a versatile solution, functioning seamlessly as either a plug-in for professional rendering tools or as a standalone system for rapid, high-quality UV generation.
abstract_zh: UV 展开是计算机图形学中的一项基础任务，可在渲染流程中支持各种视觉编辑操作。然而，现有的 UV 展开方法存在耗时、碎片化、缺乏语义性以及 UV 岛不规则等问题，限制了其实用性。艺术家风格的 UV 贴图不仅必须满足无重叠映射和最小失真等基本标准，还需要符合更高层次的要求，包括边界整洁、空间利用高效以及语义连贯。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Palu: KV-Cache Compression with Low-Rank Projection
title_zh: Palu：带低秩投影的键值缓存压缩
abstract:
Post-training KV-Cache compression methods typically either sample a subset of effectual tokens or quantize the data into lower numerical bit width. However, these methods cannot exploit redundancy in the hidden dimension of the KV tenors. This paper presents a hidden dimension compression approach called Palu, a KV-Cache compression framework that utilizes low-rank projection to reduce inference-time LLM memory usage. Palu decomposes the linear layers into low-rank matrices, caches compressed intermediate states, and reconstructs the full keys and values on the fly. To improve accuracy, compression rate, and efficiency, Palu further encompasses (1) a medium-grained low-rank decomposition scheme, (2) an efficient rank search algorithm, (3) low-rank-aware quantization compatibility enhancements, and (4) an optimized GPU kernel with matrix fusion. Extensive experiments with popular LLMs show that Palu compresses KV-Cache by 50% while maintaining strong accuracy and delivering up to 1.89× speedup on the RoPE-based attention module. When combined with quantization, Palu’s
inherent quantization-friendly design yields small to negligible extra accuracy degradation while saving additional memory than quantization-only methods and achieving up to 2.91× speedup for the RoPE-based attention. Moreover, it maintains comparable or even better accuracy (up to 1.19 lower perplexity) compared to quantization-only methods. These results demonstrate Palu’s superior capability to effectively address the efficiency and memory challenges of LLM inference posed by KV-Cache. Our code is publicly available at: https://github.com/shadowpa0327/Palu.
abstract_zh: 训练后KV缓存压缩方法通常要么采样有效令牌的子集，要么将数据量化为更低的数值位宽，但这些方法无法利用KV张量隐藏维度中存在的冗余。本文提出了一种名为Palu的隐藏维度压缩方法，这是一种利用低秩投影降低大语言模型推理阶段内存占用的KV缓存压缩框架。Palu将线性层分解为低秩矩阵，缓存压缩后的中间状态，并动态重构完整的键与值。为提升准确率、压缩率与效率，Palu进一步包含以下设计：（1）中等粒度低秩分解方案，（2）高效秩搜索算法，（3）低秩感知量化兼容性增强模块，（4）支持矩阵融合的优化GPU内核。针对主流大语言模型的大量实验表明，Palu将KV缓存压缩50%的同时保持了优异的准确率，并且在基于RoPE的注意力模块上实现了最高1.89倍的加速。当与量化技术结合时，Palu本身的量化友好设计只会带来极小甚至可忽略的额外准确率下降，同时相比仅采用量化的方法能进一步节省内存，且在基于RoPE的注意力模块上实现最高2.91倍的加速。此外，与仅采用量化的方法相比，Palu的准确率与之相当甚至更优，困惑度最高可降低1.19。这些结果表明，Palu具备出色的能力，可有效解决KV缓存给大语言模型推理带来的效率与内存挑战。我们的代码已公开于https://github.com/shadowpa0327/Palu。
notes: 论文核心是面向 LLM 推理阶段的 KV-Cache 低秩压缩，并辅以 GPU kernel/matrix fusion 优化；不属于负样本。

title: HQA-VLAttack: Towards High Quality Adversarial Attack on Vision-Language Pre-Trained Models
title_zh: HQA-VLAttack：面向视觉语言预训练模型的高质量对抗攻击
abstract: Black-box adversarial attack on vision-language pre-trained models is a practical and challenging task, as text and image perturbations need to be considered simultaneously, and only the predicted results are accessible. Research on this problem is in its infancy, and only a handful of methods are available. Nevertheless, existing methods either rely on a complex iterative cross-search strategy, which inevitably consumes numerous queries, or only consider reducing the similarity of positive image-text pairs but ignore that of negative ones, which will also be implicitly diminished, thus inevitably affecting the attack performance. To alleviate the above issues, we propose a simple yet effective framework to generate high-quality adversarial examples on vision-language pre-trained models, named HQA-VLAttack, which consists of text and image attack stages. For text perturbation generation, it leverages the counter-fitting word vector to generate the substitute word set, thus guaranteeing the semantic consistency between the substitute word and the original word. For image perturbation generation, it first initializes the image adversarial example via the layer-importance guided strategy, and then utilizes contrastive learning to optimize the image adversarial perturbation, which ensures that the similarity of positive image-text pairs is decreased while that of negative image-text pairs is increased. In this way, the optimized adversarial images and texts are more likely to retrieve negative examples, thereby enhancing the attack success rate. Experimental results on three benchmark datasets demonstrate that HQA-VLAttack significantly outperforms strong baselines in terms of attack success rate.
abstract_zh: 针对视觉-语言预训练模型的黑盒对抗攻击是一项具有实用价值且富有挑战性的任务，因为需要同时考虑文本和图像扰动，并且只能访问预测结果。关于这一问题的研究尚处于起步阶段，可用方法屈指可数。然而，现有方法要么依赖复杂的迭代式交叉搜索策略，这不可避免地会消耗大量查询；要么只考虑降低正样本图文对的相似度，却忽略了负样本图文对的相似度也会被隐式降低，从而不可避免地影响攻击性能。为缓解上述问题，我们提出了一个简单而有效的框架，用于在视觉-语言预训练模型上生成高质量对抗样本，称为 HQA-VLAttack，该框架由文本攻击阶段和图像攻击阶段组成。对于文本扰动生成，该方法利用 counter-fitting 词向量生成替换词集合，从而保证替换词与原词之间的语义一致性。对于图像扰动生成，该方法首先通过层重要性引导策略初始化图像对抗样本，然后利用对比学习优化图像对抗扰动，从而确保正样本图文对的相似度降低，而负样本图文对的相似度提高。通过这种方式，优化后的对抗图像和文本更有可能检索到负样本，从而提升攻击成功率。在三个基准数据集上的实验结果表明，HQA-VLAttack 在攻击成功率方面显著优于强基线方法。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Delta Attention: Fast and Accurate Sparse Attention Inference by Delta Correction
title_zh: Delta Attention：通过增量校正实现快速准确的稀疏注意力推理
abstract: The attention mechanism of a transformer has a quadratic complexity, leading to high inference costs and latency for long sequences. However, attention matrices are mostly sparse, which implies that many entries may be omitted from computation for efficient inference. Sparse attention inference methods aim to reduce this computational burden; however, they also come with a troublesome performance degradation. We discover that one reason for this degradation is that the sparse calculation induces a distributional shift in the attention outputs. The distributional shift causes decoding-time queries to fail to align well with the appropriate keys from the prefill stage, leading to a drop in performance. We propose a simple, novel, and effective procedure for correcting this distributional shift, bringing the distribution of sparse attention outputs closer to that of quadratic attention. Our method can be applied on top of any sparse attention method, and results in an average 36\%pt performance increase, recovering 88\% of quadratic attention accuracy on the 131K RULER benchmark when applied on top of sliding window attention with sink tokens while only adding a small overhead. Our method can maintain approximately 98.5\% sparsity over full quadratic attention, making our model 32 times faster than Flash Attention 2 when processing 1M token prefills.
abstract_zh: Transformer的注意力机制具有二次复杂度，处理长序列时会导致推理成本高、延迟大。但注意力矩阵大多为稀疏矩阵，这意味着为实现高效推理可以省略大量矩阵条目的计算。稀疏注意力推理方法旨在降低这一计算负担，但这类方法也存在棘手的性能下降问题。我们发现，导致这种性能下降的一个原因是稀疏计算会引发注意力输出的分布偏移。这种分布偏移会导致解码阶段的查询无法与预填充阶段的匹配键良好对齐，进而造成性能下滑。我们提出了一种简单、新颖且有效的流程来校正这种分布偏移，使稀疏注意力输出的分布更接近二次注意力的输出分布。我们的方法可叠加在任意稀疏注意力方法之上，当将其应用于带水槽标记的滑动窗口注意力时，可实现平均36个百分点的性能提升，在131K RULER基准测试上恢复了88%的二次注意力准确率，且仅增加了极小的开销。相较于完整的二次注意力，我们的方法可保持约98.5%的稀疏度，在处理100万标记的预填充任务时，模型速度比Flash Attention 2快32倍。
notes: 论文聚焦 LLM 长上下文稀疏注意力推理，通过 delta correction 缓解 prefill-to-decoding 分布偏移；更偏向注意力/推理结构优化，并涉及长上下文阶段的 prefill-decoding 对齐。

title: Topology-Aware Conformal Prediction for Stream Networks
title_zh: 针对流网络的拓扑感知保形预测
abstract: Stream networks, a unique class of spatiotemporal graphs, exhibit complex directional flow constraints and evolving dependencies, making uncertainty quantification a critical yet challenging task. Traditional conformal prediction methods struggle in this setting due to the need for joint predictions across multiple interdependent locations and the intricate spatio-temporal dependencies inherent in stream networks. Existing approaches either neglect dependencies, leading to overly conservative predictions, or rely solely on data-driven estimations, failing to capture the rich topological structure of the network. To address these challenges, we propose Spatio-Temporal Adaptive Conformal Inference (STACI), a novel framework that integrates network topology and temporal dynamics into the conformal prediction framework. STACI introduces a topology-aware nonconformity score that respects directional flow constraints and dynamically adjusts prediction sets to account for temporal distributional shifts. We provide theoretical guarantees on the validity of our approach and demonstrate its superior performance on both synthetic and real-world datasets. Our results show that STACI effectively balances prediction efficiency and coverage, outperforming existing conformal prediction methods for stream networks.
abstract_zh: 流网络是一类独特的时空图，具有复杂的有向流动约束和不断演化的依赖关系，这使得不确定性量化成为一项关键但又充满挑战的任务。传统保形预测方法在这种场景下面临困难，因为它们需要对多个相互依赖的位置进行联合预测，同时还要处理流网络中固有的复杂时空依赖。现有方法要么忽视这些依赖关系，导致预测过于保守；要么仅依赖数据驱动的估计，无法捕捉网络丰富的拓扑结构。为应对这些挑战，我们提出了时空自适应保形推断（STACI），这是一种将网络拓扑和时间动态整合到保形预测框架中的新方法。STACI 引入了一种具有拓扑感知能力的不符合度评分，能够遵循有向流动约束，并动态调整预测集以应对时间上的分布漂移。我们为该方法提供了有效性的理论保证，并在合成数据集和真实数据集上展示了其优越性能。结果表明，STACI 能够有效平衡预测效率与覆盖率，在流网络保形预测任务上优于现有方法。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Cut the Crap: An Economical Communication Pipeline for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
title_zh: 去冗存精：面向基于大语言模型的多智能体系统的低开销通信流水线
abstract:
Recent advancements in large language model (LLM)-powered agents have shown that collective intelligence can significantly outperform individual capabilities, largely attributed to the meticulously designed inter-agent communication topologies. Though impressive in performance, existing multi-agent pipelines inherently introduce substantial token overhead, as well as increased economic costs, which pose challenges for their large-scale deployments. In response to this challenge, we propose an economical, simple, and robust multi-agent communication framework, termed $\texttt{AgentPrune}$, which can seamlessly integrate into mainstream multi-agent systems and prunes redundant or even malicious communication messages. Technically, $\texttt{AgentPrune}$ is the first to identify and formally define the $\textit{Communication Redundancy}$ issue present in current LLM-based multi-agent pipelines, and efficiently performs one-shot pruning on the spatial-temporal message-passing graph, yielding a token-economic and high-performing communication topology.
Extensive experiments across six benchmarks demonstrate that $\texttt{AgentPrune}$ $\textbf{(I)}$ achieves comparable results as state-of-the-art topologies at merely $\\$5.6$ cost compared to their $\\$43.7$, $\textbf{(II)}$ integrates seamlessly into existing multi-agent frameworks with $28.1\\%\sim72.8\\%\downarrow$ token reduction, and $\textbf{(III)}$ successfully defend against two types of agent-based adversarial attacks with $3.5\\%\sim10.8\\%\uparrow$ performance boost. The source code is available at \url{https://github.com/yanweiyue/AgentPrune}.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）驱动的智能体近期研究进展表明，群体智能的表现可显著优于个体能力，这很大程度上得益于精心设计的智能体间通信拓扑结构。尽管现有多智能体管线性能表现优异，但其固有特性会产生大量token开销，同时带来更高的经济成本，这对其大规模部署构成了挑战。针对这一挑战，我们提出了一种经济、简洁且鲁棒的多智能体通信框架AgentPrune，该框架可无缝集成到主流多智能体系统中，剪去冗余甚至恶意的通信消息。从技术层面来看，AgentPrune首次识别并正式定义了当前基于大语言模型的多智能体管线中存在的通信冗余问题，可对时空消息传递图高效执行一次性剪枝，从而生成token用量经济、性能优异的通信拓扑结构。我们在6个基准测试上开展了大量实验，结果表明：第一，AgentPrune仅需5.6美元的成本即可取得与当前最优拓扑结构相当的效果，而后者的成本为43.7美元；第二，该框架可无缝集成到现有多智能体框架中，可降低28.1%~72.8%的token用量；第三，该框架可成功抵御两类基于智能体的对抗攻击，实现3.5%~10.8%的性能提升。源代码可从https://github.com/yanweiyue/AgentPrune获取。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: LLMs Must Think Thrice to Solve Executable Counterfactuals
title_zh: LLMs必须三次思考才能解决可执行的反事实
abstract: Counterfactual reasoning, a hallmark of intelligence, consists of three steps: inferring latent variables from observations (abduction), constructing alternative situations (interventions), and predicting the outcomes of the alternatives (prediction). This skill is essential for advancing LLMs' causal understanding and expanding their applications in high-stakes domains such as scientific research and healthcare. However, existing efforts in assessing LLM's counterfactual reasoning capabilities tend to skip the abduction step, effectively reducing to interventional reasoning and leading to over-estimated LLM performance. To address this, we introduce executable counterfactuals, a novel framework that operationalizes causal reasoning through code and math problems. Our framework explicitly  requires all three steps of counterfactual reasoning and enables scalable synthetic data creation with varying difficulty, creating a new frontier for evaluating and improving LLM's reasoning. Our results reveal substantial drop in accuracy (25-40%) from interventional to counterfactual reasoning for state-of-the-art models such as o4-mini and Claude-4-Sonnet. To address this gap, we construct a training set comprising counterfactual code problems having if-condition and test on out-of-distribution code structures (e.g.,  having while-loop); we also test whether a model trained on code would generalize to counterfactual math word problems.  While Supervised Finetuning (SFT) on stronger models' reasoning traces improves in-distribution performance of Qwen models, it leads to a decrease in accuracy on out-of-distribution tasks such as counterfactual math problems. In contrast, reinforcement learning (RL) induces the core cognitive behaviors and generalizes to new distributions, yielding substantial accuracy gains over the base model on both code (improvement of 1.5x-2x) and counterfactual math problems. Analysis of the reasoning traces further reinforces these findings and highlights the promise of RL with scalable data generation for improving LLMs' counterfactual reasoning.
abstract_zh: 反事实推理是智能的标志性能力，由三个步骤组成：从观测中推断潜变量（溯因），构建替代情境（干预），以及预测这些替代情境的结果（预测）。这种能力对于推进大语言模型的因果理解以及拓展其在科学研究和医疗保健等高风险领域中的应用至关重要。然而，现有对大语言模型反事实推理能力的评估工作往往跳过溯因这一步，实际上将问题简化为干预推理，从而高估了大语言模型的表现。为了解决这一问题，我们提出可执行反事实（executable counterfactuals），这是一个通过代码和数学问题将因果推理操作化的新框架。我们的框架明确要求反事实推理的三个步骤全部参与，并能够构建具有不同难度的可扩展合成数据，为评估和提升大语言模型的推理能力开辟了新方向。我们的结果表明，对于 o4-mini 和 Claude-4-Sonnet 等最先进模型，从干预推理转向反事实推理时，其准确率会显著下降（25%–40%）。为弥补这一差距，我们构建了一个训练集，其中包含带有 if 条件的反事实代码问题，并在分布外代码结构（例如包含 while 循环）上进行测试；我们还测试了在代码上训练的模型是否能够泛化到反事实数学应用题。尽管在更强模型的推理轨迹上进行监督微调（SFT）能提升 Qwen 模型在分布内任务上的表现，但它会导致模型在反事实数学问题等分布外任务上的准确率下降。相比之下，强化学习（RL）能够诱导出核心认知行为，并泛化到新的分布，在代码任务和反事实数学问题上都相较于基础模型带来了显著的准确率提升，其中代码任务提升了 1.5 倍到 2 倍。对推理轨迹的分析进一步强化了这些发现，并凸显了结合可扩展数据生成的强化学习在提升大语言模型反事实推理能力方面的前景。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Capacity-Aware Inference: Mitigating the Straggler Effect in Mixture of Experts
title_zh: 容量感知推理：缓解混合专家模型中的掉队者效应
abstract:
The Mixture of Experts (MoE) is an effective architecture for scaling large language models by leveraging sparse expert activation to balance performance and efficiency. However, under expert parallelism, MoE suffers from inference inefficiencies due to imbalanced token-to-expert assignment, where underloaded experts complete computations early but must wait for overloaded experts, leading to global delays. We define this phenomenon as the \textbf{\textit{Straggler Effect}}, as the most burdened experts dictate the overall inference latency. To address this, we first propose \textit{\textbf{Capacity-Aware Token Drop}}, which enforces expert capacity limits by discarding excess tokens from overloaded experts, effectively reducing load imbalance with minimal performance impact (e.g., $30\%$ speedup with only $0.9\%$ degradation on OLMoE).  
Next, given the presence of low-load experts remaining well below the capacity threshold, we introduce \textit{\textbf{Capacity-Aware Expanded Drop}}, which allows tokens to include additional local experts in their candidate set before enforcing strict local capacity constraints, thereby improving load balance and enhancing the utilization of underused experts. 
Extensive experiments on both language and multimodal MoE models demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach, yielding substantial gains in expert utilization, model performance, and inference efficiency, e.g., applying Expanded Drop to Mixtral-8$\times$7B-Instruct yields a {0.2\%} average performance improvement and a {1.85$\times$} inference speedup. The code is released at: https://github.com/CASE-Lab-UMD/Capacity-Aware-MoE.
abstract_zh:
混合专家模型（MoE）是借助稀疏专家激活平衡性能与效率，从而实现大语言模型扩展的有效架构。但在专家并行模式下，令牌到专家的分配不均衡会导致MoE出现推理效率低下的问题：负载偏低的专家早早完成计算，却必须等待过载专家完成计算，进而引发全局延迟。我们将这一现象定义为拖尾效应，因为整体推理延迟由负载最高的专家决定。为解决该问题，我们首先提出容量感知令牌丢弃策略，该策略通过丢弃过载专家的多余令牌强制执行专家容量限制，能够在对性能影响极小的前提下有效降低负载不均衡程度，例如在OLMoE上实现30%的速度提升，而性能仅下降0.9%。
接下来，考虑到仍有不少低负载专家的负载远低于容量阈值，我们提出容量感知扩展丢弃策略，该策略允许令牌在执行严格的本地容量约束前，将额外的本地专家纳入候选集，从而改善负载均衡，提升未被充分利用的专家的利用率。
我们在语言类和多模态类MoE模型上开展了大量实验，结果证明了所提方法的有效性，该方法在专家利用率、模型性能和推理效率方面均取得了显著提升，例如将扩展丢弃策略应用于Mixtral-8×7B-Instruct时，平均性能提升0.2%，推理速度提升1.85倍。代码发布地址为：https://github.com/CASE-Lab-UMD/Capacity-Aware-MoE。
notes: 调度专项得分=20；命中组=capacity-aware inference, llm_serving, moe_scheduling；augment_batch=20260402-scheduling；augment_score=20

title: Align-SAM: Seeking Flatter Minima for Better Cross-Subset Alignment
title_zh: Align-SAM 寻求更平坦的极小值以改善跨子集对齐
abstract: Sharpness-Aware Minimization (SAM) has proven effective in enhancing deep neural network training by simultaneously minimizing the training loss and the sharpness of the loss landscape, thereby guiding models toward flatter minima that are empirically linked to improved generalization. From another perspective, generalization can be seen as a model’s ability to remain stable under distributional variability. In particular, effective learning requires that updates derived from different subsets or resamplings of the same data distribution remain consistent. In this work, we investigate the connection between the flatness induced by SAM and the alignment of gradients across random subsets of the data distribution, and propose \textit{Align-SAM} as a novel strategy to further enhance model generalization. Align-SAM extends the core principles of SAM by promoting optimization toward flatter minima on a primary subset (the training set), while simultaneously enforcing low loss on an auxiliary subset drawn from the same distribution. This dual-objective approach leads to solutions that are not only resilient to local perturbations but also robust against distributional shifts in each training iteration. Empirical evaluations demonstrate that Align-SAM consistently improves generalization across diverse datasets and challenging settings, including scenarios with noisy labels and limited data availability.
abstract_zh: Sharpness-Aware Minimization（SAM）已被证明能够有效提升深度神经网络训练效果，它通过同时最小化训练损失和损失景观的尖锐性，引导模型趋向更平坦的极小值，而经验上这类极小值与更好的泛化能力相关。从另一个角度看，泛化可以被视为模型在分布变化下保持稳定的能力。具体而言，有效学习要求从同一数据分布的不同子集或重采样中得到的更新保持一致。在这项工作中，我们研究了由SAM诱导的平坦性与数据分布随机子集之间梯度对齐的关系，并提出了作为一种新策略的Align-SAM，以进一步增强模型泛化能力。Align-SAM扩展了SAM的核心原则，在促进模型在主子集（训练集）上优化到更平坦极小值的同时，还在一个从同一分布中抽取的辅助子集上强制实现低损失。这种双目标方法得到的解不仅对局部扰动具有韧性，而且在每次训练迭代中也能抵抗分布偏移。实证评估表明，Align-SAM在多种数据集和具有挑战性的设置中都能持续提升泛化能力，包括含有噪声标签和数据可用性有限的场景。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: ThinKV: Thought-Adaptive KV Cache Compression for Efficient Reasoning Models
title_zh: ThinKV: 面向高效推理模型的思维自适应 KV 缓存压缩
abstract: The long-output context generation of large reasoning models enables extended chain of thought (CoT) but also drives rapid growth of the key–value (KV) cache, quickly overwhelming GPU memory. To address this challenge, we propose ThinKV, a thought-adaptive KV cache compression framework. ThinKV is based on the observation that attention sparsity reveals distinct thought types with varying importance within the CoT. It applies a hybrid quantization–eviction strategy, assigning token precision by thought importance and progressively evicting tokens from less critical thoughts as reasoning trajectories evolve. Furthermore, to implement ThinKV, we design a kernel that extends PagedAttention to enable efficient reuse of evicted tokens' memory slots, eliminating compaction overheads. Extensive experiments on DeepSeek-R1-Distill, GPT-OSS, and NVIDIA AceReason across mathematics and coding benchmarks show that ThinKV achieves near-lossless accuracy with less than 5% of the original KV cache, while improving performance with up to 5.8x higher inference throughput over SoTA baselines.
abstract_zh: 大型推理模型的长输出上下文生成可实现扩展思维链（CoT），但也会导致键值（KV）缓存快速增长，迅速占满GPU内存。为应对这一挑战，我们提出ThinKV，一种思维自适应KV缓存压缩框架。ThinKV的设计基于如下观察：注意力稀疏性能够反映思维链中重要性各不相同的不同思维类型。该框架采用混合量化-驱逐策略，根据思维重要性分配令牌精度，并随着推理轨迹的演进逐步驱逐重要性更低的思维所包含的令牌。此外，为实现ThinKV，我们设计了一款扩展分页注意力（PagedAttention）的内核，可高效复用被驱逐令牌的内存槽，消除了内存整理开销。我们在数学与编程基准测试集上针对DeepSeek-R1-Distill、GPT-OSS和NVIDIA AceReason开展了大量实验，结果表明，ThinKV在仅占用不到5%的原始KV缓存的前提下实现了近乎无损的精度，同时相较于当前最优基线方案，其推理吞吐量最高可提升5.8倍。
notes: 论文聚焦推理阶段 KV cache 压缩与实现优化，核心是 thought-adaptive 量化+驱逐，并配套 PagedAttention 内核扩展。

title: Jasmine: Harnessing Diffusion Prior for Self-supervised Depth Estimation
title_zh: Jasmine：利用扩散先验进行自监督深度估计
abstract: In this paper, we propose \textbf{Jasmine}, the first Stable Diffusion (SD)-based self-supervised framework for monocular depth estimation, which effectively harnesses SD’s visual priors to enhance the sharpness and generalization of unsupervised prediction. Previous SD-based methods are all supervised since adapting diffusion models for dense prediction requires high-precision supervision. In contrast, self-supervised reprojection suffers from inherent challenges (\textit{e.g.}, occlusions, texture-less regions, illumination variance), and the predictions exhibit blurs and artifacts that severely compromise SD's latent priors. To resolve this, we construct a novel surrogate task of mix-batch image reconstruction. Without any additional supervision, it preserves the detail priors of SD models by reconstructing the images themselves while preventing depth estimation from degradation. Furthermore, to address the inherent misalignment between SD's scale and shift invariant estimation and self-supervised scale-invariant depth estimation, we build the Scale-Shift GRU. It not only bridges this distribution gap but also isolates the fine-grained texture of SD output against the interference of reprojection loss. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Jasmine achieves SoTA performance on the KITTI benchmark and exhibits superior zero-shot generalization across multiple datasets.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了Jasmine，这是首个基于 Stable Diffusion（SD）的单目深度估计自监督框架，能够有效利用 SD 的视觉先验来提升无监督预测的清晰度和泛化能力。以往基于 SD 的方法均为监督式方法，因为将扩散模型适配到稠密预测需要高精度监督。相比之下，自监督重投影面临固有挑战（例如遮挡、无纹理区域、光照变化），其预测结果会出现模糊和伪影，严重损害 SD 的潜在先验。为解决这一问题，我们构建了一种新的混合批次图像重建替代任务。在无需任何额外监督的情况下，它通过重建图像本身来保留 SD 模型的细节先验，同时防止深度估计性能退化。此外，为了解决 SD 的尺度与平移不变估计和自监督尺度不变深度估计之间固有的不对齐问题，我们构建了 Scale-Shift GRU。它不仅弥合了这种分布差距，还将 SD 输出中的细粒度纹理与重投影损失的干扰隔离开来。大量实验表明，Jasmine 在 KITTI 基准上取得了当前最优性能，并在多个数据集上展现出优越的零样本泛化能力。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: OWL: Optimized Workforce Learning for General Multi-Agent Assistance in Real-World Task Automation
title_zh: OWL  面向真实任务自动化的通用多智能体协助的优化劳动力学习
abstract:
Large Language Model (LLM)-based multi-agent systems show promise for automating real-world tasks but struggle to transfer across domains due to their domain-specific nature.
Current approaches face two critical shortcomings: they require complete architectural redesign and full retraining of all components when applied to new domains.
We introduce **Workforce**, a hierarchical multi-agent framework that decouples strategic planning from specialized execution through a modular architecture comprising:
*(i)* a *domain-agnostic* **Planner** for task decomposition,
*(ii)* a **Coordinator** for subtask management, and
*(iii)* specialized **Workers** with *domain-specific* tool-calling capabilities.
This decoupling enables cross-domain transferability during both inference and training phases:
During inference, Workforce seamlessly adapts to new domains by adding or modifying worker agents;
For training, we introduce **Optimized Workforce Learning (OWL)**, which improves generalization across domains by optimizing a domain-agnostic planner with reinforcement learning from real-world feedback.
To validate our approach, we evaluate Workforce on the GAIA benchmark, covering various realistic, multi-domain agentic tasks.
Experimental results demonstrate Workforce achieves open-source state-of-the-art performance (**69.70%**), outperforming commercial systems like OpenAI's Deep Research by **2.34%**.
More notably, our OWL-trained 32B model achieves **52.73%** accuracy (**+16.37%**) and demonstrates performance comparable to GPT-4o on challenging tasks.
To summarize, by enabling scalable generalization and modular domain transfer, our work establishes a foundation for the next generation of general-purpose AI assistants.

*Our code is available at [Anonymous URL](https://anonymous.4open.science/r/annonymous-owl/), and our data is available at [Anonymous URL](https://huggingface.co/anonymous21016).*
abstract_zh: 大语言模型驱动的多智能体系统在自动化现实任务方面潜力巨大，但因领域高度专一而难以跨域迁移。现有方法在迁移新领域时有两个关键短板：必须彻底重新设计架构并对所有组件重新训练。我们提出 Workforce——一个层次化多智能体框架，通过模块化架构将战略规划与专业执行解耦，包含：（i）面向任务分解的领域无关 Planner，（ii）负责子任务调度的 Coordinator，以及（iii）具备领域特定工具调用能力的专业 Workers。这种解耦在推理和训练阶段都能实现跨域可迁移性：推理过程中，新增或调整 Worker 即可适配新领域；训练阶段，我们引入 Optimized Workforce Learning（OWL），通过来自真实反馈的强化学习优化领域无关的 Planner，从而强化跨域泛化能力。在 GAIA 基准上评估多领域、多任务的 Agent 性能，实验证明 Workforce 达成开源最佳成绩 69.70%，比 OpenAI Deep Research 高出 2.34%。更值得注意的是，使用 OWL 训练的 32B 模型在难题上取得 52.73% 的准确率，提升 16.37%，其表现已接近 GPT-4o。综上，通过实现可扩展的泛化能力和模块化的领域迁移，我们为下一代通用 AI 助手奠定了基础。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Continuous Audio Language Models
title_zh: 连续音频语言模型
abstract:
Audio Language Models (ALM) have emerged as the dominant paradigm for speech and music generation by representing audio as sequences of discrete tokens. Yet, unlike text tokens, which are invertible, audio tokens are extracted from lossy codecs with a limited bitrate. As a consequence, increasing audio quality requires generating more tokens, which imposes a trade-off between fidelity and computational cost. We address this issue by studying Continuous Audio Language Models (CALM). These models instantiate a large Transformer backbone that produces a contextual embedding at every timestep. This sequential information then conditions an MLP that generates the next continuous frame of an audio VAE through consistency modeling. By avoiding lossy compression, CALM achieves higher quality at lower computational cost than their discrete counterpart. Experiments on speech and music demonstrate improved efficiency and fidelity over state-of-the-art discrete audio language models, facilitating lightweight, high-quality audio generation.
Samples are available at iclr-continuous-audio-language-models.github.io. Finally, we release Pocket TTS, an open-source 100M-parameter text-to-speech model that can run faster than real time on a laptop CPU: github.com/kyutai-labs/pocket-tts.
abstract_zh: 音频语言模型（ALM）通过将音频表示为离散标记序列，已成为语音和音乐生成的主导范式。然而，与可逆的文本标记不同，音频标记是从比特率有限的有损编解码器中提取的。因此，提高音频质量需要生成更多标记，这在保真度与计算成本之间带来了权衡。我们通过研究连续音频语言模型（CALM）来解决这一问题。这类模型实例化了一个大型 Transformer 主干网络，在每个时间步产生一个上下文化嵌入。随后，这一序列信息通过一致性建模来条件化一个 MLP，以生成音频 VAE 的下一个连续帧。通过避免有损压缩，CALM 以低于其离散对应模型的计算成本实现了更高的质量。在语音和音乐上的实验表明，与最先进的离散音频语言模型相比，CALM 在效率和保真度方面都有所提升，从而促进了轻量级、高质量的音频生成。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Generative Caching for Structurally Similar Prompts and Responses
title_zh: 面向结构相似提示与响应的生成式缓存
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly being used to plan, reason, and execute tasks across diverse scenarios. In use cases like repeatable workflows and agentic settings, prompts are often reused with minor variations while having a similar structure for recurring tasks. This opens up opportunities for caching. However, exact prompt matching fails on such structurally similar prompts, while semantic caching may produce incorrect responses by ignoring critical differences. To address this, we introduce GenCache, a generative cache that produces variation-aware responses for structurally similar prompts. GenCache identifies reusable response patterns across similar prompt structures and synthesizes customized outputs for new requests. We show that GenCache achieves 83\% cache hit rate, while having minimal incorrect hits on datasets without prompt repetition. In agentic workflows, it improves cache hit rate by $\sim$20\% and reduces end-to-end execution latency by $\sim$34\% compared to standard prompt matching.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）正被越来越多地用于各类场景下的任务规划、推理与执行。在可重复工作流、智能体设置等用例中，针对重复性任务的提示词通常结构相似，仅存在微小差异便被复用，这为缓存技术的应用创造了机会。不过，精确提示词匹配无法适配这类结构相似的提示词，而语义缓存可能因忽略关键差异生成错误响应。为解决该问题，我们提出GenCache，这是一种生成式缓存，可为结构相似的提示词生成感知差异的响应。GenCache能够识别相似提示词结构下可复用的响应模式，为新请求合成定制化输出。实验表明，GenCache的缓存命中率可达83%，且在无提示词重复的数据集上的错误命中极少。在智能体工作流中，与标准提示词匹配方法相比，GenCache的缓存命中率提升了约20%，端到端执行延迟降低了约34%。
notes: 调度专项得分=7；命中组=llm_serving；augment_batch=20260402-scheduling；augment_score=7

title: Transformer Copilot: Learning from The Mistake Log in LLM Fine-tuning
title_zh: Transformer Copilot：在大语言模型微调中从错误日志学习
abstract: Large language models are typically adapted to downstream tasks through supervised fine-tuning on domain-specific data. While standard fine-tuning focuses on minimizing generation loss to optimize model parameters, we take a deeper step by retaining and leveraging the model’s own learning signals, analogous to how human learners reflect on past mistakes to improve future performance. We first introduce the concept of Mistake Log to systematically track the model’s learning behavior and recurring errors throughout fine-tuning. Treating the original transformer-based model as the Pilot, we correspondingly design a Copilot model to refine the Pilot’s inference performance via logits rectification. We name the overall Pilot-Copilot framework the Transformer Copilot, which introduces (i) a novel Copilot model design, (ii) a joint training paradigm where the Copilot continuously learns from the evolving Mistake Log alongside the Pilot, and (iii) a fused inference paradigm where the Copilot rectifies the Pilot’s logits for enhanced generation. We provide both theoretical and empirical analyses on our new learning framework. Experiments on 12 benchmarks spanning commonsense, arithmetic, and recommendation tasks demonstrate that Transformer Copilot consistently improves performance by up to 34.5%, while introducing marginal computational overhead to Pilot models and exhibiting strong scalability and transferability. Our code is released at https://github.com/jiaruzouu/TransformerCopilot.
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型通常通过在特定领域数据上进行监督微调来适配下游任务。标准微调侧重于最小化生成损失以优化模型参数，而我们更进一步，保留并利用模型自身的学习信号，这类似于人类学习者通过反思过去的错误来提升未来表现。我们首先提出“错误日志”（Mistake Log）的概念，用于在微调过程中系统地跟踪模型的学习行为和反复出现的错误。将原始基于 Transformer 的模型视为主驾驶（Pilot），我们相应地设计了一个副驾驶（Copilot）模型，通过 logits 校正来提升主驾驶的推理性能。我们将这一整体的主驾驶-副驾驶框架命名为 Transformer Copilot，其引入了：(i) 一种新的副驾驶模型设计；(ii) 一种联合训练范式，其中副驾驶与主驾驶一起从不断演化的错误日志中持续学习；以及 (iii) 一种融合推理范式，其中副驾驶对主驾驶的 logits 进行校正以增强生成效果。我们从理论和实验两方面对这一新的学习框架进行了分析。在涵盖常识、算术和推荐任务的 12 个基准测试上的实验表明，Transformer Copilot 能够稳定地将性能提升最高达 34.5%，同时仅为主驾驶模型引入很小的计算开销，并表现出很强的可扩展性和可迁移性。我们的代码已发布于 https://github.com/jiaruzouu/TransformerCopilot。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Sem-MoE: Semantic-aware Model-Data Collaborative Scheduling for Efficient MoE Inference
title_zh: Sem-MoE：面向高效MoE推理的语义感知模型-数据协同调度
abstract:
Prevailing LLM (Large Language Model) serving engines employ expert parallelism (EP) to implement multi-device inference of massive Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models. However, the efficiency of expert parallel inference is largely bounded by inter-device communication, as EP embraces expensive all-to-all collectives to route tokens to the remote experts if not collocating on the same GPU/NPU device. Nevertheless, state-of-the-art schemes treat expert device-placement and request (or token) device-scheduling as separate concerns, triggering excessive communication between them and compromising inference efficiency

This paper proposes Sem-MoE, a novel \textbf{model-data} collaborative scheduling framework to minimize the steep communication costs in EP-centric MoE serving. Sem-MoE maximally collocates experts and their activating tokens onto the same device using proactively modeled activation likelihood between them and introduces three key techniques: (1) Offline model scheduling, which preliminarily clusters and collocates experts onto devices based on their co-activation tendencies for certain classes of input. (2) Online inter-request data scheduling for Attention-DP setups, which proactively rebatches incoming requests onto the device that hosts experts most likely and frequently activated by the corresponding requests. (3) Online intra-request data scheduling for Attention-TP setups, which seamlessly fuses a token reshuffling procedure into the original inference pipeline and proactively reschedules tokens to devices to reduce dispersed remote routing. We build Sem-MoE into a prevailing LLM serving engine SGLANG. Experiments show our collaborative scheduling approach can effectively reduce the all-to-all communication volume in EP and achieve superior inference throughput compared to existing solutions.
abstract_zh:
主流大语言模型（Large Language Model，LLM）服务引擎采用专家并行（Expert Parallelism，EP）实现大规模混合专家（Mixture-of-Experts，MoE）模型的多设备推理。然而，专家并行推理的效率在很大程度上受限于设备间通信，若令牌未被共置在同一GPU/NPU设备上，专家并行需要采用开销极高的全对全集合通信将令牌路由到远端专家。但目前的最优方案将专家的设备部署与请求（或令牌）的设备调度视为两个独立的问题，引发二者之间的过量通信，进而降低推理效率。
本文提出Sem-MoE，这是一种全新的模型-数据协同调度框架，旨在最小化以专家并行为核心的MoE服务中的高额通信成本。Sem-MoE通过主动建模专家与令牌之间的激活概率，最大程度将专家及其所激活的令牌共置在同一设备上，它包含三项核心技术：（1）离线模型调度：基于专家针对特定类别输入的共激活倾向，预先对专家进行聚类并将其部署到对应设备上。（2）面向注意力数据并行（Attention-DP）架构的在线跨请求数据调度：主动将到达的请求重新组批，调度到承载了对应请求最可能、最频繁激活的专家的设备上。（3）面向注意力张量并行（Attention-TP）架构的在线请求内数据调度：将令牌重排流程无缝融合到原有推理流水线中，主动将令牌重新调度到各设备，减少分散的远端路由。我们将Sem-MoE集成到主流LLM服务引擎SGLANG中。实验表明，与现有解决方案相比，我们提出的协同调度方法能够有效降低专家并行中的全对全通信量，实现更优异的推理吞吐量。
notes: 调度专项得分=25；命中组=sem-moe, llm_serving, moe_scheduling；augment_batch=20260402-scheduling；augment_score=25

title: Efficient Low Rank Attention for Long-Context Inference in Large Language Models
title_zh: 大规模语言模型中用于长上下文推理的高效低秩注意力
abstract:
As the length of input text grows, the key-value (KV) cache in LLMs imposes prohibitive GPU memory costs and limits long‐context inference on resource‐constrained devices.
  Existing approaches, such as KV quantization and pruning, reduce memory usage but suffer from numerical precision loss or suboptimal retention of key-value pairs.
  We introduce Low Rank Query and Key attention (LRQK), a two‐stage framework that jointly decomposes the full‐precision query and key matrices into compact rank-\(r\) factors during the prefill stage, and then uses these low-dimensional projections to compute proxy attention scores in \(\mathcal{O}(lr)\) time at each decode step.
  By selecting only the top-\(k\) tokens and a small fixed set of recent tokens, LRQK employs a mixed GPU–CPU cache with a hit-and-miss mechanism that transfers only missing full-precision KV pairs, thereby preserving exact attention outputs while reducing CPU–GPU data movement.
  Extensive experiments on the RULER and LongBench benchmarks with LLaMA-3-8B and Qwen2.5-7B demonstrate that LRQK matches or surpasses leading sparse-attention methods in long context settings, while delivering significant memory savings with minimal loss in accuracy. Our code is available at \url{https://github.com/tenghuilee/LRQK}.
abstract_zh: 随着输入文本长度增长，大语言模型中的键值（KV）缓存会产生难以负担的GPU内存成本，限制了资源受限设备上的长上下文推理。现有方法如KV量化和剪枝虽能降低内存占用，但存在数值精度损失或键值对保留效果不佳的问题。我们提出低秩查询与键注意力（LRQK），这一两阶段框架在预填充阶段将全精度查询矩阵和键矩阵联合分解为紧凑的秩为r的因子，随后在每一步解码阶段使用这些低维投影以$\mathcal{O}(lr)$的时间复杂度计算代理注意力分数。通过仅选择前k个令牌和一小部分固定的最近令牌，LRQK采用带命中-失效机制的GPU-CPU混合缓存，仅传输缺失的全精度键值对，从而在保留精确注意力输出的同时减少CPU-GPU数据传输量。我们使用LLaMA-3-8B和Qwen2.5-7B模型在RULER和LongBench基准上开展了大量实验，结果表明，在长上下文场景下，LRQK的性能与领先的稀疏注意力方法相当甚至更优，同时能够大幅节省内存，且精度损失极小。我们的代码可从https://github.com/tenghuilee/LRQK获取。
notes: 论文聚焦 LLM 长上下文推理，通过低秩注意力缓解 KV cache 带来的显存开销，兼具缓存优化与推理侧结构优化特征。

title: SepLLM: Accelerate Large Language Models by Compressing One Segment into One Separator
title_zh: SepLLM 通过将一个段压缩为一个分隔符来加速大型语言模型
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have exhibited exceptional performance across a spectrum of natural language processing tasks. However, their substantial sizes pose considerable challenges, particularly in computational demands and inference speed, due to their quadratic complexity. In this work, we have identified a key pattern: certain seemingly meaningless separator tokens (i.e., punctuations) contribute disproportionately to attention scores compared to semantically meaningful tokens. This observation suggests that information of the segments between these separator tokens can be effectively condensed into the separator tokens themselves without significant information loss. Guided by this insight, we introduce SepLLM, a plug-and-play framework that accelerates inference by compressing these segments and eliminating redundant tokens. Additionally, we implement efficient kernels for training acceleration. Experimental results across training-free, training-from-scratch, and post-training settings demonstrate SepLLM's effectiveness. Notably, using the Llama-3-8B backbone, SepLLM achieves over 50% reduction in KV cache on the GSM8K-CoT benchmark while maintaining comparable performance. Furthermore, in streaming settings, SepLLM effectively processes sequences of up to 4 million tokens or more while maintaining consistent language modeling capabilities.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型在各类自然语言处理任务中均展现出优异性能。然而，由于其复杂度为二次方，庞大的模型体量带来了极大挑战，尤其是在计算需求与推理速度方面。本研究发现了一项关键规律：与具备语义意义的词元相比，部分看似无意义的分隔符词元（即标点符号）对注意力得分的贡献远高于预期。这一观测结果表明，这些分隔符之间的片段信息可被有效压缩至分隔符词元本身，且不会造成显著信息损失。基于这一洞见，我们提出SepLLM，这是一种即插即用的框架，通过压缩上述片段、消除冗余词元来加快推理速度。此外，我们实现了高效内核以加速训练过程。在免训练、从头训练和训练后三种设置下的实验结果均证明了SepLLM的有效性。值得注意的是，采用Llama-3-8B作为骨干模型时，SepLLM在GSM8K-CoT基准测试中实现了50%以上的KV缓存缩减，同时保持了相当的性能水平。此外，在流式设置下，SepLLM可有效处理长度最高达400万甚至更长的序列，同时保持稳定的语言建模能力。
notes: 论文核心是基于分隔符的 token/segment 压缩来优化 LLM 推理，同时明确包含 KV cache 压缩与高效 kernels，因此这三类偏好标签都成立。

title: Learning to Zoom with Anatomical Relations for Medical Structure Detection
title_zh: 学习利用解剖关系进行医学结构检测的缩放策略
abstract:
Accurate anatomical structure detection is a critical preliminary step for diagnosing diseases characterized by structural abnormalities. In clinical practice, medical experts frequently adjust the zoom level of medical images to obtain comprehensive views for diagnosis. This common interaction results in significant variations in the apparent scale of anatomical structures across different images or fields of view. However, the information embedded in these zoom-induced scale changes is often overlooked by existing detection algorithms. 
In addition, human organs possess a priori, fixed topological knowledge. To overcome this limitation, we propose ZR-DETR, a zoom-aware probabilistic framework tailored for medical object detection. ZR-DETR uniquely incorporates scale-sensitive zoom embeddings, anatomical relation constraints, and a Gaussian Process-based detection head. This architecture enables the framework to jointly model semantic context, enforce anatomical plausibility, and quantify detection uncertainty. Empirical validation across three diverse medical imaging benchmarks demonstrates that ZR-DETR consistently outperforms strong baselines in both single-domain and unsupervised domain adaptation scenarios.
abstract_zh: 准确的解剖结构检测是诊断以结构异常为特征的疾病的关键预备步骤。在临床实践中，医学专家经常调整医学图像的缩放级别，以获得用于诊断的全面视图。这种常见交互会导致不同图像或视野中解剖结构表观尺度的显著变化。然而，现有检测算法往往忽视了这些由缩放引起的尺度变化中所蕴含的信息。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: TCD-Arena: Assessing Robustness of Time Series Causal Discovery Methods Against Assumption Violations
title_zh: TCD-Arena: 评估时间序列因果发现方法在假设违背下的鲁棒性
abstract: Causal Discovery (CD) is a powerful framework for scientific inquiry. Yet, its practical adoption is hindered by a reliance on strong, often unverifiable assumptions and a lack of robust performance assessment. To address these limitations and advance empirical CD evaluation, we present **TCD-Arena**, a modularized and extendable testing kit to assess the robustness of time series CD algorithms against stepwise more severe assumption violations. For demonstration, we conduct an extensive empirical study comprising over 50 million individual CD attempts and reveal nuanced robustness profiles for 27 distinct assumption violations. Further, we investigate CD ensembles and find that they can boost general robustness, which has implications for real-world applications. With this, we strive to ultimately facilitate the development of CD methods that are reliable for a diverse range of synthetic and potentially real-world data conditions.
abstract_zh: 因果发现（CD）是科学探究中的一种强大框架。然而，其实际应用受到以下因素的阻碍：对强且往往无法验证的假设的依赖，以及缺乏稳健的性能评估。为解决这些局限并推动因果发现的经验评估，我们提出了 TCD-Arena，这是一个模块化且可扩展的测试工具包，用于评估时间序列因果发现算法在面对逐步加重的假设违背时的鲁棒性。作为展示，我们开展了一项大规模实证研究，包含超过 5000 万次独立的因果发现尝试，并揭示了针对 27 种不同假设违背情形的细致鲁棒性特征。此外，我们还研究了因果发现集成方法，发现它们能够提升整体鲁棒性，这对现实世界应用具有启示意义。借此，我们最终致力于促进因果发现方法的发展，使其能够在多样化的合成数据条件以及潜在的真实世界数据条件下保持可靠。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: MetaDesigner: Advancing Artistic Typography through AI-Driven, User-Centric, and Multilingual WordArt Synthesis
title_zh: MetaDesigner：通过人工智能驱动、以用户为中心的多语种艺术字合成技术推进艺术排版发展
abstract: MetaDesigner introduces a transformative framework for artistic typography synthesis, powered by Large Language Models (LLMs) and grounded in a user-centric design paradigm. Its foundation is a multi-agent system comprising the Pipeline, Glyph, and Texture agents, which collectively orchestrate the creation of customizable WordArt, ranging from semantic enhancements to intricate textural elements. A central feedback mechanism leverages insights from both multimodal models and user evaluations, enabling iterative refinement of design parameters. Through this iterative process, MetaDesigner dynamically adjusts hyperparameters to align with user-defined stylistic and thematic preferences, consistently delivering WordArt that excels in visual quality and contextual resonance. Empirical evaluations underscore the system's versatility and effectiveness across diverse WordArt applications, yielding outputs that are both aesthetically compelling and context-sensitive.
abstract_zh: MetaDesigner提出了一种面向艺术字体合成的变革性框架，该框架以大语言模型（LLM）为支撑，遵循以用户为中心的设计范式。其基础是由流程代理、字形代理与纹理代理构成的多智能体系统，三者协同统筹可定制艺术字的创作，覆盖从语义增强到复杂纹理元素设计的全范围。系统的核心反馈机制融合了多模态模型的分析结论与用户评估结果，可实现设计参数的迭代优化。通过该迭代流程，MetaDesigner可动态调整超参数以匹配用户定义的风格与主题偏好，持续输出视觉质量优异、语境契合度高的艺术字。实证评估结果凸显了该系统在各类艺术字应用场景中的通用性与有效性，其生成的内容兼具审美吸引力与语境敏感性。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: NNsight and NDIF: Democratizing Access to Open-Weight Foundation Model Internals
title_zh: NNsight与NDIF：实现开放权重基础模型内部机制的普惠访问
abstract:
We introduce NNsight and NDIF, technologies that work in tandem to enable scientific study of the representations and computations learned by very large neural networks. NNsight is an open-source system that extends PyTorch to introduce deferred remote execution. The National Deep Inference Fabric (NDIF) is a scalable inference service that executes NNsight requests, allowing users to share GPU resources and pretrained models. These technologies are enabled by the Intervention Graph, an architecture developed to decouple experimental design from model runtime. Together, this framework provides transparent and efficient access to the internals of deep neural networks such as very large language models (LLMs) without imposing the cost or complexity of hosting customized models individually. We conduct a quantitative survey of the machine learning literature that reveals a growing gap in the study of the internals of large-scale AI. We demonstrate the design and use of our framework to address this gap by enabling a range of research methods on huge models. Finally, we conduct benchmarks to compare performance with previous approaches.

Code, documentation, and tutorials are available at https://nnsight.net/.
abstract_zh: 我们介绍NNsight与NDIF，二者为协同运作的技术，可支撑对超大型神经网络习得的表征与计算过程开展科学研究。NNsight是一款开源系统，其对PyTorch进行拓展以引入延迟远程执行功能。国家深度推理架构（National Deep Inference Fabric, NDIF）是可执行NNsight请求的可扩展推理服务，能够支持用户共享GPU资源与预训练模型。这些技术的实现依托于干预图（Intervention Graph），这是为将实验设计与模型运行时解耦而开发的架构。该框架整体可透明、高效地访问大型语言模型（LLM）等深度神经网络的内部结构，无需用户单独承担定制化模型部署相关的成本与复杂度负担。我们对机器学习领域的文献开展了定量调研，结果显示当前大规模人工智能内部机制的研究缺口正日益扩大。我们展示了本框架的设计思路与使用方式，通过支持在超大型模型上应用一系列研究方法来填补上述缺口。最后我们开展了基准测试，将本框架的性能与现有方法进行对比。代码、文档与教程可在https://nnsight.net/ 获取。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Stochastic Optimal Control for Continuous-Time fMRI Representation Learning
title_zh: 连续时间 fMRI 表征学习的随机最优控制
abstract: Learning robust representations from functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) is fundamentally challenged by the temporal irregularity and noise inherent in data from heterogeneous sources. Existing self-supervised learning (SSL) methods often discard critical temporal information by discretizing or averaging fMRI signals. To address this, we introduce a novel framework that reframes SSL as a Stochastic Optimal Control (SOC) problem. Our approach models brain activity as continuous-time latent dynamics, learning a robust representation of brain dynamics by optimizing a control policy that is agnostic to the temporal irregularity. This SOC framework naturally unifies masked autoencoding (MAE) and joint-embedding prediction (JEPA) to extract compact, control-derived representations. Furthermore, a simulation-free inference strategy ensures computational efficiency and scalability for large-scale fMRI datasets. Our model demonstrates state-of-the-art performance across diverse downstream applications, highlighting the potential of the SOC-based continuous-time representation learning framework.
abstract_zh: 从功能性磁共振成像（fMRI）中学习鲁棒表征，根本上受到异构来源数据中固有的时间不规则性和噪声的挑战。现有的自监督学习（SSL）方法通常通过对 fMRI 信号进行离散化或平均化而丢弃关键的时间信息。为了解决这一问题，我们提出了一种新颖框架，将 SSL 重新表述为一个随机最优控制（SOC）问题。我们的方法将脑活动建模为连续时间的潜在动力学，通过优化一种对时间不规则性不敏感的控制策略，学习脑动力学的鲁棒表征。该 SOC 框架自然地统一了掩码自编码（MAE）和联合嵌入预测（JEPA），以提取紧凑的、由控制导出的表征。此外，一种无需仿真的推断策略确保了在大规模 fMRI 数据集上的计算效率和可扩展性。我们的模型在多种下游应用中展现出最先进的性能，凸显了基于 SOC 的连续时间表征学习框架的潜力。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Fiddler: CPU-GPU Orchestration for Fast Inference of Mixture-of-Experts Models
title_zh: Fiddler：面向混合专家模型快速推理的CPU-GPU协同编排
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) with the Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures have shown promising performance on various tasks. However, due to the huge model sizes, running them in resource-constrained environments where the GPU memory is not abundant is challenging. Some existing systems propose to use CPU resources to solve that, but they either suffer from the significant overhead of frequently moving data between CPU and GPU, or fail to consider distinct characteristics of CPUs and GPUs. This paper proposes Fiddler, a resource-efficient inference system for MoE models with limited GPU resources. Fiddler strategically utilizes CPU and GPU resources by determining the optimal execution strategy. Our evaluation shows that, unlike state-of-the-art systems that optimize for specific scenarios such as single batch inference or long prefill, Fiddler performs better in all scenarios. Compared against different baselines, Fiddler achieves 1.26 times speed up in single batch inference, 1.30 times in long prefill processing, and 11.57 times in beam search inference. The code of Fiddler is publicly available at https://github.com/efeslab/fiddler.
abstract_zh: 采用混合专家（MoE）架构的大语言模型（LLM）在各类任务上展现出可观的性能。但由于模型体量庞大，在GPU显存不足的资源受限环境中运行这类模型颇具挑战。现有部分系统提出利用CPU资源解决该问题，但这些系统要么存在CPU与GPU之间频繁数据迁移带来的显著开销，要么未考虑CPU与GPU的差异化特性。本文提出Fiddler，一款适用于GPU资源有限场景的资源高效型MoE模型推理系统。Fiddler通过确定最优执行策略，对CPU和GPU资源进行统筹利用。我们的评估结果显示，现有前沿系统往往仅针对单批次推理、长预填充等特定场景进行优化，而Fiddler在所有场景下的表现均更优。与不同基线系统相比，Fiddler在单批次推理任务中速度提升1.26倍，长预填充处理中速度提升1.30倍，束搜索推理中速度提升11.57倍。Fiddler的代码已在https://github.com/efeslab/fiddler公开。
notes: 调度专项得分=2；augment_batch=20260403-scheduling；augment_score=2

title: Certifying Stability of Reinforcement Learning Policies using Generalized Lyapunov Functions
title_zh: 使用广义李雅普诺夫函数为强化学习策略认证稳定性
abstract: Establishing stability certificates for closed-loop systems under reinforcement learning (RL) policies is essential to move beyond empirical performance and offer guarantees of system behavior. Classical Lyapunov methods require a strict stepwise decrease in the Lyapunov function but such certificates are difficult to construct for learned policies. The RL value function is a natural candidate but it is not well understood how it can be adapted for this purpose. To gain intuition, we first study the linear quadratic regulator (LQR) problem and make two key observations. First, a Lyapunov function can be obtained from the value function of an LQR policy by augmenting it with a residual term related to the system dynamics and stage cost. Second, the classical Lyapunov decrease requirement can be relaxed to a generalized Lyapunov condition requiring only decrease on average over multiple time steps. Using this intuition, we consider the nonlinear setting and formulate an approach to learn generalized Lyapunov functions by augmenting RL value functions with neural network residual terms. Our approach successfully certifies the stability of RL policies trained on Gymnasium and DeepMind Control benchmarks. We also extend our method to jointly train neural controllers and stability certificates using a multi-step Lyapunov loss, resulting in larger certified inner approximations of the region of attraction compared to the classical Lyapunov approach. Overall, our formulation enables stability certification for a broad class of systems with learned policies by making certificates easier to construct, thereby bridging classical control theory and modern learning-based methods.
abstract_zh: 为强化学习（RL）策略控制下的闭环系统建立稳定性证书，对于超越经验性能并提供系统行为保证至关重要。经典李雅普诺夫方法要求李雅普诺夫函数在每一步都严格下降，但这类证书对学习得到的策略而言很难构造。RL值函数是一个自然的候选，但如何将其调整用于这一目的仍缺乏充分理解。为获得直观认识，我们首先研究线性二次调节器（LQR）问题，并提出两个关键观察。第一，可以通过在线性二次调节器策略的值函数中加入一个与系统动力学和阶段代价相关的残差项，得到一个李雅普诺夫函数。第二，经典的李雅普诺夫下降要求可以放宽为一种广义李雅普诺夫条件，即只要求在多个时间步上的平均意义下下降。基于这一直观认识，我们进一步考虑非线性情形，并提出一种方法：通过用神经网络残差项增强RL值函数，来学习广义李雅普诺夫函数。我们的方法成功验证了在Gymnasium和DeepMind Control基准上训练得到的RL策略的稳定性。我们还将该方法扩展为利用多步李雅普诺夫损失联合训练神经控制器和稳定性证书，与经典李雅普诺夫方法相比，得到对吸引域更大的已认证内部近似。总体而言，我们的公式化方法通过使稳定性证书更易于构造，从而为具有学习策略的一大类系统实现了稳定性认证，搭建了经典控制理论与现代基于学习方法之间的桥梁。
primary_research_object: 强化学习 / 序列决策
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Lifelong Test-Time Adaptation via Online Learning in Tracked Low-Dimensional Subspace
title_zh: 通过跟踪的低维子空间中的在线学习实现终身测试时适应
abstract: Test-time adaptation (TTA) aims to adapt a source model to a target domain using only test data. Existing methods predominantly rely on unsupervised entropy minimization or its variants, which suffer from degeneration, leading to trivial solutions with low-entropy but inaccurate predictions. In this work, we identify *entropy-deceptive* (ED) samples, instances where the model makes highly confident yet incorrect predictions, as the underlying cause of degeneration. Further, we reveal that the gradients of entropy minimization in TTA have an intrinsic low-dimensional structure, driven primarily by *entropy-truthful* (ET) samples whose gradients are highly correlated. In contrast, ED samples have scattered, less correlated gradients. Leveraging this observation, we show that the detrimental impact of ED samples can be suppressed by constraining model updates within the principal subspace of backward gradients. Building on this insight, we propose LCoTTA, a lifelong continual TTA method that tracks the principal subspace of gradients online and utilizes their projections onto this subspace for adaptation. Further, we provide theoretical analysis to show that the proposed subspace-based method can enhance the robustness against detrimental ED samples. Extensive experiments demonstrate that LCoTTA effectively overcomes degeneration and significantly outperforms existing methods in long-term continual adaptation scenarios. Code is available online.
abstract_zh: 测试时自适应（TTA）旨在仅利用测试数据将源模型适应到目标域。现有方法主要依赖无监督熵最小化或其变体，但这些方法会遭遇退化问题，导致得到熵低但预测不准确的平凡解。在这项工作中，我们将熵欺骗（ED）样本识别为退化的根本原因，即模型对这些样本会做出高度自信但错误的预测。进一步地，我们揭示出，TTA 中熵最小化的梯度具有内在的低维结构，这主要由熵真实（ET）样本驱动，因为这些样本的梯度高度相关。相比之下，ED 样本的梯度则更为分散、相关性更弱。基于这一观察，我们表明，通过将模型更新约束在反向梯度的主子空间内，可以抑制 ED 样本的不利影响。基于这一见解，我们提出了 LCoTTA，这是一种终身持续式 TTA 方法，能够在线跟踪梯度的主子空间，并利用梯度在该子空间上的投影进行适应。此外，我们还给出了理论分析，说明所提出的基于子空间的方法能够增强对有害 ED 样本的鲁棒性。大量实验表明，在长期持续适应场景中，LCoTTA 能够有效克服退化问题，并显著优于现有方法。代码已在线公开。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Memory-Efficient Visual Autoregressive Modeling with Scale-Aware KV Cache Compression
title_zh: 具有尺度感知KV缓存压缩的内存高效视觉自回归建模
abstract: Visual Autoregressive (VAR) modeling has garnered significant attention for its innovative next-scale prediction approach, which yields substantial improvements in efficiency, scalability, and zero-shot generalization. Nevertheless, the coarse-to-fine methodology inherent in VAR results in exponential growth of the KV cache during inference, causing considerable memory consumption and computational redundancy. To address these bottlenecks, we introduce ScaleKV, a novel KV cache compression framework tailored for VAR architectures. ScaleKV leverages two critical observations: varying cache demands across transformer layers and distinct attention patterns at different scales. Based on these insights, ScaleKV categorizes transformer layers into two functional groups: drafters and refiners. Drafters exhibit dispersed attention across multiple scales, thereby requiring greater cache capacity. Conversely, refiners focus attention on the current token map to process local details, consequently necessitating substantially reduced cache capacity. ScaleKV optimizes the multi-scale inference pipeline by identifying scale-specific drafters and refiners, facilitating differentiated cache management tailored to each scale. Evaluation on the state-of-the-art text-to-image VAR model family, Infinity, demonstrates that our approach effectively reduces the required KV cache memory to 10% while preserving pixel-level fidelity.
abstract_zh: 视觉自回归（VAR）建模凭借其创新的下一尺度预测方法获得了广泛关注，该方法在效率、可扩展性和零样本泛化能力上实现了大幅提升。然而，VAR固有的从粗到细的方法会导致推理过程中KV缓存呈指数级增长，造成极高的内存消耗和计算冗余。为解决这些瓶颈，我们提出了ScaleKV，这是一种专为VAR架构设计的新型KV缓存压缩框架。ScaleKV基于两个关键观察：Transformer不同层的缓存需求存在差异，且不同尺度下的注意力模式各不相同。基于这些发现，ScaleKV将Transformer层划分为两个功能组：起草层与细化层。起草层的注意力分散在多个尺度上，因此需要更大的缓存容量。相反，细化层将注意力集中在当前令牌图上以处理局部细节，因此所需的缓存容量大幅降低。ScaleKV通过识别特定尺度对应的起草层与细化层优化了多尺度推理流水线，可实现适配各个尺度的差异化缓存管理。在最先进的文本到图像VAR模型系列Infinity上的评估表明，我们的方法可有效将所需KV缓存内存降至原有的10%，同时保持像素级保真度。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Robust Estimation Under Heterogeneous Corruption Rates
title_zh: 在异质腐败率下的鲁棒估计
abstract:
We study the problem of robust estimation under heterogeneous corruption rates, where each sample may be independently corrupted with a known but non-identical probability. 
  This setting arises naturally in distributed and federated learning, crowdsourcing, and sensor networks, yet existing robust estimators typically assume uniform or worst-case corruption, ignoring structural heterogeneity.
  For mean estimation for multivariate bounded distributions and univariate gaussian distributions, we give tight minimax rates for all heterogeneous corruption patterns.
  For multivariate gaussian mean estimation and linear regression, we establish the minimax rate for squared error up to a factor of $\sqrt{d}$, where $d$ is the dimension.
    Roughly, our findings suggest that samples beyond a certain corruption threshold may be discarded by the optimal estimators -- this threshold is determined by the empirical distribution of the corruption rates given.
abstract_zh: 我们研究了异质扰动率下的鲁棒估计问题，即每个样本可能以已知但不相同的概率独立被扰动。这一设定在分布式与联邦学习、众包以及传感器网络中自然出现，而现有鲁棒估计器通常假设扰动是均匀或最坏情况，忽略了结构性的异质性。针对多元有界分布和一元高斯分布的均值估计，我们对所有异质扰动模式给出了精确的极小极大收敛速率；对于多元高斯均值估计与线性回归，我们则将平方误差的极小极大率确定到一个 \(\sqrt{d}\) 的因子内，其中 \(d\) 是维度。大体来看，我们的结果表明超出某个扰动阈值的样本可能会被最优估计器舍弃——该阈值由给定扰动率的经验分布决定。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Markov Persuasion Processes: Learning to Persuade From Scratch
title_zh: 马尔可夫说服过程：从零开始学习如何进行说服
abstract:
In Bayesian persuasion, an informed sender strategically discloses information to a receiver so as to persuade them to undertake desirable actions. Recently, Markov persuasion processes (MPPs) have been introduced to capture sequential scenarios where a sender faces a stream of myopic receivers in a Markovian environment. The MPPs studied so far in the literature suffer from issues that prevent them from being fully operational in practice, e.g., they assume that the sender knows receivers' rewards. We fix such issues by addressing MPPs where the sender has no knowledge about the environment. We design a learning algorithm for the sender, working with partial feedback.
We prove that its regret with respect to an optimal information-disclosure policy grows sublinearly in the number of episodes, as it is the case for the loss in persuasiveness cumulated while learning. Moreover, we provide lower bounds for our setting matching the guarantees of our algorithm.
abstract_zh: 在贝叶斯劝说中，掌握信息的发送者会有策略地向接收者披露信息，以说服他们采取期望的行动。近年来，人们提出了马尔可夫劝说过程（MPP），用于刻画这样一类序列性场景：在马尔可夫环境中，发送者面对一连串目光短浅的接收者。迄今为止，文献中研究的 MPP 存在一些问题，使其无法在实践中得到完全有效的应用，例如，它们假设发送者知道接收者的收益。我们通过研究发送者对环境毫无了解的 MPP，修正了这些问题。我们为发送者设计了一种在部分反馈条件下工作的学习算法。
primary_research_object: 强化学习 / 序列决策
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: What Data Enables Optimal Decisions? An Exact Characterization for Linear Optimization
title_zh: 什么数据能促成最优决策？线性优化的精确刻画
abstract:
We study the fundamental question of how informative a dataset is for solving a given decision-making task. In our setting, the dataset provides partial information about unknown parameters that influence task outcomes. Focusing on linear programs, we characterize when a dataset is sufficient to recover an optimal decision, given an uncertainty set on the cost vector. Our main contribution is a sharp geometric characterization that identifies the directions of the cost vector that matter for optimality, relative to the task constraints and uncertainty set.
We further develop a practical algorithm that, for a given task, constructs a minimal or least-costly sufficient dataset. 
Our results reveal that small, well-chosen datasets can often fully determine optimal decisions---offering a principled foundation for task-aware data selection.
abstract_zh: 我们研究了这样一个基本问题：对于求解给定决策任务而言，一个数据集的信息量究竟有多大。在我们的设定中，数据集提供了关于影响任务结果的未知参数的部分信息。聚焦于线性规划，我们刻画了在给定成本向量不确定性集合的情况下，何时一个数据集足以恢复最优决策。我们的主要贡献是一个精确的几何刻画，它相对于任务约束和不确定性集合，识别出成本向量中那些对最优性有影响的方向。
primary_research_object: 强化学习 / 序列决策
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: QCircuitBench: A Large-Scale Dataset for Benchmarking Quantum Algorithm Design
title_zh: QCircuitBench：用于量子算法设计基准测试的大规模数据集
abstract:
Quantum computing is an emerging field recognized for the significant speedup it offers over classical computing through quantum algorithms. However, designing and implementing quantum algorithms pose challenges due to the complex nature of quantum mechanics and the necessity for precise control over quantum states. Despite the significant advancements in AI, there has been a lack of datasets specifically tailored for this purpose. In this work, we introduce QCircuitBench, the first benchmark dataset designed to evaluate AI's capability in designing and implementing quantum algorithms in the form of quantum circuit codes. Unlike using AI for writing traditional codes, this task is fundamentally more complicated due to highly flexible design space. 

Our key contributions include: 
1. A general framework which formulates the key features of quantum algorithm design task for Large Language Models.
2. Implementation for quantum algorithms from basic primitives to advanced applications, spanning 3 task suites, 25 algorithms, and 120,290 data points.
3. Automatic validation and verification functions, allowing for iterative and interactive evaluation without human inspection.
4. Promising potential as a training dataset through primitive fine-tuning results.

We observed several interesting experimental phenomena: fine-tuning does not always outperform few-shot learning, and LLMs tend to exhibit consistent error patterns. QCircuitBench provides a comprehensive benchmark for AI-driven quantum algorithm design, while also revealing some limitations of LLMs in this domain.
abstract_zh: 量子计算是一个新兴领域，因其通过量子算法相较经典计算所提供的显著加速而受到认可。然而，由于量子力学本身的复杂性以及对量子态进行精确控制的必要性，量子算法的设计与实现面临挑战。尽管人工智能已取得显著进展，但一直缺乏专门针对此目的定制的数据集。在这项工作中，我们引入了 QCircuitBench，这是首个旨在评估人工智能以量子电路代码形式设计和实现量子算法能力的基准数据集。与使用人工智能编写传统代码不同，由于设计空间具有高度灵活性，这一任务在根本上更加复杂。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: DynamicRAG: Leveraging Outputs of Large Language Model as Feedback for Dynamic Reranking in Retrieval-Augmented Generation
title_zh: DynamicRAG 在检索增强生成中将大型语言模型的输出作为动态重排序反馈
abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems combine large language models (LLMs) with external knowledge retrieval, making them highly effective for knowledge-intensive tasks. A crucial but often under-explored component of these systems is the reranker, which refines retrieved documents to enhance generation quality and explainability. The challenge of selecting the optimal number of documents (k) remains unsolved: too few may omit critical information, while too many introduce noise and inefficiencies. Although recent studies have explored LLM-based rerankers, they primarily leverage internal model knowledge and overlook the rich supervisory signals that LLMs can provide, such as using response quality as feedback for optimizing reranking decisions. In this paper, we propose DynamicRAG, a novel RAG framework where the reranker dynamically adjusts both the order and number of retrieved documents based on the query. We model the reranker as an agent optimized through reinforcement learning (RL), using rewards derived from LLM output quality. Across seven knowledge-intensive datasets, DynamicRAG demonstrates superior performance, achieving state-of-the-art results.
abstract_zh: 检索增强生成（RAG）系统将大型语言模型（LLM）与外部知识检索相结合，使其在知识密集型任务中表现出很高的有效性。这类系统中一个关键但常被低估的组成部分是重排序器，它通过优化检索文档来提升生成质量和可解释性。如何选择最优的文档数量（k）这一挑战仍未解决：文档过少可能遗漏关键信息，而文档过多则会引入噪声并造成效率低下。尽管近期研究已经探索了基于 LLM 的重排序器，但它们主要利用模型内部知识，忽视了 LLM 所能提供的丰富监督信号，例如将响应质量作为反馈来优化重排序决策。在本文中，我们提出了 DynamicRAG，这是一种新颖的 RAG 框架，其中重排序器会根据查询动态调整检索文档的顺序和数量。我们将重排序器建模为一个通过强化学习（RL）进行优化的智能体，并使用源自 LLM 输出质量的奖励信号。在七个知识密集型数据集上，DynamicRAG 展现出更优的性能，取得了当前最先进的结果。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: ABBA-Adapters: Efficient and Expressive Fine-Tuning of Foundation Models
title_zh: ABBA-Adapters：基础模型高效且具表现力的微调
abstract:
Large Language Models have demonstrated strong performance across a wide range of tasks, but adapting them efficiently to new domains remains a key challenge. Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) methods address this by introducing lightweight, trainable modules while keeping most pre-trained weights fixed. The prevailing approach, LoRA, models updates using a low-rank decomposition, but its expressivity is inherently constrained by the rank. Recent methods like HiRA aim to increase expressivity by incorporating a Hadamard product with the frozen weights, but still rely on the structure of the pre-trained model. We introduce ABBA, a new PEFT architecture that reparameterizes the update as a Hadamard product of two independently learnable low-rank matrices. In contrast to prior work, ABBA fully decouples the update from the pre-trained weights, enabling both components to be optimized freely. This leads to significantly higher expressivity under the same parameter budget, a property we validate through matrix reconstruction experiments. 
Empirically, ABBA achieves state-of-the-art results on arithmetic and commonsense reasoning benchmarks, consistently outperforming existing PEFT methods by a significant margin across multiple models. Our code is publicly available at: https://github.com/CERT-Lab/abba.
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型在广泛任务上已展现出强大性能，但如何将其高效适配到新领域仍是一个关键挑战。参数高效微调（PEFT）方法通过引入轻量、可训练的模块，同时保持大多数预训练权重固定，来解决这一问题。当前主流方法 LoRA 使用低秩分解来建模更新，但其表达能力天然受限于秩。近期方法如 HiRA 试图通过与冻结权重结合引入 Hadamard 乘积来提升表达能力，但仍然依赖于预训练模型的结构。我们提出了 ABBA，这是一种新的 PEFT 架构，将更新重新参数化为两个彼此独立可学习的低秩矩阵的 Hadamard 乘积。与先前工作相比，ABBA 将更新与预训练权重完全解耦，使两个组成部分都能够被自由优化。在相同参数预算下，这带来了显著更高的表达能力，我们通过矩阵重建实验验证了这一性质。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: On the Interpolation Effect of Score Smoothing in Diffusion Models
title_zh: 论扩散模型中分数平滑的插值效应
abstract: Score-based diffusion models have achieved remarkable progress in various domains with the ability to generate new data samples that do not exist in the training set. In this work, we study the hypothesis that such creativity arises from an interpolation effect caused by a smoothing of the empirical score function. Focusing on settings where the training set lies uniformly in a one-dimensional subspace, we probe the interplay between score smoothing and the denoising dynamics with analytical solutions and numerical experiments. In particular, we demonstrate how a smoothed score function can lead to the generation of samples that interpolate among the training data within their subspace while avoiding full memorization. Moreover, we present theoretical and empirical evidence that learning score functions with regularized neural networks can have a similar effect as score smoothing, including in simple nonlinear settings.
abstract_zh: 基于分数的扩散模型在多个领域取得了显著进展，能够生成训练集中不存在的新数据样本。在这项工作中，我们研究这样一个假设：这种创造性源于由经验分数函数平滑化所导致的插值效应。我们聚焦于训练集均匀位于一维子空间中的情形，通过解析解和数值实验考察分数平滑与去噪动力学之间的相互作用。具体而言，我们展示了平滑后的分数函数如何能够在避免完全记忆的同时，生成位于该子空间内、对训练数据进行插值的样本。此外，我们还从理论和实验两方面表明，使用正则化神经网络学习分数函数能够产生与分数平滑类似的效果，这一点也适用于简单的非线性情形。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Robo2VLM: Improving Visual Question Answering using Large-Scale Robot Manipulation Data
title_zh: Robo2VLM：利用大规模机器人操控数据提升视觉问答
abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) acquire real-world knowledge and general reasoning ability through Internet-scale image-text corpora. They can augment robotic systems with scene understanding and task planning, and assist visuomotor policies that are trained on robot trajectory data. We explore the reverse paradigm — using rich, real, multi-modal robot trajectory data to enhance and evaluate VLMs. In this paper, we present Robo2VLM, a Visual Question Answering (VQA) dataset generation framework for VLMs. Given a human tele-operated robot demonstration with video and robot data, Robo2VLM derives ground-truth from non-visual and non-descriptive sensory modalities, such as end-effector pose, gripper aperture, and force sensing. Based on these modalities, it segments the robot trajectory into a sequence of manipulation phases. At each phase, Robo2VLM uses scene and interaction understanding to identify 3D properties of the robot, task goal, and the target object. The properties are used to generate representative VQA queries – images with textural multiple-choice questions – based on spatial, goal-conditioned, and interaction reasoning question templates. We use a subset of Open X-Embodiment to generate Robo2VLM-1, a large-scale in-the-wild dataset with 684,710 questions based on 463 distinct scenes and 3,396 robotic manipulation tasks from 176k real robot trajectories. Results suggest that Robo2VLM-1 can benchmark and improve VLM capabilities in spatial and interaction reasoning.
