Leveraging a lightweight rule-based reward (e.g., the similarity score between ground-truth and LLM-generated solutions), SWE-RL enables LLMs to autonomously recover a developer's reasoning processes and solutions by learning from extensive open-source software evolution data -- the record of a software's entire lifecycle, including its code snapshots, code changes, and events such as issues and pull requests. Trained on top of Llama 3, our resulting reasoning model, Llama3-SWE-RL-70B, achieves a 41.0% solve rate on SWE-bench Verified -- a human-verified collection of real-world GitHub issues. To our knowledge, this is the best performance reported for medium-sized (<100B) LLMs to date, even comparable to leading proprietary LLMs like GPT-4o. Surprisingly, despite performing RL solely on software evolution data, Llama3-SWE-RL has even emerged with generalized reasoning skills. For example, it shows improved results on five out-of-domain tasks, namely, function coding, library use, code reasoning, mathematics, and general language understanding, whereas a supervised-finetuning baseline even leads to performance degradation on average. Overall, SWE-RL opens up a new direction to improve the reasoning capabilities of LLMs through reinforcement learning on massive software engineering data.
abstract_zh: 近期发布的 DeepSeek-R1 展示了强化学习（RL）在提升大语言模型（LLM）通用推理能力方面的巨大潜力。尽管 DeepSeek-R1 及其他后续工作主要聚焦于将 RL 应用于竞赛编程和数学问题，本文提出了 SWE-RL，这是首个将基于 RL 的 LLM 推理扩展到真实世界软件工程的方法。SWE-RL 利用轻量级的基于规则的奖励机制（例如真实解与 LLM 生成解之间的相似度分数），使 LLM 能够通过学习大规模开源软件演化数据，自主恢复开发者的推理过程和解决方案。这里的软件演化数据指的是软件整个生命周期的记录，包括其代码快照、代码变更，以及问题单和拉取请求等事件。在 Llama 3 的基础上训练得到的推理模型 Llama3-SWE-RL-70B，在 SWE-bench Verified 上达到了 41.0% 的解决率。SWE-bench Verified 是一个经过人工验证的真实世界 GitHub 问题集合。据我们所知，这是迄今为止中等规模（<100B）LLM 所报告的最佳性能，甚至可与 GPT-4o 等领先的专有 LLM 相媲美。令人惊讶的是，尽管仅在软件演化数据上进行 RL，Llama3-SWE-RL 仍然涌现出了泛化推理能力。例如，它在五个域外任务上都表现出改进结果，即函数编码、库使用、代码推理、数学和通用语言理解；而监督微调基线平均来看甚至导致了性能下降。总体而言，SWE-RL 开辟了一条通过在海量软件工程数据上进行强化学习来提升 LLM 推理能力的新方向。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Accelerating Linear Recurrent Neural Networks for the Edge with Unstructured Sparsity
title_zh: 加速边缘设备上的线性递归神经网络：利用非结构化稀疏性
abstract:
Linear recurrent neural networks enable powerful long-range sequence modeling with constant memory usage and time-per-token during inference. These architectures hold promise for streaming applications at the edge, but deployment in resource-constrained environments requires hardware-aware optimizations to minimize latency and energy consumption. 
Unstructured sparsity offers a compelling solution, enabling substantial reductions in compute and memory requirements--when accelerated by compatible hardware platforms. 
In this paper, we conduct a scaling study to investigate the Pareto front of performance and efficiency across inference compute budgets.
We find that highly sparse linear RNNs *consistently* achieve better efficiency-performance trade-offs than dense baselines, with $2\times$ less compute and $36$\% less memory at iso-accuracy.
Our models achieve state-of-the-art results on a real-time streaming task for audio denoising.
By quantizing our sparse models to fixed-point arithmetic and deploying them on the Intel Loihi 2 neuromorphic chip for real-time processing, we translate model compression into tangible gains of $42\times$ lower latency and $149\times$ lower energy consumption compared to a dense model on an edge GPU.
Our findings showcase the transformative potential of unstructured sparsity, paving the way for highly efficient recurrent neural networks in real-world, resource-constrained environments.
abstract_zh:
线性循环神经网络可实现性能优异的长程序列建模，推理过程中内存占用恒定，每令牌处理时间固定。这类架构在边缘端流式应用领域具有良好前景，但要在资源受限环境中部署，需要开展硬件感知优化以最大限度降低延迟与能耗。
非结构化稀疏提供了极具吸引力的解决方案，在兼容硬件平台的加速支持下，可大幅削减计算与内存需求。
本文开展缩放研究，探究不同推理计算预算下性能与效率的帕累托最优前沿。我们发现，高稀疏度线性循环神经网络的效率-性能权衡表现始终优于稠密基线模型，在同精度条件下，计算量降低2倍，内存占用减少36%。我们的模型在音频降噪实时流式任务上取得了当前最优结果。通过将稀疏模型量化为定点运算，并部署在英特尔Loihi 2神经形态芯片上开展实时处理，我们将模型压缩效果转化为实际收益：与运行在边缘GPU上的稠密模型相比，延迟降低42倍，能耗降低149倍。我们的研究结果展现了非结构化稀疏的变革性潜力，为高效循环神经网络应用于现实资源受限环境铺平了道路。
notes: 论文核心聚焦边缘部署加速与资源约束下的硬件感知优化；“非结构化稀疏”直接对应模型压缩信号。

title: LLaVA-Mini: Efficient Image and Video Large Multimodal Models with One Vision Token
title_zh: LLaVA-Mini：使用一个视觉令牌的高效图像与视频大规模多模态模型
abstract: The advent of real-time large multimodal models (LMMs) like GPT-4o has sparked considerable interest in efficient LMMs. LMM frameworks typically encode visual inputs into vision tokens (continuous representations) and integrate them and textual instructions into the context of large language models (LLMs), where large-scale parameters and numerous context tokens (predominantly vision tokens) result in substantial computational overhead. Previous efforts towards efficient LMMs always focus on replacing the LLM backbone with smaller models, while neglecting the crucial issue of token quantity. In this paper, we introduce LLaVA-Mini, an efficient LMM with minimal vision tokens. To achieve a high compression ratio of vision tokens while preserving visual information, we first analyze how LMMs understand vision tokens and find that most vision tokens only play a crucial role in the early layers of LLM backbone, where they mainly fuse visual information into text tokens. Building on this finding, LLaVA-Mini introduces modality pre-fusion to fuse visual information into text tokens in advance, thereby facilitating the extreme compression of vision tokens fed to LLM backbone into one token. LLaVA-Mini is a unified large multimodal model that can support the understanding of images, high-resolution images, and videos in an efficient manner. Experiments across 11 image-based and 7 video-based benchmarks demonstrate that LLaVA-Mini outperforms LLaVA-v1.5 with just 1 vision token instead of 576. Efficiency analyses reveal that LLaVA-Mini can reduce FLOPs by 77%, deliver low-latency responses within 40 milliseconds, and process over 10,000 frames of video on the GPU hardware with 24GB of memory.
abstract_zh: GPT-4o等实时大型多模态模型（LMM）的出现引发了人们对高效大型多模态模型的浓厚兴趣。大型多模态模型框架通常将视觉输入编码为视觉令牌（连续表示），再将其与文本指令一同整合到大型语言模型（LLM）的上下文中，而大规模参数与大量上下文令牌（以视觉令牌为主）会产生极高的计算开销。此前针对高效大型多模态模型的研究始终聚焦于用更小的模型替换大型语言模型主干，却忽略了令牌数量这一关键问题。本文提出LLaVA-Mini，这是一款视觉令牌数量极少的高效大型多模态模型。为了在保留视觉信息的同时实现视觉令牌的高压缩比，我们首先分析了大型多模态模型对视觉令牌的理解机制，发现绝大多数视觉令牌仅在大型语言模型主干的前几层发挥关键作用，主要负责将视觉信息融合到文本令牌中。基于这一发现，LLaVA-Mini引入模态预融合机制，提前将视觉信息融合到文本令牌中，从而可将输入至大型语言模型主干的视觉令牌极致压缩为单个令牌。LLaVA-Mini是一款统一的大型多模态模型，能够高效支持普通图像、高分辨率图像及视频的理解任务。在11个图像类基准测试和7个视频类基准测试上开展的实验表明，LLaVA-Mini仅使用1个视觉令牌而非LLaVA-v1.5的576个，性能便优于LLaVA-v1.5。效率分析显示，LLaVA-Mini可减少77%的浮点运算量，能在40毫秒内生成低延迟响应，且在显存为24GB的GPU硬件上可处理超过10000帧视频。
notes: 论文核心是通过模态预融合与单视觉 token 设计降低多模态推理开销，明显属于多模态 LLM；偏好上更直接对应上下文 token 压缩与Negative，而非系统调度或算子内核层优化。

title: BlockScan: Detecting Anomalies in Blockchain Transactions
title_zh: BlockScan：检测区块链交易中的异常
abstract:
We propose BlockScan, a customized Transformer for anomaly detection in blockchain transactions.
Unlike existing methods that rely on rule-based systems or directly apply off-the-shelf large language models (LLMs), BlockScan introduces a series of customized designs to effectively model the unique data structure of blockchain transactions.
First, a blockchain transaction is multi-modal, containing blockchain-specific tokens, texts, and numbers.
We design a novel modularized tokenizer to handle these multi-modal inputs, balancing the information across different modalities.
Second, we design a customized masked language modeling mechanism for pretraining the Transformer architecture, incorporating RoPE embedding and FlashAttention for handling longer sequences.
Finally, we design a novel anomaly detection method based on the model outputs.
We further provide theoretical analysis for the detection method of our system. 
Extensive evaluations on Ethereum and Solana transactions demonstrate BlockScan's exceptional capability in anomaly detection while maintaining a low false positive rate.
Remarkably, BlockScan is the only method that successfully detects anomalous transactions on Solana with high accuracy, whereas all other approaches achieved very low or zero detection recall scores.
This work sets a new benchmark for applying Transformer-based approaches in blockchain data analysis.
abstract_zh: 我们提出 BlockScan，一种为区块链交易异常检测量身定制的 Transformer。不同于依赖规则系统或直接使用通用大型语言模型（LLM）的方法，BlockScan 通过一系列定制设计来有效建模区块链交易的独特数据结构。首先，区块链交易是多模态的，包含区块链专用的令牌、文本以及数值等信息；我们设计了新颖的模块化分词器来处理这些多模态输入，平衡来自不同模态的信息权重。其次，我们为 Transformer 架构设计了定制的掩码语言建模预训练机制，并结合 RoPE 编码与 FlashAttention，用以应对更长序列。最后，我们基于模型输出提出了新的异常检测方法，并进一步提供了该检测方法的理论分析。对以太坊与 Solana 交易的广泛评估表明，BlockScan 在保持低误报率的同时展现出卓越的异常检测能力。尤其值得一提的是，BlockScan 是唯一能在 Solana 上以高准确率成功检测出异常交易的方法，而其他所有方法的召回率都非常低甚至为零。本工作为基于 Transformer 的区块链数据分析设立了新的基准。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: LLaVA-4D: Embedding SpatioTemporal Prompt into LMMs for 4D Scene Understanding
title_zh: LLaVA-4D 将时空提示嵌入 LMMs 以实现 4D 场景理解
abstract: Despite achieving significant progress in 2D image understanding, large multimodal models (LMMs) struggle in the physical world due to the lack of spatial representation. Typically, existing 3D LMMs mainly embed 3D positions as fixed spatial prompts within visual features to represent the scene. However, these methods are limited to understanding the static background and fail to capture temporally varying dynamic objects. In this paper, we propose LLaVA-4D, a general LMM framework with a novel spatiotemporal prompt for visual representation in 4D scene understanding. The spatiotemporal prompt is generated by encoding 3D position and 1D time into a dynamic-aware 4D coordinate embedding. Moreover, we demonstrate that spatial and temporal components disentangled from visual features are more effective in distinguishing the background from objects. This motivates embedding the 4D spatiotemporal prompt into these features to enhance the dynamic scene representation. By aligning visual spatiotemporal embeddings with language embeddings, LMMs gain the ability to understand both spatial and temporal characteristics of static background and dynamic objects in the physical world. Additionally, we construct a 4D vision-language dataset with spatiotemporal coordinate annotations for instruction fine-tuning LMMs. Extensive experiments have been conducted to demonstrate the superiority of our method on various tasks of 4D scene understanding. Our code will be open-sourced on paper acceptance.
abstract_zh: 尽管大型多模态模型（LMMs）在二维图像理解方面已取得显著进展，但由于缺乏空间表征，它们在物理世界中的表现仍然受限。通常，现有的三维 LMM 主要将三维位置作为固定的空间提示嵌入到视觉特征中以表示场景。然而，这些方法仅限于理解静态背景，无法捕捉随时间变化的动态物体。本文提出了 LLaVA-4D，这是一种用于四维场景理解的通用 LMM 框架，采用了一种新颖的时空提示进行视觉表征。该时空提示通过将三维位置和一维时间编码为具有动态感知能力的四维坐标嵌入来生成。此外，我们证明了从视觉特征中解耦出的空间和时间成分在区分背景与物体方面更为有效。这启发我们将四维时空提示嵌入这些特征中，以增强动态场景表征。通过将视觉时空嵌入与语言嵌入对齐，LMMs 获得了理解物理世界中静态背景和动态物体的空间与时间特征的能力。此外，我们构建了一个带有时空坐标标注的四维视觉语言数据集，用于对 LMMs 进行指令微调。大量实验表明，我们的方法在多种四维场景理解任务上具有优越性。我们的代码将在论文被接收后开源。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Causally Reliable Concept Bottleneck Models
title_zh: 因果可靠的概念瓶颈模型
abstract: Concept-based models are an emerging paradigm in deep learning that constrains the inference process to operate through human-interpretable variables, facilitating explainability and human interaction. However, these architectures, on par with popular opaque neural models, fail to account for the true causal mechanisms underlying the target phenomena represented in the data. This hampers their ability to support causal reasoning tasks, limits out-of-distribution generalization, and hinders the implementation of fairness constraints. To overcome these issues, we propose Causally reliable Concept Bottleneck Models (C$^2$BMs), a class of concept-based architectures that enforce reasoning through a bottleneck of concepts structured according to a model of the real-world causal mechanisms. We also introduce a pipeline to automatically learn this structure from observational data and unstructured background knowledge (e.g., scientific literature). Experimental evidence suggests that C$^2$BMs are more interpretable, causally reliable, and improve responsiveness to interventions w.r.t. standard opaque and concept-based models, while maintaining their accuracy.
abstract_zh: 基于概念的模型是深度学习中新兴的一种范式，它约束推理过程必须通过人类可解释的变量进行，从而促进可解释性和人机交互。然而，这类架构与流行的黑箱神经模型一样，未能刻画数据所表示的目标现象背后的真实因果机制。这削弱了它们支持因果推理任务的能力，限制了分布外泛化能力，并妨碍了公平性约束的实施。为克服这些问题，我们提出了因果可靠的概念瓶颈模型（C$^2$BM），这是一类基于概念的架构，它们强制通过一个按照现实世界因果机制模型构建的概念瓶颈进行推理。我们还引入了一条流程，用于从观测数据和非结构化背景知识（如科学文献）中自动学习这一结构。实验证据表明，与标准的黑箱模型和基于概念的模型相比，C$^2$BM具有更好的可解释性和因果可靠性，并且在保持准确率的同时，提高了对干预的响应能力。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Contrastive Representations for Temporal Reasoning
title_zh: 用于时间推理的对比表示
abstract:
In classical AI, perception relies on learning state-based representations, while planning --- temporal reasoning over action sequences --- 
is typically achieved through search. We study whether such reasoning can instead emerge from representations that capture both perceptual and temporal structure.  We show that standard temporal contrastive learning, despite its popularity, often fails to capture temporal structure due to its reliance on spurious features. To address this, we introduce Contrastive Representations for Temporal Reasoning (CRTR), a method that uses a negative sampling scheme to provably remove these spurious features and facilitate temporal reasoning. CRTR achieves strong results on domains with complex temporal structure, such as Sokoban and Rubik’s Cube. In particular, for the Rubik’s Cube, CRTR learns representations that generalize across all initial states and allow it to solve the puzzle using fewer search steps than BestFS — though with longer solutions. To our knowledge, this is the first method that efficiently solves arbitrary Cube states using only learned representations, without relying on an external search algorithm.
abstract_zh:
对比表示用于时序推理
在经典的人工智能中，感知依赖于学习基于状态的表征，而规划——对动作序列的时序推理——通常通过搜索来实现。我们研究是否可以用同时捕获感知结构与时间结构的表征来替代这种推理方式。我们发现，尽管流行，标准的时间对比学习往往由于依赖伪特征而无法真实捕捉时间结构。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种方法：用于时序推理的对比表征（CRTR），它通过一种负采样方案，理论上剔除了这些伪特征，从而促进时间推理。CRTR在具有复杂时间结构的领域，如推箱子和魔方，取得了强劲结果。尤其在魔方实验中，CRTR学习的表征可以泛化到所有初始状态，并使用比BestFS更少的搜索步数（尽管得到的解路径更长）解决魔方。据我们所知，这是首个仅靠学习到的表示而非外部搜索算法，就能高效解决任意魔方状态的方法。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Many Minds, One Goal: Time Series Forecasting via Sub-task Specialization and Inter-agent Cooperation
title_zh: 众心所向：通过子任务专业化与代理间协作进行时间序列预测
abstract: Time series forecasting is a critical and complex task, characterized by diverse temporal patterns, varying statistical properties, and different prediction horizons across datasets and domains. Conventional approaches typically rely on a single, unified model architecture to handle all forecasting scenarios. However, such monolithic models struggle to generalize across dynamically evolving time series with shifting patterns. In reality, different types of time series may require distinct modeling strategies. Some benefit from homogeneous multi-scale forecasting awareness, while others rely on more complex and heterogeneous signal perception. Relying on a single model to capture all temporal diversity and structural variations leads to limited  performance and poor interpretability. To address this challenge, we propose a Multi-Agent Forecasting System (MAFS) that abandons the one-size-fits-all paradigm. MAFS decomposes the forecasting task into multiple sub-tasks, each handled by a dedicated agent trained on specific temporal perspectives (e.g., different forecasting resolutions or signal characteristics).  Furthermore, to achieve holistic forecasting, agents share and refine information through different communication topology, enabling cooperative reasoning across different temporal views. A lightweight voting aggregator then integrates their outputs into consistent final predictions. Extensive experiments across 11 benchmarks demonstrate that MAFS significantly outperforms traditional single-model approaches, yielding more robust and adaptable forecasts.
abstract_zh: 时间序列预测是一项关键且复杂的任务，其特点是在不同数据集和领域中存在多样的时间模式、变化的统计属性以及不同的预测跨度。传统方法通常依赖单一统一的模型架构来处理所有预测场景。然而，这类单体模型难以在模式不断变化的动态时间序列上实现良好的泛化。事实上，不同类型的时间序列可能需要不同的建模策略。有些受益于同构的多尺度预测感知，而另一些则依赖于更复杂且异构的信号感知。依赖单一模型去捕捉所有时间多样性和结构变化，会导致性能受限且可解释性较差。为应对这一挑战，我们提出了一种多智能体预测系统（MAFS），摒弃了一刀切的范式。MAFS将预测任务分解为多个子任务，每个子任务由一个专门的智能体处理，这些智能体基于特定的时间视角进行训练（例如不同的预测分辨率或信号特征）。此外，为了实现整体性预测，智能体通过不同的通信拓扑共享并细化信息，从而能够在不同时间视角之间进行协同推理。随后，一个轻量级投票聚合器将它们的输出整合为一致的最终预测。在11个基准上的大量实验表明，MAFS显著优于传统的单模型方法，能够产生更稳健且更具适应性的预测。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Flow-based Conformal Prediction for Multi-dimensional Time Series
title_zh: 基于流的多维时间序列保形预测
abstract: Time series prediction underpins a broad range of downstream tasks across many scientific domains. Recent advances and increasing adoption of black-box machine learning models for time series prediction highlight the critical need for reliable uncertainty quantification. While conformal prediction has gained attention as a reliable uncertainty quantification method, conformal prediction for time series faces two key challenges: (1) adaptively leveraging correlations in features and non-conformity scores to overcome the exchangeability assumption, and (2) constructing prediction sets for multi-dimensional outcomes. To address these challenges jointly, we propose a novel conformal prediction method for time series using flow with classifier-free guidance. We provide coverage guarantees by establishing exact non-asymptotic marginal coverage and a finite-sample bound on conditional coverage for the proposed method. Evaluations on real-world time series datasets demonstrate that our method constructs significantly smaller prediction sets than existing conformal prediction methods while maintaining target coverage.
abstract_zh: 时间序列预测支撑着许多科学领域中广泛的下游任务。近年来，黑箱机器学习模型在时间序列预测中的进展与日益广泛的采用，凸显了可靠不确定性量化的关键需求。尽管保形预测作为一种可靠的不确定性量化方法已受到关注，但面向时间序列的保形预测面临两个关键挑战：（1）自适应地利用特征与非一致性分数中的相关性，以克服可交换性假设；（2）为多维输出构造预测集。为联合解决这些挑战，我们提出了一种针对时间序列的新型保形预测方法，该方法使用带有无分类器引导的流模型。我们通过为所提出的方法建立精确的非渐近边际覆盖保证以及条件覆盖的有限样本界，提供了覆盖率保证。在真实世界时间序列数据集上的评估表明，在保持目标覆盖率的同时，我们的方法构造的预测集显著小于现有的保形预测方法。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: ACT as Human: Multimodal Large Language Model Data Annotation with Critical Thinking
title_zh: ACT作为人类：具备批判性思维的多模态大型语言模型数据注释
abstract: Supervised learning relies on high-quality labeled data, but obtaining such data through human annotation is both expensive and time-consuming. Recent work explores using large language models (LLMs) for annotation, but LLM-generated labels still fall short of human-level quality. To address this problem, we propose the Annotation with Critical Thinking (ACT) data pipeline, where LLMs serve not only as annotators but also as judges to critically identify potential errors.  Human effort is then directed towards reviewing only the most "suspicious" cases, significantly improving the human annotation efficiency. Our major contributions are as follows: (1) ACT is applicable to a wide range of domains, including natural language processing (NLP), computer vision (CV), and multimodal understanding, by leveraging multimodal-LLMs (MLLMs). (2) Through empirical studies, we derive 7 insights on how to enhance annotation quality while efficiently reducing the human cost, and then translate these findings into user-friendly guidelines. (3) We theoretically analyze how to modify the loss function so that models trained on ACT data achieve similar performance to those trained on fully human-annotated data. Our experiments show that the performance gap can be reduced to less than 2% on most benchmark datasets while saving up to 90% of human costs.
abstract_zh: 监督学习依赖高质量的标注数据，但通过人工标注获取此类数据既昂贵又耗时。近期工作探索使用大语言模型（LLM）进行标注，但由 LLM 生成的标签在质量上仍未达到人类水平。为解决这一问题，我们提出了带有批判性思维的标注（ACT）数据流程，在该流程中，LLM 不仅充当标注者，还充当评判者，以批判性地识别潜在错误。随后，人工只需审查最“可疑”的样本，从而显著提升人工标注效率。我们的主要贡献如下：（1）通过利用多模态大语言模型（MLLM），ACT 可适用于广泛领域，包括自然语言处理（NLP）、计算机视觉（CV）和多模态理解。（2）通过实证研究，我们总结出 7 条关于如何在高效降低人工成本的同时提升标注质量的见解，并将这些发现转化为用户友好的指导原则。（3）我们从理论上分析了如何修改损失函数，使得在 ACT 数据上训练的模型能够达到与在完全人工标注数据上训练的模型相近的性能。我们的实验表明，在大多数基准数据集上，性能差距可缩小到 2% 以内，同时最多可节省 90% 的人工成本。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: On Universality Classes of Equivariant Networks
title_zh: 关于等变网络的普适性类别
abstract:
Equivariant neural networks provide a principled framework for incorporating symmetry into learning architectures and have been extensively analyzed through the lens of their *separation power*, that is, the ability to distinguish inputs modulo symmetry. This notion plays a central role in settings such as graph learning, where it is often formalized via the Weisfeilern–Leman hierarchy. In contrast, the *universality* of equivariant models—their capacity to approximate target functions—remains comparatively underexplored. 
In this work, we investigate the approximation power of equivariant neural networks beyond separation constraints.
We show that separation power does not fully capture expressivity: models with identical separation power may differ in their approximation ability.
To demonstrate this, we characterize the universality classes of shallow invariant networks, providing a general framework for understanding which functions these architectures can approximate. Since equivariant models reduce to invariant ones under projection, this analysis yields sufficient conditions under which shallow equivariant networks fail to be universal.
Conversely, we identify settings where shallow models do achieve separation-constrained universality.
These positive results, however, depend critically on structural properties of the symmetry group, such as the existence of adequate normal subgroups, which may not hold in important cases like permutation symmetry.
abstract_zh: 等变神经网络为将对称性纳入学习架构提供了一个有原则的框架，并且已经通过其“分离能力”的视角得到了广泛分析，也就是区分模对称输入的能力。这一概念在图学习等场景中起着核心作用，在这些场景里，它通常通过 Weisfeilern–Leman 层级加以形式化。相比之下，等变模型的“通用性”——即它们逼近目标函数的能力——仍然相对缺乏研究。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Native Segmentation Vision Transformers
title_zh: 原生分割视觉Transformer
abstract:
Uniform downsampling remains the de facto standard for reducing spatial resolution in vision backbones. In this work, we propose an alternative design built around a content-aware spatial grouping layer that dynamically assigns tokens to a reduced set based on image boundaries and their semantic content. Stacking our grouping layer across consecutive backbone stages results in hierarchical segmentation that arises *natively* in the feature extraction process, resulting in our coined Native Segmentation Vision Transformer.
We show that a careful design of our architecture enables the emergence of strong segmentation masks solely from grouping layers, that is, without additional segmentation-specific heads. This sets the foundation for a new paradigm of *native*, backbone-level segmentation, which enables strong zero-shot results without mask supervision, as well as a minimal and efficient standalone model design for downstream segmentation tasks.
abstract_zh: 均匀下采样仍然是视觉骨干网络中降低空间分辨率的事实标准。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种替代性设计，其核心是一个内容感知的空间分组层，该层根据图像边界及其语义内容，动态地将标记分配到一个缩减后的集合中。将我们的分组层堆叠到连续的骨干网络阶段中，会形成一种层次化分割，这种分割在特征提取过程中原生地产生，由此得到我们所称的原生分割视觉Transformer。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: LLM-Explorer: A Plug-in Reinforcement Learning Policy Exploration Enhancement Driven by Large Language Models
title_zh: LLM-Explorer：一种由大语言模型驱动的插件式强化学习策略探索增强方法
abstract:
Policy exploration is critical in reinforcement learning (RL), where existing approaches include $\epsilon$-greedy, Gaussian process, etc.
However, these approaches utilize preset stochastic processes and are indiscriminately applied in all kinds of RL tasks without considering task-specific features that influence policy exploration. Moreover, during RL training, the evolution of such stochastic processes is rigid, which typically only incorporates a decay in the variance, failing to adjust flexibly according to the agent's real-time learning status.
Inspired by the analyzing and reasoning capability of large language models (LLMs), we design **LLM-Explorer** to adaptively generate task-specific exploration strategies with LLMs, enhancing the policy exploration in RL. In our design, we sample the learning trajectory of the agent during the RL training in a given task and prompt the LLM to analyze the agent's current policy learning status and then generate a probability distribution for future policy exploration. Updating the probability distribution periodically, we derive a stochastic process specialized for the particular task and dynamically adjusted to adapt to the learning process. Our design is a plug-in module compatible with various widely applied RL algorithms, including the DQN series, DDPG, TD3, and any possible variants developed based on them. Through extensive experiments on the Atari and MuJoCo benchmarks, we demonstrate LLM-Explorer's capability to enhance RL policy exploration, achieving an average performance improvement up to 37.27%. Our code is open-source at https://github.com/tsinghua-fib-lab/LLM-Explorer for reproducibility.
abstract_zh: 政策探索在强化学习中至关重要，现有方法如ε-贪心、高斯过程等均使用预设的随机过程，并在各种任务中一视同仁地应用，忽略了影响策略探索的任务特定特征；此外，在训练过程中这些随机过程的演化僵硬，通常只通过方差衰减，无法根据智能体的实时学习状态灵活调整。受到大型语言模型分析推理能力的启发，我们设计了LLM-Explorer，通过LLM自适应生成任务特定的探索策略，提升强化学习中的策略探索。在设计中，我们在某一任务的训练过程中采样智能体的学习轨迹，并以此提示LLM分析当前策略学习状态，随后生成未来策略探索的概率分布。通过周期性更新该分布，我们得到一个针对特定任务、随着学习过程动态调整的随机过程。本设计是一个即插即用模块，可与包括DQN系列、DDPG、TD3及其可能的变体在内的多种主流强化学习算法兼容。在Atari与MuJoCo基准测试上的大规模实验表明，LLM-Explorer能够显著改善策略探索，平均性能提升可达37.27%。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: EAMET: ROBUST MASSIVE MODEL EDITING VIA EMBEDDING ALIGNMENT OPTIMIZATION
title_zh: EAMET：通过嵌入对齐优化实现的鲁棒大规模模型编辑
abstract:
Model editing techniques are essential for efficiently updating knowledge in
large language models (LLMs). However, the effectiveness of existing approaches
degrades in massive editing scenarios, particularly when evaluated with
practical metrics. Their robustness is also limited in context-rich settings or
when editing multiple facts of the same subject simultaneously. We attribute
these failures to the embedding misalignment among knowledge items, which
undermines editing reliability at scale. To address this, we propose EAMET
(Embedding Alignment Model Editing in Transformers), which addresses this issue
by aligning the space of key and residual embeddings. Extensive experiments
across six LLMs and three datasets demonstrate that EAMET consistently
outperforms existing methods, achieving about 90\% editing efficacy when editing
10k facts.
abstract_zh:
译文**
EAMET（基于嵌入对齐优化的Transformer模型编辑）提出后，解决了大规模知识编辑任务中常见的嵌入空间错配问题。现有方法在实际度量下对批量编辑（尤其是对同一主题的多个事实）表现出效率下降和上下文敏感性差，而这种失败主要源于不同知识项间键向量与残差向量的嵌入偏移，导致编辑在大规模场景中失去一致性和鲁棒性。EAMET通过对齐关键嵌入与残差嵌入的空间结构，使得编辑过程在规模扩展时仍保持可靠。涵盖六种大模型与三个数据集的广泛实验表明：在编辑一万条事实时，EAMET的编辑效果稳定在约九成，显著优于已有方法。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: OCTDiff: Bridged Diffusion Model for Portable OCT Super-Resolution and Enhancement
title_zh: 便携式OCT超分辨与增强的OCTDiff: 桥接扩散模型
abstract: Medical imaging super-resolution is critical for improving diagnostic utility and reducing costs, particularly for low-cost modalities such as portable Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT). We propose OCTDiff, a bridged diffusion model designed to enhance image resolution and quality from portable OCT devices. Our image-to-image diffusion framework addresses key challenges in the conditional generation process of denoising diffusion probabilistic models (DDPMs). We introduce Adaptive Noise Aggregation (ANA), a novel module to improve denoising dynamics within the reverse diffusion process. Additionally, we integrate Multi-Scale Cross-Attention (MSCA) into the U-Net backbone to capture local dependencies across spatial resolutions. To address overfitting on small clinical datasets and to preserve fine structural details essential for retinal diagnostics, we design a customized loss function guided by clinical quality scores. OCTDiff outperforms convolutional baselines and standard DDPMs, achieving state-of-the-art performance on clinical portable OCT datasets. Our model and its downstream applications have the potential to generalize to other medical imaging modalities and revolutionize the current workflow of ophthalmic diagnostics. The code is available at https://github.com/AI4VSLab/OCTDiff.
abstract_zh: 医学影像超分辨率对于提高诊断效用和降低成本至关重要，尤其是对于便携式光学相干断层扫描（OCT）等低成本成像方式。我们提出了 OCTDiff，这是一种桥接扩散模型，旨在提升便携式 OCT 设备获取图像的分辨率和质量。我们的图像到图像扩散框架解决了去噪扩散概率模型（DDPM）条件生成过程中的关键挑战。我们引入了自适应噪声聚合（ANA），这是一种用于改善反向扩散过程中去噪动态的新模块。此外，我们将多尺度交叉注意力（MSCA）集成到 U-Net 主干中，以捕获跨空间分辨率的局部依赖关系。为了解决小规模临床数据集上的过拟合问题，并保留对视网膜诊断至关重要的精细结构细节，我们设计了一种由临床质量评分引导的定制损失函数。OCTDiff 优于卷积基线方法和标准 DDPM，在临床便携式 OCT 数据集上实现了最先进的性能。我们的模型及其下游应用有望泛化到其他医学成像模态，并革新当前的眼科诊断工作流程。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Textual Bayes: Quantifying Uncertainty in LLM-Based Systems
title_zh: 文本贝叶斯：量化基于大语言模型系统中的不确定性
abstract: Although large language models (LLMs) are becoming increasingly capable of solving challenging real-world tasks, accurately quantifying their uncertainty remains a critical open problem—one that limits their applicability in high-stakes domains. This challenge is further compounded by the closed-source, black-box nature of many state-of-the-art LLMs. Moreover, LLM-based systems can be highly sensitive to the prompts that bind them together, which often require significant manual tuning (i.e., prompt engineering). In this work, we address these challenges by viewing LLM-based systems through a Bayesian lens. We interpret prompts as textual parameters in a statistical model, allowing us to use a small training dataset to perform Bayesian inference over these prompts. This novel perspective enables principled uncertainty quantification over both the model’s textual parameters and its downstream predictions, while also incorporating prior beliefs about these parameters expressed in free-form text. To perform Bayesian inference— a difficult problem even for well-studied data modalities—we introduce Metropolis-Hastings through LLM Proposals (MHLP), a novel Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) algorithm that combines prompt optimization techniques with standard MCMC methods. MHLP is a turnkey modification to existing LLM pipelines, including those that rely exclusively on closed-source models. Empirically, we demonstrate that our method yields improvements in both predictive accuracy and uncertainty quantification (UQ) on a range of LLM benchmarks and UQ tasks. More broadly, our work demonstrates a viable path for incorporating methods from the rich Bayesian literature into the era of LLMs, paving the way for more reliable and calibrated LLM-based systems.
abstract_zh: 尽管大语言模型（LLM）在解决具有挑战性的现实世界任务方面正变得越来越强大，但如何准确量化其不确定性仍然是一个关键的开放性问题，这限制了它们在高风险领域中的适用性。许多最先进的 LLM 具有闭源、黑箱的性质，这进一步加剧了这一挑战。此外，基于 LLM 的系统可能对将其各部分连接起来的提示词高度敏感，而这些提示词通常需要大量人工调优（即提示工程）。在这项工作中，我们通过贝叶斯视角来看待基于 LLM 的系统，以应对这些挑战。我们将提示词解释为统计模型中的文本参数，从而能够利用一个小规模训练数据集对这些提示词进行贝叶斯推断。这一新颖视角使我们能够对模型的文本参数及其下游预测进行有原则的不确定性量化，同时还能纳入以自由文本形式表达的关于这些参数的先验信念。为了执行贝叶斯推断这一即使对于研究充分的数据模态而言也很困难的问题，我们提出了通过 LLM 提议进行 Metropolis-Hastings（MHLP），这是一种新颖的马尔可夫链蒙特卡罗（MCMC）算法，将提示优化技术与标准 MCMC 方法结合起来。MHLP 是对现有 LLM 流水线的一种开箱即用修改，包括那些完全依赖闭源模型的流水线。在实证上，我们表明，该方法在一系列 LLM 基准和不确定性量化任务上，同时提升了预测准确性和不确定性量化（UQ）效果。更广泛地说，我们的工作展示了一条可行路径，可将丰富的贝叶斯文献中的方法引入 LLM 时代，为构建更可靠、校准更好的基于 LLM 的系统铺平道路。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: From Concepts to Components: Concept-Agnostic Attention Module Discovery in Transformers
title_zh: 从概念到组件：在 Transformer 中发现概念无关的注意力模块
abstract: Transformers have achieved state-of-the-art performance across diverse language and vision tasks. This success drives the imperative to interpret their internal mechanisms with the dual goals of enhancing performance and improving behavioral control. Attribution methods help advance interpretability by assigning model outputs associated with a target concept to specific model components. Current attribution research primarily studies multi-layer perceptron (MLP) neurons and addresses relatively simple concepts such as factual associations (e.g., Paris is located in France). This focus tends to overlook the impact of the attention mechanism and lacks a unified approach for analyzing more complex concepts. To fill these gaps, we introduce Scalable Attention Module Discovery (SAMD), a concept-agnostic method for mapping arbitrary, complex concepts to specific attention heads of general transformer models. We accomplish this by representing each concept as a vector, calculating its cosine similarity with each attention head, and selecting the TopK-scoring heads to construct the concept-associated attention module. We then propose Scalar Attention Module Intervention (SAMI), a simple strategy to diminish or amplify the effects of a concept by adjusting the attention module using only a single scalar parameter. Empirically, we demonstrate SAMD on concepts of varying complexity, and visualize the locations of their corresponding modules. Our results demonstrate that module locations remain stable before and after LLM post-training, and confirm prior work on the mechanics of LLM multi-lingualism. Through SAMI, we facilitate jailbreaking on HarmBench (+72.7%) by diminishing “safety” and improve performance on the GSM8K benchmark (+1.6%) by amplifying “reasoning”. Lastly, we highlight the domain-agnostic nature of our approach by suppressing the image classification accuracy of vision transformers on ImageNet.
abstract_zh: Transformer 在多种语言和视觉任务中都取得了最先进的性能。这一成功推动了对其内部机制进行解释的迫切需求，其双重目标是提升性能并改善行为控制。归因方法通过将与目标概念相关的模型输出分配给特定模型组件，帮助推进可解释性研究。当前的归因研究主要考察多层感知机（MLP）神经元，并处理相对简单的概念，例如事实关联（如巴黎位于法国）。这种关注往往忽视了注意力机制的影响，也缺乏一种用于分析更复杂概念的统一方法。为填补这些空白，我们引入了可扩展注意力模块发现（SAMD），这是一种与概念无关的方法，用于将任意复杂概念映射到通用 Transformer 模型的特定注意力头。我们通过将每个概念表示为一个向量，计算其与每个注意力头的余弦相似度，并选择得分最高的 TopK 个头来构建与该概念相关的注意力模块。随后，我们提出了标量注意力模块干预（SAMI），这是一种简单策略，只需调整单个标量参数即可通过调节注意力模块来削弱或增强某一概念的影响。在实验上，我们在不同复杂度的概念上展示了 SAMD，并对其对应模块的位置进行了可视化。结果表明，这些模块的位置在大语言模型后训练前后保持稳定，并验证了先前关于大语言模型多语言机制的研究。通过 SAMI，我们通过削弱“安全性”在 HarmBench 上促进了越狱（+72.7%），并通过增强“推理能力”提升了 GSM8K 基准上的性能（+1.6%）。最后，我们通过压制视觉 Transformer 在 ImageNet 上的图像分类准确率，强调了我们方法的领域无关性。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: OrthoLoC: UAV 6-DoF Localization and Calibration Using Orthographic Geodata
title_zh: OrthoLoC：利用正射地理数据进行 UAV 6 自由度定位与校准
abstract: Accurate visual localization from aerial views is a fundamental problem with applications in mapping, large-area inspection, and search-and-rescue operations. In many scenarios, these systems require high-precision localization while operating with limited resources (e.g., no internet connection or GNSS/GPS support), making large image databases or heavy 3D models impractical. Surprisingly, little attention has been given to leveraging orthographic geodata as an alternative paradigm, which is lightweight and increasingly available through free releases by governmental authorities (e.g., the European Union). To fill this gap, we propose OrthoLoC, the first large-scale dataset comprising 16,425 UAV images from Germany and the United States with multiple modalities. The dataset addresses domain shifts between UAV imagery and geospatial data. Its paired structure enables fair benchmarking of existing solutions by decoupling image retrieval from feature matching, allowing isolated evaluation of localization and calibration performance. Through comprehensive evaluation, we examine the impact of domain shifts, data resolutions, and covisibility on localization accuracy. Finally, we introduce a refinement technique called AdHoP, which can be integrated with any feature matcher, improving matching by up to 95% and reducing translation error by up to 63%. The dataset and code are available at: https://deepscenario.github.io/OrthoLoC .
abstract_zh: 来自空中视角的精确视觉定位是一个基础问题，在测绘、大范围巡检和搜救等应用中具有重要作用。在许多场景下，这些系统需要在资源受限的条件下实现高精度定位（例如，没有互联网连接或 GNSS/GPS 支持），这使得大型图像数据库或庞重的三维模型变得不切实际。令人惊讶的是，作为一种替代范式，利用正射地理数据这一方式却鲜少受到关注；而这种方式不仅轻量，而且随着政府机构（如欧盟）免费发布相关数据而日益可获得。为填补这一空白，我们提出了 OrthoLoC，这是首个大规模数据集，包含来自德国和美国的 16,425 张具有多种模态的无人机图像。该数据集针对无人机影像与地理空间数据之间的域偏移问题进行了设计。其成对结构通过将图像检索与特征匹配解耦，使现有方法能够得到公平基准测试，从而可以对定位与标定性能进行独立评估。通过全面评估，我们考察了域偏移、数据分辨率和共视性对定位精度的影响。最后，我们提出了一种名为 AdHoP 的细化技术，它可与任何特征匹配器集成，使匹配效果最高提升 95%，并将平移误差最高降低 63%。数据集和代码可见：https://deepscenario.github.io/OrthoLoC 。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: PM-KVQ: Progressive Mixed-precision KV Cache Quantization for Long-CoT LLMs
title_zh: 面向长链式推理大语言模型的渐进式混合精度KV缓存量化
abstract:
Recently, significant progress has been made in developing reasoning-capable Large Language Models (LLMs) through long Chain-of-Thought (CoT) techniques.
However, this long-CoT reasoning process imposes substantial memory overhead due to the large Key-Value (KV) Cache memory overhead.
Post-training KV Cache quantization has emerged as a promising compression technique and has been extensively studied in short-context scenarios.
However, directly applying existing methods to long-CoT LLMs causes significant performance degradation due to the following two reasons: 
(1) Large cumulative error: Existing methods fail to adequately leverage available memory, and they directly quantize the KV Cache during each decoding step, leading to large cumulative quantization error.
(2) Short-context calibration: Due to Rotary Positional Embedding (RoPE), the use of short-context data during calibration fails to account for the distribution of less frequent channels in the Key Cache, resulting in performance loss.
We propose Progressive Mixed-Precision KV Cache Quantization (PM-KVQ) for long-CoT LLMs to address the above issues in two folds:
(1) To reduce cumulative error, we design a progressive quantization strategy to gradually lower the bit-width of KV Cache in each block. Then, we propose block-wise memory allocation to assign a higher bit-width to more sensitive transformer blocks. 
(2) To increase the calibration length without additional overhead, we propose a new calibration strategy with positional interpolation that leverages short calibration data with positional interpolation to approximate the data distribution of long-context data.
Extensive experiments on 7B–70B long-CoT LLMs show that PM-KVQ improves reasoning benchmark performance by up to 8% over SOTA baselines under the same memory budget and achieves 2.73–5.18$\times$ throughput over the original 16-bit LLMs.
Our code will be released soon.
abstract_zh: 近年来，通过长思维链（CoT）技术开发具备推理能力的大语言模型（LLM）已取得显著进展。然而，这种长思维链推理过程会因庞大的键值（KV）缓存内存开销带来极高的内存负担。训练后KV缓存量化作为一种极具前景的压缩技术，已在短上下文场景中得到广泛研究。但直接将现有方法应用于长思维链大语言模型会导致性能严重下降，原因主要有两点：（1）累积误差大：现有方法无法充分利用可用内存，会在每个解码步骤直接量化KV缓存，导致量化累积误差偏大。（2）短上下文校准：受旋转位置嵌入（RoPE）影响，校准时使用短上下文数据无法覆盖键缓存中低频通道的分布，进而造成性能损失。我们针对长思维链大语言模型提出渐进式混合精度KV缓存量化（PM-KVQ）方法，从两个方面解决上述问题：（1）为降低累积误差，我们设计了渐进式量化策略，逐步降低每个模块中KV缓存的位宽；随后提出逐模块内存分配机制，为敏感度更高的Transformer模块分配更高的位宽。（2）为在不引入额外开销的前提下提升校准长度，我们提出一种结合位置插值的新型校准策略，利用经过位置插值处理的短校准数据来近似长上下文数据的分布。在7B至70B参数规模的长思维链大语言模型上开展的大量实验表明，在相同内存预算下，PM-KVQ相较现有最优基线的推理基准性能最高提升8%，且吞吐量可达原始16位大语言模型的2.73至5.18倍。我们的代码即将发布。
notes: 论文聚焦长链路推理LLM中的KV Cache量化，通过渐进式混合精度压缩缓存，直接对应上下文/缓存优化与模型压缩。

title: Noise Stability of Transformer Models
title_zh: Transformer 模型的噪声稳定性
abstract: Understanding simplicity biases in deep learning offers a promising path toward developing reliable AI. A common metric for this, inspired by Boolean function analysis, is average sensitivity, which captures a model's robustness to single-token perturbations. We argue that average sensitivity has two key limitations: it lacks a natural generalization to real-valued domains and fails to explain the "junta-like" input dependence we empirically observe in modern LLMs. To address these limitations, we propose *noise stability* as a more comprehensive simplicity metric. Noise stability expresses a model's robustness to correlated noise applied to *all* input coordinates simultaneously. We provide a theoretical analysis of noise stability for single-layer attention and ReLU MLP layers and tackle the multi-layer propagation problem with a covariance interval propagation approach. Building on this theory, we develop a practical *noise stability regularization* method. Experiments on algorithmic and next-token-prediction tasks show that our regularizer consistently catalyzes grokking and accelerates training by approximately $35$\% and $75$\% respectively. Our results establish noise stability as a powerful tool for understanding and improving modern Transformers.
abstract_zh: 理解深度学习中的简单性偏置为开发可靠的人工智能提供了一条很有前景的路径。受布尔函数分析启发，一个常用的度量是平均敏感度，它刻画了模型对单个词元扰动的鲁棒性。我们认为，平均敏感度有两个关键局限：它缺乏向实值域的自然推广，并且无法解释我们在现代大语言模型中通过实验观察到的类似“junta”的输入依赖性。为了解决这些局限，我们提出将噪声稳定性作为一个更全面的简单性度量。噪声稳定性刻画了模型在所有输入坐标同时受到相关噪声作用时的鲁棒性。我们对单层注意力和 ReLU MLP 层的噪声稳定性进行了理论分析，并使用协方差区间传播方法处理了多层传播问题。在这一理论基础上，我们开发了一种实用的噪声稳定性正则化方法。在算法任务和下一词元预测任务上的实验表明，我们的正则项能够稳定地促进 grokking，并分别将训练速度加快约35%和75%。我们的结果确立了噪声稳定性是理解和改进现代 Transformer 的有力工具。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: AdaDecode: Accelerating LLM Decoding with Adaptive Layer Parallelism
title_zh: AdaDecode：通过自适应层并行加速大型语言模型解码
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for long-content generation (e.g., long Chain-of-Thought reasoning) where decoding efficiency becomes a critical bottleneck: Autoregressive decoding is inherently limited by its sequential token generation process, where each token must be generated before the next can be processed. This sequential dependency restricts the ability to fully leverage modern hardware’s parallel processing capabilities. Existing methods like speculative decoding and layer skipping offer potential speedups but have notable drawbacks: speculative decoding relies on an auxiliary “drafter” model, which can be challenging to acquire and increases memory overhead, while layer skipping may introduce discrepancies in the generated outputs due to the missing key-value cache at skipped layers. In this work, we propose AdaDecode, which accelerates LLM decoding without requiring auxiliary models or changes to the original model parameters, while ensuring output consistency. AdaDecode leverages the insight that many tokens—particularly simple or highly-predictable ones—can accurately be generated at intermediate layers, as further layers often do not significantly alter predictions once the model reaches a certain confidence. By adaptively generating tokens at intermediate layers when confidence is high, AdaDecode enables the next token’s computation to begin immediately. The remaining layer computations for early-predicted tokens are deferred and executed in parallel with subsequent tokens when needed, maximizing hardware utilization and reducing decoding latency. A final verification step ensures that early predictions match the results of standard autoregressive decoding, preserving output parity. Experiments across diverse generation tasks shows that AdaDecode consistently achieves superior decoding throughput compared to baselines with up to 1.73$\times$ speedup, while guaranteeing output parity with standard autoregressive decoding.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）正被越来越多地用于长内容生成场景（例如长思维链推理），这类场景下解码效率成为关键瓶颈：自回归解码本质上受限于其序列式词元生成过程，必须先生成当前词元才能处理下一个词元。这种序列依赖关系限制了对现代硬件并行处理能力的充分利用。投机解码、层跳过等现有方法虽具备提速潜力，但存在明显缺陷：投机解码依赖辅助“草稿”模型，这类模型获取难度较高，还会增加内存开销；而层跳过方法由于被跳过的层缺失键值缓存，可能导致生成输出出现偏差。本研究提出AdaDecode方法，无需辅助模型，也无需修改原始模型参数即可加速LLM解码，同时还能保障输出一致性。AdaDecode基于如下洞见：许多词元，尤其是简单或可预测性极高的词元，可在中间层准确生成，因为一旦模型达到一定置信度，后续层通常不会明显改变预测结果。当置信度较高时，AdaDecode会自适应地在中间层生成词元，从而可立即启动下一个词元的计算。提前预测得到的词元的剩余层计算会被延后，在需要时与后续词元的计算并行执行，最大化硬件利用率并降低解码延迟。最后的验证步骤可确保提前预测结果与标准自回归解码的结果一致，维持输出对等性。在多种生成任务上开展的实验表明，与基线方法相比，AdaDecode始终具备更优的解码吞吐量，提速幅度最高可达1.73倍，同时能够保证与标准自回归解码的输出完全一致。
notes: 论文核心是 LLM 推理解码加速，通过自适应中间层提前预测与分层并行执行提升吞吐，明确属于解码优化；同时涉及并行执行与硬件利用率提升，属于系统与调度优化。摘要提到 KV cache 问题主要是对比现有方法的缺陷，AdaDecode 本身并非直接提出新的缓存机制，因此该标签可保留但证据相对次要。

title: Reasoning with Sampling: Your Base Model is Smarter Than You Think
title_zh: 使用抽样推理：你的基础模型比你想象的更聪明
abstract: Frontier reasoning models have exhibited incredible capabilities across a wide array of disciplines, driven by posttraining large language models (LLMs) with reinforcement learning (RL). However, despite the widespread success of this paradigm, much of the literature has been devoted to disentangling truly novel behaviors that emerge during RL but are not present in the base models. In our work, we approach this question from a different angle, instead asking whether comparable reasoning capabilities can be elicited from base models at inference time by pure sampling, without any additional training. Inspired by Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) techniques for sampling from sharpened distributions, we propose a simple iterative sampling algorithm leveraging the base models' own likelihoods. Over different base models, we show that our algorithm offers substantial boosts in reasoning that nearly match and even outperform those from RL on a wide variety of single-shot tasks, including MATH500, HumanEval, and GPQA. Moreover, our sampler avoids the collapse in diversity over multiple samples that is characteristic of RL-posttraining. Crucially, our method does not require training, curated datasets, or a verifier, suggesting broad applicability beyond easily verifiable domains.
abstract_zh: 前沿推理模型在广泛学科上展现出了惊人的能力，这得益于使用强化学习对大型语言模型进行后训练。然而，尽管这一范式已广泛成功，现有大量文献主要致力于剖析那些在强化学习过程中真正涌现、但在基础模型中并不存在的新行为。在我们的工作中，我们从不同角度切入这一问题，转而探究是否可以仅通过纯采样、在不进行任何额外训练的情况下，于推理时从基础模型中激发出可比的推理能力。受用于从锐化分布中采样的马尔可夫链蒙特卡罗技术启发，我们提出了一种利用基础模型自身似然的简单迭代采样算法。在不同的基础模型上，我们表明，该算法能够显著提升推理能力，在包括 MATH500、HumanEval 和 GPQA 在内的多种单次作答任务上，这种提升几乎可与强化学习带来的效果相匹配，甚至超过后者。此外，我们的采样器避免了强化学习后训练所特有的、多样性在多次采样中发生坍缩的问题。关键在于，我们的方法不需要训练、人工整理的数据集或验证器，这表明它在易于验证领域之外也具有广泛的适用性。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Solving Token Gradient Conflict in Mixture-of-Experts for Large Vision-Language Model
title_zh: 解决大型视觉语言模型混合专家架构中的令牌梯度冲突
abstract:
The Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) has gained increasing attention in studying Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs). It uses a sparse model to replace the dense model, achieving comparable performance while activating fewer parameters during inference, thus significantly reducing the inference cost. Existing MoE methods in LVLM encourage different experts to specialize in different tokens, and they usually employ a router to predict the routing of each token. However, the router is not optimized concerning distinct parameter optimization directions generated from tokens within an expert. This may lead to severe interference between tokens within an expert. To address this problem, we propose to use the token-level gradient analysis to Solving Token Gradient Conflict (STGC) in this paper. Specifically, we first use token-level gradients to identify conflicting tokens in experts. After that, we add a regularization loss tailored to encourage conflicting tokens routing from their current experts to other experts, for reducing interference between tokens within an expert. Our method can serve as a plug-in for diverse LVLM methods, and extensive experimental results demonstrate its effectiveness. demonstrate its effectiveness. 
The code will be publicly available at https://github.com/longrongyang/STGC.
abstract_zh: 专家混合（MoE）在大视觉语言模型（LVLM）研究领域受到越来越多的关注。它采用稀疏模型替代稠密模型，在推理阶段仅激活更少参数即可实现相当的性能，因此能够显著降低推理成本。现有大视觉语言模型中的MoE方法鼓励不同专家专攻不同令牌，通常采用路由器预测每个令牌的路由路径。但路由器并未针对同一专家内不同令牌产生的差异化参数优化方向进行优化，这可能导致同一专家内的令牌之间出现严重干扰。为解决这一问题，本文提出采用令牌层级梯度分析来解决令牌梯度冲突（STGC）。具体而言，我们首先利用令牌层级梯度识别专家内存在冲突的令牌，随后添加专门设计的正则化损失，推动冲突令牌从当前所属专家路由至其他专家，以此降低同一专家内令牌间的干扰。本方法可作为插件集成到各类大视觉语言模型方法中，大量实验结果验证了该方法的有效性。代码将公开发布于https://github.com/longrongyang/STGC。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Vocabulary In-Context Learning in Transformers: Benefits of Positional Encoding
title_zh: Transformer 中的上下文词汇学习：位置编码的优势
abstract: Numerous studies have demonstrated that the Transformer architecture possesses the capability for in-context learning (ICL). In scenarios involving function approximation, context can serve as a control parameter for the model, endowing it with the universal approximation property (UAP). In practice, context is represented by tokens from a finite set, referred to as a vocabulary, which is the case considered in this paper, i.e., vocabulary in-context learning (VICL). We demonstrate that VICL in single-layer Transformers, without positional encoding, does not possess the UAP; however, it is possible to achieve the UAP when positional encoding is included. Several sufficient conditions for the positional encoding are provided. Our findings reveal the benefits of positional encoding from an approximation theory perspective in the context of in-context learning.
abstract_zh: 大量研究表明，Transformer 架构具备上下文学习（ICL）的能力。在涉及函数逼近的情形中，上下文可以作为模型的控制参数，使其具有通用逼近性质（UAP）。在实践中，上下文由来自有限集合的词元表示，该集合称为词表，本文考虑的正是这种情形，即词表上下文学习（VICL）。我们证明了，在不使用位置编码的情况下，单层 Transformer 中的 VICL 不具备 UAP；然而，当加入位置编码时，则有可能实现 UAP。本文还给出了位置编码的若干充分条件。我们的发现从逼近理论的角度揭示了在上下文学习中位置编码的益处。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Adaptive Pruning of Pretrained Transformer via Differential Inclusions
title_zh: 通过微分包含对预训练Transformer进行自适应剪枝
abstract: Large transformers have demonstrated remarkable success, making it necessary to compress these models to reduce inference costs while preserving their performance. Current compression algorithms prune transformers at fixed compression ratios, requiring a unique pruning process for each ratio, which results in high computational costs. In contrast, we propose pruning of pretrained transformers at any desired ratio within a single pruning stage, based on a differential inclusion for a mask parameter. This dynamic can generate the whole regularization solution path of the mask parameter, whose support set identifies the network structure. Therefore, the solution path identifies a Transformer weight family with various sparsity levels, offering greater flexibility and customization.In this paper, weintroduce such an effective pruning method, termed SPP (Solution Path Pruning). To achieve effective pruning, we segment the transformers into paired modules, including query-key pairs, value-projection pairs, and sequential linear layers, and apply low-rank compression to these pairs, maintaining the output structure while enabling structural compression within the inner states. Extensive experiments conducted on various well-known transformer backbones have demonstrated the efficacy of SPP.
abstract_zh: 大型Transformer已取得极为出色的表现，因此有必要对这类模型进行压缩，在保留其性能的同时降低推理成本。现有压缩算法以固定压缩率对Transformer执行剪枝，每种压缩率都需要单独的剪枝流程，导致计算成本极高。与之不同，我们基于掩码参数的微分包含，提出可在单次剪枝阶段内以任意目标压缩率对预训练Transformer执行剪枝的方法。这种动态过程可以生成掩码参数的完整正则化解路径，其支撑集可确定网络结构。因此，该解路径可得到具备不同稀疏度水平的Transformer权重族，灵活性与可定制性更强。本文提出了这种高效剪枝方法，命名为解路径剪枝（Solution Path Pruning，SPP）。为实现有效剪枝，我们将Transformer拆分为成对模块，包括查询-键对、值-投影对以及连续线性层，对这些成对模块应用低秩压缩，在维持输出结构的同时可在内层状态中实现结构压缩。在多个知名Transformer骨干网络上开展的大量实验证明了SPP的有效性。
notes: 论文核心是对预训练 Transformer 做可变压缩率的剪枝与低秩压缩，属于模型压缩；未体现系统调度、缓存或内核优化。

title: NestedFP: High-Performance, Memory-Efficient Dual-Precision Floating Point Support for LLMs
title_zh: NestedFP：面向大型语言模型的高性能、低内存双精度浮点支持
abstract: Meeting service-level objectives (SLOs) in Large Language Models (LLMs) serving is critical, but managing the high variability in load presents a significant challenge. Recent advancements in FP8 inference, backed by native hardware support, offer a potential solution: executing FP16 models by default, while switching to FP8 models during sudden load surges to achieve higher throughput at the cost of a slight quality degradation. Although this approach facilitates effective SLO management, it introduces additional memory overhead due to storing two versions of the same model. In response, this paper proposes NestedFP, an LLM serving technique that supports both FP16 and FP8 models in a memoryefficient manner by overlaying FP8 parameters onto FP16 parameters, allowing both models to share the same FP16 memory footprint. By leveraging a compact data format for the overlay and a specialized GEMM kernel optimized for this format, NestedFP ensures minimal degradation in both model quality and inference throughput across both FP8 and FP16 modes. NestedFP provides a flexible platform for dynamic, SLO-aware precision selection. The code is available at https://github.com/SNU-ARC/NestedFP.
abstract_zh: 在大语言模型（LLM）服务中满足服务水平目标（SLO）至关重要，但管控负载的高波动性是一项重大挑战。近期获得原生硬件支持的FP8推理技术进展提供了一种潜在解决方案：默认运行FP16模型，在负载突发飙升时切换到FP8模型，以略微降低质量为代价实现更高吞吐量。尽管该方法可实现高效的SLO管理，但由于需要存储同一模型的两个版本，会引入额外的内存开销。针对这一问题，本文提出NestedFP，这是一种LLM服务技术，通过将FP8参数叠加到FP16参数上，允许两种模型共享相同的FP16内存占用，从而以内存高效的方式同时支持FP16和FP8模型。通过为叠加层采用紧凑数据格式，以及针对该格式优化的专用GEMM内核，NestedFP确保FP8和FP16两种模式下的模型质量与推理吞吐量均仅有极小幅度的下降。NestedFP为动态、SLO感知的精度选择提供了灵活平台。代码可在https://github.com/SNU-ARC/NestedFP获取。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Rotated Runtime Smooth: Training-Free Activation Smoother for accurate INT4 inference
title_zh: Rotated Runtime Smooth：无需训练的激活平滑器用于精确的 INT4 推理
abstract:
Large language models have demonstrated promising capabilities upon scaling up parameters. However, serving large language models incurs substantial computation and memory movement costs due to their large scale. Quantization methods have been employed to reduce service costs and latency. Nevertheless, outliers in activations hinder the development of INT4 weight-activation quantization. Existing approaches separate outliers and normal values into two matrices or migrate outliers from activations to weights, suffering from high latency or accuracy degradation. Based on observing activations from large language models, outliers can be classified into channel-wise and spike outliers.
In this work, we propose Rotated Runtime Smooth (**RRS**), a plug-and-play activation smoother for quantization, consisting of Runtime Smooth and the Rotation operation. Runtime Smooth (**RS**) is introduced to eliminate **channel-wise outliers** by smoothing activations with channel-wise maximums during runtime. The Rotation operation can narrow the gap between **spike outliers** and normal values, alleviating the effect of victims caused by channel-wise smoothing.
The proposed method outperforms the state-of-the-art method in the LLaMA and Qwen families and improves WikiText-2 perplexity from 57.33 to 6.66 for INT4 inference.
abstract_zh:
大型语言模型在参数规模扩大后展现出可观的性能。然而，由于规模庞大，部署大型语言模型会产生极高的计算与内存移动开销。量化方法已被用于降低服务成本与延迟，但激活值中的异常值阻碍了INT4权重-激活量化的发展。现有方法要么将异常值与正常值拆分至两个矩阵，要么将异常值从激活值迁移到权重中，存在延迟过高或精度下降的问题。基于对大型语言模型激活值的观察，异常值可分为通道级异常值和尖峰异常值。
本研究提出旋转运行时平滑（RRS），这是一种用于量化场景的即插即用激活平滑器，由运行时平滑与旋转操作两部分构成。我们引入运行时平滑（RS），在运行期间通过通道最大值对激活值进行平滑处理，以消除通道级异常值。旋转操作能够缩小尖峰异常值与正常值之间的差距，缓解通道级平滑引发的受损值带来的负面影响。
所提方法在LLaMA与Qwen系列模型上的表现优于当前最优方法，将INT4推理的WikiText-2困惑度从57.33优化至6.66。
notes: 论文面向大语言模型推理量化，核心是通过 training-free activation smoother 改善 INT4 weight-activation quantization 的精度与可用性，属于模型压缩方向。

title: Compute or Load KV Cache? Why Not Both?
title_zh: 计算还是加载 KV 缓存？为什么不两者兼得？
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in large-scale online services, enabling sophisticated applications. However, the computational overhead of generating key-value (KV) caches in the prefill stage presents a major bottleneck, particularly for long-context inputs. Prefix caching mitigates this issue by storing KV caches for reuse, reducing redundant computation. Despite its advantages, prefix caching suffers from high latency due to the limited I/O bandwidth of storage devices, constraining inference efficiency. To address this challenge, we introduce Cake, a novel KV cache loading system that optimally utilizes both computational and I/O resources in parallel. Cake employs a bidirectional scheduling strategy that dynamically balances KV cache computation and loading, ensuring efficient resource utilization. Additionally, Cake incorporates an adaptive scheduling mechanism that seamlessly integrates with non-prefix caching requests, improving system throughput and adapting to fluctuating resource availabilty. Through extensive evaluations across various hardware configurations, datasets, and storage conditions, Cake achieves on average 2.6× reduction in Time to First Token (TTFT) compared to compute-only and I/O-only methods. Our findings highlight Cake as an effective and practical solution for optimizing long-context LLM inference, bridging the gap between computation and I/O efficiency in large-scale AI deployments.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）正越来越多地部署在大规模在线服务中，为各类复杂应用提供支撑。然而，预填充阶段生成键值（KV）缓存的计算开销是一项主要瓶颈，在长上下文输入场景下尤为突出。前缀缓存通过存储KV缓存以供复用、减少冗余计算，能够缓解该问题。尽管存在上述优势，前缀缓存因存储设备I/O带宽有限存在高延迟问题，制约了推理效率。为应对这一挑战，我们提出Cake，这是一种新型KV缓存加载系统，可并行最优利用计算与I/O资源。Cake采用双向调度策略，动态平衡KV缓存的计算与加载过程，确保资源高效利用。此外，Cake集成了自适应调度机制，可与非前缀缓存请求无缝集成，提升系统吞吐量并适配波动的资源可用性。我们在不同硬件配置、数据集和存储条件下开展了大量评估，结果表明，与仅采用计算或仅采用I/O的方法相比，Cake的首令牌生成时间（TTFT）平均降低2.6倍。研究结果表明，Cake是优化长上下文大语言模型推理的有效实用方案，能够弥合大规模AI部署中计算效率与I/O效率之间的差距。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Prompt and Parameter Co-Optimization for Large Language Models
title_zh: 大语言模型的提示与参数协同优化
abstract:
Prompt optimization and fine-tuning are two major approaches to improve the performance of Large Language Models (LLMs).
They enhance the capabilities of LLMs from complementary perspectives: the former through explicit natural language, and the latter through implicit parameter updates. 
However, prior work has typically studied them in isolation, leaving their synergistic potential largely underexplored. To bridge this gap, in this paper, we introduce MetaTuner, a novel framework that jointly integrates prompt optimization and fine-tuning for LLM training.
Specifically, we introduce two neural networks to generate prompts and parameters, respectively, while allowing them to share a common bottom encoding layer to enable knowledge sharing.
By the guidance of the final supervised signals, our framework is optimized to discover the optimal combinations between the prompts and parameters.
Given that prompt learning involves discrete optimization while fine-tuning operates in a continuous parameter space, we design a supervised regularization loss to train our framework effectively.
Extensive experiments across diverse benchmarks show that our method consistently outperforms the baselines.  To benefit the research community, we have released our project at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/metatuner.
abstract_zh: 本文提出 MetaTuner 框架，结合提示优化和微调两种方法训练大模型。具体地，引入两套神经网络分别负责生成提示和参数，并让它们共用底层编码层以促进知识共享；在监督信号指导下，框架优化提示与参数组合以挖掘最优配对。鉴于提示学习属于离散优化而微调在连续参数空间中进行，作者设计了有监督的正则化损失以保障协同训练效果。大规模基准测试表明，MetaTuner 在多个任务上均优于基线，并已经将项目开源以供社区使用。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Libra: Effective yet Efficient Load Balancing for Large-scale MoE Inference
title_zh: Libra：面向大规模混合专家（MoE）推理的兼具有效性与高效性的负载均衡机制
abstract: Distributed inference of large-scale Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models faces a critical challenge: expert load imbalance. Numerous system-level approaches have been proposed for load balancing, but they either fail to achieve a satisfactory level of balance or introduce new bottlenecks due to the overhead of the load balancing mechanism itself. To this end, we propose Libra, a system that achieves near-optimal load balancing with minimal overhead. Libra adopts sophisticated mechanisms that accurately predict future expert activations and, based on these predictions, systematically perform load balancing. At the same time, it effectively hides the associated overhead by reconstructing the execution flow so that these costs are overlapped with MoE computation. Evaluations with two large-scale state-of-the-art MoE models on 8 H200 GPUs demonstrate that Libra improves throughput by up to 19.2\%.
abstract_zh: 大规模混合专家（MoE）模型的分布式推理面临一项关键挑战：专家负载失衡。目前已有大量系统层面的负载均衡方法被提出，但这些方法要么无法达到令人满意的均衡水平，要么会因负载均衡机制本身的开销引入新的瓶颈。为此，我们提出Libra，这一系统能够以极小开销实现接近最优的负载均衡。Libra采用精心设计的机制准确预测未来的专家激活情况，并基于这些预测系统性地执行负载均衡。同时，它通过重构执行流，让相关开销与MoE计算重叠，从而有效隐藏了负载均衡对应的成本。在8张H200 GPU上对两个大规模当前最先进的MoE模型开展的评估表明，Libra最多可将吞吐量提升19.2%。
notes: 调度专项得分=7；命中组=llm_serving；augment_batch=20260402-scheduling；augment_score=7

title: Variational Deep Learning via Implicit Regularization
title_zh: 通过隐式正则化的变分深度学习
abstract: Modern deep learning models generalize remarkably well in-distribution, despite being overparametrized and trained with little to no explicit regularization. Instead, current theory credits implicit regularization imposed by the choice of architecture, hyperparameters and optimization procedure. However, deep neural networks can be surprisingly non-robust, resulting in overconfident predictions and poor out-of-distribution generalization. Bayesian deep learning addresses this via model averaging, but typically requires significant computational resources as well as carefully elicited priors to avoid overriding the benefits of implicit regularization. Instead, in this work, we propose to regularize variational neural networks solely by relying on the implicit bias of (stochastic) gradient descent. We theoretically characterize this inductive bias in overparametrized linear models as generalized variational inference and demonstrate the importance of the choice of parametrization. Empirically, our approach demonstrates strong in- and out-of-distribution performance without additional hyperparameter tuning and with minimal computational overhead.
abstract_zh: 尽管现代深度学习模型是过参数化的，并且在训练时几乎没有使用显式正则化，但它们在分布内仍表现出极好的泛化能力。相反，当前理论将这种能力归因于由模型架构、超参数和优化过程的选择所施加的隐式正则化。然而，深度神经网络可能出人意料地缺乏鲁棒性，从而导致过度自信的预测以及较差的分布外泛化。贝叶斯深度学习通过模型平均来应对这一问题，但通常需要大量计算资源，并且还需要精心设定先验，以避免抵消隐式正则化带来的好处。相反，在这项工作中，我们提出仅依赖于（随机）梯度下降的隐式偏置来对变分神经网络进行正则化。我们从理论上将这种归纳偏置在过参数化线性模型中刻画为广义变分推断，并展示了参数化选择的重要性。在经验上，我们的方法在无需额外超参数调优且仅有极小计算开销的情况下，展现出强大的分布内和分布外性能。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Relaxed Recursive Transformers: Effective Parameter Sharing with Layer-wise LoRA
title_zh: 松弛递归变换器：借助逐层 LoRA 实现高效参数共享
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are expensive to deploy. Parameter sharing offers a possible path towards reducing their size and cost, but its effectiveness in modern LLMs remains fairly limited. In this work, we revisit "layer tying" as form of parameter sharing in Transformers, and introduce novel methods for converting existing LLMs into smaller "Recursive Transformers" that share parameters across layers, with minimal loss of performance. Here, our Recursive Transformers are efficiently initialized from standard pretrained Transformers, but only use a single block of unique layers that is then repeated multiple times in a loop. We further improve  performance by introducing Relaxed Recursive Transformers that add flexibility to the layer tying constraint via depth-wise low-rank adaptation (LoRA) modules, yet still preserve the compactness of the overall model. We show that our recursive models (e.g., recursive Gemma 1B) outperform both similar-sized vanilla pretrained models (such as TinyLlama 1.1B and Pythia 1B) and knowledge distillation baselines---and can even recover most of the performance of the original "full-size" model (e.g., Gemma 2B with no shared parameters). Finally, we propose Continuous Depth-wise Batching, a promising new inference paradigm enabled by the Recursive Transformer when paired with early exiting. In a theoretical analysis, we show  that this has the potential to lead to significant (2-3$\times$) gains in inference throughput.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）部署成本高昂。参数共享是减小其规模、降低成本的可行路径，但在现代大语言模型中的有效性仍十分有限。本研究重新审视作为Transformer参数共享形式的“层绑定”，并提出新方法将现有大语言模型转换为更小的“递归Transformer”，这类模型在层间共享参数，性能损失极小。本文提出的递归Transformer可从标准预训练Transformer高效初始化，仅使用单组唯一的层块，再将该层块循环重复多次。我们进一步引入松弛递归Transformer以提升性能，该模型通过深度方向低秩适配（LoRA）模块为层绑定约束增加灵活性，同时仍能保持整体模型的紧凑性。实验表明，我们的递归模型（例如递归Gemma 1B）既优于同等规模的普通预训练模型（如TinyLlama 1.1B和Pythia 1B）以及知识蒸馏基线，甚至可以恢复原“全尺寸”模型（如无参数共享的Gemma 2B）的大部分性能。最后，我们提出连续深度批处理，这是递归Transformer结合提前退出机制时可实现的一种极具潜力的新推理范式。理论分析表明，该范式有望带来推理吞吐量的显著提升（可达2-3倍）。
notes: ['核心贡献是通过层共享与depth-wise LoRA构造更紧凑的Recursive Transformer，明显属于模型压缩。', 'Continuous Depth-wise Batching配合early exiting直接面向推理吞吐优化，可归入系统与调度优化。', '摘要未涉及算子/内核级实现细节，也不属于解码策略优化。']

title: Temporal Generalization: A Reality Check
title_zh: 时间泛化：现实检验
abstract: Machine learning (ML) models often struggle to maintain performance under distribution shifts, leading to inaccurate predictions on unseen future data. In this work, we investigate whether and under what conditions models can achieve such a generalization when relying solely on past data. We explore two primary approaches: convex combinations of past model parameters (parameter interpolation) and explicit extrapolation beyond the convex hull of past parameters (parameter extrapolation). We benchmark several methods within these categories on a diverse set of temporal tasks, including language modeling, news summarization, news tag prediction, academic paper categorization, satellite image-based land use classification over time, and historical yearbook photo gender prediction. Our empirical findings show that none of the evaluated methods consistently outperforms the simple baseline of using the latest available model parameters in all scenarios. In the absence of access to future data or robust assumptions about the underlying data-generating process, these results underscore the inherent difficulties of generalizing and extrapolating to future data and warrant caution when evaluating claims of such generalization.
abstract_zh: 机器学习（ML）模型在分布发生偏移时往往难以维持性能，从而在未见过的未来数据上产生不准确的预测。在这项工作中，我们研究了模型是否能够以及在何种条件下仅依赖过去数据实现这种泛化。我们探索了两种主要方法：过去模型参数的凸组合（参数插值）以及超出过去参数凸包的显式外推（参数外推）。我们在一组多样化的时间任务上对这些类别中的若干方法进行了基准评测，这些任务包括语言建模、新闻摘要、新闻标签预测、学术论文分类、基于卫星图像的土地利用时序分类，以及历史年鉴照片中的性别预测。我们的实证结果表明，在所有场景中，被评估的方法都无法持续优于使用最新可用模型参数这一简单基线。在无法获取未来数据或对底层数据生成过程缺乏稳健假设的情况下，这些结果凸显了对未来数据进行泛化和外推的内在困难，并提示在评估此类泛化主张时应保持谨慎。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Interactive Agents to Overcome Underspecificity in Software Engineering
title_zh: 交互式代理以克服软件工程中的不明确性
abstract: AI agents are increasingly being deployed to automate tasks, often based on underspecified user instructions. Making unwarranted assumptions to compensate for the missing information and failing to ask clarifying questions can lead to suboptimal outcomes, safety risks due to tool misuse, and wasted computational resources. In this work, we study the ability of LLM agents to handle underspecified instructions in interactive code generation settings by evaluating proprietary and open-weight models on their performance across three key steps: (a) detecting underspecificity, (b) asking targeted clarification questions, and (c) leveraging the interaction to improve performance in underspecified scenarios. Our findings reveal that models struggle to distinguish between well-specified and underspecified instructions. However, when models interact for underspecified inputs, they effectively obtain vital information from the user leading to significant improvements in performance, up to 74\% over the non-interactive settings, underscoring the value of effective interaction. Our study highlights critical gaps in how current state-of-the-art models handle missing information in complex software engineering tasks and structures the evaluation into distinct steps to enable targeted improvements.
abstract_zh: AI 智能体正越来越多地被部署用于自动化任务，而这些任务往往基于用户不够明确的指令。为了弥补缺失信息而做出没有根据的假设，以及未能提出澄清性问题，都会导致次优结果、因工具误用而带来的安全风险，以及计算资源的浪费。在这项工作中，我们通过评估专有模型和开放权重模型在三个关键步骤上的表现，研究了大语言模型智能体在交互式代码生成场景中处理不够明确指令的能力，这三个步骤包括：（a）检测指令表述不充分，（b）提出有针对性的澄清问题，以及（c）利用交互来提升在表述不充分场景中的表现。我们的研究结果表明，模型难以区分表述充分的指令和表述不充分的指令。然而，当模型针对表述不充分的输入进行交互时，它们能够有效地从用户处获取关键信息，从而显著提升性能，相比非交互式设置最高可提升 74%，这凸显了有效交互的价值。我们的研究揭示了当前最先进模型在复杂软件工程任务中处理缺失信息方式上的关键缺口，并将评估划分为不同步骤，以便实现有针对性的改进。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Learning Relative Gene Expression Trends from Pathology Images in Spatial Transcriptomics
title_zh: 从空间转录组中的病理图像学习相对基因表达趋势
abstract:
Gene expression estimation from pathology images has the potential to reduce the RNA sequencing cost.
Point-wise loss functions have been widely used to minimize the discrepancy between predicted and absolute gene expression values. 
However, due to the complexity of the sequencing techniques and intrinsic variability across cells, the observed gene expression contains stochastic noise and batch effects, and estimating the absolute expression values accurately remains a significant challenge.
To mitigate this, we propose a novel objective of learning relative expression patterns rather than absolute levels.
We assume that the relative expression levels of genes exhibit consistent patterns across independent experiments, even when absolute expression values are affected by batch effects and stochastic noise in tissue samples.
Based on the assumption, we model the relation and propose a novel loss function called STRank that is robust to noise and batch effects.
Experiments using synthetic datasets and real datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.
The code is available at https://github.com/naivete5656/STRank.
abstract_zh: 我们提出从病理图像中学习相对基因表达模式，旨在减轻测序噪声和批次影响，而非追求难以精确估计的绝对表达值。传统点对点的损失函数试图最小化预测与实际表达的差距，但由于测序工艺复杂、细胞内在差异以及样本中的随机噪声与批次效应，绝对表达的准确估算仍面临巨大挑战。我们假设即便绝对水平受噪声干扰，各实验间的基因相对表达趋势仍保持一致，由此设计出一个新的模型结构与鲁棒损失 STRank，通过显式建模基因间关系来捕捉相对表达的稳定模式，从而抵抗随机噪声与批次漂移。合成数据与真实数据实验均验证了该方法在保留相对趋势方面的有效性。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Diagnosing and Improving Diffusion Models by Estimating Optimal Loss Value
title_zh: 通过估计最优损失值诊断并提升扩散模型
abstract: Diffusion models have achieved remarkable success in generative modeling. Despite more stable training, the loss of diffusion models is not indicative of absolute data-fitting quality, since its optimal value is typically not zero but unknown, leading to the confusion between large optimal loss and insufficient model capacity. In this work, we advocate the need to estimate the optimal loss value for diagnosing and improving diffusion models. We first derive the optimal loss in closed form under a unified formulation of diffusion models, and develop effective estimators for it, including a stochastic variant scalable to large datasets with proper control of variance and bias. With this tool, we unlock the inherent metric for diagnosing training quality of representative diffusion model variants, and develop a more performant training schedule based on the optimal loss. Moreover, using models with 120M to 1.5B parameters, we find that the power law is better demonstrated after subtracting the optimal loss from the actual training loss, suggesting a more principled setting for investigating the scaling law for diffusion models.
abstract_zh: 扩散模型在生成建模方面取得了显著成功。尽管训练更加稳定，扩散模型的损失并不能表征绝对的数据拟合质量，因为其最优值通常不是零且未知，这会导致难以区分是最优损失本身较大，还是模型容量不足。在这项工作中，我们提出有必要估计最优损失值，以用于诊断和改进扩散模型。我们首先在统一的扩散模型表述下推导出闭式形式的最优损失，并为其开发了有效的估计器，包括一种随机变体；该变体能够在适当控制方差和偏差的情况下扩展到大型数据集。借助这一工具，我们揭示了用于诊断代表性扩散模型变体训练质量的内在度量，并基于最优损失开发出一种性能更优的训练日程。此外，基于参数规模从1.2亿到15亿的模型，我们发现，在从实际训练损失中减去最优损失之后，幂律关系得到了更好的体现，这表明为研究扩散模型的缩放定律提供了一种更具原理性的设定。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: TROLL: Trust Regions Improve Reinforcement Learning for Large Language Models
title_zh: TROLL：信赖域提升大语言模型强化学习
abstract:
Reinforcement Learning (RL) with PPO-like clip objectives has become the standard choice for reward-based fine-tuning of large language models (LLMs). 
Although recent work has explored improved estimators of advantages and normalization, the clipping mechanism itself has remained untouched.
Originally introduced as a proxy for principled KL-based trust regions, clipping is a crude approximation that often causes unstable updates and suboptimal performance. 
We replace the clip objective with a novel discrete differentiable trust region projection, which provides principled token-level KL constraints.
The projection operates on a sparse subset of the model’s most important token logits to balance computational cost and projection effectiveness. 
Our approach, Trust Region Optimization for Large Language Models (TROLL), serves as a direct replacement for PPO-like clipping during training and does not alter the model’s inference behavior.
Across mathematical reasoning and code generation tasks, model families, as well as advantage-estimation methods, TROLL consistently outperforms PPO-like clipping in terms of training speed, stability, and final success rates.
abstract_zh: 我们用类似PPO的剪辑目标进行强化学习（RL）以针对奖励精调大语言模型（LLM）已成主流。尽管近期研究探索了改进的优势估计器和归一化手段，但剪辑机制本身仍沿用原样。剪辑最初作为一个KL约束型信赖域的近似代理，实为粗糙近似，常导致更新不稳定且性能欠佳。我们用一种新颖的离散可微信赖域投影替换剪辑目标，从而提供原则性的逐令牌KL约束。这种投影仅作用于模型最关键令牌logits的稀疏子集，以在计算开销与投影效果之间找到平衡。我们提出的“大语言模型信赖域优化”（TROLL）可直接替代训练中的PPO式剪辑，且不改变推理行为。在数学推理与代码生成任务、不同模型族以及各种优势估计方法上，TROLL在训练速度、稳定性和最终成功率方面均持续优于PPO式剪辑。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: AnyBCQ: Hardware Efficient Flexible Binary-Coded Quantization for Multi-Precision LLMs
title_zh: AnyBCQ：面向多精度大语言模型的硬件高效灵活二进制编码量化
abstract:
The deployment of large language models (LLMs) is increasingly constrained by memory and latency bottlenecks, motivating the need for quantization techniques that flexibly balance accuracy and efficiency. 
Recent work has introduced multi-precision models, which enable inference at multiple precisions within a single model depending on runtime constraints.
To support such flexibility, quantized weights are often stored as bit-planes, where hardware efficiency improves when the compute operates directly at the bit-plane level and activates only the precision required by each request.
In this work, we present AnyBCQ, a hardware-friendly multi-precision extension of Binary-Coded Quantization (BCQ) that supports direct bit-plane operations.
By representing weights as binary bit-planes with corresponding scale factors, AnyBCQ enables bit-plane–level computation and maps naturally to accelerator-friendly, bit-parallel arithmetic.
Our progressive precision expansion mechanism incrementally refines scaling factors while reusing previously assigned binary codes, yielding monotonic improvements in accuracy as additional bits are enabled. 
We further co-design a specialized kernel that exploits the BCQ structure to support dynamic per-request precision selection with negligible overhead. 
Experiments on recent LLMs demonstrate that AnyBCQ significantly narrows the accuracy drop in the low-bit regime (e.g. 2-bit), remains competitive at higher precision, and achieves throughput gains of up to $3.0\times$ over half precision and $1.2\times$ over state-of-the-art multi-precision methods. 
By aligning algorithmic flexibility with hardware efficiency, AnyBCQ provides a practical foundation for multi-precision LLM deployment across diverse service-level objectives.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型的部署正日益受到内存和延迟瓶颈的限制，这推动了对能够灵活平衡精度与效率的量化技术的需求。最新研究提出了多精度模型，这类模型可根据运行时约束在单个模型内以多种精度完成推理。为支撑这种灵活性，量化后的权重通常以位平面形式存储，当计算直接在位平面层面执行且仅激活每个请求所需的精度时，硬件效率会得到提升。本研究提出AnyBCQ，这是二进制编码量化（BCQ）的硬件友好型多精度扩展方案，支持直接位平面操作。AnyBCQ将权重表示为带有对应缩放因子的二进制位平面，从而支持位平面级计算，并且可自然适配适合加速器的位并行算术。我们提出的渐进式精度扩展机制在复用已分配的二进制代码的同时逐步优化缩放因子，可在启用更多比特位时实现精度的单调提升。我们进一步协同设计了专用内核，该内核利用BCQ结构支持动态的逐请求精度选择，且开销可忽略不计。在最新大语言模型上开展的实验表明，AnyBCQ大幅降低了低比特区间（如2比特）的精度损失，在更高精度下性能仍具备竞争力，并且相较于半精度实现了最高3.0倍的吞吐量提升，相较于最先进的多精度方法实现了最高1.2倍的吞吐量提升。通过将算法灵活性与硬件效率相匹配，AnyBCQ为面向不同服务级别目标的多精度大语言模型部署提供了实用基础。
notes: 论文核心是面向LLM部署的量化方法，目标直指压缩模型表示并改善内存/时延效率，属于模型压缩方向。

title: Semi-infinite Nonconvex Constrained Min-Max Optimization
title_zh: 半无限非凸约束极大极小优化
abstract: Semi-Infinite Programming (SIP) has emerged as a powerful framework for modeling problems with infinite constraints, however, its theoretical development in the context of nonconvex and large-scale optimization remains limited. In this paper, we investigate a class of nonconvex min-max optimization problems with nonconvex infinite constraints, motivated by applications such as adversarial robustness and safety-constrained learning. We propose a novel inexact dynamic barrier primal-dual algorithm and establish its convergence properties. Specifically, under the assumption that the squared infeasibility residual function satisfies the Lojasiewicz inequality with exponent $\theta \in (0,1)$, we prove that the proposed method achieves $\mathcal{O}(\epsilon^{-3})$, $\mathcal{O}(\epsilon^{-6\theta})$, and $\mathcal{O}(\epsilon^{-3\theta/(1-\theta)})$ iteration complexities to achieve an $\epsilon$-approximate stationarity, infeasibility, and complementarity slackness, respectively. Numerical experiments on robust multitask learning with task priority further illustrate the practical effectiveness of the algorithm.
abstract_zh: 半无限规划（SIP）已成为对具有无限约束的问题进行建模的强大框架，然而其在非凸和大规模优化背景下的理论发展仍然有限。本文研究了一类带有非凸无限约束的非凸极小极大优化问题，其动机来自对抗鲁棒性和安全约束学习等应用。我们提出了一种新的非精确动态障碍原始-对偶算法，并建立了其收敛性质。具体而言，在平方不可行残差函数满足指数为$\theta \in (0,1)$的Lojasiewicz不等式的假设下，我们证明所提出的方法分别以$\mathcal{O}(\epsilon^{-3})$、$\mathcal{O}(\epsilon^{-6\theta})$和$\mathcal{O}(\epsilon^{-3\theta/(1-\theta)})$的迭代复杂度达到$\epsilon$-近似驻点性、不可行性和互补松弛性。关于带任务优先级的鲁棒多任务学习的数值实验进一步说明了该算法在实践中的有效性。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: MRMR: A Realistic and Expert-Level Multidisciplinary Benchmark for Reasoning-Intensive Multimodal Retrieval
title_zh: MRMR：一个面向推理密集型多模态检索的现实、专家级多学科基准
abstract: We introduce MRMR, the first expert-level multidisciplinary multimodal retrieval benchmark requiring intensive reasoning. MRMR contains 1,435 queries spanning 23 domains, with positive documents carefully verified by human experts. Compared to prior benchmarks, MRMR introduces three key advancements. First, it challenges retrieval systems across diverse areas of expertise, enabling fine-grained model comparison across domains. Second, queries are reasoning-intensive, with images requiring deeper interpretation such as diagnosing microscopic slides. We further introduce Contradiction Retrieval, a novel task requiring models to identify conflicting concepts. Finally, queries and documents are constructed as image–text interleaved sequences. Unlike earlier benchmarks restricted to single images or unimodal documents, MRMR offers a realistic setting with multi-image queries and mixed-modality corpus documents. We conduct an extensive evaluation of 4 categories of multimodal retrieval systems and 14 frontier models on MRMR. The text embedding model Qwen3-Embedding with LLM-generated image captions achieves the highest performance, highlighting substantial room for improving multimodal retrieval models. Although latest multimodal models such as Ops-MM-Embedding perform competitively on expert-domain queries, they fall short on reasoning-intensive tasks. We believe that MRMR paves the way for advancing multimodal retrieval in more realistic and challenging scenarios.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了 MRMR，这是首个需要高强度推理的专家级多学科多模态检索基准。MRMR 包含 1435 个查询，涵盖 23 个领域，正样本文档均经过人工专家仔细核验。相比以往基准，MRMR 带来了三项关键进展。首先，它在多样化的专业领域中挑战检索系统，从而能够对模型在不同领域中的表现进行细粒度比较。其次，查询具有较强的推理密集性，其中图像需要更深层次的解读，例如对显微切片进行诊断。我们还进一步引入了“矛盾检索”这一新任务，要求模型识别相互冲突的概念。最后，查询和文档都被构造成图文交错的序列。不同于早期仅限于单张图像或单模态文档的基准，MRMR 提供了一个更贴近现实的设定，其中查询包含多张图像，语料库文档则为混合模态。我们在 MRMR 上对 4 类多模态检索系统和 14 个前沿模型进行了广泛评测。文本嵌入模型 Qwen3-Embedding 配合由大语言模型生成的图像描述取得了最高性能，这表明多模态检索模型仍有很大的提升空间。尽管最新的多模态模型如 Ops-MM-Embedding 在专家领域查询上表现出竞争力，但在推理密集型任务上仍显不足。我们相信，MRMR 将为在更真实、更具挑战性的场景中推进多模态检索铺平道路。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: ConvRec-R1: Training LLM-based Conversational Recommender Systems with Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: ConvRec-R1：使用强化学习训练基于大语言模型的对话推荐系统
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are reshaping the recommender system paradigm by enabling users to express preferences and receive recommendations through conversations. Yet, aligning LLMs to the recommendation task remains challenging: pretrained LLMs often generate out-of-catalog items, violate required output formats, and their ranking quality degrades sharply toward the end of the generated list. To this end, we propose ConvRec-R1, a two-stage framework for end-to-end training of LLM-based conversational recommender systems. In Stage 1, we construct a behavioral-cloning dataset with a Remap-Reflect-Adjust pipeline, which produces high-quality, catalog-grounded demonstrations from powerful blackbox LLMs to warm-start the RL training. In Stage 2, we propose Rank-GRPO, a principled extension of group relative policy optimization (GRPO) tailored to tasks with rank-style outputs. Rank-GRPO treats each rank in the recommendation list as the unit instead of token (too fine-grained) or sequence (too coarse), redefining rewards to remove non-causal credit assignment and introducing a rank-level importance ratio based on the geometric mean of rank-wise token probabilities to stabilize policy updates. Experiments on the public Reddit-v2 dataset show that ConvRec-R1 converges faster and achieves higher Recall and NDCG than GRPO-style baselines. Code and datasets are released at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/ConvRec-R1-5615.
