abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）正在通过使用户能够通过对话表达偏好并接收推荐来重塑推荐系统范式。然而，使 LLM 与推荐任务对齐仍然具有挑战性：预训练 LLM 常常会生成目录外物品，违反要求的输出格式，并且其排序质量在生成列表的末尾会急剧下降。为此，我们提出了 ConvRec-R1，这是一个用于端到端训练基于 LLM 的对话式推荐系统的两阶段框架。在阶段 1 中，我们通过 Remap-Reflect-Adjust 流水线构建行为克隆数据集，该流水线利用强大的黑盒 LLM 生成高质量、以目录为依据的示范数据，用于对强化学习训练进行热启动。在阶段 2 中，我们提出 Rank-GRPO，这是针对具有排序式输出任务而定制的组相对策略优化（GRPO）的一个原则性扩展。Rank-GRPO 将推荐列表中的每个排序位置作为单位，而不是 token（过于细粒度）或序列（过于粗粒度），通过重新定义奖励来消除非因果的信用分配，并基于各排序位置 token 概率的几何平均数引入排序级重要性比率，以稳定策略更新。在公开的 Reddit-v2 数据集上的实验表明，ConvRec-R1 比 GRPO 风格的基线方法收敛更快，并取得了更高的 Recall 和 NDCG。代码和数据集已发布于 https://anonymous.4open.science/r/ConvRec-R1-5615。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Neural MJD: Neural Non-Stationary Merton Jump Diffusion for Time Series Prediction
title_zh: Neural MJD 用于时间序列预测的神经非平稳 Merton 跳跃扩散
abstract: While deep learning methods have achieved strong performance in time series prediction, their black-box nature and inability to explicitly model underlying stochastic processes often limit their robustness handling non-stationary data, especially in the presence of abrupt changes. In this work, we introduce Neural MJD, a neural network based non-stationary Merton jump diffusion (MJD) model. Our model explicitly formulates forecasting as a stochastic differential equation (SDE) simulation problem, combining a time-inhomogeneous Itô diffusion to capture non-stationary stochastic dynamics with a time-inhomogeneous compound Poisson process to model abrupt jumps. To enable tractable learning, we introduce a likelihood truncation mechanism that caps the number of jumps within small time intervals and provide a theoretical error bound for this approximation. Additionally, we propose an Euler-Maruyama with restart solver, which achieves a provably lower error bound in estimating expected states and reduced variance compared to the standard solver. Experiments on both synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate that Neural MJD consistently outperforms state-of-the-art deep learning and statistical learning methods. Our code is available at https://github.com/DSL-Lab/neural-MJD.
abstract_zh: 尽管深度学习方法在时间序列预测中取得了较强的性能，但其黑箱性质以及无法显式建模潜在随机过程，常常限制了其在处理非平稳数据时的鲁棒性，尤其是在存在突发变化的情况下。在这项工作中，我们提出了 Neural MJD，一种基于神经网络的非平稳 Merton 跳跃扩散（MJD）模型。我们的模型将预测显式表述为一个随机微分方程（SDE）模拟问题，结合时间非齐次 Itô 扩散来捕捉非平稳随机动态，并结合时间非齐次复合泊松过程来建模突发跳跃。为了实现可处理的学习，我们引入了一种似然截断机制，将小时间区间内的跳跃次数限制在一个上限之内，并为这种近似给出了理论误差界。此外，我们提出了一种带重启的 Euler-Maruyama 求解器，与标准求解器相比，它在估计期望状态时能够达到可证明更低的误差界，并降低方差。在合成数据集和真实世界数据集上的实验表明，Neural MJD 始终优于最先进的深度学习和统计学习方法。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/DSL-Lab/neural-MJD 获取。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Training Robust Graph Neural Networks by Modeling Noise Dependencies
title_zh: 通过建模噪声依赖训练稳健的图神经网络
abstract: In real-world applications, node features in graphs often contain noise from various sources, leading to significant performance degradation in GNNs. Although several methods have been developed to enhance robustness, they rely on the unrealistic assumption that noise in node features is independent of the graph structure and node labels, thereby limiting their applicability. To this end, we introduce a more realistic noise scenario, dependency-aware noise on graphs (DANG), where noise in node features create a chain of noise dependencies that propagates to the graph structure and node labels. We propose a novel robust GNN, DA-GNN, which captures the causal relationships among variables in the data generating process (DGP) of DANG using variational inference. In addition, we present new benchmark datasets that simulate DANG in real-world applications, enabling more practical research on robust GNNs. Extensive experiments demonstrate that DA-GNN consistently outperforms existing baselines across various noise scenarios, including both DANG and conventional noise models commonly considered in this field.
abstract_zh: 在现实世界应用中，图中的节点特征往往包含来自各种来源的噪声，这会导致图神经网络（GNN）的性能显著下降。尽管已经提出了若干增强鲁棒性的方法，但它们依赖于一个不切实际的假设，即节点特征中的噪声独立于图结构和节点标签，因此限制了其适用性。为此，我们引入了一种更现实的噪声场景，即图上的依赖感知噪声（DANG），其中节点特征中的噪声会形成一条噪声依赖链，并传播到图结构和节点标签。我们提出了一种新颖的鲁棒图神经网络 DA-GNN，利用变分推断来捕捉 DANG 的数据生成过程（DGP）中各变量之间的因果关系。此外，我们还提出了新的基准数据集，用于模拟现实应用中的 DANG，从而使针对鲁棒 GNN 的研究更具实践意义。大量实验表明，DA-GNN 在各种噪声场景下都持续优于现有基线方法，包括 DANG 以及该领域中通常考虑的传统噪声模型。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Track3R: Joint Point Map and Trajectory Prior for Spatiotemporal 3D Understanding
title_zh: Track3R：用于时空三维理解的联合点图与轨迹先验
abstract:
Understanding the 3D world from 2D monocular videos is a crucial ability for AI.
Recently, to tackle this underdetermined task, end-to-end 3D geometry priors have been sought after, such as pre-trained point map models at scale.
These models enable robust 3D understanding from casually taken videos, providing accurate object shapes disentangled from uncertain camera parameters.
However, they still struggle when affected by object deformation and dynamics, failing to establish consistent correspondence over the frames.
Furthermore, their architectures are typically limited to pairwise frame processing, which is insufficient for capturing complex motion dynamics over extended sequences.
To address these limitations, we introduce Track3R, a novel framework that integrates a new architecture and task to jointly predict point map and motion trajectories across multiple frames from video input.
Specifically, our key idea is modeling two disentangled trajectories for each point: one representing object motion and the other camera poses.
This design not only can enable understanding of the 3D object dynamics, but also facilitates the learning of more robust priors for 3D shapes in dynamic scenes.
In our experiments, Track3R demonstrates significant improvements in a joint point mapping and 3D motion estimation task for dynamic scenes, such as 25.8% improvements in the motion estimation, and 15.7% in the point mapping accuracy.
abstract_zh: Track3R 提出一种全新框架，从视频输入中跨多帧联合预测点图与运动轨迹，通过为每个点建模两条可分离轨迹（物体运动与相机位姿）实现对三维对象动态的理解，并增强动态场景下三维形状先验的鲁棒性。该框架以新的体系结构与任务设计，克服了现有大规模预训练点图模型在处理变形、动态对象时难以建立跨帧一致对应、以及仅限两帧处理无法捕捉复杂运动动态的局限。Track3R 在动态场景的联合点绘制与三维运动估计任务上表现出显著提升：运动估计准确率提高 25.8%，点映射准确率提升 15.7%。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Rethinking the Role of Verbatim Memorization in LLM Privacy
title_zh: 重新审视逐字记忆在大模型隐私中的作用
abstract:
Conventional wisdom in machine learning privacy research states that memorization 
directly implies a loss of privacy. In contrast, a well-generalized model only remembers distributional patterns and preserves privacy of its training data.  In this work, we show that this relationship is much more complex for LLMs trained for chat,
and depends heavily on how knowledge is encoded and manipulated. To this end, we fine-tune language models on synthetically generated biographical information including PIIs, and try to extract them in different ways after instruction fine-tuning.  We find counter to conventional wisdom that better verbatim memorization does not necessarily increase data leakage via chat. We also find that it is easier to extract information via chat from an LLM that is better able to manipulate and process knowledge even if it is smaller, and that not all attributes are equally extractable. This suggests that the relationship between privacy, memorization and language understanding of LLMs is very intricate, and that examining memorization in isolation can lead to misleading conclusions.
abstract_zh: 重新思考逐字记忆在大型语言模型隐私中的作用：传统机器学习隐私研究认为记忆必然导致隐私泄露，而泛化良好的模型仅保留分布式模式，从而保护训练数据隐私。但我们发现，对于用于聊天的大型语言模型而言，这种关系更为复杂，极大地依赖于知识如何被编码和操作。为此，我们在包含PII的合成传记信息上进行微调，并在指令微调后尝试不同方式提取这些信息。结果与传统观点相反：模型更强的逐字记忆能力并不必然导致通过聊天途径的数据泄露增加；反而，虽然模型更小，但如果能更好地操作和处理知识，通过聊天窗口提取信息更加容易。同时我们还发现并非所有属性都同样容易被抽取。这表明大型语言模型的隐私、记忆以及语言理解之间存在极为复杂的关系，仅孤立地考察记忆可能会得出误导性的结论。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Towards a Theoretical Understanding of In-context Learning: Stability and Non-I.I.D Generalisation
title_zh: 迈向对上下文学习的理论理解：稳定性与非独立同分布泛化
abstract: In-context learning (ICL) has demonstrated significant performance improvements in transformer-based large models. This study identifies two key factors influencing ICL generalisation under complex non-i.i.d. scenario: algorithmic stability and distributional discrepancy. First, we establish a stability bound for transformer-based models trained with mini-batch gradient descent, revealing how specific optimization configurations interact with the smoothness of the loss landscape to ensure the stability of non-linear Transformers. Next, we introduce a distribution-level discrepancy measure that highlights the importance of aligning the ICL prompt distribution with the training data distribution to achieve effective generalisation. Building on these insights, we derive a generalisation error bound for ICL with asymptotic convergence guarantees, which further reveals that token-wise prediction errors accumulate over time and even lead to generalisation collapse if the prediction length is not properly constrained. Finally, empirical evaluations are provided to validate our theoretical findings.
abstract_zh: 上下文学习（ICL）在基于 Transformer 的大模型中已展现出显著的性能提升。本研究识别出在复杂非独立同分布场景下影响 ICL 泛化的两个关键因素：算法稳定性和分布差异。首先，我们为采用小批量梯度下降训练的基于 Transformer 的模型建立了稳定性界，揭示了特定优化配置如何与损失景观的平滑性相互作用，以保证非线性 Transformer 的稳定性。接着，我们引入了一种分布层面的差异度量，强调了要实现有效泛化，使 ICL 提示分布与训练数据分布对齐的重要性。基于这些见解，我们推导出了具有渐近收敛保证的 ICL 泛化误差界，并进一步揭示逐 token 的预测误差会随时间累积，若预测长度未得到适当约束，甚至会导致泛化崩溃。最后，我们提供了实证评估来验证我们的理论发现。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: PolicyFlow: Policy Optimization with Continuous Normalizing Flow in Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: PolicyFlow：强化学习中使用连续归一化流进行策略优化
abstract: Among on-policy reinforcement learning algorithms, Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) demonstrates is widely favored for its simplicity, numerical stability, and strong empirical performance. Standard PPO relies on surrogate objectives defined via importance ratios, which require evaluating policy likelihood that is typically straightforward when the policy is modeled as a Gaussian distribution. However, extending PPO to more expressive, high-capacity policy models such as continuous normalizing flows (CNFs), also known as flow-matching models, is challenging because likelihood evaluation along the full flow trajectory is computationally expensive and often numerically unstable. To resolve this issue, we propose PolicyFlow, a novel on-policy CNF-based reinforcement learning algorithm that integrates expressive CNF policies with PPO-style objectives without requiring likelihood evaluation along the full flow path. PolicyFlow approximates importance ratios using velocity field variations along a simple interpolation path, reducing computational overhead without compromising training stability. To further prevent mode collapse and further encourage diverse behaviors, we propose the Brownian Regularizer, an implicit policy entropy regularizer inspired by Brownian motion, which is conceptually elegant and computationally lightweight. Experiments on diverse tasks across vairous environments including MultiGoal, PointMaze, IsaacLab and MuJoCo Playground show that PolicyFlow achieves competitive or superior performance compared to PPO using Gaussian policies and flow-based baselines including FPO and DPPO. Notably, results on MultiGoal highlight PolicyFlow’s ability to capture richer multimodal action distributions.
abstract_zh: 在同策略强化学习算法中，近端策略优化（PPO）因其简单性、数值稳定性和强大的经验性能而被广泛青睐。标准 PPO 依赖于通过重要性比率定义的替代目标，这要求评估策略似然；当策略被建模为高斯分布时，这通常较为直接。然而，将 PPO 扩展到更具表达力、更高容量的策略模型，如连续归一化流（CNF），也称为流匹配模型，是具有挑战性的，因为沿完整流轨迹进行似然评估的计算开销高昂，且往往数值不稳定。为解决这一问题，我们提出了 PolicyFlow，这是一种新颖的基于 CNF 的同策略强化学习算法，它将高表达力的 CNF 策略与 PPO 风格的目标相结合，而无需沿完整流路径进行似然评估。PolicyFlow 通过简单插值路径上的速度场变化来近似重要性比率，在不损害训练稳定性的前提下降低了计算开销。为进一步防止模式坍塌并进一步鼓励行为多样性，我们提出了布朗正则项（Brownian Regularizer），这是一种受布朗运动启发的隐式策略熵正则项，在概念上简洁优雅且计算开销很小。在包括 MultiGoal、PointMaze、IsaacLab 和 MuJoCo Playground 在内的多种环境上的多样任务实验表明，与使用高斯策略的 PPO 以及包括 FPO 和 DPPO 在内的基于流的基线方法相比，PolicyFlow 实现了具有竞争力或更优的性能。值得注意的是，MultiGoal 上的结果突出了 PolicyFlow 捕获更丰富多峰动作分布的能力。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: DenseDPO: Fine-Grained Temporal Preference Optimization for Video Diffusion Models
title_zh: DenseDPO：面向视频扩散模型的细粒度时间偏好优化
abstract:
Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) has recently been applied as a post‑training technique for text-to-video diffusion models.
To obtain training data, annotators are asked to provide preferences between two videos generated from independent noise.
However, this approach prohibits fine-grained comparisons, and we point out that it biases the annotators towards low-motion clips as they often contain fewer visual artifacts.
In this work, we introduce DenseDPO, a method that addresses these shortcomings by making three contributions.
First, we create each video pair for DPO by denoising corrupted copies of a ground truth video.
This results in aligned pairs with similar motion structures while differing in local details, effectively neutralizing the motion bias.
Second, we leverage the resulting temporal alignment to label preferences on short segments rather than entire clips, yielding a denser and more precise learning signal.
With only one‑third of the labeled data, DenseDPO greatly improves motion generation over vanilla DPO, while matching it in text alignment, visual quality, and temporal consistency.
Finally, we show that DenseDPO unlocks automatic preference annotation using off-the-shelf Vision Language Models (VLMs): GPT accurately predicts segment-level preferences similar to task-specifically fine-tuned video reward models, and DenseDPO trained on these labels achieves performance close to using human labels.
abstract_zh: DenseDPO 从处理数据源开始，通过对同一真实视频的两个加噪拷贝进行降噪，生成姿态与运动结构一致、但细节不同的配对样本，从而抹平了传统 DPO 训练中偏向低运动片段的问题；利用这种时间上的对齐，DenseDPO 将偏好标注从整段视频细化到短时间片段，使监督信号更密、更精；仅用原始标注的三分之一数据，就在运动生成质量上显著超越原始 DPO，同时在文本对齐、视觉质量和时间一致性上与其持平；此外，DenseDPO 还能结合现成的视觉语言模型实现偏好自动标注：GPT 在片段级偏好预测上表现接近专门微调的视频奖励模型，而用这些自动标注训练出的 DenseDPO 性能也接近人类标注。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Spike-timing-dependent Hebbian learning as noisy gradient descent
title_zh: 突触时序依赖的Hebbian学习等价于带噪声的梯度下降
abstract: Hebbian learning is a key principle underlying learning in biological neural networks. We relate a Hebbian spike-timing-dependent plasticity rule to noisy gradient descent with respect to a non-convex loss function on the probability simplex. Despite the constant injection of noise and the non-convexity of the underlying optimization problem, one can rigorously prove that the considered Hebbian learning dynamic identifies the presynaptic neuron with the highest activity and that the convergence is exponentially fast in the number of iterations. This is non-standard and surprising as typically noisy gradient descent with fixed noise level only converges to a stationary regime where the noise causes the dynamic to fluctuate around a minimiser.
abstract_zh: Hebbian学习是生物神经网络中学习机制所依据的一个关键原理。我们将一种Hebbian脉冲时序依赖可塑性规则，与在概率单纯形上的一个非凸损失函数下进行的带噪声梯度下降联系起来。尽管噪声被持续注入，且底层优化问题是非凸的，仍然可以严格证明，所考虑的Hebbian学习动力学能够识别出活动性最高的突触前神经元，并且其收敛速度关于迭代次数呈指数级快速。这一点并不常见且令人惊讶，因为通常在固定噪声水平下，带噪声梯度下降只会收敛到一个平稳状态，在该状态中噪声会使动力学在极小值附近波动。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: LycheeDecode: Accelerating Long-Context LLM Inference via Hybrid-Head Sparse Decoding
title_zh: LycheeDecode：通过混合头稀疏解码加速长上下文大语言模型推理
abstract: The proliferation of long-context large language models (LLMs) exposes a key bottleneck: the rapidly expanding key-value cache during decoding, which imposes heavy memory and latency costs. While recent approaches attempt to alleviate this by sharing a single set of crucial tokens across layers, such coarse-grained sharing undermines model performance by neglecting the functional diversity of attention heads. To address this, we propose LycheeDecode, an efficient decoding method centered on a fine-grained hybrid-head attention mechanism that employs a hardware-efficient top-$k$ selection strategy. Specifically, the novel HardKuma-based mechanism partitions attention heads into a small subset of retrieval heads that dynamically identify crucial tokens and a majority of sparse heads that reuse them for efficient computation. Through extensive experiments on leading models like Llama3 and Qwen3 across diverse benchmarks for long-context understanding (e.g., LongBench, RULER) and complex reasoning (e.g., AIME24, OlympiadBench), we demonstrate that LycheeDecode achieves generative quality comparable to, and at times surpassing even the full-attention baseline. Crucially, this is accomplished with up to a 2.7x speedup at a 128K context length. By preserving the functional diversity of attention heads, our fine-grained strategy overcomes the performance bottlenecks of existing methods, providing a powerful and validated pathway to both efficient and high-quality long-context LLM inference. The implementation code, kernels, and models will be publicly available.
abstract_zh: 长上下文大语言模型的普及暴露出一个关键瓶颈：解码过程中键值缓存快速膨胀，带来了极高的内存与延迟成本。尽管现有方案尝试通过跨层共享单组关键令牌缓解这一问题，但这种粗粒度共享忽略了注意力头的功能多样性，会损害模型性能。针对该问题，我们提出LycheeDecode，这是一种以细粒度混合头注意力机制为核心的高效解码方法，采用了硬件高效的top-k选择策略。具体而言，这种基于HardKuma的全新机制将注意力头划分为两类：一小部分检索头负责动态识别关键令牌，占绝大多数的稀疏头则复用这些令牌以实现高效计算。我们针对Llama3、Qwen3等主流模型，在长上下文理解（如LongBench、RULER）和复杂推理（如AIME24、OlympiadBench）的各类基准测试上开展了大量实验，结果表明LycheeDecode的生成质量与全注意力基线相当，有时甚至更优。尤为重要的是，在128K上下文长度下，LycheeDecode最多可实现2.7倍加速。我们的细粒度策略保留了注意力头的功能多样性，克服了现有方法的性能瓶颈，为实现高效且高质量的长上下文大语言模型推理提供了一条经过验证的有效路径。本工作的实现代码、内核与模型将公开发布。
notes: 论文聚焦长上下文 LLM 推理加速，核心是基于混合头稀疏注意力的解码与 KV/关键 token 复用；包含硬件高效 top-k 与 kernels 表述，但未明显涉及系统级调度。

title: Lexico: Extreme KV Cache Compression via Sparse Coding over Universal Dictionaries
title_zh: Lexico：通过通用词典上的稀疏编码实现极限KV缓存压缩
abstract: We introduce Lexico, a novel KV cache compression method that leverages sparse coding with a universal dictionary. Our key finding is that key-value cache in modern LLMs can be accurately approximated using sparse linear combination from a small, input-agnostic dictionary of ~4k atoms, enabling efficient compression across different input prompts, tasks and models. Using orthogonal matching pursuit for sparse approximation, Lexico achieves flexible compression ratios through direct sparsity control. On GSM8K, across multiple model families (Mistral, Llama 3, Qwen2.5), Lexico maintains 90-95% of the original performance while using only 15-25% of the full KV-cache memory, outperforming both quantization and token eviction methods. Notably, Lexico remains effective in low memory regimes where 2-bit quantization fails, achieving up to 1.7x better compression on LongBench and GSM8K while maintaining high accuracy.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了Lexico，一种借助通用字典稀疏编码实现的新型KV缓存压缩方法。我们的核心发现是，现代大语言模型中的键值缓存可通过由约4000个原子组成的小型、与输入无关的字典的稀疏线性组合得到精准近似，从而能够在不同输入提示、任务和模型间实现高效压缩。通过正交匹配追踪算法完成稀疏近似，Lexico可通过直接控制稀疏度实现灵活的压缩比调节。在GSM8K数据集上，跨多个模型系列（Mistral、Llama 3、Qwen2.5）测试时，Lexico仅占用15%~25%的全量KV缓存内存，同时保留了原始模型90%~95%的性能，表现优于量化和令牌逐出两类方法。值得注意的是，在2比特量化失效的低内存场景下Lexico仍然有效，在LongBench和GSM8K数据集上可实现最高1.7倍的压缩比提升，同时保持较高准确率。
notes: ['主题直接聚焦 KV cache 压缩，属于上下文与缓存优化。', '方法通过稀疏编码和通用字典降低缓存存储开销，也符合模型压缩。']

title: Scaling Knowledge Editing in LLMs to 100,000 Facts with Neural KV Database
title_zh: 在大型语言模型中利用神经键值数据库将知识编辑扩展到 100,000 条事实
abstract: Efficiently editing knowledge stored in Large Language Models (LLMs) enables model updates without large-scale training. One promising solution is Locate-and-Edit (L\&E), allowing simultaneous modifications of a massive number of factual knowledge. However, such editing may compromise the general abilities of LLMs and even result in forgetting edited facts when scaling up to thousands of edits.  In this paper, we model existing linear L\&E methods as querying a Key-Value (KV) database. From this perspective, we then propose NeuralDB, an editing framework that explicitly represents the edited facts as a neural KV database equipped with a non-linear gated retrieval module. With simple modification over L\&E methods, our framework not only significantly extends the capacity of knowledge editing but also eliminates the associated side effects. Comprehensive experiments involving the editing of 10,000 facts were conducted on the ZsRE and CounterFact datasets, including GPT2-XL, GPT-J (6B) and Llama-3 (8B). The results demonstrate that NeuralDB excels in all metrics of editing success while maintaining original performance evaluated by six representative text understanding and generation tasks. Further experiments indicate that NeuralDB maintains its effectiveness even when scaled to 100,000 facts (\textbf{50}$\mathbf{\times}$ more than in prior work).
abstract_zh:
高效地编辑存储在大型语言模型（LLM）中的知识，使得无需大规模训练即可更新模型。一种很有前景的解决方案是定位并编辑（Locate-and-Edit，L&E），它允许同时修改海量的事实性知识。然而，这种编辑在扩展到数千次编辑时，可能会损害LLM的通用能力，甚至导致对已编辑事实的遗忘。
在本文中，我们将现有的线性L&E方法建模为对一个键值（KV）数据库的查询。从这一视角出发，我们提出了NeuralDB，这是一种编辑框架，它将编辑后的事实显式表示为一个神经KV数据库，并配备了一个非线性的门控检索模块。只需对L&E方法做简单修改，我们的框架不仅显著扩展了知识编辑的容量，还消除了相关副作用。我们在ZsRE和CounterFact数据集上开展了涉及10,000条事实编辑的全面实验，涵盖GPT2-XL、GPT-J（6B）和Llama-3（8B）。结果表明，NeuralDB在编辑成功的所有指标上都表现优异，同时在六项具有代表性的文本理解与生成任务评估中保持了原有性能。进一步实验表明，即使扩展到100,000条事实时，NeuralDB仍能保持其有效性（比先前工作高出50倍）。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: GOATex: Geometry & Occlusion-Aware Texturing
title_zh: GOATex：几何与遮挡感知贴图
abstract: We present GOATex, a diffusion-based method for 3D mesh texturing that generates high-quality textures for both exterior and interior surfaces. While existing methods perform well on visible regions, they inherently lack mechanisms to handle occluded interiors, resulting in incomplete textures and visible seams. To address this, we introduce an occlusion-aware texturing framework based on the concept of hit levels, which quantify the relative depth of mesh faces via multi-view ray casting. This allows us to partition mesh faces into ordered visibility layers, from outermost to innermost. We then apply a two-stage visibility control strategy that progressively reveals interior regions with structural coherence, followed by texturing each layer using a pretrained diffusion model. To seamlessly merge textures obtained across layers, we propose a soft UV-space blending technique that weighs each texture’s contribution based on view-dependent visibility confidence. Empirical results demonstrate that GOATex consistently outperforms existing methods, producing seamless, high-fidelity textures across both visible and occluded surfaces. Unlike prior works, GOATex operates entirely without costly fine-tuning of a pretrained diffusion model and allows separate prompting for exterior and interior mesh regions, enabling fine-grained control over layered appearances.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了GOATex，这是一种基于扩散模型的3D网格纹理生成方法，能够为外表面和内表面生成高质量纹理。尽管现有方法在可见区域上表现良好，但它们本质上缺乏处理被遮挡内部区域的机制，因而会导致纹理不完整和可见接缝。为了解决这一问题，我们引入了一种基于命中层级概念的遮挡感知纹理框架，该概念通过多视角光线投射来量化网格面片的相对深度。这使我们能够将网格面片划分为有序的可见性层，从最外层到最内层。随后，我们采用一种两阶段可见性控制策略，以结构一致的方式逐步显露内部区域，并使用预训练扩散模型对每一层进行纹理生成。为了无缝融合跨层获得的纹理，我们提出了一种软UV空间混合技术，根据与视角相关的可见性置信度来加权各纹理的贡献。实验结果表明，GOATex持续优于现有方法，能够在可见表面和被遮挡表面上生成无缝且高保真的纹理。不同于以往工作，GOATex完全无需对预训练扩散模型进行高成本微调，并且允许对网格的外部区域和内部区域分别进行提示，从而实现对分层外观的细粒度控制。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: How Text Quality Interventions Reshape Neural Scaling Laws for LLMs: Empirical Study
title_zh: 文本质量干预如何重塑大型语言模型的神经扩展定律：实证研究
abstract: Neural scaling laws are widely used for performance projection and resource planning, yet their sensitivity to data quality interventions remains poorly understood.  We present an empirical study of how interventions—deduplication, heuristic filtering, and LLM-guided rewriting—reshape scaling behavior in large language model training. Using QualityPajama, a suite of 23 systematically filtered and synthetic datasets, we train over 2,000 models (100M–8B parameters, 100M–200B tokens) to measure how data quality affects scaling-law parameters and compute-optimal design decisions. Our results show that data interventions reshape scaling dynamics in non-trivial ways not captured by current theory, simultaneously moving exponents, coefficients, and constants in conflicting directions that exert opposing forces on loss. For example, an intervention may improve constants but hurt the exponents. Strategies that appear optimal at small scale can reverse at larger scale, and compute-optimal token–parameter ratios can vary by orders of magnitude depending on the intervention. These findings demonstrate that data curation and scaling strategy are deeply intertwined, and that evaluating interventions only at fixed scales can lead to misleading conclusions. We recommend evaluating interventions through their full scaling trajectories using scaling law projections.
abstract_zh: 神经缩放定律被广泛用于性能预测和资源规划，但其对数据质量干预的敏感性仍然缺乏充分理解。我们开展了一项实证研究，考察去重、启发式过滤和 LLM 引导改写等干预如何重塑大语言模型训练中的缩放行为。我们使用 QualityPajama 这一由 23 个经过系统化过滤和合成的数据集组成的数据集套件，训练了超过 2000 个模型（参数规模为 1 亿至 80 亿，训练词元数为 1 亿至 2000 亿），以测量数据质量如何影响缩放定律参数以及计算最优设计决策。我们的结果表明，数据干预会以当前理论尚无法刻画的非平凡方式重塑缩放动力学，同时使指数、系数和常数项朝相互冲突的方向变化，从而对损失施加彼此对立的影响。例如，一种干预可能改善常数项，却损害指数。那些在小规模下看似最优的策略，在更大规模下可能发生逆转；而计算最优的词元—参数比会因干预不同而相差若干个数量级。这些发现表明，数据整理与缩放策略是深度耦合的，仅在固定规模下评估干预可能会得出误导性结论。我们建议通过缩放定律投影，沿完整的缩放轨迹来评估干预。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Speculate Deep and Accurate: Lossless and Training-Free Acceleration for Offloaded LLMs via Substitute Speculative Decoding
title_zh: 深入且精确的推测：通过替代投机解码对卸载的大型语言模型实现无损无训练加速
abstract:
The immense model sizes of large language models (LLMs) challenge deployment on memory-limited consumer GPUs.
    Although model compression and parameter offloading are common strategies to address memory limitations, compression can degrade quality, and offloading maintains quality but suffers from slow inference.
    Speculative decoding presents a promising avenue to accelerate parameter offloading, utilizing a fast draft model to propose multiple draft tokens, which are then verified by the target LLM in parallel with a single forward pass. This method reduces the time-consuming data transfers in forward passes that involve offloaded weight transfers.
    Existing methods often rely on pretrained weights of the same family, but require additional training to align with custom-trained models. Moreover, approaches that involve draft model training usually yield only modest speedups. This limitation arises from insufficient alignment with the target model, preventing higher token acceptance lengths.
    To address these challenges and achieve greater speedups, we propose SubSpec, a plug-and-play method to accelerate parameter offloading that is lossless and training-free. SubSpec constructs a highly aligned draft model by generating low-bit quantized substitute layers from offloaded target LLM portions. Additionally, our method shares the remaining GPU-resident layers and the KV-Cache, further reducing memory overhead and enhance alignment.
    SubSpec achieves a high average acceptance length, delivering 9.1$\times$ speedup for Qwen2.5 7B on MT-Bench (8GB VRAM limit) and an average of 12.5$\times$ speedup for Qwen2.5 32B on popular generation benchmarks (24GB VRAM limit).
abstract_zh: 大语言模型庞大的模型尺寸对其在内存有限的消费级GPU上的部署构成了挑战。模型压缩和参数卸载是解决内存限制问题的常用策略，但压缩会降低模型质量，卸载虽能保留模型质量却存在推理速度缓慢的问题。投机解码为加速参数卸载提供了极具前景的方向，该方法利用快速草稿模型生成多个草稿令牌，随后仅通过目标大语言模型的一次前向传播即可并行完成所有草稿令牌的验证，减少了前向传播过程中涉及卸载权重传输的耗时数据传输操作。现有方法通常依赖同一系列的预训练权重，需要额外训练才能与自定义训练的模型对齐，此外涉及草稿模型训练的方法通常只能实现有限的加速效果，这一局限源于其与目标模型的对齐度不足，无法实现更高的令牌接受长度。为应对上述挑战并实现更显著的加速效果，我们提出了SubSpec，这是一种可即插即用的参数卸载加速方法，具备无损且无需训练的特点。SubSpec通过从已卸载的目标大语言模型部分生成低比特量化的替代层，构建出对齐度极高的草稿模型。此外，该方法共用了剩余的GPU驻留层和KV缓存，进一步降低了内存开销并提升了对齐度。SubSpec实现了较高的平均接受长度，在MT-Bench基准测试中，针对8GB显存限制下的Qwen2.5 7B模型实现了9.1倍加速；在常用生成基准测试中，针对24GB显存限制下的Qwen2.5 32B模型实现了平均12.5倍的加速。
notes: 论文核心是面向离线/卸载式 LLM 推理的 speculative decoding 加速；主标签应为解码策略优化，同时与 offloading 场景下的系统侧推理调度密切相关。

title: Flexible inference for animal learning rules using neural networks
title_zh: 使用神经网络灵活推理动物学习规则
abstract: Understanding how animals learn is a central challenge in neuroscience, with growing relevance to the development of animal- or human-aligned artificial intelligence. However, existing approaches tend to assume fixed parametric forms for the learning rule (e.g., Q-learning, policy gradient), which may not accurately describe the complex forms of learning employed by animals in realistic settings. Here we address this gap by developing a framework to infer learning rules directly from behavioral data collected during *de novo* task learning. We assume that animals follow a decision policy parameterized by a generalized linear model (GLM), and we model their learning rule—the mapping from task covariates to per-trial weight updates—using a deep neural network (DNN). This formulation allows flexible, data-driven inference of learning rules while maintaining an interpretable form of the decision policy itself. To capture more complex learning dynamics, we introduce a recurrent neural network (RNN) variant that relaxes the Markovian assumption that learning depends solely on covariates of the current trial, allowing for learning rules that integrate information over multiple trials. Simulations demonstrate that the framework can recover ground-truth learning rules. We applied our DNN and RNN-based methods to a large behavioral dataset from mice learning to perform a sensory decision-making task and found that they outperformed traditional RL learning rules at predicting the learning trajectories of held-out mice. The inferred learning rules exhibited reward-history–dependent learning dynamics, with larger updates following sequences of rewarded trials. Overall, these methods provide a flexible framework for inferring learning rules from behavioral data in *de novo* learning tasks, setting the stage for improved animal training protocols and the development of behavioral digital twins.
abstract_zh: 理解动物如何学习是神经科学中的核心挑战，并且与开发与动物或人类对齐的人工智能日益相关。然而，现有方法往往假设学习规则具有固定的参数形式（例如 Q-learning、策略梯度），这可能无法准确描述动物在真实情境中采用的复杂学习形式。本文通过开发一个框架来弥补这一空白，该框架可直接从在全新任务学习过程中收集的行为数据中推断学习规则。我们假设动物遵循一个由广义线性模型（GLM）参数化的决策策略，并用深度神经网络（DNN）对其学习规则进行建模，即从任务协变量到逐试次权重更新的映射。该表述在保持决策策略本身具有可解释性的同时，实现了对学习规则灵活、数据驱动的推断。为了捕捉更复杂的学习动态，我们引入了一个循环神经网络（RNN）变体，放宽了学习仅依赖当前试次协变量的马尔可夫假设，从而允许学习规则整合多个试次的信息。仿真结果表明，该框架能够恢复真实的学习规则。我们将基于 DNN 和 RNN 的方法应用于一个大型行为数据集，该数据集来自学习执行感觉决策任务的小鼠，结果发现，与传统强化学习规则相比，这些方法在预测留出小鼠的学习轨迹方面表现更优。所推断的学习规则表现出依赖奖励历史的学习动态，即在连续获得奖励的试次之后会出现更大的更新。总体而言，这些方法为从全新学习任务中的行为数据推断学习规则提供了一个灵活框架，并为改进动物训练方案和开发行为数字孪生奠定了基础。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: ElasticMM: Efficient Multimodal LLMs Serving with Elastic Multimodal Parallelism
title_zh: ElasticMM：基于弹性多模态并行的高效多模态大语言模型服务
abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) extend LLMs to handle images, videos, and audio by incorporating feature extractors and projection modules. However, these additional components—combined with complex inference pipelines and heterogeneous workloads—introduce significant inference overhead. Therefore, efficiently serving MLLMs remains a major challenge. Current tightly coupled serving architectures struggle to distinguish between mixed request types or adapt parallelism strategies to different inference stages, leading to increased time-to-first-token (TTFT) and poor resource utilization. To address this, we introduce Elastic Multimodal Parallelism (EMP), a new serving paradigm that elastically adapts to resource heterogeneity across request types and inference stages. Building upon EMP, we develop ElasticMM, an MLLM serving system that (1) separates requests into independent modality groups with dynamic resource allocation via a modality-aware load balancer; (2) decouples inference stages and enables parallelism adjustment and adaptive scaling via elastic partition scheduling; and (3) improves inference efficiency through unified multimodal prefix caching and non-blocking encoding. Experiments on diverse real-world datasets show that ElasticMM outperforms state-of-the-art (SOTA) serving systems, reducing TTFT by up to 4.2$\times$ and achieving 3.2–4.5$\times$ higher throughput while meeting service-level objectives (SLOs).
abstract_zh: 多模态大语言模型（MLLM）通过引入特征提取器和投影模块拓展了大语言模型（LLM）的能力，使其可处理图像、视频和音频数据。然而，这些新增组件结合复杂的推理流水线与异构工作负载，会带来显著的推理开销，因此高效部署MLLM服务仍是一项重大挑战。当前的紧耦合服务架构难以区分混合请求类型，也无法针对不同推理阶段调整并行策略，会导致首token时延（TTFT）升高、资源利用率低下。为解决这一问题，我们提出弹性多模态并行（EMP），这是一种新型服务范式，可弹性适配不同请求类型和推理阶段的资源异质性。基于EMP，我们开发了MLLM服务系统ElasticMM，该系统具备三项核心能力：一是通过模态感知负载均衡器将请求划分为独立的模态组，实现动态资源分配；二是解耦各推理阶段，通过弹性分区调度支持并行策略调整与自适应扩缩容；三是通过统一多模态前缀缓存与无阻塞编码提升推理效率。在多类真实数据集上开展的实验表明，ElasticMM性能优于现有最先进（SOTA）服务系统，在满足服务水平目标（SLO）的前提下，首token时延最高可降低4.2倍，吞吐量提升3.2至4.5倍。
notes: 调度专项得分=9；命中组=llm_serving；augment_batch=20260402-scheduling；augment_score=9

title: LBMKGC: Large Model-Driven Balanced Multimodal Knowledge Graph Completion
title_zh: LBMKGC：大模型驱动的平衡多模态知识图谱补全
abstract:
Multi-modal Knowledge Graph Completion (MMKGC) aims to predict missing entities, relations, or attributes in knowledge graphs by collaboratively modeling the triple structure and multimodal information (e.g., text, images, videos) associated with entities.
This approach facilitates the automatic discovery of previously unobserved factual knowledge.
However, existing MMKGC methods encounter several critical challenges: (i) the imbalance of inter-entity information across different modalities; (ii) the heterogeneity of intra-entity multimodal information; and (iii) for a given entity, the informational contributions of different modalities are inconsistent across contexts.
In this paper, we propose a novel **L**arge model-driven **B**alanced **M**ultimodal **K**nowledge **G**raph **C**ompletion framework, termed LBMKGC.
Subsequently, to bridge the semantic gap between heterogeneous modalities, LBMKGC aligns the multimodal embeddings of entities semantically by using the CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pre-Training) model.
Furthermore, LBMKGC adaptively fuses multimodal embeddings with relational guidance by distinguishing between the perceptual and conceptual attributes of triples.
Finally, extensive experiments conducted against 21 state-of-the-art baselines demonstrate that LBMKGC achieves superior performance across diverse datasets and scenarios while maintaining efficiency and generalizability.
Our code and data are publicly available at: https://github.com/guoynow/LBMKGC.
abstract_zh: LBMKGC提出的大模型驱动的平衡多模态知识图谱补全框架，利用实体的三元组结构与其关联的多模态信息（如文字、图像、视频）联合建模，自动发现未观测的事实知识。为解决现有方法在不同模态间信息不平衡、实体内部多模态信息异构以及同一实体在不同上下文中各模态贡献不一致的挑战，LBMKGC首先通过CLIP模型在语义层面对齐实体的多模态嵌入，从而弥合异质模态之间的语义鸿沟；随后，在区分三元组的感知属性与概念属性之后，利用关系引导自适应融合多模态嵌入；大量实验对比21个最新基线表明，LBMKGC在多种数据集与场景下均取得了性能与效率兼顾的领先结果，并具备良好泛化能力。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Generalization or Hallucination? Understanding Out-of-Context Reasoning in Transformers
title_zh: 泛化还是幻觉？理解变换器中的脱离上下文推理
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) can acquire new knowledge through fine-tuning, but this process exhibits a puzzling duality: models can generalize remarkably from new facts, yet are also prone to hallucinating incorrect information. However, the reasons for this phenomenon remain poorly understood. In this work, we argue that both behaviors stem from a single mechanism known as out-of-context reasoning (OCR): the ability to deduce implications by associating concepts, even those without a causal link. Our experiments across five prominent LLMs confirm that OCR indeed drives both generalization and hallucination, depending on whether the associated concepts are causally related. To build a rigorous theoretical understanding of this phenomenon, we then formalize OCR as a synthetic factual recall task. We empirically show that a one-layer single-head attention-only transformer with factorized output and value matrices can learn to solve this task, while a model with combined weights cannot, highlighting the crucial role of matrix factorization. Our theoretical analysis shows that the OCR capability can be attributed to the implicit bias of gradient descent, which favors solutions that minimize the nuclear norm of the combined output-value matrix. This structure explains why the model learns to associate facts and implications with high sample efficiency, regardless of whether the correlation is causal or merely spurious. Ultimately, our work provides a theoretical foundation for understanding the OCR phenomenon, offering a new lens for analyzing and mitigating undesirable behaviors from knowledge injection.
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）可以通过微调获得新知识，但这一过程表现出一种令人困惑的二重性：模型既能从新事实中实现显著泛化，又容易产生错误信息的幻觉。然而，这一现象背后的原因仍然缺乏充分理解。在这项工作中，我们认为这两种行为都源于同一种机制，即脱离上下文推理（out-of-context reasoning，OCR）：即使概念之间不存在因果联系，也能通过概念关联来推导其含义的能力。我们在五种具有代表性的大型语言模型上的实验确认，OCR 的确同时驱动了泛化和幻觉，具体取决于被关联的概念是否具有因果关系。为了对这一现象建立严格的理论理解，我们进一步将 OCR 形式化为一个合成的事实回忆任务。我们通过经验研究表明，具有因子化输出矩阵和值矩阵的单层单头纯注意力 Transformer 可以学会解决这一任务，而具有合并权重的模型则不能，这凸显了矩阵因子化的关键作用。我们的理论分析表明，OCR 能力可归因于梯度下降的隐式偏置，这种偏置偏好最小化合并后的输出-值矩阵的核范数的解。这样的结构解释了为什么模型能够以很高的样本效率学习事实与其含义之间的关联，而不论这种相关性是因果性的还是仅仅是伪相关。最终，我们的工作为理解 OCR 现象提供了理论基础，并为分析和缓解知识注入带来的不良行为提供了一个新的视角。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Search Self-Play: Pushing the Frontier of Agent Capability without Supervision
title_zh: 搜索自我对弈：在无监督条件下推动智能体能力边界
abstract: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has become the mainstream technique for training LLM agents. However, RLVR highly depends on well-crafted task queries and corresponding ground-truth answers to provide accurate rewards, which requires significant human effort and hinders the scaling of RL processes, especially in agentic scenarios. Although a few recent works explore task synthesis methods, the difficulty of generated agentic tasks can hardly be controlled to provide effective RL training advantages. To achieve agentic RLVR with higher scalability, we explore self-play training for deep search agents, in which the learning LLM utilizes multi-turn search engine calling and acts simultaneously as both a task proposer and a problem solver. The task proposer aims to generate deep search queries with well-defined ground-truth answers and increasing task difficulty. The problem solver tries to handle the generated search queries and output the correct answer predictions. To ensure that each generated search query has accurate ground truth, we collect all the searching results from the proposer's trajectory as external knowledge, then conduct retrieval-augmentation generation (RAG) to test whether the proposed query can be correctly answered with all necessary search documents provided. In this search self-play (SSP) game, the proposer and the solver co-evolve their agent capabilities through both competition and cooperation. With substantial experimental results, we find that SSP can significantly improve search agents' performance uniformly on various benchmarks without any supervision under both from-scratch and continuous RL training setups.
abstract_zh: 带有可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）已成为训练大语言模型智能体的主流技术。然而，RLVR 高度依赖精心设计的任务查询及其对应的真实答案来提供准确奖励，这需要大量人工投入，并阻碍了强化学习过程的扩展，尤其是在智能体场景中。尽管近期有少量工作探索了任务合成方法，但生成的智能体任务的难度几乎无法被控制，因此难以为强化学习训练提供有效优势。为了实现具有更高可扩展性的智能体 RLVR，我们探索了面向深度搜索智能体的自对弈训练，其中正在学习的大语言模型利用多轮搜索引擎调用，并同时充当任务提出者和问题求解者。任务提出者的目标是生成具有明确真实答案且任务难度不断提升的深度搜索查询。问题求解者则尝试处理所生成的搜索查询并输出正确的答案预测。为了确保每个生成的搜索查询都具有准确的真实答案，我们收集提出者轨迹中的所有搜索结果作为外部知识，然后进行检索增强生成（RAG），以测试在提供所有必要搜索文档的情况下，所提出的查询是否能够被正确回答。在这种搜索自对弈（SSP）博弈中，提出者和求解者通过竞争与合作共同进化其智能体能力。大量实验结果表明，在从零开始训练和持续强化学习训练两种设置下，SSP 都能够在无任何监督的情况下，显著且一致地提升搜索智能体在各种基准测试上的性能。
primary_research_object: 强化学习 / 序列决策
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Large Language Bayes
title_zh: 大型语言贝叶斯
abstract: Many domain experts do not have the time or expertise to write formal Bayesian models. This paper takes an informal problem description as input, and combines a large language model and a probabilistic programming language to define a joint distribution over formal models, latent variables, and data. A posterior over latent variables follows by conditioning on observed data and integrating over formal models. This presents a challenging inference problem. We suggest an inference recipe that amounts to generating many formal models from the large language model, performing approximate inference on each, and then doing a weighted aver- age. This is justified and analyzed as a combination of self-normalized importance sampling, MCMC, and importance-weighted variational inference. Experimentally, this produces sensible predictions from only data and an informal problem description, without the need to specify a formal model.
abstract_zh: 许多领域专家没有时间或专业能力来编写形式化的贝叶斯模型。本文以非形式化的问题描述作为输入，结合大语言模型与概率编程语言，在形式模型、潜变量和数据之上定义一个联合分布。通过对观测数据进行条件化并对形式模型积分，可以得到潜变量的后验分布。这带来了一个具有挑战性的推断问题。我们提出了一种推断方案，其做法相当于从大语言模型生成许多形式模型，对每个模型执行近似推断，然后进行加权平均。我们将其论证并分析为自归一化重要性采样、MCMC 和重要性加权变分推断的结合。从实验上看，仅凭数据和非形式化的问题描述，而无需指定形式模型，该方法就能产生合理的预测。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Validating LLM-as-a-Judge Systems under Rating Indeterminacy
title_zh: 在评分不确定性下验证LLM-as-a-Judge系统
abstract: The LLM-as-a-judge paradigm, in which a judge LLM system replaces human raters in rating the outputs of other generative AI (GenAI) systems, plays a critical role in scaling and standardizing GenAI evaluations. To validate such judge systems, evaluators assess human--judge agreement by first collecting multiple human ratings for each item in a validation corpus, then aggregating the ratings into a single, per-item gold label rating. For many items, however, rating criteria may admit multiple valid interpretations, so a human or LLM rater may deem multiple ratings "reasonable" or "correct."  We call this condition rating indeterminacy. Problematically, many rating tasks that contain rating indeterminacy rely on forced-choice elicitation, whereby raters are instructed to select only one rating for each item. In this paper, we introduce a framework for validating LLM-as-a-judge systems under rating indeterminacy. We draw theoretical connections between different measures of judge system performance under different human--judge agreement metrics, and different rating elicitation and aggregation schemes. We demonstrate that differences in how humans and LLMs resolve rating indeterminacy when responding to forced-choice rating instructions can heavily bias LLM-as-a-judge validation. Through extensive experiments involving 11 real-world rating tasks and 9 commercial LLMs, we show that standard validation approaches that rely upon forced-choice ratings select judge systems that are highly suboptimal, performing as much as 31% worse than judge systems selected by our approach that uses multi-label "response set" ratings to account for rating indeterminacy. We conclude with concrete recommendations for more principled approaches to LLM-as-a-judge validation.
abstract_zh: LLM 作为裁判的范式中，由一个裁判 LLM 系统替代人工评分者，对其他生成式人工智能（GenAI）系统的输出进行评分，这在扩展和标准化 GenAI 评估方面发挥着关键作用。为了验证这类裁判系统，评估者会先为验证语料中的每个条目收集多个人工评分，然后将这些评分聚合为每个条目的单一黄金标签评分。然而，对于许多条目，评分标准可能允许多种有效解释，因此人工评分者或 LLM 评分者可能会认为多个评分都是“合理的”或“正确的”。我们将这种情况称为评分不确定性。问题在于，许多包含评分不确定性的评分任务依赖于强制选择式引导，即要求评分者对每个条目只能选择一个评分。本文提出了一个在评分不确定性条件下验证 LLM 作为裁判系统的框架。我们从理论上建立了不同裁判系统性能度量、不同的人类—裁判一致性指标，以及不同评分引导与聚合方案之间的联系。我们表明，当人类和 LLM 在响应强制选择评分指令时对评分不确定性的消解方式存在差异，这会对 LLM 作为裁判的验证造成严重偏差。通过涉及 11 个真实世界评分任务和 9 个商用 LLM 的大量实验，我们显示，依赖强制选择评分的标准验证方法所选出的裁判系统高度次优，其表现最多比采用我们的方法、使用多标签“响应集合”评分以处理评分不确定性所选出的裁判系统差 31%。最后，我们针对如何以更有原则的方法验证 LLM 作为裁判系统提出了具体建议。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: DreamPRM: Domain-reweighted Process Reward Model for Multimodal Reasoning
title_zh: DreamPRM：面向多模态推理的领域重加权过程奖励模型
abstract: Reasoning has substantially improved the performance of large language models (LLMs) on complicated tasks. Central to the current reasoning studies, Process Reward Models (PRMs) offer a fine-grained evaluation of intermediate reasoning steps and guide the reasoning process. However, extending PRMs to multimodal large language models (MLLMs) introduces challenges. Since multimodal reasoning covers a wider range of tasks compared to text-only scenarios, the resulting distribution shift from the training to testing sets is more severe, leading to greater generalization difficulty. Training a reliable multimodal PRM, therefore, demands large and diverse datasets to ensure sufficient coverage. However, current multimodal reasoning datasets suffer from a marked quality imbalance, which degrades PRM performance and highlights the need for an effective data selection strategy. To address the issues, we introduce DreamPRM, a domain-reweighted training framework for multimodal PRMs which employs bi-level optimization. In the lower-level optimization, DreamPRM performs fine-tuning on multiple datasets with domain weights, allowing the PRM to prioritize high-quality reasoning signals and alleviating the impact of dataset quality imbalance. In the upper-level optimization, the PRM is evaluated on a separate meta-learning dataset; this feedback updates the domain weights through an aggregation loss function, thereby improving the generalization capability of trained PRM. Extensive experiments on multiple multimodal reasoning benchmarks covering both mathematical and general reasoning show that test-time scaling with DreamPRM consistently improves the performance of state-of-the-art MLLMs. Further comparisons reveal that DreamPRM's domain-reweighting strategy surpasses other data selection methods and yields higher accuracy gains than existing test-time scaling approaches. Notably, DreamPRM achieves a top-1 accuracy of 85.2% on the MathVista leaderboard using the o4-mini model, demonstrating strong generalization capability in complex multimodal reasoning tasks.
abstract_zh: 推理显著提升了大型语言模型（LLM）在复杂任务上的表现。在当前的推理研究中，过程奖励模型（PRM）处于核心地位，它能够对中间推理步骤进行细粒度评估，并引导推理过程。然而，将 PRM 扩展到多模态大语言模型（MLLM）会带来挑战。由于多模态推理相比纯文本场景涵盖更广泛的任务范围，从训练集到测试集所产生的分布偏移更加严重，导致泛化难度更高。因此，训练一个可靠的多模态 PRM 需要大规模且多样化的数据集，以确保足够的覆盖范围。然而，当前多模态推理数据集存在显著的质量失衡问题，这会降低 PRM 的性能，并凸显出有效数据选择策略的必要性。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了 DreamPRM，这是一种用于多模态 PRM 的领域重加权训练框架，采用双层优化。在下层优化中，DreamPRM 使用领域权重在多个数据集上进行微调，使 PRM 能够优先关注高质量推理信号，并减轻数据集质量失衡的影响。在上层优化中，PRM 在一个独立的元学习数据集上进行评估；该反馈通过一个聚合损失函数更新领域权重，从而提升训练后 PRM 的泛化能力。在涵盖数学推理和通用推理的多个多模态推理基准上的大量实验表明，在测试时采用 DreamPRM 的扩展策略能够持续提升最先进 MLLM 的性能。进一步比较显示，DreamPRM 的领域重加权策略优于其他数据选择方法，并且比现有测试时扩展方法带来更高的准确率提升。值得注意的是，DreamPRM 使用 o4-mini 模型在 MathVista 榜单上取得了 85.2% 的 top-1 准确率，展现出其在复杂多模态推理任务中的强大泛化能力。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: The Open Proof Corpus: A Large-Scale Study of LLM-Generated Mathematical Proofs
title_zh: 开放证明语料库：针对大型语言模型生成的数学证明的大规模研究
abstract: In recent months, large language models (LLMs) have made significant progress in mathematical proof generation, but further advancement is hindered by the lack of a large-scale, high-quality dataset of human-evaluated proofs. While expensive to create, such a dataset is essential for driving improvements in training and addressing key open questions in the field of automated proof generation. Specifically, it remains unknown (1) how large the gap is between natural language and formal proof generation, (2) how final-answer accuracy relates to full proof correctness, and (3) how best-of-n selection strategies can affect proof quality. In this work, we present the Open Proof Corpus (OPC), a dataset comprising over 5,000 human-evaluated proofs produced by state-of-the-art LLMs. The OPC was specifically designed for broad applicability and downstream usage in proof generation research and is the first large dataset of LLM-generated solutions to problems from prestigious mathematics competitions such as the USAMO and IMO. Using the OPC, we address the open questions outlined above and provide new insights into LLMs' strengths and limitations in mathematical reasoning. Finally, to showcase the utility of the OPC, we finetune an 8B-parameter model on the dataset, obtaining a model that matches Gemini-2.5-Pro, and performs close to the best model, GPT-5, on evaluating proof correctness.
abstract_zh: 近几个月来，大型语言模型（LLM）在数学证明生成方面取得了显著进展，但由于缺乏大规模、高质量、经人工评估的证明数据集，进一步发展受到阻碍。尽管此类数据集的构建成本高昂，但它对于推动训练改进以及解决自动化证明生成领域中的关键开放问题至关重要。具体而言，目前仍不清楚：（1）自然语言证明生成与形式化证明生成之间的差距有多大；（2）最终答案的准确性与完整证明的正确性之间有何关系；（3）best-of-n 选择策略会如何影响证明质量。在这项工作中，我们提出了 Open Proof Corpus（OPC），这是一个由最先进的 LLM 生成、包含超过 5000 份经人工评估证明的数据集。OPC 专为广泛适用性以及在证明生成研究中的下游使用而设计，并且是首个包含由 LLM 生成的、针对 USAMO 和 IMO 等知名数学竞赛题目解答的大型数据集。利用 OPC，我们回答了上述开放问题，并为 LLM 在数学推理中的优势与局限提供了新的见解。最后，为了展示 OPC 的实用价值，我们在该数据集上对一个 80 亿参数模型进行了微调，得到的模型在证明正确性评估方面可与 Gemini-2.5-Pro 相当，并且表现接近最佳模型 GPT-5。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Learning Efficient and Interpretable Multi-Agent Communication
title_zh: 学习高效且可解释的多智能体通信
abstract: Effective communication is crucial for multi-agent cooperation in partially observable environments. However, a fundamental trilemma exists among task performance, communication efficiency, and human interpretability. To resolve this, we propose a multi-agent communication framework via $\textbf{G}$rounding $\textbf{L}$anguage and $\textbf{C}$ontrastive learning (GLC) to learns  efficient and interpretable communication protocols. Specifically, GLC employs an autoencoder to learn discretized and compressed communication symbols, ensuring high communication efficiency. These symbols are then semantically aligned with human concepts using data generated by a Large Language Model (LLM), making them human-interpretable. Furthermore, a contrastive learning objective is introduced to ensure consistency and mutual intelligibility among all agents, thereby securing high task utility. GLC dynamically balances these objectives by the Information Bottleneck principle. Extensive experiments show that GLC outperforms state-of-the-art methods across multiple benchmarks, delivering superior task performance, higher communication efficiency, and enhanced human interpretability.
abstract_zh: 在部分可观测环境中，有效通信对于多智能体协作至关重要。然而，任务性能、通信效率与人类可解释性三者之间存在一个基本的三难困境。为了解决这一问题，我们提出了一个通过基于语言落地和对比学习的多智能体通信框架（GLC），以学习高效且可解释的通信协议。具体而言，GLC采用自编码器来学习离散化且压缩的通信符号，以确保较高的通信效率。随后，利用由大语言模型（LLM）生成的数据，将这些符号在语义上与人类概念对齐，使其具有人类可解释性。此外，我们引入了一个对比学习目标，以确保所有智能体之间的一致性和相互可理解性，从而保证较高的任务效用。GLC依据信息瓶颈原理动态平衡这些目标。大量实验表明，在多个基准上，GLC优于最先进的方法，能够带来更优的任务性能、更高的通信效率以及更强的人类可解释性。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: DexFlyWheel: A Scalable and Self-improving Data Generation Framework for Dexterous Manipulation
title_zh: DexFlyWheel：一个可扩展、自我优化的灵巧操作数据生成框架
abstract: Dexterous manipulation is critical for advancing robot capabilities in real-world applications, yet diverse and high-quality datasets remain scarce. Existing data collection methods either rely on human teleoperation or require significant human engineering, or generate data with limited diversity, which restricts their scalability and generalization. In this paper, we introduce DexFlyWheel, a scalable data generation framework that employs a self-improving cycle to continuously enrich data diversity. Starting from efficient seed demonstrations warmup, DexFlyWheel expands the dataset through iterative cycles. Each cycle follows a closed-loop pipeline that integrates Imitation Learning (IL), residual Reinforcement Learning (RL), rollout trajectory collection, and data augmentation. Specifically, IL extracts human-like behaviors from demonstrations, and residual RL enhances policy generalization. The learned policy is then used to generate trajectories in simulation, which are further augmented across diverse environments and spatial configurations before being fed back into the next cycle. Over successive iterations, a self-improving data flywheel effect emerges, producing datasets that cover diverse scenarios and thereby scaling policy performance. Experimental results demonstrate that DexFlyWheel generates over 2,000 diverse demonstrations across four challenging tasks. Policies trained on our dataset achieve an average success rate of 81.9\% on the challenge test sets and successfully transfer to the real world through digital twin, achieving a 78.3\% success rate on dual-arm lift tasks.
abstract_zh: 灵巧操作对于提升机器人在现实世界应用中的能力至关重要，然而多样且高质量的数据集仍然十分稀缺。现有的数据采集方法要么依赖人类遥操作，要么需要大量人工工程设计，或者生成的数据多样性有限，这限制了其可扩展性和泛化能力。本文提出 DexFlyWheel，一种可扩展的数据生成框架，通过自我改进循环持续丰富数据多样性。DexFlyWheel 从高效的种子演示预热开始，并通过迭代循环扩展数据集。每个循环都遵循一个闭环流程，集成了模仿学习（IL）、残差强化学习（RL）、轨迹 rollout 采集和数据增强。具体而言，IL 从演示中提取类人的行为，而残差 RL 提升策略的泛化能力。随后，利用学得的策略在仿真中生成轨迹，并在多样环境和空间配置下进一步增强，再反馈到下一轮循环中。经过连续迭代后，会形成一种自我改进的数据飞轮效应，生成覆盖多样场景的数据集，从而提升策略性能的可扩展性。实验结果表明，DexFlyWheel 在四项具有挑战性的任务上生成了超过 2000 条多样化演示。基于我们数据集训练的策略在挑战测试集上取得了平均 81.9%的成功率，并通过数字孪生成功迁移到真实世界，在双臂抬升任务上实现了 78.3%的成功率。
primary_research_object: 强化学习 / 序列决策
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: NeurIPS should lead scientific consensus on AI policy
title_zh: NeurIPS 应引领人工智能政策的科学共识
abstract: Designing wise AI policy is a grand challenge for society. To design such policy, policymakers should place a premium on rigorous evidence and scientific consensus. While several mechanisms exist for evidence generation, and nascent mechanisms tackle evidence synthesis, we identify a complete void on consensus formation. In this position paper, we argue NeurIPS should actively catalyze scientific consensus on AI policy. Beyond identifying the current deficit in consensus formation mechanisms, we argue that NeurIPS is the best option due its strengths and the paucity of compelling alternatives. To make progress, we recommend initial pilots for NeurIPS by distilling lessons from the IPCC's leadership to build scientific consensus on climate policy. We dispel predictable counters that AI researchers disagree too much to achieve consensus and that policy engagement is not the business of NeurIPS. NeurIPS leads AI on many fronts, and it should champion scientific consensus to create policy that better realizes the benefits of AI.
abstract_zh: 为社会制定明智的人工智能政策是一项重大挑战。要设计这样的政策，决策者应高度重视严格证据和科学共识。尽管目前已有若干证据生成机制，而新兴机制也开始处理证据综合，我们指出，在共识形成方面却完全空白。在这篇立场论文中，我们主张 NeurIPS 应积极催化人工智能政策方面的科学共识。除了指出当前共识形成机制的缺失之外，我们还认为，鉴于其自身优势以及有说服力替代方案的匮乏，NeurIPS 是最佳选择。为推动进展，我们建议 NeurIPS 开展初步试点，从 IPCC 在建立气候政策科学共识方面的领导经验中提炼教训。我们反驳了几种可预见的反对意见，包括人工智能研究者分歧过大因而无法达成共识，以及参与政策并非 NeurIPS 的职责。NeurIPS 已在许多方面引领人工智能领域，也应倡导科学共识，以制定更好实现人工智能益处的政策。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Divergence-Free Neural Networks with Application to Image Denoising
title_zh: 无散度神经网络及其在图像去噪中的应用
abstract: We introduce a resource-efficient neural network architecture with zero divergence by design, adapted for high-dimensional problems. Our method is directly applicable to image denoising, for which divergence-free estimators are particularly well-suited for self-supervised learning, in accordance with Stein's unbiased risk estimation theory. Comparisons of our parameterization on popular denoising datasets demonstrate that it retains sufficient expressivity to remain competitive with other divergence-based approaches, while outperforming its counterparts when the noise level is unknown and varies across the training data.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种按设计具有零散度且资源高效的神经网络架构，并将其适配于高维问题。我们的方法可直接应用于图像去噪；根据 Stein 无偏风险估计理论，无散度估计器尤其适合自监督学习。在常用去噪数据集上的比较表明，我们的参数化方式保留了足够的表达能力，因此能够与其他基于散度的方法保持竞争力；而当噪声水平未知且在训练数据中变化不同时，它的表现优于同类方法。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: PhyScensis: Physics-Augmented LLM Agents for Complex Physical Scene Arrangement
title_zh: PhyScensis：用于复杂物理场景布置的物理增强大语言模型代理
abstract:
Automatically generating interactive 3D environments is crucial for scaling up robotic data collection in simulation. While prior work has primarily focused on 3D asset placement, it often overlooks the physical relationships between objects (e.g., contact, support, balance, and containment), which are essential for creating complex and realistic manipulation scenarios such as tabletop arrangements, shelf organization, or box packing. Compared to classical 3D layout generation, producing complex physical scenes introduces additional challenges: (a) higher object density and complexity (e.g., a small shelf may hold dozens of books), (b) richer supporting relationships and compact spatial layouts, and (c) the need to accurately model both spatial placement and physical properties.
To address these challenges, we propose PhyScensis, an LLM agent-based framework powered by a physics engine, to produce physically plausible scene configurations with high complexity.
Specifically, our framework consists of three main components: an LLM agent iteratively proposes assets with spatial and physical predicates; a solver, equipped with a physics engine, realizes these predicates into a 3D scene; and feedback from the solver informs the agent to refine and enrich the configuration. 
Moreover, our framework preserves strong controllability over fine-grained textual descriptions and numerical parameters (e.g., relative positions, scene stability), enabled through probabilistic programming for stability and a complementary heuristic that jointly regulates stability and spatial relations.
Experimental results show that our method outperforms prior approaches in scene complexity, visual quality, and physical accuracy, offering a unified pipeline for generating complex physical scene layouts for robotic manipulation.
abstract_zh: 自动生成交互式三维环境对于在仿真中扩大机器人数据采集规模至关重要。尽管以往工作主要聚焦于三维资产摆放，但往往忽视了物体之间的物理关系（例如接触、支撑、平衡和容纳），而这些关系对于创建复杂且真实的操作场景至关重要，例如桌面摆放、货架整理或装箱。与经典的三维布局生成相比，生成复杂物理场景会带来额外挑战：（a）更高的物体密度和复杂性（例如一个小书架可能容纳几十本书），（b）更丰富的支撑关系和更紧凑的空间布局，以及（c）需要准确建模空间放置和物理属性。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Majority of the Bests: Improving Best-of-N via Bootstrapping
title_zh: “大多数中最佳：通过自举改进Best-of-N”
abstract: Sampling multiple outputs from a Large Language Model (LLM) and selecting the most frequent (Self-consistency) or highest-scoring (Best-of-N) candidate is a popular approach to achieve higher accuracy in tasks with discrete final answers. Best-of-N (BoN) selects the output with the highest reward, and with perfect rewards, it often achieves near-perfect accuracy. With imperfect rewards from reward models, however, BoN fails to reliably find the correct answer and its performance degrades drastically. We consider the distribution of BoN’s outputs and highlight that, although the correct answer does not usually have a probability close to one under imperfect rewards, it is often the most likely outcome. This suggests that the mode of this distribution can be more reliably correct than a sample from it. Based on this idea, we propose Majority-of-the-Bests (MoB), a novel selection mechanism that estimates the output distribution of BoN via bootstrapping and selects its mode. Experimental results across five benchmarks, three different base LLMs, and two reward models demonstrate consistent improvements over BoN in 25 out of 30 setups. We also provide theoretical results for the consistency of the bootstrapping. MoB serves as a simple, yet strong alternative to BoN and self-consistency, and more broadly, motivates further research in more nuanced selection mechanisms.
abstract_zh: 从大语言模型（LLM）中采样多个输出，并选择出现频率最高的候选（自一致性）或得分最高的候选（Best-of-N），是在具有离散最终答案的任务中获得更高准确率的一种流行方法。Best-of-N（BoN）选择奖励最高的输出，在奖励完美时，它通常能达到近乎完美的准确率。然而，当奖励来自并不完美的奖励模型时，BoN 无法可靠地找到正确答案，其性能会急剧下降。我们考察了 BoN 输出的分布，并指出，尽管在不完美奖励下，正确答案通常并不具有接近 1 的概率，但它往往仍然是最有可能的结果。这表明，该分布的众数可能比从中抽取的一个样本更可靠地给出正确答案。基于这一想法，我们提出了 Majority-of-the-Bests（MoB），这是一种新的选择机制，它通过自助法估计 BoN 的输出分布，并选择其众数。在五个基准、三种不同的基础 LLM 和两种奖励模型上的实验结果表明，在 30 种设置中，MoB 有 25 种始终优于 BoN。我们还给出了关于该自助过程一致性的理论结果。MoB 作为 BoN 和自一致性的一种简单而强有力的替代方案，同时也更广泛地激发了对更细致选择机制的进一步研究。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Speculative Speculative Decoding
title_zh: 投机式投机解码
abstract: Autoregressive decoding is bottlenecked by its *sequential* nature. Speculative decoding has become a standard way to accelerate inference by using a fast *draft model* to predict upcoming tokens from a slower *target model*, and then verifying them *in parallel* with a single target model forward pass. However, speculative decoding itself relies on a *sequential* dependence between speculation and verification. We introduce *speculative speculative decoding* (SSD) to parallelize these operations. While a verification is ongoing, the draft model *predicts* likely verification outcomes and prepares speculations pre-emptively for them. If the actual verification outcome is then in the predicted set, a speculation can be returned immediately, thereby eliminating all speculation overhead.  We identify three key challenges presented by speculative speculative decoding, and put forth principled methods to solve each after theoretical analysis. The result is **Saguaro**, an optimized SSD algorithm which is up to twice as fast as optimized speculative decoding baselines and up to 5× faster than autoregressive decoding with open source inference engines.  **Saguaro** can be combined with existing methods like EAGLE and token tree speculation for further gains, and permits scaling draft compute to better predict verification outcomes, introducing new tradeoffs between compute and latency.
abstract_zh: 自回归解码的瓶颈在于其序列属性。推测解码已成为推理加速的标准方法，它使用快速的草稿模型预测更慢的目标模型的后续词元，再通过目标模型的单轮前向传播并行验证这些词元。但推测解码本身在推测步骤与验证步骤之间存在序列依赖关系。我们提出推测式推测解码（SSD）来并行化这两类操作。当验证正在进行时，草稿模型会预测可能的验证结果，并预先为这些结果准备好推测内容。如果后续实际验证结果落在预测集合内，就可以立即返回推测结果，从而消除所有推测开销。我们明确了推测式推测解码存在的三项核心挑战，经过理论分析后，提出了有理论依据的方法逐一解决上述挑战。最终得到的优化SSD算法Saguaro，在开源推理引擎下的速度最高可达优化后的推测解码基线的2倍，最高可达自回归解码的5倍。Saguaro可与EAGLE、词元树推测等现有方法结合以进一步提升性能，同时支持扩展草稿计算量以更好地预测验证结果，为计算量与延迟之间带来了新的权衡空间。
notes: 论文聚焦 LLM 推理解码加速，通过将 speculation 与 verification 进一步并行化来降低时延，既属于解码策略优化，也涉及推理流程中的并行调度与系统级延迟优化。

title: KV-Cache Transform Coding for Compact Storage in LLM Inference
title_zh: 用于LLM推理的KV-Cache变换编码以实现紧凑存储
abstract: Serving large language models (LLMs) at scale necessitates efficient key-value (KV) cache management. KV caches can be reused across conversation turns via shared-prefix prompts that are common in iterative code editing and chat. However, stale caches consume scarce GPU memory, require offloading, or force recomputation. We present KVTC, a lightweight transform coder that compresses KV caches for compact on-GPU and off-GPU storage. Drawing on classical media compression, KVTC combines PCA-based feature decorrelation, adaptive quantization, and entropy coding. It requires only a brief initial calibration and leaves model parameters unchanged. By exploiting redundancies in KV caches, KVTC achieves up to 20x compression, while maintaining reasoning and long-context accuracy in Llama 3.1, Mistral-NeMo, and R1-Qwen 2.5. Across benchmarks including AIME25, LiveCodeBench, GSM8K, MMLU, Qasper, RULER, and MATH500, KVTC consistently outperforms inference-time baselines such as token eviction, quantization, and SVD-based methods, delivering substantially higher compression ratios. These results support KVTC as a practical building block for memory-efficient LLM serving with reusable KV caches.
abstract_zh: 规模化提供大语言模型（LLM）推理服务需要高效的键值（KV）缓存管理。KV缓存可在多轮对话中通过共享前缀提示词复用，这类共享前缀提示在迭代式代码编辑和对话场景中十分常见。然而，陈旧缓存会消耗稀缺的GPU显存，需要被卸载，或是迫使系统重新计算缓存。我们提出KVTC这一轻量级变换编码器，可对KV缓存进行压缩，实现GPU内存和GPU外存储的紧凑存储。KVTC借鉴经典媒体压缩技术，结合了基于主成分分析（PCA）的特征去相关、自适应量化和熵编码三种技术。它仅需短暂的初始校准，且不会修改模型参数。通过利用KV缓存中的冗余信息，KVTC可实现最高20倍的压缩比，同时在Llama 3.1、Mistral-NeMo和R1-Qwen 2.5模型上保持了推理能力和长上下文准确率。在AIME25、LiveCodeBench、GSM8K、MMLU、Qasper、RULER和MATH500等基准测试中，KVTC的表现始终优于推理阶段的基线方法，包括令牌驱逐、量化和基于奇异值分解（SVD）的方法，可实现显著更高的压缩比。这些结果表明，KVTC可作为支持可复用KV缓存的内存高效型LLM推理服务的实用组件。
notes: 论文核心是针对 LLM 推理中的 KV cache 做压缩与存储优化，最匹配上下文与缓存优化，其次是模型压缩；未直接聚焦系统调度或算子内核实现。

title: World Models Should Prioritize the Unification of Physical and Social Dynamics
title_zh: 世界模型应优先统一物理与社会动态
abstract: World models, which explicitly learn environmental dynamics to lay the foundation for planning, reasoning, and decision-making, are rapidly advancing in predicting both physical dynamics and aspects of social behavior, yet predominantly in separate silos. This division results in a systemic failure to model the crucial interplay between physical environments and social constructs, rendering current models fundamentally incapable of adequately addressing the true complexity of real-world systems where physical and social realities are inextricably intertwined. This position paper argues that the systematic, bidirectional unification of physical and social predictive capabilities is the next crucial frontier for world model development. We contend that comprehensive world models must holistically integrate objective physical laws with the subjective, evolving, and context-dependent nature of social dynamics. Such unification is paramount for AI to robustly navigate complex real-world challenges and achieve more generalizable intelligence. This paper substantiates this imperative by analyzing core impediments to integration, proposing foundational guiding principles (ACE Principles), and outlining a conceptual framework alongside a research roadmap towards truly holistic world models.
abstract_zh: 世界模型通过显式学习环境动态，为规划、推理和决策奠定基础，在预测物理动态和社会行为某些方面都在迅速进步，但迄今主要仍是在彼此分离的孤岛中发展。这种分裂导致系统性地无法建模物理环境与社会结构之间至关重要的相互作用，使当前模型从根本上无法充分应对现实世界系统的真实复杂性，因为在这些系统中，物理现实与社会现实是密不可分地交织在一起的。本文这篇立场论文认为，对物理与社会预测能力进行系统性的、双向的统一，是世界模型发展的下一个关键前沿。我们主张，全面的世界模型必须整体性地整合客观的物理规律，以及社会动态所具有的主观性、演化性和情境依赖性。这样的统一对于人工智能稳健地应对复杂现实世界挑战并实现更具泛化能力的智能至关重要。本文通过分析整合面临的核心障碍、提出基础性指导原则（ACE 原则），并勾勒通向真正整体性世界模型的概念框架及研究路线图，来论证这一迫切需求。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: UNIVERSAL AND EFFICIENT LOADING BALANCING FOR RL TRAINING OF LARGE MULTIMODAL MODELS
title_zh: 面向大型多模态模型强化学习训练的通用高效负载均衡
abstract:
Reinforcement learning (RL) is crucial for aligning Vision-Language Models (VLMs), but its practical application is hampered by significant system-level bottlenecks. The typical RL pipeline, encompassing data loading, inference-based rollouts, and model updates, suffers from severe inefficiencies when applied to VLMs due to the extreme heterogeneity of multimodal data. Centralized data loading creates I/O bottlenecks with large media files, while variations in sequence length across text, image, and video inputs lead to critical load imbalance during computation, leaving expensive GPU resources underutilized. Existing systems either focus on text-only RL or employ general load-balancing techniques that are incompatible with the small-batch, iterative nature of RL training.

To address these challenges, we present FlexRL, a holistic system designed to optimize the end-to-end VLM RL pipeline. FlexRL introduces two core contributions: (1) a \textbf{Decentralized Data Pipeline} that parallelizes data fetching and preprocessing across worker nodes, facilitates metadata-only scheduling on the single controller, eliminating the central bottleneck and accelerating data-intensive stages; and (2) a novel \textbf{Hybrid Sequence Sharding} mechanism that partitions sequences into fine-grained chunks. This enables sub-sequence level load balancing for both inference and training, effectively mitigating workload skew. Our evaluation on a 128-GPU cluster shows that FlexRL significantly improves training efficiency by 4.2~7.7x end-to-end speedups compared to production baselines, enabling more efficient and scalable RL for large multimodal models.
abstract_zh:
强化学习（RL）是视觉语言模型（VLM）对齐的关键技术，但其实际应用受严重的系统级瓶颈制约。典型的强化学习工作流包含数据加载、基于推理的轨迹推演、模型更新三个环节，由于多模态数据存在极强的异构性，该工作流应用于视觉语言模型时效率极低。集中式数据加载会因大体积媒体文件产生I/O瓶颈，而文本、图像、视频输入的序列长度差异会导致计算过程中出现严重的负载失衡，造成昂贵的GPU资源利用率不足。现有系统要么仅面向纯文本场景的强化学习设计，要么采用的通用负载均衡技术与强化学习训练小批量、迭代式的特性不兼容。
为应对上述挑战，我们提出了FlexRL，这是一套专为端到端优化视觉语言模型强化学习训练流程设计的整体系统。FlexRL包含两项核心贡献：（1）去中心化数据流水线，该架构在工作节点上并行执行数据拉取与预处理，仅在单一控制节点上进行元数据调度，消除了中心瓶颈，提升了数据密集型阶段的处理速度；（2）新型混合序列分片机制，可将序列切分为细粒度块，在推理和训练阶段均能实现子序列级的负载均衡，有效缓解工作负载倾斜。我们在128卡GPU集群上开展的评估结果显示，与生产环境基线方案相比，FlexRL的端到端训练效率显著提升了4.2~7.7倍，可为大型多模态模型提供更高效、可扩展性更强的强化学习训练支持。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: What Layers When: Learning to Skip Compute in LLMs with Residual Gates
title_zh: 何时激活哪些层：在 LLMs 中通过残差门学习跳过计算
abstract: We introduce GateSkip, a simple residual-stream gating mechanism that enables token-wise layer skipping in decoder-only LMs. Each Attention/MLP branch is equipped with a sigmoid-linear gate that compresses the branch’s output before it re-enters the residual stream. During inference we rank tokens by the gate and skip low-importance ones using a per-layer budget. While early-exit or router-based Mixture-of-Depths models are known to be unstable and need extensive retraining, our smooth, differentiable gates fine-tune stably on top of pretrained models. On long-form reasoning, we save up to 15% compute while retaining >90% of baseline accuracy. On instruction-tuned models we see accuracy gains at full compute and match baseline quality near 50% savings. The learned gates give insight into transformer information flow (e.g., BOS tokens act as anchors), and the method combines easily with quantization, pruning, and self-speculative decoding.
abstract_zh: 我们提出GateSkip，这是一种简单的残差流门控机制，能够在仅解码器大语言模型中实现逐令牌的层跳过。每个注意力/MLP分支都配备一个sigmoid线性门，在分支输出重新进入残差流之前对其进行压缩。推理阶段，我们根据门控值对令牌排序，再通过每层计算预算跳过低重要性的令牌。现有早期退出或基于路由的深度混合模型普遍存在不稳定、需要大量重新训练的问题，而我们的平滑可微分门控可在预训练模型的基础上稳定微调。在长文本推理任务上，我们最多可节省15%的计算量，同时保持90%以上的基线准确率。在指令微调模型上，我们的方法在满计算量下可实现准确率提升，在节省近50%计算量时性能可与基线水平持平。学习得到的门控为理解Transformer的信息流提供了新视角（例如序列起始（BOS）令牌起到锚点作用），且该方法可轻松与量化、剪枝、自投机解码等技术结合使用。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Reconstruct, Inpaint, Test-Time Finetune: Dynamic Novel-view Synthesis from Monocular Videos
title_zh: 从单目视频动态生成新视角：重建、修复与测试时微调
abstract: We explore novel-view synthesis for dynamic scenes from monocular videos. Prior approaches rely on costly test-time optimization of 4D representations or do not preserve scene geometry when trained in a feed-forward manner. Our approach is based on three key insights: (1) covisible pixels (that are visible in both the input and target views) can be rendered by first reconstructing the dynamic 3D scene and rendering the reconstruction from the novel-views and (2) hidden pixels in novel views can be ``inpainted" with feed-forward 2D video diffusion models. Notably, our video inpainting diffusion model (CogNVS) can be self-supervised from 2D videos, allowing us to train it on a large corpus of in-the-wild videos. This in turn allows for (3) CogNVS to be applied zero-shot to novel test videos via test-time finetuning. We empirically verify that CogNVS outperforms almost all prior art for novel-view synthesis of dynamic scenes from monocular videos.
abstract_zh: 我们探索了从单目视频中对动态场景进行新视角合成。现有方法要么依赖对4D表示进行代价高昂的测试时优化，要么在以前馈方式训练时无法保持场景几何结构。我们的方法基于三个关键见解：（1）共视像素（即在输入视角和目标视角中都可见的像素）可以通过先重建动态3D场景，再从新视角渲染该重建结果来生成；（2）新视角中的隐藏像素可以用前馈式2D视频扩散模型进行“补绘”。值得注意的是，我们的视频补绘扩散模型（CogNVS）可以从2D视频中进行自监督学习，这使我们能够在大量真实世界视频语料上训练它。这进一步使得（3）CogNVS能够通过测试时微调以零样本方式应用于新的测试视频。我们的实证验证表明，在从单目视频对动态场景进行新视角合成方面，CogNVS优于几乎所有现有最先进方法。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Portcullis: A Scalable and Verifiable Privacy Gateway for Third-Party LLM Inference
title_zh: Portcullis：面向第三方大语言模型推理的可扩展可验证隐私网关
abstract:
Businesses using third-party LLMs face privacy risks from exposed prompts. This paper presents Portcullis, a privacy-preserving gateway that safeguards sensitive data while supporting efficient and accurate LLM responses. Portcullis functions as a mediator, anonymizing sensitive data in prompts through parallel substitution, securely interacting with LLMs, and accurately reconstructing responses. It ensures all data processing occurs within secure encrypted memory. The gateway is attested to ensure trustworthiness and protect user privacy. Portcullis is the first of its kind, offering a verifiable and scalable privacy gateway for third-party LLM inferences.

We assess Portcullis's efficiency as a confidential container platform, demonstrating that its startup time scales linearly, ensuring scalability. Additionally, we evaluate its runtime performance using the PII and Enron Email Dataset. For masking and unmasking workloads, Portcullis outperforms Hide-and-Seek by 96x speed up, while maintaining equal or better false positive and false negative rates compared to existing solutions. On the Enron dataset, Portcullis achieves notably higher accuracy, surpassing Hide-and-Seek by over 0.1 for GPT-4o mini.
abstract_zh:
使用第三方大语言模型的企业面临提示词泄露引发的隐私风险。本文提出Portcullis，这是一款隐私保护网关，能够在支持大语言模型高效、准确返回响应的同时防护敏感数据。Portcullis充当中介角色，通过并行替换实现提示词中敏感数据的匿名化，与大语言模型安全交互，并准确重建响应内容。它可确保所有数据处理操作均在安全加密内存中完成。该网关经过可信证明，可保障自身可信度并保护用户隐私。Portcullis是同类方案中的首创，能够为第三方大语言模型推理提供可验证、可扩展的隐私网关。
我们将Portcullis作为机密容器平台评估其效率，实验结果表明其启动时间呈线性增长，可保障可扩展性。此外，我们使用个人可识别信息（PII）数据集与安然电子邮件数据集评估其运行时性能。在掩码与去掩码工作负载下，Portcullis的性能较Hide-and-Seek提升96倍，同时与现有解决方案相比，其假阳性率与假阴性率相当甚至更优。在安然数据集上，Portcullis的准确率显著更高，在针对GPT-4o mini的测试中，其表现较Hide-and-Seek高出0.1以上。
primary_research_object: AI 系统 / 基础设施
notes: 该工作主要面向第三方 LLM 推理场景下的隐私保护与可验证系统基础设施，属于 AI 系统/基础设施方向；虽提到并行替换与可扩展性，但不构成给定偏好标签中的优化主题。

title: Zero-Shot Learning for Materials Science Texts: Leveraging Duck Typing Principles
title_zh: 利用鸭子类型原则的材料科学文本零样本学习
abstract: Materials science text mining (MSTM), involving tasks like property extraction and synthesis action retrieval, is pivotal for advancing research by deriving critical insights from scientific literature. Descriptors, serving as essential task labels, often vary in meaning depending on researchers' usage purposes across different mining tasks. (e.g., 'Material' can refer to both synthesis components and participants in fuel cell experiment). This meaning difference makes it difficult for existing methods, fine-tuned to specific task, to handle the same descriptors in other tasks. To overcome above limitation, we propose MatDuck, a simple and effective approach for Zero-Shot MSTM by evoking material knowledge within Large Language Models (LLMs). Specifically, inspired by the Duck Typing principles in programming languages, we present a ClassDefinition-Style Descriptor generation method that evokes task-specific characteristics to address usage variation. Subsequently, we introduce code-style in-context learning for zero-shot tasks, reframing them into code to leverage LLMs' proficiency in code understanding. Extensive experiments on eight benchmark datasets demonstrate that MatDuck, as a plug-and-play approach, significantly improves the Zero-Shot MSTM performance of LLMs by an average of 11.3% across seven tasks.
abstract_zh: 材料科学文本挖掘（MSTM）包含属性提取、合成动作检索等任务，可从科学文献中提炼关键洞见，对推动研究发展至关重要。作为核心任务标签的描述符，其含义往往会根据不同挖掘任务中研究者的使用目的发生变化，例如“材料”既可以指合成组分，也可以指燃料电池实验中的参与物。这种含义差异使得针对特定任务微调的现有方法难以处理其他任务中出现的相同描述符。为克服上述局限，我们提出MatDuck，这是一种通过调用大语言模型（LLM）内部存储的材料知识实现零样本材料科学文本挖掘的简单有效方法。具体而言，受编程语言中鸭子类型原则的启发，我们提出了类定义风格描述符生成方法，该方法可调用任务专属特征以解决描述符的使用差异问题。随后我们为零样本任务引入了代码风格的上下文学习方法，将任务重写为代码形式，以利用大语言模型出色的代码理解能力。在8个基准数据集上开展的大量实验表明，作为即插即用方法的MatDuck可显著提升大语言模型的零样本材料科学文本挖掘性能，在7项任务上的性能平均提升11.3%。
notes: 论文主体是面向材料科学文本挖掘的零样本LLM方法，核心贡献是描述符构造与code-style in-context learning，属于LLM应用/提示方法，不属于解码、缓存、调度、算子、推理结构优化或模型压缩。

title: HiRED: Attention-Guided Token Dropping for Efficient Inference of High-Resolution Vision-Language Models
title_zh: HiRED：用于高分辨率视觉语言模型高效推理的注意力引导令牌删减
abstract: High-resolution Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are widely used in multimodal tasks to enhance accuracy by preserving detailed image information. However, these models often generate an excessive number of visual tokens due to the need to encode multiple partitions of a high-resolution image input. Processing such a large number of visual tokens poses significant computational challenges, particularly for resource-constrained commodity GPUs. To address this challenge, we propose High-Resolution Early Dropping (HiRED), a plug-and-play token-dropping method designed to operate within a fixed token budget. HiRED leverages the attention of CLS token in the vision transformer (ViT) to assess the visual content of the image partitions and allocate an optimal token budget for each partition accordingly. The most informative visual tokens from each partition within the allocated budget are then selected and passed to the subsequent Large Language Model (LLM). We showed that HiRED achieves superior accuracy and performance, compared to existing token-dropping methods. Empirically, HiRED-20% (i.e., a 20% token budget) on LLaVA-Next-7B achieves a 4.7x increase in token generation throughput, reduces response latency by 78%, and saves 14% of GPU memory for single inference on an NVIDIA TESLA P40 (24 GB). For larger batch sizes (e.g., 4), HiRED-20% prevents out-of-memory errors by cutting memory usage by 30%, while preserving throughput and latency benefits.
abstract_zh: 高分辨率视觉语言模型（VLM）广泛应用于多模态任务，可通过保留图像细节信息提升准确率。然而，由于需要对高分辨率输入图像的多个分块进行编码，这类模型通常会生成数量过多的视觉令牌。处理如此大量的视觉令牌会带来极大的计算挑战，在资源受限的消费级GPU上问题尤为突出。为解决这一难题，我们提出高分辨率早丢弃（HiRED）方法，这是一种即插即用的令牌丢弃方法，专为在固定令牌预算下运行而设计。HiRED利用视觉Transformer（ViT）中<[BOS_never_used_51bce0c785ca2f68081bfa7d91973934]>令牌的注意力权重评估各图像分块的视觉内容，据此为每个分块分配最优令牌预算，随后从每个分块中选取分配预算内信息量最高的视觉令牌，传入后续的大语言模型（LLM）。实验结果表明，与现有令牌丢弃方法相比，HiRED实现了更优异的准确率与性能。实证测试显示，在LLaVA-Next-7B上部署HiRED-20%（即设置20%的令牌预算）后，在NVIDIA TESLA P40（24GB）显卡上进行单样本推理时，令牌生成吞吐量提升至4.7倍，响应延迟降低78%，同时GPU显存占用减少14%。针对更大的批次规模（例如批次大小为4），HiRED-20%可将显存占用降低30%，避免出现显存不足错误，同时保留吞吐量与延迟方面的优势。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Ultra-High-Definition Dynamic Multi-Exposure Image Fusion via Infinite Pixel Learning
title_zh: 通过无限像素学习实现超高清动态多曝光图像融合
abstract: With the continuous improvement of device imaging resolution, the popularity of Ultra-High-Definition (UHD) images is increasing. Unfortunately, existing methods for fusing multi-exposure images in dynamic scenes are designed for low-resolution images, which makes them inefficient for generating high-quality UHD images on a resource-constrained device. To alleviate the limitations of extremely long-sequence inputs, inspired by the Large Language Model (LLM) for processing infinitely long texts, we propose a novel learning paradigm to achieve UHD multi-exposure dynamic scene image fusion on a single consumer-grade GPU, named Infinite Pixel Learning (IPL). The design of our approach comes from three key components: The first step is to slice the input sequences to relieve the pressure generated by the model processing the data stream; Second, we develop an attention cache technique, which is similar to the KV cache for infinite data stream processing; Finally, we design a method for attention cache compression to alleviate the storage burden of the cache on the device. In addition, we provide a new UHD benchmark to evaluate the effectiveness of our method. Extensive experimental results show that our method maintains high-quality visual performance while fusing UHD dynamic multi-exposure images in real-time (>40fps) on a single consumer-grade GPU.
abstract_zh: 随着设备成像分辨率的不断提升，超高清（UHD）图像的普及度持续增长。遗憾的是，现有动态场景多曝光图像融合方法均针对低分辨率图像设计，这导致它们在资源受限设备上生成高质量超高清图像时效率低下。为缓解极长序列输入带来的限制，受大语言模型（LLM）处理无限长度文本的启发，我们提出了一种可在单块消费级GPU上实现超高清多曝光动态场景图像融合的新型学习范式，命名为无限像素学习（IPL）。本方法的设计包含三个核心组件：第一步是对输入序列进行切分，以缓解模型处理数据流时产生的压力；其次，我们开发了一种注意力缓存技术，其作用与用于无限数据流处理的KV缓存类似；最后，我们设计了一种注意力缓存压缩方法，以缓解缓存在设备上带来的存储负担。此外，我们提供了一个全新的超高清基准测试集，用于评估所提方法的有效性。大量实验结果表明，我们的方法在单块消费级GPU上实时（帧率高于40fps）融合超高清动态多曝光图像的同时，能够保持高质量的视觉表现。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Recoverable Compression: A Multimodal Vision Token Recovery Mechanism Guided by Text Information
title_zh: 可恢复压缩：由文本信息引导的多模态视觉令牌恢复机制
abstract: With the advancement of large-scale language modeling techniques, large multimodal models combining visual encoders with large language models have demonstrated exceptional performance in various visual tasks. Most of the current large multimodal models achieve this by mapping visual features obtained from the visual encoder into a large language model and using them as inputs alongside text for downstream tasks. Therefore, the number of visual tokens directly affects the training and inference speed of the model. There has been significant work on token pruning for visual transformers, but for large multimodal models, only relying on visual information for token pruning or compression may lead to significant loss of important information. On the other hand, the textual input in the form of a question may contain valuable information that can aid in answering the question, providing additional knowledge to the model. To address the potential oversimplification and excessive pruning that can occur with most purely visual token pruning methods, we propose a text information-guided dynamic visual token recovery mechanism that does not require training. This mechanism leverages the similarity between the question text and visual tokens to recover visually meaningful tokens with important text information while merging other less important tokens, to achieve efficient computation for large multimodal models. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed method achieves comparable performance to the original approach while compressing the visual tokens to an average of 10\% of the original quantity.
abstract_zh: 随着大规模语言建模技术的发展，结合视觉编码器与大语言模型的大多模态模型已在各类视觉任务中展现出优异性能。当前大多数大多模态模型通过将视觉编码器提取得到的视觉特征映射至大语言模型，将其与文本共同作为下游任务的输入以实现上述性能。因此，视觉令牌的数量直接影响模型的训练与推理速度。目前已有大量针对视觉Transformer的令牌剪枝研究，但对于大多模态模型而言，仅依靠视觉信息进行令牌剪枝或压缩可能导致重要信息出现显著损失。另一方面，以问题形式存在的文本输入可能包含可辅助回答问题的有价值信息，可为模型提供额外知识。为解决绝大多数纯视觉令牌剪枝方法可能存在的过于简化、过度剪枝问题，我们提出了一种无需训练、由文本信息引导的动态视觉令牌恢复机制。该机制利用问题文本与视觉令牌之间的相似性，恢复携带重要文本信息、具备视觉意义的令牌，同时合并其余重要性更低的令牌，以实现大多模态模型的高效计算。实验结果表明，我们提出的方法在将视觉令牌平均压缩至原有数量的10%的同时，取得了与原始方法相当的性能。
