A minimal implementation is available at https://github.com/EleutherAI/nanoGPT-mup/tree/completep.
abstract_zh: 我们研究了在使用不同参数化方式时大语言模型训练的计算效率，这里的参数化方式指的是随着模型规模变化而调整模型和优化器超参数（HP）的规则。有些参数化方式无法在模型深度变化时迁移最优的基础超参数（如学习率），这就要求实践者在扩大规模时要么重新调优这些超参数（代价高昂），要么在无法重新调优时接受次优训练。即使它们能够实现超参数迁移，我们也通过理论分析表明，这些参数化方式仍可能处于惰性学习状态，在这种状态下，各层学习到的特征仅接近其线性化附近的特征，从而无法有效利用深度和非线性。最后，我们识别并采用了一种称为 CompleteP 的参数化方式，它同时实现了沿深度方向的超参数迁移，以及所有层中的非惰性学习。CompleteP 使更广泛的模型宽度/深度比能够保持计算高效，从而释放出更适合不同硬件设置和运行场景的模型形状。此外，相比先前最先进的方法，CompleteP 可将计算效率提升 12% 到 34%。所有实验均在 Cerebras CS-3 系统上运行。一个最小实现可见于 https://github.com/EleutherAI/nanoGPT-mup/tree/completep。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: FaithCoT-Bench: Benchmarking Instance-Level Faithfulness of Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
title_zh: FaithCoT-Bench：链式思维推理实例级可信度基准
abstract:
Large language models (LLMs) increasingly rely on Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting to improve problem-solving and provide seemingly transparent explanations. However, growing evidence shows that CoT often fail to faithfully represent the underlying reasoning process, raising concerns about their reliability in high-risk applications. Although prior studies have focused on mechanism-level analyses showing that CoTs can be unfaithful, they leave open the practical challenge of deciding whether a specific trajectory is faithful to the internal reasoning of the model. To address this gap, we introduce FaithCoT-Bench, a unified benchmark for instance-level CoT unfaithfulness detection. Our framework establishes a rigorous task formulation that formulates unfaithfulness detection as a discriminative decision problem, and provides FINE-CoT (Faithfulness instance evaluation for Chain-of-Thought), an expert-annotated collection of over 1,000 trajectories generated by four representative LLMs across four domains, including more than 300 unfaithful instances with fine-grained causes and step-level evidence. We further conduct a systematic evaluation of eleven representative detection methods spanning counterfactual, logit-based, and LLM-as-judge paradigms, deriving empirical insights that clarify the strengths and weaknesses of existing approaches and reveal the increased challenges of detection in knowledge-intensive domains and with more advanced models.
To the best of our knowledge, FaithCoT-Bench establishes the first comprehensive benchmark for instance-level CoT faithfulness, setting a solid basis for future research toward more interpretable and trustworthy reasoning in LLMs.
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）越来越依赖思维链（CoT）提示来提升问题求解能力，并提供看似透明的解释。然而，越来越多的证据表明，CoT 往往无法忠实地反映其潜在的推理过程，这引发了人们对其在高风险应用中可靠性的担忧。尽管先前研究已聚焦于机制层面的分析，表明 CoT 可能是不忠实的，但它们仍未解决一个实际挑战：如何判断某一条具体轨迹是否忠实于模型的内部推理。为弥补这一空白，我们提出了 FaithCoT-Bench，这是一个用于实例级 CoT 不忠实检测的统一基准。我们的框架建立了严格的任务形式化，将不忠实检测表述为一个判别式决策问题，并提供了 FINE-CoT（用于思维链的忠实性实例评估），这是一个由专家标注的数据集，包含来自四个代表性大型语言模型、覆盖四个领域的 1000 多条轨迹，其中包括 300 多个不忠实实例，并附有细粒度成因和步骤级证据。我们还对 11 种具有代表性的检测方法进行了系统评估，这些方法涵盖反事实、基于 logit 和 LLM-as-judge 范式，并由此得出经验性见解，阐明了现有方法的优势与弱点，同时揭示了在知识密集型领域以及面对更先进模型时，检测所面临的更大挑战。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Smooth Sailing: Lipschitz-Driven Uncertainty Quantification for Spatial Associations
title_zh: 顺风顺水：基于Lipschitz驱动的空间关联不确定性量化
abstract: Estimating associations between spatial covariates and responses — rather than merely predicting responses — is central to environmental science, epidemiology, and economics. For instance, public health officials might be interested in whether air pollution has a strictly positive association with a health outcome, and the magnitude of any effect. Standard machine learning methods often provide accurate predictions but offer limited insight into covariate-response relationships. And we show that existing methods for constructing confidence (or credible) intervals for associations can fail to provide nominal coverage in the face of model misspecification and nonrandom locations — despite both being essentially always present in spatial problems. We introduce a method that constructs valid frequentist confidence intervals for associations in spatial settings. Our method requires minimal assumptions beyond a form of spatial smoothness and a homoskedastic Gaussian error assumption. In particular, we do not require model correctness or covariate overlap between training and target locations. Our approach is the first to guarantee nominal coverage in this setting and outperforms existing techniques in both real and simulated experiments. Our confidence intervals are valid in finite samples when the noise of the Gaussian error is known, and we provide an asymptotically consistent estimation procedure for this noise variance when it is unknown.
abstract_zh: 估计空间协变量与响应之间的关联，而不仅仅是预测响应，是环境科学、流行病学和经济学中的核心问题。例如，公共卫生官员可能关心空气污染是否与某一健康结果存在严格为正的关联，以及该效应的大小。标准机器学习方法通常能够提供准确预测，但对协变量与响应关系的洞察有限。我们表明，现有用于构造关联置信区间（或可信区间）的方法，在模型设定错误和位置非随机的情况下，可能无法提供名义覆盖率，尽管这两种情况在空间问题中几乎总是存在。我们提出了一种方法，用于在空间场景下为关联构造有效的频率学派置信区间。我们的方法除了一种形式的空间平滑性和同方差高斯误差假设之外，只需极少的额外假设。特别地，我们不要求模型正确，也不要求训练位置与目标位置之间存在协变量重叠。我们的方法是首个在该设定下保证名义覆盖率的方法，并且在真实实验和模拟实验中都优于现有技术。当高斯误差的噪声已知时，我们的置信区间在有限样本下是有效的；当其未知时，我们提供了一种对该噪声方差渐近一致的估计过程。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Conformal Prediction in The Loop: A Feedback-Based Uncertainty Model for Trajectory Optimization
title_zh: 环路中的Conformal Prediction：一种用于轨迹优化的基于反馈的不确定性模型
abstract: Conformal Prediction (CP) is a powerful statistical machine learning tool to construct uncertainty sets with coverage guarantees, which has fueled its extensive adoption in generating prediction regions for decision-making tasks, e.g., Trajectory Optimization (TO) in uncertain environments. However, existing methods predominantly employ a sequential scheme, where decisions rely unidirectionally on the prediction regions, and consequently the information from decision-making fails to be fed back to instruct CP. In this paper, we propose a novel Feedback-Based CP (Fb-CP) framework for shrinking-horizon TO with a joint risk constraint over the entire mission time. Specifically, a CP-based posterior risk calculation method is developed by fully leveraging the realized trajectories to adjust the posterior allowable risk, which is then allocated to future times to update prediction regions. In this way, the information in the realized trajectories is continuously fed back to the CP, enabling attractive feedback-based adjustments of the prediction regions and a provable online improvement in trajectory performance. Furthermore, we theoretically prove that such adjustments consistently maintain the coverage guarantees of the prediction regions, thereby ensuring provable safety. Additionally, we develop a decision-focused iterative risk allocation algorithm with theoretical convergence analysis for allocating the posterior allowable risk which closely aligns with Fb-CP. Furthermore, we extend the proposed method to handle distribution shift. The effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method are demonstrated through benchmark experiments.
abstract_zh: 保形预测（CP）是一种强大的统计机器学习工具，可用于构造具有覆盖率保证的不确定性集合，这推动了其在为决策任务生成预测区域中的广泛应用，例如不确定环境中的轨迹优化（TO）。然而，现有方法主要采用一种顺序式方案，其中决策单向依赖于预测区域，因此决策过程中的信息无法反馈回来指导CP。本文提出了一种新颖的基于反馈的CP（Fb-CP）框架，用于具有覆盖整个任务时域联合风险约束的收缩时域轨迹优化。具体而言，我们通过充分利用已实现轨迹，提出了一种基于CP的后验风险计算方法，以调整后验可允许风险，并将其分配到未来时刻以更新预测区域。通过这种方式，已实现轨迹中的信息被持续反馈给CP，从而能够对预测区域进行有效的基于反馈的调整，并在理论上保证轨迹性能的在线改进。此外，我们从理论上证明，此类调整始终保持预测区域的覆盖率保证，从而确保可证明的安全性。进一步地，我们提出了一种面向决策的迭代风险分配算法，并给出了理论收敛性分析，用于分配与Fb-CP紧密一致的后验可允许风险。此外，我们将所提方法扩展到处理分布偏移的情形。基准实验表明了所提方法的有效性和优越性。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Think-While-Generating: On-the-Fly Reasoning for Personalized Long-Form Generation
title_zh: Think-While-Generating：面向个性化长文本生成的即时推理
abstract: Preference alignment has enabled large language models (LLMs) to better reflect human expectations, but current methods mostly optimize for population-level preferences, overlooking individual users. Personalization is essential, yet early approaches—such as prompt customization or fine-tuning—struggle to reason over implicit preferences, limiting real-world effectiveness. Recent “think-then-generate” methods address this by reasoning before response generation. However, they face challenges in long-form generation: their static one-shot reasoning must capture all relevant information for the full response generation, making learning difficult and limiting adaptability to evolving content. To address this issue, we propose **FlyThinker**, an efficient “think-while-generating” framework for personalized long-form generation. FlyThinker employs a separate reasoning model that generates latent token-level reasoning in parallel, which is fused into the generation model to dynamically guide response generation. This design enables reasoning and generation to run concurrently, ensuring inference efficiency. In addition, the reasoning model is designed to depend only on previous responses rather than its own prior outputs, which preserves training parallelism across different positions—allowing all reasoning tokens for training data to be produced in a single forward pass like standard LLM training, ensuring training efficiency. Extensive experiments on real-world benchmarks demonstrate that FlyThinker achieves better personalized generation while keeping training and inference efficiency.
abstract_zh: 偏好对齐使大型语言模型（LLM）能够更好地反映人类期望，但当前方法大多针对群体层面的偏好进行优化，忽视了个体用户。个性化至关重要，然而早期方法，如提示定制或微调，难以对隐式偏好进行推理，从而限制了其在现实世界中的有效性。近期的“先思考再生成”方法通过在响应生成之前进行推理来解决这一问题。然而，在长文本生成中，这类方法面临挑战：其静态的一次性推理必须为完整响应的生成捕获所有相关信息，这使得学习变得困难，并限制了其对不断演化内容的适应性。为了解决这一问题，我们提出了 FlyThinker，这是一种高效的“边思考边生成”个性化长文本生成框架。FlyThinker 使用一个独立的推理模型，并行生成潜在的词元级推理，然后将其融合到生成模型中，以动态引导响应生成。这种设计使推理与生成能够并发运行，从而保证了推理效率。此外，该推理模型被设计为仅依赖先前的响应，而不是其自身先前的输出，这保留了不同位置上的训练并行性，从而使训练数据的所有推理词元都能像标准 LLM 训练那样通过一次前向传播生成，保证了训练效率。在真实世界基准上的大量实验表明，FlyThinker 在保持训练和推理效率的同时，实现了更好的个性化生成。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Towards a Sharp Analysis of Learning Offline $f$-Divergence-Regularized Contextual Bandits
title_zh: 迈向对离线 $f$-散度正则化上下文赌博机的精细分析
abstract:
Many offline reinforcement learning algorithms are underpinned by $f$-divergence regularization, but their sample complexity *defined with respect to regularized objectives* still lacks tight analyses, especially in terms of concrete data coverage conditions. In this paper, we study the exact concentrability requirements to achieve the $\tilde{\Theta}(\epsilon^{-1})$ sample complexity for offline $f$-divergence-regularized contextual bandits. For reverse Kullback–Leibler (KL) divergence, arguably the most commonly used one, we achieve an
$\tilde{O}(\epsilon^{-1})$ sample complexity under single-policy concentrability for the first time via a novel pessimism-based analysis,
surpassing existing $\tilde{O}(\epsilon^{-1})$ bound under all-policy concentrability and $\tilde{O}(\epsilon^{-2})$ bound under
single-policy concentrability. We also propose a near-matching lower bound, demonstrating that a multiplicative dependency on single-policy concentrability is necessary to maximally exploit the curvature property of reverse KL. Moreover, for $f$-divergences with strongly convex $f$, to which reverse KL *does not* belong, we show that the sharp sample complexity $\tilde{\Theta}(\epsilon^{-1})$ is achievable even without pessimistic estimation or single-policy concentrability. We further corroborate our theoretical insights with numerical experiments and extend our analysis to contextual dueling bandits. We believe these results take a significant step towards a comprehensive understanding of objectives with $f$-divergence regularization.
abstract_zh: 许多离线强化学习算法都以 $f$-散度正则化为基础，但它们相对于正则化目标所定义的样本复杂度仍然缺乏紧致分析，尤其是在具体的数据覆盖条件方面。在本文中，我们研究了实现离线 $f$-散度正则化上下文赌博机的 $\tilde{\Theta}(\epsilon^{-1})$ 样本复杂度所需的精确可集中性要求。对于反向 Kullback–Leibler（KL）散度，也可以说是最常用的一种，我们实现了一个
primary_research_object: 强化学习 / 序列决策
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: PROS: Towards Compute-Efficient RLVR via Rollout Prefix Reuse
title_zh: PROS：通过重用Rollout前缀迈向计算高效的RLVR
abstract: Large reasoning models (LRMs) trained with *Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards* (RLVR) have achieved remarkable progress on complex reasoning tasks. However, RLVR heavily relies on on-policy rollout generation, whose cost grows rapidly with rollout length and model size, eventually becoming the training bottleneck. Our empirical analysis reveals that independent rollouts for the same query often share similar early steps, indicating substantial redundancy. To address this, we propose **Pros** (**P**refix **R**euse for **O**n-policy **S**ampling), a paradigm that reuses promising prefixes of historical rollouts in RLVR training. **Pros** appends these self-generated partial rollouts to the original queries to form *Augmented Queries*, which are then used as regular training inputs in subsequent iterations, thereby reducing redundant computation. To select training batch from augmented queries, **Pros** adopts a hierarchical Bayesian model to estimate their pass rates and prioritize those with the highest reward uncertainty. Experiments across diverse settings show that **Pros** consistently improves training efficiency and achieves higher accuracy than strong baselines. These results highlight **Pros** as a practical path toward scalable and compute-efficient RLVR.
abstract_zh: 采用*具有可验证奖励的强化学习*（RLVR）训练的大型推理模型（LRM）在复杂推理任务上取得了显著进展。然而，RLVR 严重依赖按策略的 rollout 生成，其成本会随着 rollout 长度和模型规模迅速增长，最终成为训练瓶颈。我们的经验分析表明，对于同一查询，彼此独立的 rollouts 往往共享相似的早期步骤，这说明其中存在大量冗余。为了解决这一问题，我们提出了 **Pros**（**P**refix **R**euse for **O**n-policy **S**ampling，按策略采样的前缀复用），这是一种在 RLVR 训练中复用历史 rollouts 中有前景前缀的范式。**Pros** 将这些模型自生成的部分 rollouts 追加到原始查询后，形成*增强查询*，并在后续迭代中将其作为常规训练输入使用，从而减少冗余计算。为了从增强查询中选择训练批次，**Pros** 采用分层贝叶斯模型来估计它们的通过率，并优先选择奖励不确定性最高的样本。跨多种设置的实验表明，**Pros** 能够稳定提升训练效率，并取得高于强基线的准确率。这些结果表明，**Pros** 是迈向可扩展且计算高效的 RLVR 的一条实用途径。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: CAS-Spec: Cascade Adaptive Self-Speculative Decoding for On-the-Fly Lossless Inference Acceleration of LLMs
title_zh: CAS-Spec：用于大语言模型即刻无损推理加速的级联自适应自推理解码
abstract:
Speculative decoding has become a widely adopted as an effective technique for lossless inference acceleration when deploying large language models (LLMs).
While on-the-fly self-speculative methods offer seamless integration and broad utility, they often fall short of the speed gains achieved by methods relying on specialized training.
Cascading a hierarchy of draft models promises further acceleration and flexibility, but the high cost of training multiple models has limited its practical application.
In this paper, we propose a novel Cascade Adaptive Self-Speculative Decoding (CAS-Spec) method which constructs speculative draft models by leveraging dynamically switchable inference acceleration (DSIA) strategies, including layer sparsity and activation quantization.
Furthermore, traditional vertical and horizontal cascade algorithms are inefficient when applied to self-speculative decoding methods.
We introduce a Dynamic Tree Cascade (DyTC) algorithm that adaptively routes the multi-level draft models and assigns the draft lengths, based on the heuristics of acceptance rates and latency prediction.
Our CAS-Spec method achieves state-of-the-art acceleration compared to existing on-the-fly speculative decoding methods, with an average speedup from $1.1\times$ to $2.3\times$ over autoregressive decoding across various LLMs and datasets.
DyTC improves the average speedup by $47$\% and $48$\% over cascade-based baseline and tree-based baseline algorithms, respectively.
CAS-Spec can be easily integrated into most existing LLMs and holds promising potential for further acceleration as self-speculative decoding techniques continue to evolve.
abstract_zh: 推测解码是大语言模型部署过程中广泛采用的一种有效无损推理加速技术。即时自推测方法虽具备无缝集成、适用范围广的优势，但其提速效果往往不及依赖专项训练的方法。级联分层草稿模型有望进一步提升加速效果与灵活性，但训练多个模型的高昂成本限制了其实际应用。本文提出一种新型级联自适应自推测解码方法，该方法利用包含层稀疏化、激活量化在内的动态可切换推理加速策略构建推测草稿模型。此外，传统的垂直与水平级联算法应用于自推测解码方法时效率较低。我们提出动态树级联算法，该算法基于接受率启发式规则与延迟预测，自适应路由多级草稿模型并分配草稿长度。与现有即时推测解码方法相比，本文提出的级联自适应自推测解码方法实现了当前最优的加速效果，在各类大语言模型与数据集上，其推理速度较自回归解码平均提升1.1倍至2.3倍。与基于级联的基准算法、基于树的基准算法相比，动态树级联算法的平均提速幅度分别高出47%与48%。级联自适应自推测解码方法可轻松集成至绝大多数现有大语言模型中，且随着自推测解码技术不断发展，其有望实现进一步的提速。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=25；命中=speculative decoding, self-speculative, draft model, latency, quantization, sparsity, inference, decoding

title: Preble: Efficient Distributed Prompt Scheduling for LLM Serving
title_zh: Preble：用于大语言模型服务的高效分布式提示调度
abstract:
Prompts to large language models (LLMs) have evolved beyond simple user questions.
For LLMs to solve complex problems, today’s practices are to include domain-specific
instructions, illustration of tool usages, and/or long context such as textbook chapters in
prompts. As such, many parts of prompts are repetitive across requests. Recent works
propose to cache and reuse KV state of prompts. However, they are all confined to a single-
GPU optimization, while production LLM serving systems are distributed by nature.

This paper proposes Preble, the first distributed LLM serving platform that targets and op-
timizes for prompt sharing. We designed a distributed scheduling system that co-optimizes
KV state reuse and computation load-balancing with a new scheduling algorithm and a
hierarchical scheduling mechanism. Our evaluation of Preble with real workloads and re-
quest arrival patterns on two open-source LLMs shows that Preble outperforms the SOTA
serving systems by 1.5× to 14.5× on average latency and 2× to 10× on p99 latency.
abstract_zh:
大语言模型（LLM）的提示已不再局限于简单的用户问题。为使大语言模型能够解决复杂问题，当前的通行做法是在提示中加入领域专属指令、工具使用说明，以及/或教科书章节这类长上下文内容。因此，不同请求的提示存在大量重复部分。近期研究提出缓存并复用提示的键值（KV）状态，但这类研究均仅限于单GPU优化，而生产环境的大语言模型服务系统本质上是分布式的。
本文提出Preble，这是首个针对提示共享进行优化的分布式大语言模型服务平台。我们设计了一套分布式调度系统，通过全新的调度算法和分层调度机制，对KV状态复用与计算负载均衡进行协同优化。我们在两个开源大语言模型上采用真实工作负载和请求到达模式对Preble开展评估，结果表明，Preble的性能优于现有最先进的服务系统，平均延迟的优化幅度达1.5倍至14.5倍，p99延迟的优化幅度达2倍至10倍。
notes: 仲裁采纳：codex

title: TESSAR: Geometry-Aware Active Regression via Dynamic Voronoi Tessellation
title_zh: TESSAR：通过动态 Voronoi 镶嵌实现几何感知的主动回归
abstract: Active learning improves training efficiency by selectively querying the most informative samples for labeling. While it naturally fits classification tasks–where informative samples tend to lie near the decision boundary–its application to regression is less straightforward, as information is distributed across the entire dataset. Distance-based sampling is commonly used to promote diversity but tends to overemphasize peripheral regions while neglecting dense, informative interior regions. To address this, we propose a Voronoi-based active learning framework that leverages geometric structure for sample selection. Central to our method is the Voronoi-based Least Disagree Metric (VLDM), which estimates a sample’s proximity to Voronoi faces by measuring how often its cell assignment changes under perturbations of the labeled sites. We further incorporate a distance-based term to capture the periphery and a Voronoi-derived density score to reflect data representativity. The resulting algorithm, *TESSAR* (TESsellation-based Sampling for Active Regression), unifies interior coverage, peripheral exploration, and representativity into a single acquisition score. Experiments on various benchmarks demonstrate that TESSAR consistently achieves competitive or superior performance compared to prior state-of-the-art baselines.
abstract_zh: 主动学习通过有选择地查询最具信息量的样本进行标注，从而提高训练效率。虽然它天然适用于分类任务——其中信息量大的样本往往位于决策边界附近——但其在回归中的应用则不那么直接，因为信息分布在整个数据集中。基于距离的采样常用于促进多样性，但往往会过度强调边缘区域，同时忽视稠密且信息丰富的内部区域。为了解决这一问题，我们提出了一种基于 Voronoi 的主动学习框架，利用几何结构进行样本选择。我们方法的核心是基于 Voronoi 的最小分歧度量（VLDM），它通过测量在已标注站点发生扰动时样本所属单元变化的频率，来估计样本与 Voronoi 面的接近程度。我们进一步引入一个基于距离的项以捕捉边缘区域，并结合一个源自 Voronoi 的密度分数来反映数据代表性。最终得到的算法 TESSAR（基于镶嵌的主动回归采样）将内部覆盖、边缘探索和代表性统一到单一的采集分数中。在多个基准上的实验表明，与先前最先进的基线方法相比，TESSAR 始终能够取得具有竞争力或更优的性能。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: AgentBreeder: Mitigating the AI Safety Risks of Multi-Agent Scaffolds via Self-Improvement
title_zh: AgentBreeder：通过自我改进缓解多智能体支架中的人工智能安全风险
abstract: Scaffolding Large Language Models (LLMs) into multi-agent systems often improves performance on complex tasks, but the safety impact of such scaffolds has not been thoroughly explored. We introduce AgentBreeder, a framework for multi-objective self-improving evolutionary search over scaffolds. We evaluate discovered scaffolds on widely recognized reasoning, mathematics, and safety benchmarks and compare them with popular baselines. In "blue" mode, we see a 79.4% average uplift in safety benchmark performance while maintaining or improving capability scores. In "red" mode, we find adversarially weak scaffolds emerging concurrently with capability optimization. Our work demonstrates the risks of multi-agent scaffolding and provides a framework for mitigating them. Code is available at \url{https://github.com/jrosseruk/AgentBreeder}.
abstract_zh: 将大型语言模型（LLM）构建为多智能体系统的脚手架通常会提升其在复杂任务上的表现，但这类脚手架对安全性的影响尚未得到充分探索。我们提出了 AgentBreeder，一个针对脚手架进行多目标、自我改进的进化搜索框架。我们在广泛认可的推理、数学和安全基准上评估所发现的脚手架，并将其与流行的基线方法进行比较。在“蓝队”模式下，我们观察到安全基准性能平均提升 79.4%，同时能力得分得以保持或提高。在“红队”模式下，我们发现在能力优化的同时，会并发涌现出对对抗性攻击较为脆弱的脚手架。我们的工作展示了多智能体脚手架的风险，并提供了一个用于缓解这些风险的框架。代码可在 https://github.com/jrosseruk/AgentBreeder 获取。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Quamba: A Post-Training Quantization Recipe for Selective State Space Models
title_zh: Quamba：用于选择性状态空间模型的后训练量化方案
abstract: State Space Models (SSMs) have emerged as an appealing alternative to Transformers for large language models, achieving state-of-the-art accuracy with constant memory complexity which allows for holding longer context lengths than attention-based networks. The superior computational efficiency of SSMs in long sequence modeling positions them favorably over Transformers in many scenarios. However, improving the efficiency of SSMs on request-intensive cloud-serving and resource-limited edge applications is still a formidable task. SSM quantization is a possible solution to this problem, making SSMs more suitable for wide deployment, while still maintaining their accuracy. Quantization is a common technique to reduce the model size and to utilize the low bit-width acceleration features on modern computing units, yet existing quantization techniques are poorly suited for SSMs. Most notably, SSMs have highly sensitive feature maps within the selective scan mechanism (i.e., linear recurrence) and massive outliers in the output activations which are not present in the output of token-mixing in the self-attention modules. To address this issue, we propose a static 8-bit per-tensor SSM quantization method which suppresses the maximum values of the input activations to the selective SSM for finer quantization precision and quantizes the output activations in an outlier-free space with Hadamard transform. Our 8-bit weight-activation quantized Mamba 2.8B SSM benefits from hardware acceleration and achieves a 1.72 $\times$ lower generation latency on an Nvidia Orin Nano 8G, with only a 0.9\% drop in average accuracy on zero-shot tasks. When quantizing Jamba, a 52B parameter SSM-style language model, we observe only a $1\%$  drop in accuracy, demonstrating that our SSM quantization method is both effective and scalable for large language models, which require appropriate compression techniques for deployment. The experiments demonstrate the effectiveness and practical applicability of our approach for deploying SSM-based models of all sizes on both cloud and edge platforms.
abstract_zh: 状态空间模型（SSM）已成为大语言模型领域中Transformer的极具吸引力的替代方案，它以恒定内存复杂度实现最先进的精度，相较于基于注意力的网络可支持更长的上下文长度。SSM在长序列建模中的优异计算效率使其在众多场景中比Transformer更具优势。然而，在请求密集的云服务场景和资源受限的边缘应用中提升SSM的效率仍是一项艰巨的任务。SSM量化是解决这一问题的可行方案，可让SSM在保持自身精度的同时更适配广泛部署的需求。量化是缩减模型体积、利用现代计算单元低比特加速特性的常用技术，但现有量化技术很难适配SSM。最突出的问题是，SSM的选择性扫描机制（即线性循环）内部存在高度敏感的特征图，且输出激活中存在大量异常值，这一问题在自注意力模块的令牌混合输出中并不存在。为解决该问题，我们提出一种静态8比特逐张量SSM量化方法，该方法抑制输入至选择性SSM的输入激活的最大值，以获得更精细的量化精度，同时通过哈达玛变换在无异常值空间中对输出激活进行量化。我们实现的8比特权重-激活量化Mamba 2.8B SSM可获得硬件加速收益，在英伟达Orin Nano 8G上的生成延迟降低1.72倍，而零样本任务的平均精度仅下降0.9%。对拥有520亿参数的SSM类语言模型Jamba进行量化时，我们观察到精度仅下降1%，这表明我们的SSM量化方法对于需要适配压缩技术以完成部署的大语言模型而言兼具有效性和可扩展性。实验证明，我们的方法支持将各类规模的基于SSM的模型部署到云平台和边缘平台上，具备有效性和实际应用价值。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Balancing Performance and Costs in Best Arm Identification
title_zh: 在最优臂识别中平衡性能与成本
abstract: We consider the problem of identifying the best arm in a multi-armed bandit model. Despite a wealth of literature in the traditional fixed budget and fixed confidence regimes of the best arm identification problem, it still remains a mystery to most practitioners as to how to choose an approach and corresponding budget or confidence parameter. We propose a new formalism to avoid this dilemma altogether by minimizing a risk functional which explicitly balances the performance of the recommended arm and the cost incurred by learning this arm. In this framework, a cost is incurred for each observation during the sampling phase, and upon recommending an arm, a performance penalty is incurred for identifying a suboptimal arm. The learner's goal is to minimize the sum of the penalty and cost. This new regime mirrors the priorities of many practitioners, e.g. maximizing profit in an A/B testing framework, better than classical fixed budget or confidence settings. We derive theoretical lower bounds for the risk of each of two choices for the performance penalty, the probability of misidentification and the simple regret, and propose an algorithm called DBCARE to match these lower bounds up to polylog factors on nearly all problem instances. We then demonstrate the performance of DBCARE on a number of simulated models, comparing to fixed budget and confidence algorithms to show the shortfalls of existing BAI paradigms on this problem.
abstract_zh: 我们考虑在多臂老虎机模型中识别最优臂的问题。尽管关于最佳臂识别问题在传统固定预算和固定置信度设定下已有大量文献，但对大多数实践者而言，如何选择一种方法及其对应的预算或置信参数仍然是个谜。我们提出一种新的形式化框架，通过最小化一个风险泛函来彻底避免这一两难处境，该风险泛函明确权衡所推荐臂的性能与学习该臂所产生的成本。在这一框架中，采样阶段的每次观测都会产生一项成本，而在推荐某个臂时，若识别出的是次优臂，则会产生性能惩罚。学习者的目标是最小化惩罚与成本之和。这一新设定比经典的固定预算或固定置信度设定更能反映许多实践者的优先目标，例如在 A/B 测试框架中最大化利润。我们针对两种性能惩罚选择分别推导了风险的理论下界，这两种选择是误识别概率和简单遗憾，并提出一种名为 DBCARE 的算法，使其在几乎所有问题实例上都能在多对数因子范围内匹配这些下界。随后，我们在若干模拟模型上展示了 DBCARE 的性能，并将其与固定预算和固定置信度算法进行比较，以说明现有 BAI 范式在这一问题上的不足。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Dual-IPO: Dual-Iterative Preference Optimization for Text-to-Video Generation
title_zh: Dual-IPO：用于文本到视频生成的双重迭代偏好优化
abstract: Recent advances in video generation have enabled thrilling experiences in producing realistic videos driven by scalable diffusion transformers. However, they usually fail to produce satisfactory outputs that are aligned to users' authentic demands and preferences. In this work, we introduce Dual-Iterative Optimization (Dual-IPO), an iterative paradigm that sequentially optimizes both the reward model and the video generation model for improved synthesis quality and human preference alignment. For the reward model, our framework ensures reliable and robust reward signals via CoT-guided reasoning, voting-based self-consistency, and preference certainty estimation. Given this, we optimize video foundation models with guidance of signals from reward model's feedback, thus improving the synthesis quality in subject consistency, motion smoothness and aesthetic quality, etc. The reward model and video generation model complement each other and are progressively improved in the multi-round iteration, without requiring tediously manual preference annotations. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed Dual-IPO can effectively and consistently improve the video generation quality of base model with various architectures and sizes, even help a model with only 2B parameters surpass a 5B one. Moreover, our analysis experiments and ablation studies identify the rational of our systematic design and the efficacy of each component.
abstract_zh: 视频生成的最新进展借助可扩展的扩散Transformer，实现了由其驱动的逼真视频生成体验。然而，它们通常无法产生令人满意、且与用户真实需求和偏好相一致的输出。在这项工作中，我们引入了双重迭代优化（Dual-IPO），这是一种迭代范式，通过依次优化奖励模型和视频生成模型，以提升合成质量并增强与人类偏好的对齐。对于奖励模型，我们的框架通过CoT引导推理、基于投票的自一致性以及偏好确定性估计，确保奖励信号可靠且稳健。在此基础上，我们利用奖励模型反馈信号的指导来优化视频基础模型，从而提升主体一致性、运动平滑性和美学质量等方面的合成质量。奖励模型与视频生成模型相辅相成，并在多轮迭代中逐步改进，而无需繁琐的人工偏好标注。全面的实验表明，所提出的Dual-IPO能够有效且稳定地提升具有不同架构和规模的基础模型的视频生成质量，甚至可以帮助一个仅有20亿参数的模型超过一个50亿参数的模型。此外，我们的分析实验和消融研究验证了我们系统性设计的合理性以及各个组件的有效性。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: ThinK: Thinner Key Cache by Query-Driven Pruning
title_zh: ThinK：通过基于查询的删减实现更精简的关键缓存
abstract:
Large Language Models (LLMs) have revolutionized the field of natural language processing, achieving unprecedented performance across a variety of applications. 
However, their increased computational and memory demands present significant challenges, especially when handling long sequences.
This paper focuses on the long-context scenario, addressing the inefficiencies in KV cache memory consumption during inference. 
Unlike existing approaches that optimize the memory based on the sequence length, we identify substantial redundancy in the channel dimension of the KV cache, as indicated by an uneven magnitude distribution and a low-rank structure in the attention weights.
In response, we propose ThinK, a novel query-dependent KV cache pruning method designed to minimize attention weight loss while selectively pruning the least significant channels. Our approach not only maintains or enhances model accuracy but also achieves a reduction in KV cache memory costs by over 20\% compared with vanilla KV cache eviction and quantization methods. For instance, ThinK integrated with KIVI can achieve $2.8\times$ peak memory reduction while maintaining nearly the same quality, enabling a batch size increase from 4$\times$ (with KIVI alone) to 5$\times$ when using a single GPU. Extensive evaluations on the LLaMA and Mistral models across various long-sequence datasets verified the efficiency of ThinK.  Our code has been made available at https://github.com/SalesforceAIResearch/ThinK.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）彻底变革了自然语言处理领域，在各类应用中取得了前所未有的性能表现。然而，其不断增长的计算与内存需求带来了显著挑战，处理长序列时尤为突出。本文聚焦长上下文场景，解决推理过程中键值（KV）缓存内存消耗效率低下的问题。现有方法多基于序列长度优化内存，与这些方法不同，我们发现KV缓存的通道维度存在大量冗余，注意力权重的幅值分布不均及低秩结构均可证明这一点。对此，我们提出ThinK，这是一种全新的依赖查询的KV缓存剪枝方法，旨在选择性剪枝最不重要的通道的同时，尽可能减小注意力权重损失。我们的方法不仅能够保持甚至提升模型精度，与原生KV缓存淘汰和量化方法相比，还可将KV缓存的内存开销降低20%以上。例如，与KIVI集成的ThinK可实现2.8倍的峰值内存缩减，同时几乎保持原有效果，在使用单GPU时，可将批量大小从仅使用KIVI的4倍提升至5倍。我们在多个长序列数据集上针对LLaMA和Mistral模型开展了广泛评估，验证了ThinK的效率。我们的代码已公开于https://github.com/SalesforceAIResearch/ThinK。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=26；命中=kv cache, cache compression, cache eviction, quantization, pruning, compression, inference, language model

title: You Point, I Learn: Online Adaptation of Interactive Segmentation Models for Handling Distribution Shifts in Medical Imaging
title_zh: 你指引，我学习：医疗影像中为应对分布漂移而对交互式分割模型进行的在线自适应
abstract:
Interactive segmentation uses real-time user inputs, such as mouse clicks, to iteratively refine model predictions. Although not originally designed to address distribution shifts, this paradigm naturally lends itself to such challenges. In medical imaging, where distribution shifts are common, interactive methods can use user inputs to guide models towards improved predictions.
Moreover, once a model is deployed, user corrections can be used to adapt the network parameters to the new data distribution, mitigating distribution shift. Based on these insights, we aim to develop a practical, effective method for improving the adaptive capabilities of interactive segmentation models to new data distributions in medical imaging.  Firstly, we found that strengthening the model's responsiveness to clicks is important for the initial training process. Moreover, we show that by treating the post-interaction user-refined model output as pseudo-ground-truth, we can design a lean, practical online adaptation method that enables a model to learn effectively across sequential test images. The framework includes two components: (i) a Post-Interaction adaptation process, updating the model after the user has completed interactive refinement of an image, and (ii) a Mid-Interaction adaptation process, updating incrementally after each click. Both processes include a Click-Centered Gaussian loss that strengthens the model's reaction to clicks and enhances focus on user-guided, clinically relevant regions. Experiments on 5 fundus and 4 brain‑MRI databases show that our approach consistently outperforms existing methods under diverse distribution shifts, including unseen imaging modalities and pathologies.
Code and pretrained models will be released upon publication.
abstract_zh: 交互式分割利用实时的用户输入（如鼠标点击）迭代地精炼模型预测。尽管最初并非为应对分布漂移而设计，但该范式天生适合处理这类难题。在医疗影像中，分布漂移经常出现，交互式方法可以利用用户输入引导模型生成更好的预测。此外，一旦模型部署后，用户的纠正可以用来调整网络参数以适应新的数据分布，从而缓解分布漂移。基于这些洞察，我们致力于开发一种实用且有效的方法，以提升交互式分割模型在医疗影像中适应新数据分布的能力。首先，我们发现增强模型对点击的响应能力对初始训练过程非常关键。进一步地，我们表明将交互后用户精炼的模型输出视为伪真值，可以设计一种精简、实用的在线自适应方法，使模型能够在连续测试图像中有效学习。该框架包括两个成分：（i）交互后自适应流程，在用户完成一张图像的交互精炼后更新模型，（ii）交互中自适应流程，在每次点击后逐步更新。两个流程都包括一个以点击为中心的高斯损失，用以增强模型对点击的响应并提高对用户指导的临床相关区域的关注。基于5个眼底和4个脑部MRI数据集的实验表明，我们的方法在包括未见过的成像模态与病变在内的不同分布漂移情形下始终优于现有方法。代码与预训练模型将在发表时公开。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Enhancing Graph Classification Robustness with Singular Pooling
title_zh: 通过奇异池化提升图分类鲁棒性
abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have achieved strong performance across a range of graph representation learning tasks, yet their adversarial robustness in graph classification remains underexplored compared to node classification. While most existing defenses focus on the message-passing component, this work investigates the overlooked role of pooling operations in shaping robustness. We present a theoretical analysis of standard flat pooling methods (sum, average and max), deriving upper bounds on their adversarial risk and identifying their vulnerabilities under different attack scenarios and graph structures. Motivated by these insights, we propose Robust Singular Pooling (RS-Pool), a novel pooling strategy that leverages the dominant singular vector of the node embedding matrix to construct a robust graph-level representation. We theoretically investigate the robustness of RS-Pool and interpret the resulting bound leading to improved understanding of our proposed pooling operator. While our analysis centers on Graph Convolutional Networks (GCNs), RS-Pool is model-agnostic and can be implemented efficiently via power iteration. Empirical results on real-world benchmarks show that RS-Pool provides better robustness than the considered pooling methods when subject to state-of-the-art adversarial attacks while maintaining competitive clean accuracy.
abstract_zh: 图神经网络（GNN）在一系列图表示学习任务中取得了强劲性能，然而相较于节点分类，其在图分类中的对抗鲁棒性仍缺乏充分研究。尽管现有大多数防御方法都聚焦于消息传递组件，本文考察了池化操作在塑造鲁棒性方面这一被忽视的作用。我们对标准扁平池化方法（求和、平均和最大池化）进行了理论分析，推导出其对抗风险的上界，并识别了它们在不同攻击场景和图结构下的脆弱性。受这些发现启发，我们提出了鲁棒奇异池化（RS-Pool），这是一种新的池化策略，利用节点嵌入矩阵的主奇异向量来构建鲁棒的图级表示。我们从理论上研究了 RS-Pool 的鲁棒性，并对所得界进行了分析，从而加深了对所提出池化算子的理解。尽管我们的分析以图卷积网络（GCN）为中心，RS-Pool 具有模型无关性，并且可以通过幂迭代高效实现。在真实世界基准数据集上的实验结果表明，在遭受最先进的对抗攻击时，RS-Pool 相较于所考察的池化方法表现出更好的鲁棒性，同时保持了有竞争力的干净精度。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Follow-Your-Preference: Towards Preference-Aligned Image Inpainting
title_zh: 遵循你的偏好：迈向偏好对齐的图像修补
abstract: This paper investigates image inpainting with preference alignment. Instead of introducing a novel method, we go back to basics and revisit fundamental problems in achieving such alignment. We leverage the prominent direct preference optimization approach for alignment training and employ public reward models to construct preference training datasets. Experiments are conducted across nine reward models, two benchmarks, and two baseline models with varying structures and generative algorithms. Our key findings are as follows: (1) Most reward models deliver valid reward scores for constructing preference data, even if some of them are not reliable evaluators. (2) Preference data demonstrates robust trends in both candidate scaling and sample scaling across models and benchmarks. (3) Observable biases in reward models, particularly in brightness, composition, and color scheme, render them susceptible to cause reward hacking. (4) A simple ensemble of these models yields robust and generalizable results by mitigating such biases. Built upon these observations, our alignment models significantly outperform prior models across standard metrics, GPT-4 assessments, and human evaluations, without any changes to model structures or the use of new datasets. We hope our work can set a simple yet solid baseline, pushing this promising frontier. Our code is available at: https://github.com/shenytzzz/Follow-Your-Preference.
abstract_zh: 本文研究了具有偏好对齐的图像修复。我们没有提出一种新的方法，而是回归基础，重新审视实现此类对齐中的基本问题。我们利用主流的直接偏好优化方法进行对齐训练，并采用公开的奖励模型来构建偏好训练数据集。实验覆盖了九个奖励模型、两个基准以及两个具有不同结构和生成算法的基线模型。我们的关键发现如下：（1）大多数奖励模型都能为构建偏好数据提供有效的奖励分数，即使其中一些并不是可靠的评估器。（2）偏好数据在不同模型和基准上，在候选数量扩展和样本数量扩展两个方面都表现出稳健趋势。（3）奖励模型中可观测到的偏差，特别是在亮度、构图和配色方案方面的偏差，使其容易导致奖励黑客问题。（4）通过缓解这些偏差，对这些模型进行简单集成即可得到稳健且具有泛化性的结果。基于这些观察，我们的对齐模型在标准指标、GPT-4 评估和人工评估中均显著优于先前模型，同时无需改变模型结构，也无需使用新数据集。我们希望这项工作能够建立一个简单而扎实的基线，推动这一充满前景的前沿方向。我们的代码可在以下地址获取：https://github.com/shenytzzz/Follow-Your-Preference。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Efficiently Serving Large Multimodal Models Using EPD Disaggregation
title_zh: 使用EPD拆分高效部署大型多模态模型
abstract: Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) extend Large Language Models (LLMs) by handling diverse inputs such as images, audio, and video, but at the cost of adding a multimodal encoding stage that increases both computational and memory overhead. This step negatively affects key Service Level Objectives (SLOs), such as time to first token (TTFT) and time per output token (TPOT). We introduce Encode-Prefill-Decode (EPD) Disaggregation, a novel framework that separates the encoding, prefill, and decode stages onto dedicated resources. Unlike current systems, which bundle encoding and prefill together, our approach decouples these steps, unlocking new opportunities and optimizations. These include a mechanism to cache multimedia tokens for efficient transfer, a novel way to parallelize the encoding load within a request, a module for optimal resource allocation for disaggregated serving, and a novel role-switching method to handle changing workload characteristics. Experimental evaluations with popular LMMs show substantial gains in memory efficiency (up to 15× lower peak memory utilization), batch sizes (up to 22× larger), 10× more images per request, and 2.2× larger KV caches. Furthermore, it leads to significant improvements in SLO attainment (up to 90–100% improvement) and TTFT (up to 71% reduction), compared to systems that do not disaggregate. The code is available at [https://github.com/vbdi/epdserve](https://github.com/vbdi/epdserve).
abstract_zh: 大型多模态模型（LMM）对大语言模型（LLM）进行了扩展，可处理图像、音频、视频等多样化输入，但代价是新增了多模态编码阶段，会同时增加计算与内存开销。该步骤会对首令牌生成时间（TTFT）、单输出令牌耗时（TPOT）等关键服务水平目标（SLO）造成负面影响。我们提出编码-预填充-解码（EPD）拆分框架，这是一种将编码、预填充、解码阶段拆分至专用资源运行的新型框架。当前系统通常将编码与预填充绑定在一起，与这类系统不同，我们的方法将这些步骤解耦，解锁了新的优化空间。这些优化包括可实现高效传输的多媒体令牌缓存机制、对单请求内的编码负载进行并行处理的新方法、为拆分式服务实现最优资源分配的模块，以及可应对工作负载特征变化的新型角色切换方法。针对主流LMM的实验评估显示，该框架在内存效率（峰值内存利用率最高降低15倍）、批量大小（最高提升22倍）、单请求支持图像数量（提升10倍）、KV缓存大小（提升2.2倍）方面均实现了大幅提升。此外，与未采用拆分的系统相比，该框架还能显著改善SLO达标情况（提升幅度最高达90%–100%），且TTFT最高降低71%。代码可从https://github.com/vbdi/epdserve获取。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Difficulty–Diversity Collaborative Filtering for Data-Efficient LLM Fine-Tuning
title_zh: 面向数据高效 LLM 微调的难度-多样性协同过滤
abstract: The performance of fine-tuned language models is heavily influenced by the quality and quantity of their fine-tuning data. While scaling laws suggest that larger models benefit from more data during pretraining, the Less-is-More hypothesis highlights that downstream fine-tuning often requires only a small but high-quality dataset to effectively elicit a model’s pretrained knowledge. However, identifying such premium data, particularly in terms of difficulty and diversity, typically relies on human expertise, and existing methods offer limited guidance for automatic selection from large unannotated corpora. This work presents a novel quantitative framework that formalizes the interplay between question difficulty and diversity, and introduces *Difficulty–Diversity Collaborative Filtering* (DDCF): an automated approach that tailors data selection to the unique characteristics of each language model via collaborative filtering. By leveraging a small seed dataset to predict correctness across a large unannotated corpus, our method reduces the annotation cost by $100-200\times$, while maintaining downstream performance comparable to full-corpus fine-tuning.
abstract_zh: 微调语言模型的性能在很大程度上受到其微调数据质量和数量的影响。尽管规模定律表明，更大的模型在预训练阶段会受益于更多数据，但“少即是多”假说强调，下游微调通常只需要一个小而高质量的数据集，就能有效激发模型在预训练中获得的知识。然而，识别这类高质量数据，特别是在难度和多样性方面，通常依赖人类专家经验，而现有方法对于如何从大规模未标注语料中进行自动选择所提供的指导十分有限。本文提出了一个新颖的定量框架，将问题难度与多样性之间的相互作用形式化，并引入“难度-多样性协同过滤”（DDCF）：一种通过协同过滤根据每个语言模型的独特特性定制数据选择的自动化方法。通过利用一个小规模种子数据集来预测大规模未标注语料上的正确性，我们的方法将标注成本降低了100至200倍，同时保持了与基于完整语料微调相当的下游性能。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Constantly Improving Image Models Need Constantly Improving Benchmarks
title_zh: 不断进步的图像模型需要不断改进的基准
abstract:
Recent advances in image generation, often driven by proprietary systems like GPT-4o Image Gen, regularly introduce new capabilities that reshape how users interact with these models. Existing benchmarks often lag behind and fail to capture these emerging use cases, leaving a gap between community perceptions of progress and formal evaluation. To address this, we present ECHO, a framework for constructing benchmarks directly from real-world evidence of model use: social media posts that showcase novel prompts and qualitative user judgments. Applying this framework to GPT-4o Image Gen, we construct a dataset of over 35,000 prompts curated from such posts. Our analysis shows that ECHO (1) discovers creative and complex tasks absent from existing benchmarks, such as re-rendering product labels across languages or generating receipts with specified totals, (2) more clearly distinguishes state-of-the-art models from alternatives, and
(3) surfaces community feedback that we use to inform the design of metrics for model quality (e.g., measuring observed shifts in color, identity, and structure). Our website is at https://echo-bench.github.io.
abstract_zh: 图像生成领域的最新进展，通常由诸如 GPT-4o Image Gen 这样的专有系统推动，持续引入新的能力，重塑了用户与这些模型的交互方式。现有基准往往滞后，无法覆盖这些新出现的使用场景，导致社区对进展的认知与正式评测之间存在差距。为此，我们提出了 ECHO，一个直接基于模型真实使用证据来构建基准的框架：这些证据来自展示新颖提示词和用户定性判断的社交媒体帖子。将该框架应用于 GPT-4o Image Gen 时，我们构建了一个由此类帖子整理而成、包含超过 35,000 条提示词的数据集。我们的分析表明，ECHO（1）发现了现有基准中缺失的创造性和复杂任务，例如跨语言重新渲染产品标签，或生成具有指定总额的收据；（2）更清晰地区分了最先进模型与替代模型，且
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: GNN-as-Judge: Unleashing the Power of LLMs for Graph Few-shot Semi-supervised Learning with GNN Feedback
title_zh: GNN-as-Judge：在GNN反馈下释放LLM在图少量样本半监督学习中的威力
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown strong performance on text-attributed graphs (TAGs) due to their superior semantic understanding ability on textual node features. However, their effectiveness as predictors in the few-shot semi-supervised setting, where labeled nodes are rather limited, remains constrained since fine-tuning LLMs usually requires sufficient labeled data, especially when the TAG shows complex structural patterns. In essence, this paper targets two key challenges: (i) the difficulty of generating and selecting reliable pseudo labels on TAGs for LLMs, and (ii) the need to mitigate potential label noise when fine-tuning LLMs with pseudo labels. To counter the challenges, we propose a new framework, GNN-as-Judge, which can unleash the power of LLMs for few-shot semi-supervised learning on TAGs by incorporating the structural inductive bias of Graph Neural Networks (GNNs). Specifically, GNN-as-Judge introduces a collaborative pseudo-labeling strategy that first identifies the most influenced unlabeled nodes from labeled nodes, then exploits both the agreement and disagreement patterns between LLMs and GNNs to generate reliable labels. Furthermore, we develop a weakly-supervised LLM fine-tuning algorithm that can distill the knowledge from informative pseudo labels while mitigating the potential label noise. Experiments on different TAG datasets demonstrate that GNN-as-Judge significantly outperforms existing methods, especially under low-resource regimes.
abstract_zh: 由于对文本节点特征具有卓越的语义理解能力，大语言模型（LLM）在文本属性图（TAG）上已表现出强大的性能。然而，在少样本半监督设定下，由于标注节点相当有限，它们作为预测器的有效性仍然受限，因为微调 LLM 通常需要充足的标注数据，尤其是在 TAG 呈现复杂结构模式时。本质上，本文针对两个关键挑战：（i）为 LLM 在 TAG 上生成和选择可靠伪标签的困难；（ii）在使用伪标签微调 LLM 时需要减轻潜在标签噪声。为应对这些挑战，我们提出了一个新框架 GNN-as-Judge，它通过引入图神经网络（GNN）的结构归纳偏置，释放 LLM 在 TAG 上进行少样本半监督学习的能力。具体而言，GNN-as-Judge 引入了一种协同伪标签策略，首先从标注节点中识别出受影响最大的未标注节点，然后利用 LLM 和 GNN 之间的一致与分歧模式来生成可靠标签。此外，我们开发了一种弱监督的 LLM 微调算法，能够从信息丰富的伪标签中蒸馏知识，同时减轻潜在的标签噪声。在不同 TAG 数据集上的实验表明，GNN-as-Judge 显著优于现有方法，尤其是在低资源条件下。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: SolidGeo: Measuring Multimodal Spatial Math Reasoning in Solid Geometry
title_zh: SolidGeo：在立体几何中衡量多模态空间数学推理
abstract: Geometry is a fundamental branch of mathematics and plays a crucial role in evaluating the reasoning capabilities of multimodal large language models (MLLMs). However, existing multimodal mathematics benchmarks mainly focus on plane geometry and largely ignore solid geometry, which requires spatial reasoning and is more challenging than plane geometry. To address this critical gap, we introduce **SolidGeo**, the first large-scale benchmark specifically designed to evaluate the performance of MLLMs on mathematical reasoning tasks in solid geometry. SolidGeo consists of 3,113 real-world K–12 and competition-level problems, each paired with visual context and annotated with difficulty levels and fine-grained solid geometry categories. Our benchmark covers a wide range of 3D reasoning subjects such as projection, unfolding, spatial measurement, and spatial vector, offering a rigorous testbed for assessing solid geometry. Through extensive experiments, we observe that MLLMs encounter substantial challenges in solid geometry math tasks, with a considerable performance gap relative to human capabilities on SolidGeo. Moreover, we analyze the performance, inference effiency and error patterns of various models, offering insights into the solid geometric mathematical reasoning capabilities of MLLMs. We hope SolidGeo serves as a catalyst for advancing MLLMs toward deeper geometric reasoning and spatial intelligence. The dataset is released at https://huggingface.co/datasets/HarryYancy/SolidGeo/
abstract_zh: 几何学是数学的一个基础分支，在评估多模态大语言模型（MLLM）的推理能力方面发挥着关键作用。然而，现有的多模态数学基准主要关注平面几何，而在很大程度上忽视了需要空间推理、且比平面几何更具挑战性的立体几何。为弥补这一关键空白，我们提出了 SolidGeo，这是首个专门用于评估 MLLM 在立体几何数学推理任务上表现的大规模基准。SolidGeo 包含 3113 道真实世界的 K–12 及竞赛级题目，每道题都配有视觉上下文，并标注了难度等级和细粒度的立体几何类别。我们的基准覆盖了投影、展开图、空间度量、空间向量等广泛的三维推理主题，为评估立体几何提供了严格的测试平台。通过大量实验，我们观察到 MLLM 在立体几何数学任务中面临显著挑战，其在 SolidGeo 上的表现与人类能力相比存在相当大的差距。此外，我们还分析了各种模型的性能、推理效率和错误模式，为理解 MLLM 在立体几何数学推理方面的能力提供了洞见。我们希望 SolidGeo 能成为推动 MLLM 朝着更深层几何推理与空间智能发展的催化剂。该数据集发布于 https://huggingface.co/datasets/HarryYancy/SolidGeo/
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: OSTAR: Optimized Statistical Text-classifier with Adversarial Resistance
title_zh: OSTAR：具备对抗鲁棒性的优化统计文本分类器
abstract:
The advancements in generative models and the real-world attack of machine-generated text(MGT) create a demand for more robust detection methods. 
 The existing MGT detection methods for adversarial environments primarily consist of manually designed statistical-based methods and fine-tuned classifier-based approaches. 
 Statistical-based methods extract intrinsic features but suffer from rigid decision boundaries vulnerable to adaptive attacks, while fine-tuned classifiers achieve outstanding performance at the cost of overfitting to superficial textual feature.
 We argue that the key to detection in current adversarial environments lies in how to extract intrinsic invariant features and ensure that the classifier possesses dynamic adaptability.
 In that case,  we propose OSTAR, a novel MGT detection framework designed for adversarial environments which composed of a statistical  enhanced classifier and a Multi-Faceted Contrastive Learning(MFCL).
  In the  classifier aspect, our Multi-Dimensional Statistical Profiling (MDSP) module  extracts intrinsic difference between human and machine texts, complementing classifiers with useful stable features. 
  In the model optimization aspect, the MFCL strategy enhances robustness by contrasting feature variations before and after text attacks, jointly optimizing statistical feature mapping and baseline pre-trained models. 
  Experimental results on three public datasets under various adversarial scenarios demonstrate that our framework outperforms existing MGT detection methods, achieving state-of-the-art performance and robust against attacks.The code is available at https://github.com/BUPT-SN/OSTAR.
abstract_zh: OSTAR 提出一种面向对抗环境的机器生成文本检测框架，集成统计增强分类器与多面向对比学习（MFCL），解决现有方法决策边界僵化或过度拟合浅层特征的问题。MDSP 模块从多维统计角度提取人类与机器文本的本质差异，为分类器补充稳定的内蕴特征；MFCL 策略则通过在攻击前后对比特征变化，联合优化统计特征映射与预训练基线模型，使检测器具备动态适应能力。实验证明，在三个人工对抗环境下的公开数据集上，OSTAR 不仅超越现有 MGT 检测方法，还实现了最先进的性能与对攻击的鲁棒性。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Compositional Reasoning with Transformers, RNNs, and Chain of Thought
title_zh: 用 Transformers、RNN 和 Chain of Thought 进行组合式推理
abstract: It is understood that different neural network architectures are suited to different tasks, but is there always a single best architecture for a given task? We compare the expressive power of transformers, RNNs, and transformers with chain of thought tokens on a simple and natural class of tasks we term Compositional Reasoning Questions (CRQ). This family captures multi-step problems with tree-like compositional structure, such as evaluating Boolean formulas. We prove that under standard hardness assumptions, *none* of these three architectures is capable of solving CRQs unless some hyperparameter (depth, embedding dimension, and number of chain of thought tokens, respectively) grows with the size of the input. We then provide constructions for solving CRQs with each architecture. For transformers, our construction uses depth that is logarithmic in the problem size. For RNNs, logarithmic embedding dimension is necessary and sufficient, so long as the inputs are provided in a certain order. For transformers with chain of thought, our construction uses $n$ CoT tokens. These results show that, while CRQs are inherently hard, there are several different ways for language models to overcome this hardness. Even for a single class of problems, each architecture has strengths and weaknesses, and none is strictly better than the others.
abstract_zh: 人们普遍认为，不同的神经网络架构适用于不同的任务，但对于给定任务，是否总存在一种唯一最优的架构？我们比较了 transformers、RNN 以及带有思维链 token 的 transformers 在一类简单而自然的任务上的表达能力，这类任务我们称为组合推理问题（CRQ）。这一任务族刻画了具有树状组合结构的多步问题，例如对布尔公式求值。我们证明，在标准困难性假设下，这三种架构中没有任何一种能够求解 CRQ，除非某个超参数分别随输入规模增长（对应地是深度、嵌入维度以及思维链 token 的数量）。随后，我们给出了用每种架构求解 CRQ 的构造。对于 transformers，我们的构造使用了相对于问题规模呈对数增长的深度。对于 RNN，只要输入按某种特定顺序提供，对数级的嵌入维度就是必要且充分的。对于带有思维链的 transformers，我们的构造使用了 $n$ 个 CoT token。这些结果表明，尽管 CRQ 本质上是困难的，语言模型仍有几种不同方式可以克服这种困难。即使对于单一一类问题，每种架构也各有优劣，没有一种严格优于其他架构。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Quantifying Uncertainty in Error Consistency: Towards Reliable Behavioral Comparison of Classifiers
title_zh: 量化误差一致性中的不确定性：迈向可靠的分类器行为比较
abstract:
Benchmarking models is a key factor for the rapid progress in machine learning (ML) research. Thus, further progress depends on improving benchmarking metrics. A standard metric to measure the behavioral alignment between ML models and human observers is error consistency (EC). EC allows for more fine-grained comparisons of behavior than other metrics such as accuracy, and has been used in the influential Brain-Score benchmark to rank different DNNs by their behavioral consistency with humans.  Previously, EC values have been reported without confidence intervals. However, empirically measured EC values are typically noisy - thus, without confidence intervals, valid benchmarking conclusions are problematic. Here we improve on standard EC in two ways: First, we show how to obtain confidence intervals for EC using a bootstrapping technique, allowing us to derive significance tests for EC. Second, we propose a new computational model relating the EC between two classifiers to the implicit probability that one of them copies responses from the other. This view of EC allows us to give practical guidance to scientists regarding the number of trials required for sufficiently powerful, conclusive experiments.
Finally, we use our methodology to revisit popular NeuroAI-results. We find that while the general trend of behavioral differences between humans and machines holds up to scrutiny, many reported differences between deep vision models are statistically insignificant. Our methodology enables researchers to design adequately powered experiments that can reliably detect behavioral differences between models, providing a foundation for more rigorous benchmarking of behavioral alignment.
abstract_zh:
模型基准评测是机器学习（ML）研究快速进展的关键因素。因此，进一步的发展取决于改进基准评测指标。衡量机器学习模型与人类观察者之间行为一致性的一个标准指标是错误一致性（EC）。与准确率等其他指标相比，EC 能够对行为进行更细粒度的比较，并且已被用于具有影响力的 Brain-Score 基准中，根据不同深度神经网络（DNN）与人类的行为一致性对其进行排序。
此前，EC 数值在报告时并未附带置信区间。然而，经验测得的 EC 值通常具有噪声，因此如果没有置信区间，就难以得出有效的基准评测结论。本文从两个方面改进了标准 EC：第一，我们展示了如何使用自助法技术为 EC 获得置信区间，从而能够为 EC 推导显著性检验。第二，我们提出了一个新的计算模型，将两个分类器之间的 EC 与其中一个分类器复制另一个分类器响应的隐含概率联系起来。对 EC 的这种理解使我们能够就为获得具有足够统计效能和结论性的实验所需的试验次数，向科学家提供实用指导。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Stratify or Die: Rethinking Data Splits in Image Segmentation
title_zh: 分层抽样否则死路一条：重新思考图像分割中的数据划分
abstract: Random splitting of datasets in image segmentation often leads to unrepresentative test sets, resulting in biased evaluations and poor model generalization. While stratified sampling has proven effective for addressing label distribution imbalance in classification tasks, extending these ideas to segmentation remains challenging due to the multi-label structure and class imbalance typically present in such data. Building on existing stratification concepts, we introduce Iterative Pixel Stratification (IPS), a straightforward, label-aware sampling method tailored for segmentation tasks. Additionally, we present Wasserstein-Driven Evolutionary Stratification (WDES), a novel genetic algorithm designed to minimize the Wasserstein distance, thereby optimizing the similarity of label distributions across dataset splits. We prove that WDES is globally optimal given enough generations. Using newly proposed statistical heterogeneity metrics, we evaluate both methods against random sampling and find that WDES consistently produces more representative splits. Applying WDES across diverse segmentation tasks, including street scenes, medical imaging, and satellite imagery, leads to lower performance variance and improved model evaluation. Our results also highlight the particular value of WDES in handling small, imbalanced, and low-diversity datasets, where conventional splitting strategies are most prone to bias.
abstract_zh: 在图像分割中，对数据集进行随机划分往往会导致测试集缺乏代表性，从而造成评估偏差和模型泛化能力差。尽管分层采样已被证明能够有效解决分类任务中的标签分布不平衡问题，但由于这类数据通常具有多标签结构和类别不平衡，将这些思想扩展到分割任务仍然具有挑战性。基于现有的分层概念，我们提出了迭代像素分层（IPS），这是一种简单直接、面向标签感知、专为分割任务定制的采样方法。此外，我们提出了 Wasserstein 驱动的进化分层（WDES），这是一种新颖的遗传算法，旨在最小化 Wasserstein 距离，从而优化数据集各划分之间标签分布的相似性。我们证明了，在代数足够多的情况下，WDES 是全局最优的。利用新提出的统计异质性指标，我们将这两种方法与随机采样进行比较，发现 WDES 能够持续地产生更具代表性的划分。将 WDES 应用于多种分割任务，包括街景、医学影像和卫星图像，可降低性能方差并改进模型评估。我们的结果还凸显了 WDES 在处理小规模、不平衡和低多样性数据集时的特殊价值，因为传统划分策略在这些情况下最容易产生偏差。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Efficiently Scaling LLM Reasoning Programs with Certaindex
title_zh: 借助Certaindex高效扩展大语言模型推理程序
abstract: Test-time reasoning algorithms such as chain-of-thought, self-consistency, and MCTS enhance LLM problem-solving but can wastefully generate many tokens without improving accuracy. At the same time, we observe that these algorithms exhibit answer stabilization: their intermediate solutions often cease to change after a certain point, and further investment of compute does not change their final answer. To quantify this phenomenon, we introduce Certaindex, an algorithm-agnostic metric measuring this evolving stability, signaling when further computation is unlikely to alter the final result. Certaindex is lightweight, can accelerate reasoning program inference via early exit, and further enables dynamic token allocation, gang scheduling, and many opportunities when integrated with real-world LLM serving systems. To quantify real-world benefits, we built Certaindex as a scheduler into Dynasor, our reasoning-aware LLM serving system, and demonstrate up to 50\% compute savings and 3.3$\times$ higher throughput in real workloads with no accuracy drop. Our code is available at https://github.com/hao-ai-lab/Dynasor.git
abstract_zh: 思维链、自洽性、蒙特卡洛树搜索（MCTS）等测试时推理算法可提升大语言模型（LLM）的问题求解能力，但会生成大量无益于准确率提升的token，造成计算浪费。与此同时，我们观察到这类算法存在答案稳定现象：其中间解往往会在某一节点后停止变化，后续投入的计算资源不会改变最终答案。为量化这一现象，我们提出了Certaindex，这是一种与算法无关的指标，用于衡量这种动态演化的稳定性，指示后续计算何时不太可能改变最终结果。Certaindex轻量化程度高，可通过提前退出来加速推理程序的推断，在与实际落地的LLM服务系统集成后，还可进一步支持动态token分配、组调度等诸多功能。为量化实际应用收益，我们将Certaindex作为调度器集成到我们研发的推理感知LLM服务系统Dynasor中，实验表明，在真实工作负载下，该方案最多可节省50%的计算资源，吞吐量提升达3.3倍，且不会造成准确率下降。我们的代码可从https://github.com/hao-ai-lab/Dynasor.git获取。
notes: 调度专项得分=7；命中组=llm_serving；augment_batch=20260402-scheduling；augment_score=7

title: How Benchmark Prediction from Fewer Data Misses the Mark
title_zh: 少量数据的基准预测为何偏离目标
abstract: Large language model (LLM) evaluation is increasingly costly, prompting interest in methods that speed up evaluation by shrinking benchmark datasets. Benchmark prediction (also called efficient LLM evaluation) aims to select a small subset of evaluation points and predict overall benchmark performance from that subset. In this paper, we systematically assess the strengths and limitations of 11 benchmark prediction methods across 19 diverse benchmarks. First, we identify a highly competitive baseline: Take a random sample and fit a regression model on the sample to predict missing entries. Outperforming most existing methods, this baseline challenges the assumption that careful subset selection is necessary for benchmark prediction. Second, we discover that all existing methods crucially depend on model similarity. They work best when interpolating scores among similar models. The effectiveness of benchmark prediction sharply declines when new models have higher accuracy than previously seen models. In this setting of extrapolation, none of the previous methods consistently beat a simple average over random samples. To improve over the sample average, we introduce a new method inspired by augmented inverse propensity weighting. This method consistently outperforms the random sample average even for extrapolation. However, its performance still relies on model similarity and the gains are modest in general. This shows that benchmark prediction fails just when it is most needed: at the evaluation frontier, where the goal is to evaluate new models of unknown capabilities.
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）的评估成本日益高昂，这促使人们关注通过缩小基准数据集来加速评估的方法。基准预测（也称高效LLM评估）旨在选择一小部分评估点，并基于该子集预测整体基准表现。在本文中，我们系统评估了11种基准预测方法在19个多样化基准上的优势与局限。首先，我们识别出一个竞争力很强的基线方法：抽取一个随机样本，并在该样本上拟合回归模型以预测缺失条目。由于其表现优于大多数现有方法，这一基线对“基准预测必须进行精心的子集选择”这一假设提出了挑战。其次，我们发现所有现有方法都关键性地依赖于模型相似性。它们在相似模型之间插值分数时效果最好。当新模型的准确率高于先前见过的模型时，基准预测的有效性会急剧下降。在这种外推设定下，此前的方法没有一种能够稳定地优于对随机样本取简单平均。为了改进样本平均，我们提出了一种受增强逆倾向加权启发的新方法。即使在外推场景下，该方法也始终优于随机样本平均。然而，它的性能仍然依赖于模型相似性，而且总体上的提升幅度较为有限。这表明，基准预测恰恰在最需要它的时候失效：在评估前沿，目标是评估能力未知的新模型。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: RPM: Reasoning-Level Personalization for Black-Box Large Language Models
title_zh: RPM：面向黑盒大型语言模型的推理层个性化
abstract:
While black-box large language models are widely deployed, they produce generic outputs that overlook individual user preferences.
Current personalization methods are fundamentally limited to response-level personalization; they only match final outputs, failing to model the underlying reasoning that connects user behavior to responses. 
To address this, this work introduces reasoning-level personalization as a new paradigm and proposes RPM, the first systematic framework designed to guide the model’s reasoning process using structured rationales constructed from patterns in a user’s behavior.
RPM constructs a structured model of user behavior—built from response-influential features and statistical factors—to create personalized reasoning paths and retrieve beneficial examples for guiding inference through a feature-based retrieval mechanism. 
Extensive experiments across four diverse tasks demonstrate that RPM consistently outperforms existing response-level methods while simultaneously enhancing both personalization performance and interpretability, providing a promising direction for black-box LLM personalization.
abstract_zh: RPM 提出了一种推理层面的个性化新范式，以解决黑盒大语言模型在部署时普遍输出千篇一律、忽略用户偏好的问题。目前的个性化方法仅停留在响应层面，只匹配最终输出，无法建模连接用户行为与回答之间的潜在推理过程。为此，本工作提出 RPM——首个系统性框架，利用从用户行为模式中构建的结构化推理依据指导模型的推理过程。RPM 通过提取影响响应的特征与统计因子，构建用户行为的结构化模型，从而生成个性化的推理路径，并通过基于特征的检索机制挑选有益示例引导推理。跨越四种不同任务的大量实验表明，RPM 不仅在个性化效果上持续优于现有的响应级方法，而且在可解释性方面也有所提升，为黑盒大语言模型个性化提供了一个有希望的方向。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Data Selection Matters: Towards Robust Instruction Tuning of Large Multimodal Models
title_zh: 数据选择很重要：迈向大型多模态模型的鲁棒指令调优
abstract: Selecting a compact subset of visual instruction–following data has emerged as an effective way to align large multimodal models with human intentions while avoiding the high cost of full-dataset training. Yet we observe that both full-data training and existing state-of-the-art data selection methods tend to inherit underlying dataset biases such as position bias and spurious correlations, leading to biased model behaviors. To address this issue, we introduce ARDS, a robustness-aware targeted visual instruction-selection framework that explicitly mitigates these weaknesses, sidestepping the need for access to downstream data or time-consuming gradient computation. Specifically, we first identify the worst-case evaluation subgroups through visual and textual task-specific perturbations. The robust training mixture is then constructed by prioritizing samples that are semantically closer to these subgroups in a rich multimodal embedding space. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ARDS substantially boosts both robustness and data efficiency for visual instruction tuning. We also showcase that the robust mixtures produced with a smaller model transfer effectively to larger architectures. Our code and selected datasets that have been demonstrated transferable across models are available at https://github.com/xyang583/ARDS.
abstract_zh: 选择一个紧凑的视觉指令跟随数据子集，已成为一种有效方法，可在避免全量数据集训练高成本的同时，使大型多模态模型与人类意图对齐。然而，我们观察到，无论是全数据训练还是现有最先进的数据选择方法，都倾向于继承底层数据集偏差，例如位置偏差和伪相关性，从而导致模型产生有偏行为。为了解决这一问题，我们提出了 ARDS，这是一种面向鲁棒性的定向视觉指令选择框架，能够显式缓解这些弱点，同时无需访问下游数据，也无需进行耗时的梯度计算。具体而言，我们首先通过视觉和文本任务特定扰动来识别最坏情况下的评估子群体。随后，在丰富的多模态嵌入空间中，优先选择与这些子群体在语义上更接近的样本，以构建鲁棒的训练混合数据。大量实验表明，ARDS 在视觉指令微调中显著提升了鲁棒性和数据效率。我们还展示了，由较小模型生成的鲁棒混合数据能够有效迁移到更大的架构上。我们的代码以及已被证明可跨模型迁移的所选数据集可在 https://github.com/xyang583/ARDS 获取。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Who Matters Matters: Agent-Specific Conservative Offline MARL
title_zh: 谁重要就重要：面向智能体的保守离线多智能体强化学习
abstract:
Offline Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) enables policy learning from static datasets in multi-agent systems, eliminating the need for risky or costly environment interactions during training. A central challenge in offline MARL lies in achieving effective collaboration among heterogeneous agents under the constraints of fixed datasets, where \textbf{conservatism} is introduced to restrict behaviors to data-supported distributions. Agents with distinct roles and capabilities require individualized conservatism - yet must maintain cohesive team performance. However, existing approaches often apply uniform conservatism across all agents, leading to over-constraining critical agents and under-constraining others, which hampers effective collaboration.
To address this issue, a novel framework, \textbf{OMCDA}, is proposed, where the degree of conservatism is dynamically adjusted for individual agents based on their impact on overall system performance. The framework is characterized by two key innovations: (1) A decomposed Q-function architecture is introduced to disentangle return computation from policy deviation assessment, allowing precise evaluations of each agent's contribution; and (2) An adaptive conservatism mechanism is developed to scale constraint strength according to both behavior policy divergence and the estimated importance of agents to the system.
Experiments on MuJoCo and SMAC show OMCDA outperforms existing offline MARL methods, effectively balancing the flexibility and conservatism across agents while ensuring fair credit assignment and better collaboration.
abstract_zh: 离线多智能体强化学习（MARL）能够在多智能体系统中利用静态数据集进行策略学习，从而避免训练过程中与环境进行高风险或高成本的交互。离线 MARL 的一个核心挑战在于，在固定数据集的约束下实现异构智能体之间的有效协作，其中会引入保守性以将行为限制在数据支持的分布内。具有不同角色和能力的智能体需要个体化的保守性，同时还必须保持整体团队表现的一致性。然而，现有方法往往对所有智能体施加统一的保守性，这会导致对关键智能体约束过度，而对其他智能体约束不足，从而阻碍有效协作。
primary_research_object: 强化学习 / 序列决策
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: ShadowKV: KV Cache in Shadows for High-Throughput Long-Context LLM Inference
title_zh: ShadowKV：长上下文大模型推理中高吞吐量下的隐藏KV缓存
abstract: With the widespread deployment of long-context large language models (LLMs), there has been a growing demand for efficient support of high-throughput inference. However, as the key-value (KV) cache expands with the sequence length, the increasing memory footprint and the need to access it for decoding both result in low throughput when serving long-context LLMs. While various dynamic sparse attention methods have been proposed to accelerate inference while maintaining generation quality, they either fail to sufficiently reduce GPU memory usage or introduce significant decoding latency by offloading the KV cache to the CPU. We present ShadowKV, a high-throughput long-context LLM inference system that stores the low-rank key cache and offloads the value cache to reduce the memory footprint for larger batch sizes and longer sequences. To minimize decoding latency, ShadowKV employs an accurate KV selection strategy that reconstructs minimal sparse KV pairs on-the-fly. By evaluating ShadowKV on benchmarks like RULER, LongBench, and models such as Llama-3.1-8B and GLM-4-9B-1M, we demonstrate that it achieves up to 6$\times$ larger batch sizes and 3.04$\times$ higher throughput on an A100 GPU without sacrificing accuracy, even surpassing the performance achievable with infinite batch size under the assumption of infinite GPU memory.
abstract_zh: 随着长上下文大语言模型（LLM）的广泛部署，对高吞吐推理高效支持的需求不断增长。然而，由于键值（KV）缓存会随序列长度扩展，不断增加的内存占用以及解码时访问KV缓存的需求共同导致长上下文LLM服务时吞吐较低。尽管已提出多种动态稀疏注意力方法，用于在保持生成质量的同时加快推理速度，但这类方法要么无法充分降低GPU内存占用，要么会因将KV缓存卸载到CPU而引入显著的解码延迟。我们提出ShadowKV，一种高吞吐长上下文LLM推理系统，该系统存储低秩键缓存并卸载值缓存，以降低内存占用，从而支持更大的批大小与更长的序列。为了最小化解码延迟，ShadowKV采用精准的KV选择策略，可即时重构最少的稀疏KV对。我们在RULER、LongBench等基准测试以及Llama-3.1-8B、GLM-4-9B-1M等模型上对ShadowKV进行评估，结果表明，该系统在A100 GPU上无需牺牲精度即可实现最高6倍的批大小与3.04倍的吞吐量，其性能甚至超过了GPU内存无限假设下无限批大小可达到的性能。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=26；命中=kv cache, throughput, latency, serving, offloading, inference, decoding, generation

title: All Patches Matter, More Patches Better: Enhance AI-Generated Image Detection via Panoptic Patch Learning
title_zh: 所有补丁都重要，补丁越多越好：通过全景补丁学习提升 AI 生成图像检测
abstract:
The rapid proliferation of AI-generated images (AIGIs) highlights the pressing demand for generalizable detection methods. In this paper, we establish two key principles for AIGI detection task through systematic analysis:
**(1) All Patches Matter**, since the uniform generation process ensures that each patch inherently contains synthetic artifacts, making every patch a valuable detection source; and
**(2) More Patches Better**, as leveraging distributed artifacts across more patches improves robustness by reducing over-reliance on specific regions.
However, counterfactual analysis uncovers a critical weakness: naively trained detectors display **Few-Patch Bias**, relying disproportionately on *minority patches*.
We identify this bias to **Lazy Learner** effect, where detectors to limited patch artifacts while neglecting distributed cues.
To address this, we propose **Panoptic Patch Learning** framework, which integrates: 
(1) *Randomized Patch Reconstruction*, injecting synthetic cues into randomly selected patches to diversify artifact recognition; 
(2) *Patch-wise Contrastive Learning*, enforcing consistent discriminative capability across patches to ensure their uniform utilization.
Extensive experiments demonstrate that PPL enhances generalization and robustness across datasets.
abstract_zh: 我们系统性分析得出AI生成图像检测的两个原则：第一“每个图块都重要”，因为统一的生成流程让每个图块都包含合成痕迹，因此都具备检测价值；第二“图块越多越好”，更多图块上的分布式痕迹可以提升稳健性，降低对特定区域的依赖。然而反事实分析发现一个关键问题：任意训练的检测器存在“少图块偏差”，过度依赖少数图块，我们将其归因于“懒惰学习者”效应，即模型只关注有限的图块痕迹，而忽略其他分布式信息。为此，我们提出泛图块学习（Panoptic Patch Learning）框架，包含：其一，随机图块重建，通过在随机选取的图块中注入合成线索以丰富痕迹识别；其二，图块级对比学习，约束各图块具有一致的判别能力，从而确保图块均衡被利用。大量实验表明，PPL显著提升了模型在不同数据集上的泛化能力与稳健性。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: SpinQuant: LLM Quantization with Learned Rotations
title_zh: SpinQuant：通过学得旋转实现的LLM量化
abstract: Post-training quantization (PTQ) techniques applied to weights, activations, and the KV cache greatly reduce memory usage, latency, and power consumption of Large Language Models (LLMs), but may lead to large quantization errors when outliers are present. Rotating activation or weight matrices helps remove outliers and benefits quantization. In this work, we identify a collection of applicable rotation parameterizations that lead to identical outputs in full-precision Transformer architectures while enhancing quantization accuracy. In addition, we find that some random rotations lead to much better quantization than others, with an up to 13 points difference in downstream zero-shot reasoning performance. As a result, we propose SpinQuant, a novel approach that incorporates learned rotation matrices for optimal quantized network accuracy. With 4-bit quantization of weight, activation, and KV-cache, SpinQuant narrows the accuracy gap on zero-shot reasoning tasks with full precision to merely 2.9 points on the LLaMA-2 7B model, surpassing LLM-QAT by 19.1 points and SmoothQuant by 25.0 points. Furthermore, SpinQuant also outperforms concurrent work QuaRot, which applies random rotations to remove outliers. In particular, for LLaMA-3 8B models that are hard to quantize, SpinQuant reduces the gap to full precision by up to 45.1% relative to QuaRot. Code is available at https://github.com/facebookresearch/SpinQuant.
abstract_zh: 针对权重、激活值和KV缓存的训练后量化（PTQ）技术可大幅降低大语言模型（LLM）的内存占用、推理延迟与功耗，但存在异常值时可能导致较大的量化误差。旋转激活矩阵或权重矩阵有助于消除异常值，对量化性能有益。本研究中，我们识别出一系列适用的旋转参数化方案，这些方案在全精度Transformer架构中可得到完全一致的输出，同时能提升量化精度。此外，我们发现部分随机旋转的量化效果远优于其他方案，二者在下游零样本推理性能上的差距最高可达13个百分点。据此我们提出SpinQuant，这是一种引入可学习旋转矩阵以实现量化网络最优精度的新方法。在对权重、激活值和KV缓存均采用4位量化的设置下，SpinQuant将LLaMA-2 7B模型在零样本推理任务上与全精度模型的精度差距缩小至仅2.9个百分点，表现较LLM-QAT高出19.1个百分点，较SmoothQuant高出25.0个百分点。此外，SpinQuant的性能也优于采用随机旋转消除异常值的同期研究QuaRot。特别是对于难以量化的LLaMA-3 8B模型，与QuaRot相比，SpinQuant将其与全精度模型的精度差距相对缩小了最高45.1%。代码可从https://github.com/facebookresearch/SpinQuant获取。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=18；命中=kv cache, latency, quantization, quantized, 4-bit, language model, large language model, llm

title: FlashMD: long-stride, universal prediction of molecular dynamics
title_zh: FlashMD：长步长通用分子动力学预测
abstract: Molecular dynamics (MD) provides insights into atomic-scale processes by integrating over time the equations that describe the motion of atoms under the action of interatomic forces. Machine learning models have substantially accelerated MD by providing inexpensive predictions of the forces, but they remain constrained to minuscule time integration steps, which are required by the fast time scale of atomic motion. In this work, we propose FlashMD, a method to predict the evolution of positions and momenta over strides that are between one and two orders of magnitude longer than typical MD time steps. We incorporate considerations on the mathematical and physical properties of Hamiltonian dynamics in the architecture, generalize the approach to allow the simulation of any thermodynamic ensemble, and carefully assess the possible failure modes of a direct MD approach. We validate FlashMD’s accuracy in reproducing equilibrium and time‐dependent properties, using both system‐specific and general-purpose models, extending the ability of MD simulation to reach the long time scales needed to model microscopic processes of high scientific and technological relevance.
abstract_zh: 分子动力学（MD）通过对描述原子在原子间作用力作用下运动的方程进行时间积分，来揭示原子尺度过程的见解。机器学习模型通过提供低成本的力预测，已经显著加速了MD，但它们仍然受限于极小的时间积分步长，而这类步长是由原子运动的快速时间尺度所要求的。在这项工作中，我们提出了FlashMD，这是一种用于预测位置和动量在步幅上的演化的方法，其步幅比典型MD时间步长长一到两个数量级。我们在架构中纳入了对哈密顿动力学数学和物理性质的考量，将该方法推广为能够模拟任意热力学系综，并仔细评估了直接MD方法可能的失效模式。我们使用系统特定模型和通用模型，验证了FlashMD在再现平衡性质和时间依赖性质方面的准确性，从而扩展了MD模拟达到长时间尺度的能力，以建模具有重要科学和技术意义的微观过程。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: HiFC: High-efficiency Flash-based KV Cache Swapping for Scaling LLM Inference
title_zh: HiFC：用于扩展大模型推理的高效闪存键值缓存交换
abstract: Large‑language‑model inference with long contexts often produces key–value (KV) caches whose footprint exceeds the capacity of high‑bandwidth memory on a GPU. Prior LLM inference frameworks such as vLLM mitigate this pressure by swapping KV cache pages to host DRAM. However, the high cost of large DRAM pools makes this solution economically unattractive. Although offloading to SSDs can be a cost-effective way to expand memory capacity relative to DRAM, conventional frameworks such as FlexGen experience a substantial throughput drop since the data path that routes SSD traffic through CPU to GPU is severely bandwidth-constrained. To overcome these limitations, we introduce HiFC, a novel DRAM‑free swapping scheme that enables direct access to SSD-resident memory with low latency and high effective bandwidth. HiFC stores KV pages in pseudo-SLC (pSLC) regions of commodity NVMe SSDs, sustaining high throughput under sequential I/O and improving write endurance by up to 8$\times$. Leveraging GPU Direct Storage, HiFC enables direct transfers between SSD and GPU, bypassing host DRAM and alleviating PCIe bottlenecks. HiFC employs fine-grained block mapping to confine writes to high-performance pSLC zones, stabilizing latency and throughput under load. HiFC achieves inference throughput comparable to DRAM-based swapping under diverse long-context workloads, such as NarrativeQA, while significantly lowering the memory expansion cost of a GPU server system by 4.5$\times$ over three years.
abstract_zh: 长上下文大语言模型推理生成的键值（KV）缓存占用空间往往超出GPU高带宽内存的容量。vLLM等现有大语言模型推理框架通过将KV缓存页交换到主机动态随机存取存储器（DRAM）来缓解这一压力。然而，大容量DRAM池的高成本使得该方案在经济上缺乏吸引力。虽然相较于DRAM，卸载到固态硬盘（SSD）是一种高性价比的内存容量扩展方式，但FlexGen等传统框架会出现吞吐量大幅下降的问题，这是因为SSD流量经CPU路由到GPU的数据路径存在严重的带宽限制。为克服这些局限，我们提出HiFC，这是一种全新的无DRAM交换方案，可实现低延迟、高有效带宽的SSD驻留内存直接访问。HiFC将KV页存储在商用NVMe SSD的伪单级单元（pSLC）区域，可在顺序I/O下维持高吞吐量，同时将写入寿命最高提升8倍。借助GPU直连存储技术，HiFC可实现SSD与GPU之间的直接数据传输，绕过主机DRAM，缓解PCIe瓶颈。HiFC采用细粒度块映射将写入操作限制在高性能pSLC区域，从而在负载下稳定延迟和吞吐量。在NarrativeQA等各类长上下文工作负载下，HiFC的推理吞吐量与基于DRAM的交换方案相当，同时可将GPU服务器系统三年周期的内存扩展成本大幅降低4.5倍。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Step Rewards
title_zh: 具隐式步奖励的智能体强化学习
abstract:
Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly developed as autonomous agents using reinforcement learning (agentic RL) that reason and act in interactive environments.
However, sparse and sometimes unverifiable rewards make it extremely challenging to assign credit when training LLM agents that serve as a policy.
Recent work attempts to integrate process supervision into RL but suffers from biased annotation, reward hacking, high-variance from overly fine-grained rewards or failures when state overlap is rare.
We therefore introduce implicit step rewards for agentic RL (**iStar**), a general credit-assignment strategy that integrates seamlessly with standard RL algorithms without relying on additional rollouts or explicit step labels.
Particularly, we alternatively optimize an implicit process reward model (PRM) with the policy model to generate step rewards for each action via a multi-turn DPO objective. Theoretical analysis shows that this learning objective produces a step-wise reward function learned from trajectory preferences.
Then the implicit step rewards are used to compute step-level advantages, which are combined with trajectory (or episode)-level advantages for policy updates, creating a self-reinforcing training loop.
We evaluate our method on three challenging agent benchmarks, including WebShop and VisualSokoban, as well as open-ended social interactions with unverifiable rewards in SOTOPIA. 
Crucially, our method shows superior performance over frontier LLMs and strong RL baselines across domains, achieving state-of-the-art results with higher sample-efficiency and training stability.
Further analysis also demonstrates efficient exploration by **iStar** with increased rewards in both step- and episode-level while maintaining fewer steps to achieve task success.
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）正越来越多地以强化学习方式开发为自主体能在交互式环境中推理和行动的智能体。然而，当这些模型用作策略时，稀疏且有时无法验证的奖励使得训练过程中的信用分配极其困难。已有研究试图将过程监督融入强化学习，但常受限于偏向的标注、奖励欺骗、过于细粒度奖励带来的高方差或状态重叠稀少时失败的问题。为此，我们提出用于自主强化学习的隐式步奖励（**iStar**），这是一种无需额外 rollout 或显式步标签即可与标准强化学习算法顺利融合的通用信用分配策略。具体而言，我们交替优化隐式过程奖励模型（PRM）与策略模型，通过多轮 DPO 目标为每个动作生成步奖励。理论分析表明，该学习目标可从轨迹偏好中学习出逐步的奖励函数。随后将隐式步奖励用于计算逐步优势值，并与轨迹（或集）级优势值共同用于策略更新，形成自增强的训练闭环。我们在三个具有挑战性的智能体基准（包括 WebShop、VisualSokoban 以及具有无法验证奖励的开放式社会互动平台 SOTOPIA）上评估了该方法。关键在于，**iStar** 在多个领域均优于前沿大模型和强强化学习基线，达到更高样本效率和训练稳定性的最新水平。同时进一步分析表明，**iStar** 能在步级和集级上获得更高奖励的同时保持较少步骤以达成任务，体现出高效探索能力。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: R2-T2: Re-Routing in Test-Time for Multimodal Mixture-of-Experts
title_zh: R2-T2：面向多模态混合专家的测试时重路由
abstract:
In large multimodal models (LMMs), the perception of non-language modalities (e.g., visual representations) is usually not on par with the large language models (LLMs)' powerful reasoning capabilities, deterring LMMs' performance on challenging downstream tasks. 
This weakness has been recently mitigated by replacing the vision encoder with a mixture-of-experts (MoE), which provides rich, multi-granularity, and diverse representations required by different downstream tasks. The performance of multimodal MoE largely depends on its router, which reweights and mixes the representations of different experts for each input. However, we find that the end-to-end trained router does not always produce the optimal routing weights for every test sample. To bridge the gap, we propose a novel and efficient method ''**R**e-**R**outing in **T**est-**T**ime (R2-T2)'' that locally optimizes the vector of routing weights in test-time by moving it toward those vectors of the correctly predicted samples in a neighborhood of the test sample. We propose three R2-T2 strategies with different optimization objectives and neighbor-search spaces. R2-T2 consistently and significantly improves state-of-the-art LMMs' performance on challenging multimodal benchmarks of diverse tasks, without training any parameters in the base model. Our code can be accessed here.
abstract_zh: 在大多模态模型中，非语言模态（如视觉表征）的感知能力通常无法与大语言模型强大的推理能力相匹配，制约了大多模态模型在高难度下游任务上的性能。近期，研究人员通过将视觉编码器替换为混合专家结构缓解了这一缺陷，该结构能够提供不同下游任务所需的丰富、多粒度且多样化的表征。多模态混合专家模型的性能很大程度上取决于其路由模块，路由模块会针对每个输入对不同专家的表征进行重加权与融合。然而我们发现，经端到端训练的路由模块并非总能为每个测试样本生成最优路由权重。为弥补这一不足，我们提出了一种新颖高效的测试时重路由方法，即R2-T2，该方法在测试时将路由权重向量向测试样本邻域内预测正确样本的路由权重向量靠拢，从而实现路由权重向量的局部优化。我们提出了三种分别采用不同优化目标与邻居搜索空间的R2-T2策略。R2-T2能够在无需训练基础模型任何参数的前提下，持续且显著提升当前最先进的大多模态模型在覆盖多种任务的高难度多模态基准测试上的性能。我们的代码可通过此处获取。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Bridging Radiology and Pathology Foundation Models via Concept-Based Multimodal Co-Adaptation
title_zh: 通过基于概念的多模态共适应连接放射学与病理学基础模型
abstract: Pretrained medical foundation models (FMs) have shown strong generalization across diverse imaging tasks, such as disease classification in radiology and tumor grading in histopathology. While recent advances in parameter-efficient finetuning have enabled effective adaptation of FMs to downstream tasks, these approaches are typically designed for a single modality. In contrast, many clinical workflows rely on joint diagnosis from heterogeneous domains, such as radiology and pathology, where fully leveraging the representation capacity of multiple FMs remains an open challenge. To address this gap, we propose Concept Tuning and Fusing (CTF), a parameter-efficient framework that uses clinically grounded concepts as a shared semantic interface to enable cross-modal co-adaptation before fusion.
