By incorporating task-specific concepts that are relevant across modalities, CTF aligns radiology and pathology representations, thereby enhancing their complementarity and enabling interpretation. We further design a Global–Context–Shared Prompt (GCSP) mechanism, which employs a small set of learnable tokens to capture domain-specific priors, shared patient-level information, and cross-domain context. The resulting concept alignment scores from each modality are then fused to produce a final prediction. Extensive experiments demonstrate that CTF outperforms strong unimodal, latent-fusion, and adapter-based baselines (e.g., AUC 0.903 on TCGA-GBMLGG). Notably, CTF achieves these gains without finetuning the full FMs, requiring only 0.15\% additional parameters, thus highlighting the effectiveness of concept-based multimodal co-adaptation. Our code is anonymously available at: https://anonymous.4open.science/r/CTF-27C2.
abstract_zh: 预训练的医学基础模型（FM）已在多种成像任务中展现出很强的泛化能力，例如放射学中的疾病分类和组织病理学中的肿瘤分级。尽管参数高效微调的最新进展已使基础模型能够有效适应下游任务，但这些方法通常是为单一模态设计的。相比之下，许多临床工作流程依赖于来自异构领域的联合诊断，例如放射学和病理学，而如何充分利用多个基础模型的表征能力仍是一个尚未解决的挑战。为弥补这一空白，我们提出了概念调优与融合（CTF），这是一种参数高效的框架，它使用具有临床依据的概念作为共享语义接口，在融合之前实现跨模态协同适配。通过引入与各模态均相关的任务特定概念，CTF 对齐了放射学与病理学的表征，从而增强了它们的互补性并实现了解释性。我们进一步设计了一种全局-上下文-共享提示（GCSP）机制，该机制利用一小组可学习的标记来捕获领域特定先验、患者级共享信息以及跨领域上下文。随后，将每种模态得到的概念对齐分数进行融合，以生成最终预测。大量实验表明，CTF 优于强有力的单模态、潜在融合和基于适配器的基线方法（例如，在 TCGA-GBMLGG 上 AUC 达到 0.903）。值得注意的是，CTF 在无需微调完整基础模型的情况下实现了这些性能提升，仅需增加 0.15% 的参数，从而凸显了基于概念的多模态协同适配的有效性。我们的代码已匿名公开，地址为：https://anonymous.4open.science/r/CTF-27C2。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: CrossPL: Systematic Evaluation of Large Language Models for Cross Programming Language Interoperating Code Generation
title_zh: CrossPL：大型语言模型在跨编程语言互操作性代码生成中的系统评估
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have shown strong performance in single-language code generation, but how well they produce cross-programming-language (CPL) interoperating code, which is widely used in cross-platform and complex software systems, remains underexplored. Therefore, a benchmark for evaluating CPL interaction code generation is essential. However, Constructing such a benchmark is challenging owing to sparse interoperating code in real-world multi-programming-language projects, diverse Inter-process Communication (IPC) mechanisms, vast Foreign Function Interface (FFI) language pairs, and the difficulty of evaluation. To address this gap, we introduce CrossPL, the first benchmark for systematically assessing LLM performance of CPL code generation across two primary interoperation modes and 2534 tasks, specifically 1,982 IPC tasks spanning six languages and 522 Python–C FFI tasks. Its construction involved a review of CPL documentation, 156 finite state machines, and analysis of 19,169 multi-language GitHub repositories. Two LLM-based workflows are designed for automating the benchmark construction and evaluation, and assess 20 state-of-the-art LLMs. Results reveal clear limitations: the best model achieves only 19.5\% Pass@1 and 26.46\% Pass@5 on the FFI subset, in sharp contrast to the strong performance of these models on single-language benchmarks. These findings underscore the urgent need for improving LLMs regarding CPL interoperating code generation. The benchmark and code are available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/crosspl-2814/.
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）在单语言代码生成方面已表现出较强能力，但它们在生成跨编程语言（CPL）互操作代码方面的表现如何仍缺乏充分研究，而这类代码在跨平台和复杂软件系统中被广泛使用。因此，构建一个用于评估 CPL 交互代码生成的基准至关重要。然而，由于真实世界多编程语言项目中的互操作代码较为稀缺、进程间通信（IPC）机制多样、外部函数接口（FFI）语言对数量庞大，以及评估本身存在困难，构建这样的基准具有挑战性。为填补这一空白，我们提出了 CrossPL，这是首个用于系统评估 LLM 在 CPL 代码生成任务上表现的基准，覆盖两种主要互操作模式和 2534 个任务，具体包括跨越六种语言的 1982 个 IPC 任务以及 522 个 Python–C FFI 任务。其构建过程涉及对 CPL 文档的审查、156 个有限状态机的整理，以及对 19169 个多语言 GitHub 仓库的分析。我们设计了两套基于 LLM 的工作流，用于自动化基准构建与评估，并据此评测了 20 个最先进的 LLM。结果揭示了明显局限性：表现最好的模型在 FFI 子集上仅达到 19.5% 的 Pass@1 和 26.46% 的 Pass@5，这与这些模型在单语言基准上的强劲表现形成鲜明对比。这些发现凸显了提升 LLM 在 CPL 互操作代码生成方面能力的迫切需求。该基准和代码可在 https://anonymous.4open.science/r/crosspl-2814/ 获取。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: CONSIGN: Conformal Segmentation Informed by Spatial Groupings via Decomposition
title_zh: CONSIGN：通过分解利用空间分组指导的保形分割
abstract:
Most machine learning-based image segmentation models produce pixel-wise confidence scores that represent the model’s predicted probability for each class label at every pixel. While this information can be particularly valuable in high-stakes domains such as medical imaging, these scores are heuristic in nature and do not constitute rigorous quantitative uncertainty estimates.
Conformal prediction (CP) provides a principled framework for transforming heuristic confidence scores into statistically valid uncertainty estimates. However, applying CP directly to image segmentation ignores the spatial correlations between pixels, a fundamental characteristic of image data. This can result in overly conservative and less interpretable uncertainty estimates.
To address this, we propose CONSIGN (*Conformal Segmentation Informed by Spatial Groupings via Decomposition*), a CP-based method that incorporates spatial correlations to improve uncertainty quantification in image segmentation.
Our method generates meaningful prediction sets that come with user-specified, high-probability error guarantees.
It is compatible with any pre-trained segmentation model capable of generating multiple sample outputs.
We evaluate CONSIGN against two CP baselines across three medical imaging datasets and two COCO dataset subsets, using three different pre-trained segmentation models. Results demonstrate that accounting for spatial structure significantly improves performance across multiple metrics and enhances the quality of uncertainty estimates.
abstract_zh: 我们提出 CONSIGN（**通过分解利用空间分组的保形分割**），用于将启发式的像素级置信度分数转化为统计上有效的机制，用以量化图像分割的不确定性。传统的机器学习分割模型输出的置信度只代表每个像素对各类别的预测概率，但不包含严格的不确定性界。保形预测提供了从这些启发式分数出发构建高概率错误保障的框架，但直接应用在图像分割上会忽略像素间的空间关联——这是图像的核心特性之一——因而可能导致过于保守且难以解释的不确定性估计。为克服这一问题，CONSIGN 在 CP 体系下显式建模空间结构，通过将图像分解为空间分组并利用模型生成的多样输出样本，建立包含语义连贯区域的不确定性预测集，确保用户指定置信度下的错误上界。该方法可与任意能输出多个采样结果的预训练分割模型兼容。我们在三个人工智能医学图像数据集、两个 COCO 子集以及三种不同预训练分割模型上与两个 CP 基线方法对比，结果表明纳入空间结构显著提升了多个评估指标，生成更高质量的不确定性估计。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Readout Representation: Redefining Neural Codes by Input Recovery
title_zh: 读出表示：通过输入恢复重定义神经编码
abstract:
Sensory representation is typically understood through a hierarchical-causal framework where progressively abstract features are extracted sequentially. However, this causal view fails to explain misrepresentation, a phenomenon better handled by an informational and teleological view based on decodable content and downstream functions. This creates a tension: how does a system that abstracts away details preserve the fine-grained information needed for downstream functions? We propose readout representation to resolve this, defining representation by the information recoverable from features, rather than their causal origin.
Empirically, we show that inputs can be accurately reconstructed even from heavily perturbed mid-level features, demonstrating that a single input corresponds to a broad, redundant region of feature space, challenging the causal mapping perspective.
To quantify this property, we introduce representation size, a metric linked to model robustness and representational redundancy. Our framework offers a new lens for analyzing how both biological and artificial neural systems learn complex features while maintaining robust, information-rich representations of the world.
abstract_zh: 感觉表征通常通过一种分层因果框架来理解，在该框架中，越来越抽象的特征被依次提取。然而，这种因果观点无法解释错误表征这一现象；相较之下，基于可解码内容和下游功能的信息论与目的论观点更能处理这一现象。这带来了一个张力：一个将细节抽象掉的系统，如何保留下游功能所需的细粒度信息？我们提出读出表征来解决这一问题，将表征定义为能够从特征中恢复出的信息，而不是这些特征的因果起源。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: CMT-Benchmark: A Benchmark for Condensed Matter Theory Built by Expert Researchers
title_zh: CMT-Benchmark：由专家研究者构建的凝聚态物理理论基准
abstract:
Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable progress in coding and mathematical problem-solving; however, evaluation on advanced research-level problems in the hard sciences remains scarce. 
To fill this gap, we present \cmt, a dataset of 50 original problems covering condensed matter theory (CMT) at the level of an expert researcher. The topics cover analytical and computational approaches commonly used in quantum many-body physics as well as classical statistical mechanics. This dataset was designed and verified by a panel of expert researchers from around the world. 
We built the dataset through a collaborative environment that challenges the panel to write and refine difficult problems that the panel would like their research assistants to be able to solve, with topics including Hartree-Fock mean-field theory, exact diagonalization, quantum Monte Carlo, density matrix renormalization group, quantum statistical mechanics, classical statistical mechanics, and model building. We evaluate different LLMs by programmatically checking LLM-generated solutions against expert-supplied ground truth. 
For this, we developed machine-grading mechanisms that are suitable for advanced physics research problems. 
For example, we handle non-commuting operators that are essential for quantum many-body problems by symbolic manipulation and normal ordering. 
Our evaluations show that frontier models struggle with all of the problems in the dataset, highlighting a gap in the physical reasoning skills of current LLMs. Notably, experts identified strategies for creating increasingly difficult problems by interacting with the LLMs and exploiting common failure modes. 
While the highest-performing model, GPT5, correctly solves 30\% of the problems, average performance across 17 models (GPT, Gemini, Claude, DeepSeek, and Llama classes) is only 11.4$\pm$2.1\%.  Moreover, our benchmark contains 18 problems that {\it not a single one} of the 17 models can correctly solve, and 26 problems that are solved by {\it at most} one model. 
These currently unsolvable problems span the fields of Quantum Monte Carlo, Variational Monte Carlo, and Density Matrix Renormalization Group. 
The answers sometimes violate fundamental symmetries or have unphysical scaling dimensions. We believe that this benchmark set provides valuable guidance for the future development of language models, aiming to achieve the goal of AI research assistants and tutors.
abstract_zh: CMT-Benchmark 是一个由领域专家构建的凝聚态理论问题集合，共包含 50 个原创题目，涵盖量子多体物理与经典统计力学中常用的分析与计算方法，定位于研究级难度。该数据集由全球专家小组设计并验证，通过协作环境让专家出题、打磨艰深题目，聚焦 Hartree-Fock 平均场理论、精确对角化、量子蒙特卡洛、密度矩阵重整化群、量子统计力学、经典统计力学以及模型构建等主题。我们以程序化方式将各大语言模型的解答与专家提供的标准答案进行对比，并为高级物理研究题目开发了适用的机器评分机制，例如通过符号运算与归一顺序处理量子多体题目中的不可对易算符。结果显示最前沿模型在所有题目上仍捉襟见肘，暴露出当前大模型在物理推理上的空白。专家在与模型交互过程中识别出利用常见失败模式持续升级难度的策略。表现最好的 GPT5 仅能解出 30% 题目，而 17 个模型（包括 GPT、Gemini、Claude、DeepSeek、Llama 系列）平均准确率仅为 11.4±2.1%。此外，数据集中有 18 道题目没有任何一个模型能正确解出，另有 26 道题最多仅有一款模型成功，它们主要分布在量子蒙特卡洛、变分蒙特卡洛和密度矩阵重整化群等领域，部分回答甚至违反基本对称或呈现非物理的标度维度。我们认为这一基准集为语言模型未来的发展提供了重要引导，助力实现 AI 研究助理与辅导者的目标。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: LocMoE: A Low-overhead MoE for Large Language Model Training
title_zh: LocMoE：一种用于大语言模型训练的低开销混合专家模型
abstract: The Mixtures-of-Experts (MoE) model is a widespread distributed and integrated learning method for large language models (LLM), which is favored due to its ability to sparsify and expand models efficiently. However, the performance of MoE is limited by load imbalance and high latency of All-to-All communication, along with relatively redundant computation owing to large expert capacity. Load imbalance may result from existing routing policies that consistently tend to select certain experts. The frequent inter-node communication in the All-to-All procedure also significantly prolongs the training time. To alleviate the above performance problems, we propose a novel routing strategy that combines load balance and locality by converting partial inter-node communication to that of intra-node. Notably, we elucidate that there is a minimum threshold for expert capacity, calculated through the maximal angular deviation between the gating weights of the experts and the assigned tokens. We port these modifications on the PanGu-Σ model based on the MindSpore framework with multi-level routing and conduct experiments on Ascend clusters. The experiment results demonstrate that the proposed LocMoE reduces training time per epoch by 12.68% to 22.24% compared to classical routers, such as hash router and switch router, without impacting the model accuracy.
abstract_zh: 混合专家（MoE）模型是大语言模型领域广泛使用的分布式集成学习方法，凭借能够高效实现模型稀疏化与扩展的特性受到青睐。然而，MoE的性能受限于负载不均衡、全对全通信延迟高，以及专家容量过大带来的相对计算冗余问题。现有路由策略始终倾向于选择特定专家，可能引发负载不均衡。全对全流程中频繁的节点间通信也会显著延长训练时长。为缓解上述性能问题，我们提出一种兼顾负载均衡与局部性的新型路由策略，将部分节点间通信转换为节点内通信。值得注意的是，我们阐明了专家容量存在最小阈值，该阈值通过专家门控权重与被分配词元之间的最大角度偏差计算得出。我们将这些改进应用到基于MindSpore框架、采用多级路由的盘古-Σ模型上，并在昇腾集群上开展实验。实验结果表明，与哈希路由、交换路由等经典路由方式相比，本文提出的LocMoE在不影响模型精度的前提下，将单轮训练时长降低了12.68%至22.24%。
keywords: Natural Language Processing: NLP: Language models
Natural Language Processing: NLP: Interpretability and analysis of models for NLP
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: How hard is learning to cut? Trade-offs and sample complexity
title_zh: 学习裁剪有多难？折衷与样本复杂度
abstract: In the recent years, branch-and-cut algorithms  have been the target of data-driven approaches designed  to enhance the decision making in different phases of the algorithm such as branching, or the choice of cutting planes (cuts). In particular, for cutting plane selection two score functions have been proposed in the literature to evaluate the quality of a cut: branch-and-cut tree size and gap closed.  In this paper, we present new sample complexity lower bounds, valid for both scores. We show that for a wide family of classes $\mathcal{F}$ that maps an instance to a cut, learning over an unknown distribution of the instances to minimize those scores requires at least (up to multiplicative constants) as many samples as learning from the same class function $\mathcal{F}$ any generic target function (using square loss). Our results also extend to the case of learning from a restricted set of cuts, namely those from the Simplex tableau. To the best of our knowledge, these  constitute the first lower bounds for the learning-to-cut framework. We compare our bounds to known upper bounds in the case of neural networks and show they are nearly tight, suggesting that both scores (gap closed and tree size) are of comparable difficulty from a learning standpoint. Guided by this insight, we provide empirical evidence -- by using a  graph neural network cut selection evaluated on various integer programming problems -- that gap closed is a practical and effective proxy for minimizing the tree size. Although the gap closed score has been extensively used in the integer programming literature, this is the first principled analysis discussing both scores simultaneously both theoretically and computationally.
abstract_zh: 近年来，分支定界加割平面算法成为数据驱动方法的研究对象，这些方法旨在提升算法不同阶段中的决策质量，例如分支，或割平面（cuts）的选择。特别地，对于割平面选择，文献中提出了两种评分函数来评估一个割的质量：分支加割树的大小以及闭合的间隙。在本文中，我们给出了对这两种评分都成立的新的样本复杂度下界。我们证明，对于将一个实例映射到一个割的广泛函数类 $\mathcal{F}$，在实例的未知分布上进行学习以最小化这些评分，所需样本数至少（忽略常数因子）与从同一函数类 $\mathcal{F}$ 学习任意一般目标函数（使用平方损失）所需样本数一样多。我们的结果还扩展到了从受限割集合中学习的情形，即仅限于来自单纯形表的割。据我们所知，这些结果构成了学习选割框架中的首个下界。我们将这些下界与神经网络情形下已知的上界进行了比较，并表明它们几乎是紧的，这意味着从学习的角度看，这两种评分（闭合的间隙和树大小）具有相近的难度。在这一认识的指导下，我们还提供了经验性证据：通过使用一个图神经网络割选择器，并在多种整数规划问题上进行评估，表明闭合的间隙是最小化树大小的一个实用且有效的代理指标。尽管闭合的间隙评分已在整数规划文献中被广泛使用，但这是首次从理论和计算两个方面同时对这两种评分进行有原则的分析。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: SnapMoGen: Human Motion Generation from Expressive Texts
title_zh: SnapMoGen：从富有表现力的文本生成的人体动作
abstract: Text-to-motion generation has experienced remarkable progress in recent years. However, current approaches remain limited to synthesizing motion from short or general text prompts, primarily due to dataset constraints. This limitation undermines fine-grained controllability and generalization to unseen prompts. In this paper, we introduce SnapMoGen, a new text-motion dataset featuring high-quality motion capture data paired with accurate, \textit{expressive} textual annotations. The dataset comprises 20K motion clips totaling 44 hours, accompanied by 122 detailed textual descriptions averaging 48 words per description (vs. 12 words of HumanML3D). Importantly, these motion clips preserve original temporal continuity as they were in long sequences, facilitating research in long-term motion generation and blending. We also improve upon previous generative masked modeling approaches. Our model, MoMask++, transforms motion into \textbf{multi-scale} token sequences that better exploit the token capacity, and learns to generate all tokens using a single generative masked transformer. MoMask++ achieves state-of-the-art performance on both HumanML3D and OmniMotion benchmarks. Additionally, we demonstrate the ability to process casual user prompts by employing an LLM to reformat inputs to align with the expressivity and narration style of SnapMoGen.
abstract_zh: 近年来，文本到动作生成取得了显著进展。然而，当前方法仍然局限于根据简短或泛化的文本提示合成动作，这主要是由数据集限制所致。这一局限削弱了细粒度可控性以及对未见提示的泛化能力。本文提出了 SnapMoGen，这是一个新的文本-动作数据集，包含高质量的动作捕捉数据，并配有准确且富有表现力的文本标注。该数据集由 2 万个动作片段组成，总时长达 44 小时，并附带 122 条详细文本描述，每条描述平均 48 个词（相比之下，HumanML3D 为 12 个词）。重要的是，这些动作片段保留了其在长序列中的原始时间连续性，从而有助于长时程动作生成与动作融合的研究。我们还改进了先前的生成式掩码建模方法。我们的模型 MoMask++ 将动作转换为多尺度标记序列，以更好地利用标记容量，并使用单个生成式掩码 Transformer 学习生成所有标记。MoMask++ 在 HumanML3D 和 OmniMotion 基准上都达到了最先进的性能。此外，我们还展示了通过使用大语言模型将输入重构为与 SnapMoGen 的表现力和叙述风格相一致的形式，从而处理日常用户提示的能力。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Parameter Dynamics of Online Machine Learning and Test-time Adaptation
title_zh: 在线机器学习与测试时自适应中的参数动态
abstract: Pre-trained models based on deep neural networks hold strong potential for cross-domain adaptability. However, this potential is often impeded in online machine learning (OML) settings, where the breakdown of the independent and identically distributed (i.i.d.) assumption leads to unstable adaptation. While recent advances in test-time adaptation (TTA) have addressed aspects of this challenge under unsupervised learning, most existing methods focus exclusively on unsupervised objectives and overlook the risks posed by non-i.i.d. environments and the resulting dynamics of model parameters. In this work, we present a probabilistic framework that models the adaptation process using stochastic differential equations, enabling a principled analysis of parameter distribution dynamics over time. Within this framework, we find that the log-variance of the parameter transition distribution aligns closely with an inverse-gamma distribution under stable and high-performing adaptation conditions. Motivated by this insight, we propose Structured Inverse-Gamma Model Alignment (SIGMA), a novel algorithm that dynamically regulates parameter evolution to preserve inverse-gamma alignment throughout adaptation. Extensive experiments across diverse models, datasets, and adaptation scenarios show that SIGMA consistently enhances the performance of state-of-the-art TTA methods, highlighting the critical role of parameter dynamics in ensuring robust adaptation.
abstract_zh: 基于深度神经网络的预训练模型在跨领域适应性方面具有很强的潜力。然而，在在线机器学习（OML）场景中，独立同分布（i.i.d.）假设的失效常常会阻碍这种潜力的发挥，并导致适应过程不稳定。尽管测试时自适应（TTA）的最新进展已在无监督学习条件下解决了这一挑战的部分问题，但现有大多数方法仅专注于无监督目标，忽视了非独立同分布环境带来的风险以及由此产生的模型参数动态。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个概率框架，使用随机微分方程对适应过程进行建模，从而能够对参数分布随时间变化的动态进行有原则的分析。在该框架下，我们发现，在稳定且性能良好的适应条件下，参数转移分布的对数方差与逆伽马分布高度一致。受这一发现启发，我们提出了结构化逆伽马模型对齐（SIGMA），这是一种新算法，可在适应过程中动态调控参数演化，以始终保持与逆伽马分布的对齐。在多种模型、数据集和适应场景上的大量实验表明，SIGMA 能够持续提升最先进 TTA 方法的性能，凸显了参数动态在确保稳健适应中的关键作用。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Learning to Route: Per-Sample Adaptive Routing for Multimodal Multitask Prediction
title_zh: 学习路由：面向多模态多任务预测的逐样本自适应路由
abstract: We propose a unified framework for adaptive routing in multitask, multimodal prediction settings where data heterogeneity and task interactions vary across samples. We introduce a routing-based architecture that dynamically selects modality processing pathways and task-sharing strategies on a per-sample basis. Our model defines multiple modality paths, including raw and fused representations of text and numeric features, and learns to route each input through the most informative modality-task expert combination. Task-specific predictions are produced by shared or independent heads depending on the routing decision, and the entire system is trained end-to-end. We evaluate the model on both synthetic data and real-world psychotherapy notes, predicting depression and anxiety outcomes. Our experiments show that our method consistently outperforms fixed multitask or single-task baselines, and that the learned routing policy provides interpretable insights into modality relevance and task structure. This addresses critical challenges in personalized healthcare by providing per-subject adaptive information processing that accounts for data and task correlation heterogeneity.
abstract_zh: 我们针对多任务多模态预测场景中样本间数据异质性与任务交互存在差异的问题，提出了一种统一的自适应路由框架。我们设计了基于路由的架构，可逐样本动态选择模态处理路径和任务共享策略。我们的模型定义了多条模态路径，包括文本特征与数值特征的原始表征和融合表征，并学习将每个输入路由至信息量最高的模态-任务专家组合。根据路由决策，任务专属预测结果由共享或独立预测头输出，整个系统采用端到端方式训练。我们在合成数据和真实世界心理治疗记录数据集上评估模型，完成抑郁与焦虑结局预测任务。实验表明，我们的方法表现始终优于固定结构的多任务或单任务基线模型，且学习到的路由策略能够为模态相关性和任务结构提供可解释的洞见。该方法通过实现可兼顾数据与任务关联异质性的逐受试者自适应信息处理，解决了个性化医疗领域的关键挑战。
notes: 调度专项得分=4；augment_batch=20260402-scheduling；augment_score=4

title: GANQ: GPU-Adaptive Non-Uniform Quantization for Large Language Models
title_zh: GANQ：面向大语言模型的 GPU 自适应非均匀量化
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) face significant deployment challenges due to their substantial resource requirements. While low-bit quantized weights can reduce memory usage and improve inference efficiency, current hardware lacks native support for mixed-precision General Matrix Multiplication (mpGEMM), resulting in inefficient dequantization-based implementations. Moreover, uniform quantization methods often fail to capture weight distributions adequately, leading to performance degradation. We propose GANQ (GPU-Adaptive Non-Uniform Quantization), a layer-wise post-training non-uniform quantization framework optimized for hardware-efficient lookup table-based mpGEMM. GANQ achieves superior quantization performance by utilizing a training-free, GPU-adaptive optimization algorithm to  efficiently reduce layer-wise quantization errors. Extensive experiments demonstrate GANQ's ability to reduce the perplexity gap from the FP16 baseline compared to state-of-the-art methods for both 3-bit and 4-bit quantization. Furthermore, when deployed on a single NVIDIA RTX 4090 GPU, GANQ's quantized models achieve up to 2.57$\times$ speedup over the baseline, advancing memory and inference efficiency in LLM deployment.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）因其极高的资源需求面临严峻的部署挑战。低比特权重量化虽可降低内存占用、提升推理效率，但现有硬件缺乏对混合精度通用矩阵乘法（mpGEMM）的原生支持，导致基于反量化的实现方案效率低下。此外，均匀量化方法通常无法充分拟合权重分布，会造成性能下降。我们提出GANQ（GPU自适应非均匀量化），这是一种分层训练后非均匀量化框架，针对基于查找表的硬件高效mpGEMM进行了优化。GANQ采用免训练的GPU自适应优化算法，可有效降低分层量化误差，从而实现更优的量化性能。大量实验表明，在3比特和4比特量化场景下，与当前最优方法相比，GANQ可缩小量化模型与FP16基线之间的困惑度差距。此外，在单张NVIDIA RTX 4090 GPU上部署时，经GANQ量化的模型较基线实现了最高2.57倍的加速，提升了大语言模型部署的内存效率与推理效率。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=26；命中=gemm, quantization, quantized, 4-bit, 3-bit, low-bit, mixed-precision, inference

title: Absence Bench: Language Models Can’t See What’s Missing
title_zh: 缺失基准：语言模型无法看见缺失之处
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly capable of processing long inputs and locating specific information within them, as evidenced by their performance on the Needle in a Haystack (NIAH) test. However, while models excel at recalling surprising information, they still struggle to identify clearly omitted information. We introduce AbsenceBench to assesses LLMs' capacity to detect missing information across three domains: numerical sequences, poetry, and GitHub pull requests. AbsenceBench asks models to identify which pieces of a document were deliberately removed, given access to both the original and edited contexts. Despite the apparent straightforwardness of these tasks, our experiments reveal that even state-of-the-art models like Claude-3.7-Sonnet achieve only 69.6% F1-score with a modest average context length of 5K tokens. Our analysis suggests this poor performance stems from a fundamental limitation: Transformer attention mechanisms cannot easily attend to "gaps" in documents since these absences don't correspond to any specific keys that can be attended to. Overall, our results and analysis provide a case study of the close proximity of tasks where models are already superhuman (NIAH) and tasks where models breakdown unexpectedly (AbsenceBench).
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）越来越擅长处理长输入并在其中定位特定信息，这一点可从它们在“大海捞针”（Needle in a Haystack，NIAH）测试中的表现得到证明。然而，尽管模型在回忆那些令人意外的信息方面表现出色，它们仍然难以识别那些被明确遗漏的信息。我们提出了 AbsenceBench，用于评估 LLM 在三个领域中检测缺失信息的能力：数值序列、诗歌和 GitHub 拉取请求。AbsenceBench 要求模型在同时获得原始上下文和编辑后上下文的情况下，识别文档中哪些部分被故意删除了。尽管这些任务表面上看起来相当直接，我们的实验表明，即使是像 Claude-3.7-Sonnet 这样的最先进模型，在平均上下文长度仅为 5K token 的情况下，F1 分数也只有 69.6%。我们的分析表明，这种较差的表现源于一个根本性限制：Transformer 的注意力机制难以轻易关注文档中的“空缺”，因为这些缺失并不对应任何可被关注的特定键。总体而言，我们的结果和分析提供了一个案例研究，展示了这样两类任务之间的紧密邻近性：一类是模型已经表现出超人水平的任务（NIAH），另一类则是模型会意外失效的任务（AbsenceBench）。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: DP-LLM: Runtime Model Adaptation with Dynamic Layer-wise Precision Assignment
title_zh: DP-LLM：具有动态层级精度分配的运行时模型自适应
abstract: How can we effectively handle queries for on-device large language models (LLMs) with varying runtime constraints, such as latency and accuracy? Multi-scale quantization addresses this challenge by enabling memory-efficient runtime model adaptation of LLMs through the overlaying of multiple model variants quantized to different bitwidths. Meanwhile, an important question still remains open-ended: how can models be properly configured to match a target precision or latency? While mixed-precision offers a promising solution, we take this further by leveraging the key observation that the sensitivity of each layer dynamically changes across decoding steps. Building on this insight, we introduce DP-LLM, a novel mechanism that dynamically assigns precision to each layer based on input values. Experimental results across multiple models and benchmarks demonstrate that DP-LLM achieves a superior performance-latency trade-off, outperforming prior approaches.
abstract_zh: 我们如何有效应对设备端大语言模型（LLM）在延迟、准确率等不同运行时约束下的查询需求？多尺度量化通过叠加采用不同比特宽度量化的多个模型变体，实现大语言模型的内存高效运行时模型自适应，从而解决这一挑战。与此同时，一个重要问题仍未得到解决：如何合理配置模型以匹配目标精度或延迟？虽然混合精度是一种极具前景的解决方案，但我们利用各层的敏感性会随解码步骤动态变化这一关键观察结果，对相关研究做了进一步推进。基于这一洞察，我们提出DP-LLM，这是一种可根据输入值为每一层动态分配精度的新型机制。在多个模型和基准测试上的实验结果表明，DP-LLM实现了更优异的性能-延迟权衡，表现优于已有方法。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=24；命中=latency, runtime, quantization, quantized, mixed-precision, inference, decoding, language model

title: Reasoning-Driven Multimodal LLM for Domain Generalization
title_zh: 面向领域泛化的基于推理的多模态大语言模型
abstract:
This paper addresses the domain generalization (DG) problem in deep learning. 
While most DG methods focus on enforcing visual feature invariance, we leverage the reasoning capability of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) and explore the potential of constructing reasoning chains that derives image categories to achieve more robust predictions under domain shift.
To this end, we systematically study the role of reasoning in DG using DomainBed-Reasoning, a newly constructed extension of DomainBed dataset, in which each sample is paired with class-relevant reasoning chains.
Our analysis reveals two key challenges: (i) fine-tuning MLLMs with reasoning chains for classification is more challenging than direct label supervision, since the model must optimize complex reasoning sequences before label prediction; and (ii) mismatches in reasoning patterns between supervision signals and fine-tuned MLLMs lead to a trade-off between semantic richness (informative but harder to optimize) and optimization efficiency (easier to optimize but less informative).
To address these issues, we propose RD-MLDG (Reasoning-Driven Multimodal LLM for Domain Generalization), a framework with two components: (i) MTCT (Multi-Task Cross-Training), which introduces an additional direct classification pathway to guide reasoning supervision; and (ii) SARR (Self-Aligned Reasoning Regularization), which preserves the semantic richness of reasoning chains while mitigating reasoning-pattern mismatches via iterative self-labeling.
Experiments on standard DomainBed datasets (PACS, VLCS, OfficeHome, TerraIncognita) demonstrate that RD-MLDG achieves state-of-the-art performances, highlighting reasoning as a promising complementary signal for robust out-of-domain generalization.
abstract_zh: 本文研究深度学习中的领域泛化问题。多数方法在视觉特征层面追求不变性，而我们利用多模态大语言模型（MLLM）的推理能力，尝试构建推理链以推导图像类别，从而在域迁移情况下实现更鲁棒的预测。为此，我们使用新构建的 DomainBed-Reasoning（DomainBed 数据集的扩展版本，样本配备与类别相关的推理链）系统性地研究推理在领域泛化中的作用。分析发现两个挑战：一是将推理链用于分类的 MLLM 微调比直接标签监督更困难，因为模型须先优化复杂的推理序列再预测标签；二是监督信号与微调后的 MLLM 之间的推理模式不一致，会在语义丰富度（信息量大但难优化）与优化效率（易优化但信息量少）之间产生冲突。为解决这些问题，我们提出 RD-MLDG（Reasoning-Driven Multimodal LLM for Domain Generalization）框架，包含两个模块：MTCT（Multi-Task Cross-Training）通过新增直接分类路径引导推理监督；SARR（Self-Aligned Reasoning Regularization）则采用迭代自标注，在保留推理链语义丰富性的同时缓解推理模式差异。DomainBed 标准数据集（PACS、VLCS、OfficeHome、TerraIncognita）上的结果表明 RD-MLDG 达到最新水平，强调推理作为一种有前景的补充信号，可增强对域外样本的泛化能力。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Pareto Optimal Risk-Agnostic Distributional Bandits with Heavy-Tail Rewards
title_zh: 具有重尾奖励的帕累托最优风险无关分布式老虎机
abstract:
This paper addresses the problem of multi-risk measure agnostic multi-armed bandits in heavy-tailed reward settings. 
We propose a framework that leverages novel deviation inequalities for the $1$-Wasserstein distance to construct confidence intervals for Lipschitz risk measures. 
The distributional LCB (DistLCB) algorithm is introduced, which achieves asymptotic optimality by deriving the first lower bounds for risk measure aware bandits with explicit sub-optimality gap dependencies.
The DistLCB is further extended to multi-risk objectives, which enables Pareto-optimal solutions that consider multiple aspects of reward distributions.
Additionally, we provide a regret analysis that includes both gap-dependent and gap-independent bounds for multi-risk settings. 
Experiments validate the effectiveness of the proposed methods in synthetic and real-world applications.
abstract_zh: 本文研究重尾奖励下的多风险度量不可知多臂老虎机问题，提出基于$1$-Wasserstein距离的新型偏差不等式来构造Lipschitz风险度量的置信区间，并引入分布式下置信界（DistLCB）算法。该算法通过导出面向风险度量的带显式次优间隙依赖的首个下界，证明了渐近最优性。DistLCB还扩展至多风险目标，使得求解过程中可获得同时兼顾奖励分布多个维度的帕累托最优解。我们进一步给出了多风险设定下的遗憾分析，涵盖依赖于间隙的界与独立于间隙的界，并在合成与真实数据上通过实验验证了所提方法的有效性。
primary_research_object: 强化学习 / 序列决策
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Cache What Lasts: Token Retention for Memory-Bounded KV Cache in LLMs
title_zh: 缓存持久：面向受限内存的LLM键值缓存的令牌保留
abstract: Memory and computation remain core bottlenecks in long-horizon LLM inference due to the quadratic cost of self-attention and the ever-growing key-value (KV) cache. Existing strategies for memory-bounded inference, such as quantization, offloading, or heuristic KV eviction, either incur high orchestration costs or rely on unreliable attention-based proxies of importance. We propose TRIM-KV, a novel approach that learns each token’s intrinsic importance at creation time via a lightweight retention gate. Each gate predicts a scalar retention score that decays over time, reflecting the long-term utility of the token for a specific layer and head. Tokens with low scores are evicted when the memory budget is exceeded, ensuring that the cache always contains the most critical tokens. TRIM-KV is trained efficiently through distillation from a frozen LLM combined with a capacity loss, requiring only gate fine-tuning and adding negligible inference overhead. Across mathematical reasoning (GSM8K, MATH-500, AIME24), procedural generation (LongProc), and conversational long-memory benchmarks (LongMemEval), TRIM-KV consistently outperforms strong eviction and learnable retrieval baselines, especially in low-memory regimes. Remarkably, it even surpasses full-cache models in some settings, showing that selective retention can serve as a form of regularization, suppressing noise from uninformative tokens. Qualitative analyses further reveal that learned retention scores align with human intuition, naturally recovering heuristics such as sink tokens, sliding windows, and gist compression without explicit design. Beyond efficiency, retention scores provide insights into layer- and head-specific roles, suggesting a new path toward LLM interpretability.
abstract_zh: 由于自注意力的二次计算开销以及持续增长的键值（KV）缓存，内存与计算仍是长序列大语言模型推理的核心瓶颈。现有的内存受限推理策略，例如量化、卸载或者启发式KV驱逐方法，要么会产生高额调度成本，要么依赖基于注意力的不可靠重要性代理指标。我们提出TRIM-KV，这是一种全新的方法，通过轻量级保留门在每个词元生成时就学习其固有重要性。每个保留门会预测一个随时间衰减的标量保留分数，该分数能够反映特定层与注意力头下对应词元的长期效用。当超出内存预算时，分数较低的词元会被驱逐，从而确保缓存始终存储最为关键的词元。TRIM-KV利用冻结大语言模型的蒸馏结果结合容量损失实现高效训练，仅需对保留门进行微调，新增的推理开销可忽略不计。在数学推理（GSM8K、MATH-500、AIME24）、程序生成（LongProc）以及对话式长记忆基准（LongMemEval）测试中，TRIM-KV的表现始终优于性能强劲的驱逐基线与可学习检索基线，在低内存配置下优势尤为显著。值得注意的是，它在部分场景下的性能甚至超过全缓存模型，这表明选择性保留可作为一种正则化手段，抑制无信息词元带来的噪声。定性分析进一步显示，学习得到的保留分数符合人类直觉，无需显式设计即可自然实现下沉词元、滑动窗口、主旨压缩等启发式策略。除提升效率外，保留分数还能揭示不同层与注意力头的特定作用，为大语言模型可解释性研究开辟了新的路径。
notes: 自动关键词筛选得分=24；命中=kv cache, cache eviction, quantization, distillation, compression, offloading, inference, generation

title: Guiding Mixture-of-Experts with Temporal Multimodal Interactions
title_zh: 基于时序多模态交互的混合专家模型引导
abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures have become pivotal for large-scale multimodal models. However, their routing mechanisms typically overlook the informative, time-varying interaction dynamics between modalities. This limitation hinders expert specialization, as the model cannot explicitly leverage intrinsic modality relationships for effective reasoning. To address this, we propose a novel framework that guides MoE routing using quantified temporal interaction. A multimodal interaction-aware router learns to dispatch tokens to experts based on the nature of their interactions. This dynamic routing encourages experts to acquire generalizable interaction-processing skills rather than merely learning task-specific features. Our framework builds on a new formulation of temporal multimodal interaction dynamics, which are used to guide expert routing. We first demonstrate that these temporal multimodal interactions reveal meaningful patterns across applications, and then show how they can be leveraged to improve both the design and performance of MoE-based models. Comprehensive experiments on challenging multimodal benchmarks validate our approach, demonstrating both enhanced performance and improved interpretability.
abstract_zh: 混合专家（MoE）架构已成为大规模多模态模型的核心架构。然而其路由机制通常会忽略模态间信息丰富的时变交互动态，这一局限性会阻碍专家的专业化发展，因为模型无法明确利用内在的模态关系开展有效推理。为解决该问题，我们提出了一种利用量化时序交互引导MoE路由的新型框架。多模态交互感知路由器能够根据令牌的交互性质将其分派至对应专家，这种动态路由机制可鼓励专家习得具备泛化性的交互处理技能，而非仅学习任务专属特征。我们的框架基于一种全新的时序多模态交互动态表述构建，该表述可用于引导专家路由。我们首先证明这些时序多模态交互能够在不同应用场景中揭示有意义的模式，随后说明了如何利用这些交互改进基于MoE的模型的设计与性能。在具有挑战性的多模态基准上开展的综合实验验证了我们方法的有效性，结果表明该方法既能提升模型性能，也可增强其可解释性。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Scaling Reasoning Hop Exposes Weaknesses: Demystifying and Improving Hop Generalization in Large Language Models
title_zh: 扩展推理跳数暴露弱点：剖析并提升大语言模型中的跳数泛化
abstract:
Chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning has become the standard paradigm for enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex problems.
However, recent studies reveal a sharp performance drop in *reasoning hop generalization* scenarios, where the required number of reasoning steps exceeds training distributions while the underlying algorithm remains unchanged. 
The internal mechanisms driving this failure remain poorly understood.
In this work, we conduct a systematic study on tasks from multiple domains, and find that errors concentrate at token positions of a few critical error types, rather than being uniformly distributed. 
Closer inspection reveals that these token-level erroneous predictions stem from internal *competition mechanisms*: certain attention heads, termed *erroneous processing heads* (ep heads), tip the balance by amplifying incorrect reasoning trajectories while suppressing correct ones. 
Notably, removing individual ep heads during inference can often restore the correct predictions.
Motivated by these insights, we propose test-time correction of reasoning, a lightweight intervention method that dynamically identifies and deactivates ep heads in the reasoning process. 
Extensive experiments across different tasks and LLMs show that it consistently improves reasoning hop generalization, highlighting both its effectiveness and potential.
abstract_zh: 链式思维推理已成为大型语言模型处理复杂问题的标配，但最近研究发现，在“推理跳数泛化”场景中——所需推理步骤超出训练分布但底层算法不变——性能会骤降。本文系统分析多个领域任务后发现，错误并非在所有位置均匀分布，而是集中在少数关键错误类型所对应的 token 上。进一步调查揭示，这些 token 级错误的根源在于内部“竞争机制”：某些注意力头会被放大错误推理路径的信号并压制正确路径，使推理偏向错误路线。我们将这些注意力头称作“错误处理头”（ep heads），并观察到在推理过程中局部关闭单个 ep 头往往可以恢复正确预测。基于此，我们提出了一种推理时的动态纠正方法：在推理过程中实时识别并关闭 ep 头，实现轻量级干预。跨任务和多种大型语言模型的实验表明，该方法能稳定提升推理跳数泛化能力，凸显其有效性与潜力。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: ChartMuseum: Testing Visual Reasoning Capabilities of Large Vision-Language Models
title_zh: ChartMuseum：测试大型视觉语言模型的视觉推理能力
abstract: Chart understanding presents a unique challenge for large vision-language models (LVLMs), as it requires the integration of sophisticated textual and visual reasoning capabilities. However, current LVLMs exhibit a notable imbalance between these skills, falling short on visual reasoning that is difficult to perform in text. We conduct a case study using a synthetic dataset solvable only through visual reasoning and show that model performance degrades significantly with increasing visual complexity, while human performance remains robust. We then introduce *ChartMuseum*, a new Chart Question Answering (QA) benchmark containing 1,162 expert-annotated questions spanning multiple reasoning types, curated from real-world charts across 184 sources, specifically built to evaluate complex visual and textual reasoning. Unlike prior chart understanding benchmarks---where frontier models perform similarly and near saturation---our benchmark exposes a substantial gap between model and human performance, while effectively differentiating model capabilities: although humans achieve 93% accuracy, the best-performing model Gemini-2.5-Pro attains only 63.0%, and the leading open-source LVLM Qwen2.5-VL-72B-Instruct achieves only 38.5%. Moreover, on questions requiring primarily visual reasoning, *all* models experience a 35%-55% performance drop from text-reasoning-heavy question performance. Lastly, our qualitative error analysis reveals specific categories of visual reasoning that are challenging for current LVLMs. Both ChartMuseum and the evaluation code are available at [https://github.com/Liyan06/ChartMuseum](https://github.com/Liyan06/ChartMuseum).
abstract_zh:
图表理解对大型视觉语言模型（LVLM）提出了独特挑战，因为它要求整合复杂的文本推理与视觉推理能力。然而，当前的 LVLM 在这些能力之间表现出明显失衡，在那些难以通过文本完成的视觉推理任务上表现不足。我们使用一个只能通过视觉推理解决的合成数据集进行案例研究，结果表明，随着视觉复杂度增加，模型性能显著下降，而人类表现仍然稳健。
随后，我们提出了 ChartMuseum，一个新的图表问答基准，包含 1,162 个由专家标注的问题，涵盖多种推理类型，选自 184 个来源的真实世界图表，专门用于评估复杂的视觉与文本推理。不同于以往的图表理解基准——在那些基准上，前沿模型的表现彼此相近且接近饱和——我们的基准揭示了模型与人类表现之间的显著差距，同时能够有效区分模型能力：尽管人类准确率达到 93%，表现最好的模型 Gemini-2.5-Pro 也只有 63.0%，而领先的开源 LVLM Qwen2.5-VL-72B-Instruct 仅达到 38.5%。此外，在主要依赖视觉推理的问题上，所有模型相较于偏重文本推理的问题，性能都下降了 35% 至 55%。最后，我们的定性错误分析揭示了当前 LVLM 在若干特定视觉推理类别上的困难。ChartMuseum 及其评测代码已在 https://github.com/Liyan06/ChartMuseum 提供。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: ``Noisier'’ Noise Contrastive Estimation is (Almost) Maximum Likelihood
title_zh: 更嘈杂的噪声对比估计（几乎）等价于极大似然
abstract: Noise Contrastive Estimation (NCE) has fueled major breakthroughs in representation learning and generative modeling. Yet a long-standing challenge remains: accurately estimating ratios between distributions that differ substantially, which significantly limits the applicability of NCE on modern high-dimensional and multimodal datasets. We revisit this problem from a less explored perspective: the magnitude of the noise distribution. Specifically, we show that with a virtually scaled (i.e., artificially increased) noise magnitude, the gradient of the NCE objective can closely align with that of Maximum Likelihood, enabling a trajectory-wise approximation from NCE to MLE, and faster convergence both theoretically and empirically. Building on this insight, we introduce "Noisier" NCE, a simple drop-in modification to vanilla NCE that incurs little to no extra computational cost, while effectively handling density-ratio estimation in challenging regimes where traditional MLE and NCE struggle. Beyond improving classical density-ratio learning, "Noisier" NCE proves broadly applicable: it achieves strong results across image modeling, anomaly detection, and offline black-box optimization. On CIFAR-10 and ImageNet64×64 datasets, it yields 10-step and even 1-step samplers that match or surpass state-of-the-art methods, while cutting training iterations by up to half.
abstract_zh: 噪声对比估计（NCE）推动了表征学习和生成建模中的重大突破。然而，一个长期存在的挑战仍未解决：如何准确估计差异较大的分布之间的比率，这极大限制了 NCE 在现代高维和多模态数据集上的适用性。我们从一个较少被探索的视角重新审视这一问题：噪声分布的幅度。具体而言，我们表明，当采用虚拟放大（即人为增大）的噪声幅度时，NCE 目标的梯度可以与最大似然估计（MLE）的梯度紧密对齐，从而实现从 NCE 到 MLE 的按轨迹近似，并在理论和经验上都获得更快的收敛。基于这一见解，我们提出了 “Noisier” NCE，这是一种对标准 NCE 的简单即插即用修改，几乎不带来额外计算开销，同时能够在传统 MLE 和 NCE 都难以处理的具有挑战性的情形下有效进行密度比估计。除了改进经典的密度比学习之外，“Noisier” NCE 还被证明具有广泛适用性：它在图像建模、异常检测和离线黑盒优化等任务上都取得了强劲结果。在 CIFAR-10 和 ImageNet64×64 数据集上，它实现了 10 步甚至 1 步的采样器，性能可匹配或超过当前最先进方法，同时将训练迭代次数最多减少一半。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Taming Hyperparameter Sensitivity in Data Attribution: Practical Selection Without Costly Retraining
title_zh: 驯服数据归因中的超参数敏感性：无需代价高昂重训练的实用选择
abstract: Data attribution methods, which quantify the influence of individual training data points on a machine learning model, have gained increasing popularity in data-centric applications in modern AI. Despite a recent surge of new methods developed in this space, the impact of hyperparameter tuning in these methods remains under-explored. In this work, we present the first large-scale empirical study to understand the hyperparameter sensitivity of common data attribution methods. Our results show that most methods are indeed sensitive to certain key hyperparameters. However, unlike typical machine learning algorithms---whose hyperparameters can be tuned using computationally-cheap validation metrics---evaluating data attribution performance often requires retraining models on subsets of training data, making such metrics prohibitively costly for hyperparameter tuning. This poses a critical open challenge for the practical application of data attribution methods. To address this challenge, we advocate for better theoretical understandings of hyperparameter behavior to inform efficient tuning strategies. As a case study, we provide a theoretical analysis of the regularization term that is critical in many variants of influence function methods. Building on this analysis, we propose a lightweight procedure for selecting the regularization value without model retraining, and validate its effectiveness across a range of standard data attribution benchmarks. Overall, our study identifies a fundamental yet overlooked challenge in the practical application of data attribution, and highlights the importance of careful discussion on hyperparameter selection in future method development.
abstract_zh: 数据归因方法用于量化单个训练数据点对机器学习模型的影响，近年来在现代人工智能中以数据为中心的应用中日益流行。尽管这一领域最近涌现出大量新方法，但这些方法中的超参数调优影响仍缺乏充分研究。在这项工作中，我们开展了首个大规模实证研究，以理解常见数据归因方法对超参数的敏感性。结果表明，大多数方法确实对某些关键超参数较为敏感。然而，不同于典型的机器学习算法——其超参数可以通过计算代价较低的验证指标进行调优——评估数据归因性能通常需要在训练数据子集上重新训练模型，这使得此类指标对于超参数调优而言成本高得难以承受。这为数据归因方法的实际应用带来了一个关键的开放性挑战。为应对这一挑战，我们主张更好地从理论上理解超参数行为，以指导高效的调优策略。作为案例研究，我们对许多影响函数方法变体中至关重要的正则化项进行了理论分析。基于这一分析，我们提出了一种无需重新训练模型即可选择正则化值的轻量级过程，并在一系列标准数据归因基准上验证了其有效性。总体而言，我们的研究识别出数据归因实际应用中一个根本但被忽视的挑战，并强调了在未来方法开发中认真讨论超参数选择的重要性。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: AgentDAM: Privacy Leakage Evaluation for Autonomous Web Agents
title_zh: AgentDAM：面向自主网页代理的隐私泄露评估
abstract: Autonomous AI agents that can follow instructions and perform complex multi-step tasks have tremendous potential to boost human productivity. However, to perform many of these tasks, the agents need access to personal information from their users, raising the question of whether they are capable of using it appropriately. In this work, we introduce a new benchmark **AgentDAM** that measures if AI web-navigation agents follow the privacy principle of *"data minimization"*. For the purposes of our benchmark, data minimization means that the agent uses a piece of potentially sensitive information only if it is "necessary" to complete a particular task. Our benchmark simulates realistic web interaction scenarios end-to-end and is adaptable to all existing web navigation agents. We use AgentDAM to evaluate how well AI agents built on top of GPT-4, Llama-3 and Claude can limit processing of potentially private information, and show that they are prone to inadvertent use of unnecessary sensitive information. We also propose a prompting-based defense that reduces information leakage, and demonstrate that our end-to-end benchmarking provides a more realistic measure than probing LLMs about privacy. Our results highlight that further research is needed to develop AI agents that can prioritize data minimization at inference time. We open source our benchmark at: https://github.com/facebookresearch/ai-agent-privacy
abstract_zh: 能够遵循指令并执行复杂多步任务的自主 AI 智能体，具有极大的潜力来提升人类生产力。然而，为了执行其中许多任务，智能体需要访问用户的个人信息，这就引出了一个问题：它们是否能够恰当地使用这些信息。在这项工作中，我们引入了一个新的基准 AgentDAM，用于衡量 AI 网页导航智能体是否遵循“数据最小化”这一隐私原则。就我们的基准而言，数据最小化意味着，智能体仅在完成特定任务“必要”时，才使用某条可能敏感的信息。我们的基准端到端地模拟了真实的网页交互场景，并且可适配所有现有的网页导航智能体。我们使用 AgentDAM 评估了基于 GPT-4、Llama-3 和 Claude 构建的 AI 智能体在限制处理潜在隐私信息方面的表现，并表明它们容易无意中使用不必要的敏感信息。我们还提出了一种基于提示的防御方法，以减少信息泄露，并证明我们的端到端基准测试比通过隐私问题探测大语言模型更能提供真实的衡量结果。我们的结果突出表明，仍需要进一步研究，以开发能够在推理时优先考虑数据最小化的 AI 智能体。我们已将基准开源，地址为：https://github.com/facebookresearch/ai-agent-privacy
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Event-based HDR Structured Light
title_zh: 基于事件的 HDR 结构光
abstract: Event-based structured light (SL) systems have attracted increasing attention for their potential in high-performance 3D measurement. Despite the inherent HDR capability of event cameras, reflective and absorptive surfaces still cause event cluttering and absence, which produce overexposed and underexposed regions that degrade the reconstruction quality. In this work, we present the first HDR 3D measurement framework specifically designed for event-based SL systems. First, we introduce a multi-contrast HDR coding strategy that facilitates imaging of areas with different reflectance. Second, to alleviate inter-frame interference caused by overexposed and underexposed areas, we propose a universal confidence-driven stereo matching strategy. Specifically, we estimate a confidence map as the fusion weight for features via an energy-guided confidence estimation. Further, we propose the confidence propagation volume, an innovative cost volume that offers both effective suppression of inter-frame interference and strong representation capability. Third, we contribute an event-based SL simulator and propose the first event-based HDR SL dataset. We also collect a real-world benchmarking dataset with ground truth. We validate the effectiveness of our method with the proposed confidence-driven strategy on both synthetic and real-world datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed HDR framework enables accurate 3D measurement even under extreme conditions.
abstract_zh: 基于事件的结构光（SL）系统因其在高性能三维测量中的潜力而受到越来越多的关注。尽管事件相机天然具备高动态范围（HDR）能力，反射性和吸收性表面仍会导致事件拥挤和缺失，从而产生过曝和欠曝区域，降低重建质量。在这项工作中，我们提出了首个专为基于事件的结构光系统设计的HDR三维测量框架。首先，我们引入了一种多对比度HDR编码策略，以促进对不同反射率区域的成像。其次，为缓解由过曝和欠曝区域引起的帧间干扰，我们提出了一种通用的置信度驱动立体匹配策略。具体而言，我们通过一种能量引导的置信度估计来估计置信图，并将其作为特征融合权重。进一步地，我们提出了置信传播体，这是一种创新的代价体，既能有效抑制帧间干扰，又具有很强的表示能力。第三，我们构建了一个基于事件的结构光模拟器，并提出了首个基于事件的HDR结构光数据集。我们还采集了一个带有真实值的真实世界基准数据集。我们在合成数据集和真实世界数据集上验证了所提方法及其置信度驱动策略的有效性。实验结果表明，所提出的HDR框架即使在极端条件下也能实现精确的三维测量。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: EPIC: Efficient Position-Independent Caching for Serving Large Language Models
title_zh: EPIC：面向大语言模型服务的高效位置无关缓存
abstract:
Large Language Models (LLMs) show great capabilities in a wide range of applications, but serving them efficiently becomes increasingly challenging as requests (prompts) become more complex. Context caching improves serving performance by reusing Key-Value (KV) vectors, the intermediate representations of tokens that are repeated across requests. However, existing context caching requires exact prefix matches across requests, limiting reuse cases in settings such as few-shot learning and retrieval-augmented generation, where immutable content (e.g., documents) remains unchanged across requests but is preceded by varying prefixes. Position-Independent
Caching (PIC) addresses this issue by enabling modular reuse of the KV vectors regardless of prefixes. We formalize PIC and advance prior work by introducing EPIC, a serving system incorporating our new LegoLink algorithm, which mitigates the inappropriate “attention sink” effect at every document beginning, to maintain accuracy with minimal computation. Experiments show that EPIC achieves up to 8× improvements in Time-To-First-Token (TTFT) and 7× throughput gains over existing systems, with negligible or no accuracy loss.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）在广泛的应用场景中展现出了极强的能力，但随着请求（提示词）日趋复杂，高效提供大语言模型服务的挑战也不断加剧。上下文缓存通过复用键值（KV）向量提升服务性能，这类向量是在不同请求间重复出现的词元的中间表征。然而现有上下文缓存要求不同请求间存在完全匹配的前缀，这限制了其在少样本学习、检索增强生成等场景下的复用空间，这类场景中不可变内容（例如文档）在不同请求间保持不变，但前面会带有各不相同的前缀。位置无关缓存（PIC）通过支持KV向量的模块化复用、无需考虑前缀差异，从而解决了这一问题。我们对位置无关缓存进行了形式化定义，并在现有研究基础上提出了EPIC服务系统，该系统集成了我们新提出的LegoLink算法，可缓解每个文档开头处存在的不合理“注意力沉降”效应，从而以极低的计算开销维持模型精度。实验表明，与现有系统相比，EPIC的首次令牌生成时间（TTFT）最多可提升8倍，吞吐量最多提升7倍，且精度损失可忽略甚至不存在。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: XIFBench: Evaluating Large Language Models on Multilingual Instruction Following
title_zh: 翻译标题** XIFBench：在多语言指令遵循上的大型语言模型评估
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable instruction-following capabilities across various applications. However, their performance in multilingual settings lacks systematic investigation, with existing evaluations lacking fine-grained constraint analysis across diverse linguistic contexts. We introduce **XIFBench**, a comprehensive constraint-based benchmark for evaluating multilingual instruction-following abilities of LLMs, comprising 558 instructions with 0-5 additional constraints across five categories (*Content*, *Style*, *Situation*, *Format*, and *Numerical*) in six languages spanning different resource levels. To support reliable and consistent cross-lingual evaluation, we implement three methodological innovations: cultural accessibility annotation, constraint-level translation validation, and requirement-based evaluation using English requirements as semantic anchors across languages. Extensive experiments with various LLMs not only quantify performance disparities across resource levels but also provide detailed insights into how language resources, constraint categories, instruction complexity, and cultural specificity influence multilingual instruction-following.  Our code and data are available at https://github.com/zhenyuli801/XIFBench.
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）在各种应用中展现出显著的指令遵循能力。然而，它们在多语言环境中的表现仍缺乏系统性研究，现有评估也缺少对不同语言背景下细粒度约束的分析。我们提出了 XIFBench，这是一个用于评估 LLM 多语言指令遵循能力的综合性、基于约束的基准，包含 558 条指令，涵盖 0 至 5 个附加约束，这些约束分布在五个类别中（内容、风格、情境、格式和数值），并覆盖六种资源水平不同的语言。为支持可靠且一致的跨语言评估，我们实现了三项方法创新：文化可达性标注、约束层级的翻译验证，以及使用英文要求作为跨语言语义锚点的基于要求的评估。对多种 LLM 进行的大量实验不仅量化了不同资源水平之间的性能差异，还详细揭示了语言资源、约束类别、指令复杂度和文化特异性如何影响多语言指令遵循能力。我们的代码和数据可在 https://github.com/zhenyuli801/XIFBench 获取。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: TINY BUT MIGHTY: A SOFTWARE-HARDWARE CO- DESIGN APPROACH FOR EFFICIENT MULTIMODAL IN- FERENCE ON BATTERY-POWERED SMALL DEVICES
title_zh: 微小但强悍：面向电池供电小型设备的高效多模态推理的软件硬件协同设计方法
abstract:
Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) are inherently modular, consisting of vision
and audio encoders, projectors, and large language models. Yet, they are almost
always executed monolithically, which underutilizes the heterogeneous accelera-
tors (NPUs, GPUs, DSPs) in modern SoCs and leads to high end-to-end latency.
In this paper, we present NANOMIND, a hardware–software co-design inference
framework for Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) that breaks large models into
modular “bricks” (vision, language, audio, etc.) and maps each to its ideal accelera-
tor. The key insight is that large models can be broken into modular components and
scheduled to run on the most appropriate compute units. It performs module-level
dynamic offloading across accelerators on unified-memory SoCs. By combining
customized hardware design, system-level scheduling, and optimized low-bit com-
putation kernels, we demonstrate our framework with a compact, battery-powered
device capable of running LMMs entirely on-device. This prototype functions as
a self-contained intelligent assistant that requires no network connectivity, while
achieving higher throughput and superior power efficiency under strict resource
constraints. The design further bypasses CPU bottlenecks and reduces redundant
memory usage through token-aware buffer management and module-level coordi-
nation. Our system outperforms existing implementations in resource efficiency,
cutting energy consumption by 42.3% and GPU memory usage by 11.2%. This
enables a battery-powered device to run LlaVA-OneVision-qwen2-05B with a
camera for nearly 20.8 hours.
abstract_zh: 大型多模态模型（LMM）本质上具备模块化属性，由视觉编码器、音频编码器、投影器及大语言模型组成。但这类模型几乎始终以整体形式执行，使得现代片上系统（SoC）内置的异构加速器（神经网络处理器、图形处理器、数字信号处理器）未得到充分利用，进而导致端到端延迟偏高。本文提出NANOMIND，一款面向大型多模态模型的软硬件协同设计推理框架，该框架将大模型拆分为模块化“构件”（视觉、语言、音频等），并将每个构件映射至最适配的加速器上。该框架的核心思路是，大模型可被拆解为多个模块化组件，可调度这些组件在最合适的计算单元上运行。它在搭载统一内存的片上系统的各加速器之间执行模块级动态卸载。通过结合定制化硬件设计、系统级调度以及优化后的低比特计算内核，我们在一款紧凑型电池供电设备上验证了该框架的效果，该设备可完全在端侧运行大型多模态模型。该原型设备可作为无需网络连接的独立智能助手，在严格的资源约束下实现了更高的吞吐量和更优异的能效。该设计还通过令牌感知缓冲区管理和模块级协调规避了CPU瓶颈，减少了冗余内存占用。我们的系统在资源效率上优于现有实现方案，能耗降低42.3%，GPU内存占用降低11.2%。这使得搭载摄像头的电池供电设备运行LlaVA-OneVision-qwen2-05B模型的时长可达近20.8小时。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Learning Skill-Attributes for Transferable Assessment in Video
title_zh: 学习技能属性以实现视频中可迁移的评估
abstract: Skill assessment from video entails rating the quality of a person’s physical performance and explaining what could be done better. Today’s models specialize for an individual sport, and suffer from the high cost and scarcity of expert-level supervision across the long tail of sports. Towards closing that gap, we explore transferable video representations for skill assessment. Our CrossTrainer approach discovers skill-attributes—such as balance, control, and hand positioning—whose meaning transcends the boundaries of any given sport, then trains a multimodal language model to generate actionable feedback for a novel video, e.g., “lift hands more to generate more power” as well as its proficiency level, e.g., early expert. We validate the new model on multiple datasets for both cross-sport (transfer) and intra-sport (in-domain) settings, where it achieves gains up to 60% relative to the state of the art. By abstracting out the shared behaviors indicative of human skill, the proposed video representation generalizes substantially better than an array of existing techniques, enriching today’s multimodal large language models.
abstract_zh: 从视频中进行技能评估，意味着要对一个人物理表现的质量进行打分，并解释哪些方面可以做得更好。当今的模型通常专注于单一运动项目，并且在长尾运动项目上受到专家级监督成本高、资源稀缺的限制。为缩小这一差距，我们探索了可迁移的用于技能评估的视频表征。我们的 CrossTrainer 方法发现了技能属性，例如平衡、控制和手部位置，这些属性的含义超越了任何特定运动项目的边界；随后训练一个多模态语言模型，为新视频生成可执行的反馈，例如“更多抬高手臂以产生更大的力量”，以及其熟练度水平，例如“初级专家”。我们在多个数据集上验证了该新模型在跨运动（迁移）和运动内（域内）两种设置下的表现，其结果相较于当前最先进方法最高可提升 60%。通过抽象出表征人类技能的共享行为，所提出的视频表征相比一系列现有技术具有显著更好的泛化能力，从而增强了当今的多模态大语言模型。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: ICaRus: Identical Cache Reuse for Efficient Multi-Model Inference
title_zh: ICaRus：用于高效多模型推理的相同缓存重用
abstract:
Multi model inference, where multiple task-specialized models collaborate to solve complex real-world problems, has recently emerged as a prominent paradigm, particularly in the development of agentic AI systems. However, in such scenarios, each model must maintain its own Key-Value (KV) cache for the identical prompt, leading to explosive memory consumption.
This explosive growth of KV caches forces LLM serving systems to evict previously stored caches, which in turn introduces significant recomputation overhead whenever the evicted caches are required again. 
Moreover, prefix caching is inherently infeasible across different models, forcing each model to recompute KV cache for the identical prompt, which leads to signficant overhead. To alleviate these issues, we propose Identical Cache Reuse (ICaRus), a novel architecture that allows multiple models to share identical KV caches across all layers. ICaRus is based on the key observation that a decoder-only Transformer can be conceptually decomposed into a logical encoder, which generates KV caches, and a logical decoder, which predicts output tokens from the KV caches. ICaRus fine-tunes only the logical decoder while freezing the logical encoder, enabling multiple models to share an identical KV cache. This eliminates cache memory explosion and unexpected evictions while also allowing cross-model reuse of KV caches for new input tokens, thereby removing redundant recomputation in multi model inference achieving both efficiency and scalability. Moreover, by incorporating lightweight adapters such as LoRA, ICaRus parallelizes KV cache generation and next-token prediction during decoding. ICaRus achieves comparable accuracy to task-specific fine-tuned model across a diverse set of tasks, while allowing multiple specialized models to fully share KV caches. ICaRus achieves up to $11.1\times$ lower P95 latency and $3.8\times$ higher throughput in multi agent scenarios with 8 different models, compared to prior multi model system.
abstract_zh: 多模型推理是指多个任务专用模型协作解决复杂现实问题的推理模式，近年来已成为主流范式，在智能体AI系统开发中尤为突出。但在这类场景中，每个模型都需要为相同的提示词维护各自的键值（KV）缓存，导致内存消耗激增。KV缓存的爆炸式增长会迫使大语言模型服务系统驱逐此前存储的缓存，后续如果再次需要被驱逐的缓存，又会产生极高的重计算开销。此外，前缀缓存本质上无法跨不同模型使用，导致每个模型都需要为相同的提示词重新计算KV缓存，带来显著开销。为缓解这些问题，我们提出相同缓存复用机制ICaRus，这是一种支持多个模型跨所有层共享相同KV缓存的新型架构。ICaRus的设计基于一项关键观察：仅解码器架构的Transformer在概念上可被拆解为两个部分：生成KV缓存的逻辑编码器，以及根据KV缓存预测输出令牌的逻辑解码器。ICaRus仅微调逻辑解码器，同时冻结逻辑编码器，从而让多个模型可以共享相同的KV缓存。这种设计消除了缓存内存爆炸和意外缓存驱逐问题，还支持新输入令牌的KV缓存跨模型复用，从而消除多模型推理中的冗余重计算，同时实现高效性与可扩展性。此外，通过整合LoRA等轻量适配器，ICaRus在解码阶段实现了KV缓存生成与下一令牌预测的并行处理。在各类不同任务中，ICaRus的准确度与任务专用微调模型相当，同时支持多个专用模型完全共享KV缓存。与此前的多模型系统相比，在包含8个不同模型的多智能体场景中，ICaRus的P95延迟最高降低11.1倍，吞吐量最高提升3.8倍。
notes: 仲裁采纳：human

title: Jamais Vu: Exposing the Generalization Gap in Supervised Semantic Correspondence
title_zh: Jamais Vu：揭示监督语义对应中的泛化鸿沟
abstract: Semantic correspondence (SC) aims to establish semantically meaningful matches across different instances of an object category. We illustrate how recent supervised SC methods remain limited in their ability to generalize beyond sparsely annotated training keypoints, effectively acting as keypoint detectors. To address this, we propose a novel approach for learning dense correspondences by lifting 2D keypoints into a canonical 3D space using monocular depth estimation. Our method constructs a continuous canonical manifold that captures object geometry without requiring explicit 3D supervision or camera annotations. Additionally, we introduce SPair-U, an extension of SPair-71k with novel keypoint annotations, to better assess generalization. Experiments not only demonstrate that our model significantly outperforms supervised baselines on unseen keypoints, highlighting its effectiveness in learning robust correspondences, but  that unsupervised baselines outperform supervised counterparts when generalized across different datasets.
abstract_zh: 语义对应（SC）旨在在同一物体类别的不同实例之间建立具有语义意义的匹配。我们说明了，近期的监督式 SC 方法在超越稀疏标注训练关键点进行泛化的能力上仍然有限，本质上更像是关键点检测器。为了解决这一问题，我们提出了一种新的稠密对应学习方法，通过单目深度估计将二维关键点提升到一个规范的三维空间中。我们的方法构建了一个连续的规范流形，在不需要显式三维监督或相机标注的情况下捕捉物体几何结构。此外，我们引入了 SPair-U，这是在 SPair-71k 基础上加入新关键点标注的扩展版本，以便更好地评估泛化能力。实验不仅表明，我们的模型在未见关键点上的表现显著优于监督式基线，凸显了其在学习鲁棒对应关系方面的有效性，而且还表明，在跨不同数据集进行泛化时，无监督基线优于监督式对应方法。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Online Segment Any 3D Thing as Instance Tracking
title_zh: 将任意三维物体的在线分割视为实例跟踪
abstract:
Online, real-time, and fine-grained 3D segmentation constitutes a fundamental capability for embodied intelligent agents to perceive and comprehend their operational environments. Recent advancements employ predefined object queries to aggregate semantic information from Vision Foundation Models (VFMs) outputs that are lifted into 3D point clouds, facilitating spatial information propagation through inter-query interactions.
Nevertheless, perception, whether human or robotic, is an inherently dynamic process, rendering temporal understanding a critical yet overlooked dimension within these prevailing query-based pipelines. This deficiency in temporal reasoning can exacerbate issues such as the over-segmentation commonly produced by VFMs, necessitating more handcrafted post-processing. Therefore, to further unlock the temporal environmental perception capabilities of embodied agents, our work reconceptualizes online 3D segmentation as an instance tracking problem (AutoSeg3D). Our core strategy involves utilizing object queries for temporal information propagation, where long-term instance association promotes the coherence of features and object identities, while short-term instance update enriches 
instant observations. Given that viewpoint variations in embodied robotics often lead to partial object visibility across frames, this mechanism aids the model in developing a holistic object understanding beyond incomplete instantaneous views. Furthermore, we introduce spatial consistency learning to mitigate the fragmentation problem inherent in VFMs, yielding more comprehensive instance information for enhancing the efficacy of both long-term and short-term temporal learning. The temporal information exchange and consistency learning facilitated by these sparse object queries not only enhance spatial comprehension but also circumvent the computational burden associated with dense temporal point cloud interactions. Our method establishes a new state-of-the-art, surpassing ESAM by 2.8 AP on ScanNet200 and delivering consistent gains on ScanNet, SceneNN, and 3RScan datasets, corroborating that identity-aware temporal reasoning is a crucial, previously underemphasized component for robust 3D segmentation in real-time embodied intelligence. Code is at https://github.com/AutoLab-SAI-SJTU/AutoSeg3D.
abstract_zh: 在线、实时且细粒度的三维分割是具身智能体感知和理解操作环境的基础能力。近年来的方法通过预定义的对象查询，从提升为三维点云的视觉基础模型输出中聚合语义信息，并利用查询间交互传播空间信息。然而，无论是人类还是机器人，感知本质上是一个动态过程，这使得时间维度在现有基于查询的流水线中被忽视，而时间推理的缺失会加剧视觉基础模型常见的过度分割问题，迫使需要更多手工后处理。为此，我们将在线三维分割重新建模为实例跟踪问题（AutoSeg3D），以进一步挖掘具身智能体对环境的时间感知能力。核心策略是利用对象查询进行时间信息传播，长期实例关联可增强特征与对象身份的一致性，短期实例更新则丰富当前观测。考虑到具身机器人的视角变化常导致物体在各帧中只有部分可见，这种机制使模型能超越零碎的瞬时视角，建立更完整的对象理解。此外，我们引入空间一致性学习以缓解视觉基础模型固有的碎片化问题，从而提供更全面的实例信息，提升长期与短期时间学习的效果。这些稀疏对象查询实现的时间信息交换与一致性学习不仅增强了空间理解能力，还避免了密集时间点云交互带来的计算负担。我们的方法在 ScanNet200 上超越 ESAM 2.8 个 AP，并在 ScanNet、SceneNN 和 3RScan 数据集上持续取得提升，证明具备身份意识的时间推理对于实时具身智能的鲁棒三维分割是至关重要且此前被低估的组成部分。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Do LLM Agents Know How to Ground, Recover, and Assess? Evaluating Epistemic Competence in Information-Seeking Agents
title_zh: LLM Agents 是否懂得落地、恢复与评估？评估信息搜索智能体的认识能力
abstract:
Recent work has explored training Large Language Model (LLM) search agents with reinforcement learning (RL) for open-domain question answering. However, most evaluations focus solely on final answer accuracy, overlooking how these agents reason with and act on external evidence.
We introduce **SeekBench**, the first process-level evaluation framework for LLM search agents that operationalize *epistemic competence* through metrics derived from an annotation schema.
We develop and validate our annotation schema using an expert-annotated dataset of 190 traces (over 1,800 steps). 
To evaluate at scale, we introduce an LLM-as-judge pipeline.
Our framework provides granular analysis of whether agents demonstrate: (1) **groundedness**, by generating reasoning steps supported by observed evidence; (2) **recovery**, by adaptively reformulating searches to recover from low-quality results; and (3) **calibration**, by correctly assessing whether current evidence is sufficient to provide an answer. 
By applying our evaluation framework to state-of-the-art search agents tuned on Qwen2.5-7B, we uncover critical behavioral gaps that answer-only metrics miss, as well as specialized skills such as Search-R1's synthesis abilities. These analyses highlight distinct epistemic competencies, offering actionable insights for the development of more capable and trustworthy agents. Code is available at
https://github.com/SHAO-Jiaqi757/SeekBench.
abstract_zh: 近期的研究尝试通过强化学习训练大语言模型搜索代理用于开放域问答，但现有评估往往只关注最终答案的准确性，而忽视了代理如何基于外部证据进行推理和行动。我们提出了 **SeekBench**，这是首个面向大语言模型搜索代理的过程级评估框架，通过一套注释模式所派生的度量指标将“认知能力”具体化。我们使用专家标注的 190 条执行轨迹（共超过 1,800 步）开发并验证了这套注释模式，并设计了一个“LLM 评审”流水线以实现大规模评估。框架提供了细粒度分析，判断代理是否展现：① **落地能力**——生成以观察到证据为支持的推理步骤；② **恢复能力**——在遭遇低质量结果时能自适应地重新构造搜索；③ **校准能力**——正确评估当前证据是否足够生成答案。通过将该评估框架应用于在 Qwen2.5-7B 上调优的最新搜索代理，我们发现了仅凭答案准确率难以触及的关键行为差距，并识别出如 Search-R1 的综合能力等专长。这些分析凸显了不同的认知能力维度，能为构建更高效、更可信的代理提供可操作的改进方向。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: DynaRend: Learning 3D Dynamics via Masked Future Rendering for Robotic Manipulation
title_zh: DynaRend：通过掩码未来渲染学习用于机器人操作的三维动力学
abstract: Learning generalizable robotic manipulation policies remains a key challenge due to the scarcity of diverse real-world training data. While recent approaches have attempted to mitigate this through self-supervised representation learning, most either rely on 2D vision pretraining paradigms such as masked image modeling, which primarily focus on static semantics or scene geometry, or utilize large-scale video prediction models that emphasize 2D dynamics, thus failing to jointly learn the geometry, semantics, and dynamics required for effective manipulation. In this paper, we present DynaRend, a representation learning framework that learns 3D-aware and dynamics-informed triplane features via masked reconstruction and future prediction using differentiable volumetric rendering. By pretraining on multi-view RGB-D video data, DynaRend jointly captures spatial geometry, future dynamics, and task semantics in a unified triplane representation. The learned representations can be effectively transferred to downstream robotic manipulation tasks via action value map prediction. We evaluate DynaRend on two challenging benchmarks, RLBench and Colosseum, as well as in real-world robotic experiments, demonstrating substantial improvements in policy success rate, generalization to environmental perturbations, and real-world applicability across diverse manipulation tasks.
abstract_zh: 由于多样化真实世界训练数据的稀缺，学习具有泛化能力的机器人操作策略仍然是一项关键挑战。尽管近期方法尝试通过自监督表征学习来缓解这一问题，但大多数方法要么依赖于诸如掩码图像建模之类的二维视觉预训练范式，这类方法主要关注静态语义或场景几何；要么利用强调二维动态的大规模视频预测模型，因此无法联合学习有效操作所需的几何、语义和动态。在本文中，我们提出了 DynaRend，这是一种表征学习框架，通过使用可微体渲染进行掩码重建和未来预测，学习具备三维感知和动态感知的三平面特征。通过在多视角 RGB-D 视频数据上进行预训练，DynaRend 在统一的三平面表征中联合捕获空间几何、未来动态和任务语义。所学习到的表征可以通过动作价值图预测有效迁移到下游机器人操作任务中。我们在两个具有挑战性的基准 RLBench 和 Colosseum 以及真实世界机器人实验中对 DynaRend 进行了评估，结果表明其在策略成功率、对环境扰动的泛化能力以及跨多样化操作任务的真实世界适用性方面均取得了显著提升。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: On the Direction of RLVR Updates for LLM Reasoning: Identification and Exploitation
title_zh: 大语言模型推理中的 RLVR 更新方向：识别与利用
abstract:
Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has substantially improved the reasoning capabilities of large language models. 
While existing analyses identify that RLVR-induced changes are sparse, they primarily focus on the **magnitude** of these updates, largely overlooking their **direction**. 
In this work, we argue that the direction of updates is a more critical lens for understanding RLVR's effects, which can be captured by the signed, token-level log probability difference $\Delta\log p$ between the base and final RLVR models.
Through statistical analysis and token-replacement interventions, we demonstrate that $\Delta\log p$ more effectively identifies sparse, yet reasoning-critical updates than magnitude-based metrics (e.g., divergence or entropy).
Building on this insight, we propose two practical applications:
(1) a *test-time extrapolation* method that amplifies the policy along the learned $\Delta\log p$ direction to improve reasoning accuracy without further training;
(2) a *training-time reweighting* method that focuses learning on low-probability (corresponding to higher $\Delta\log p$) tokens, which improves reasoning performance across models and benchmarks.
Our work establishes the direction of change as a key principle for analyzing and improving RLVR.
abstract_zh: 我们认为更新方向比更新幅度更关键，通过基模型和最终 RLVR 模型之间的带符号逐令牌对数概率差 $\Delta\log p$ 可捕捉该方向。统计分析与令牌替换实验表明，与基于幅度的指标（如散度、熵）相比，$\Delta\log p$ 更能识别稀疏但对推理至关重要的更新。在此基础上提出两种实用方案：（1）推理期外推：在测试阶段沿着学习到的 $\Delta\log p$ 方向放大策略，无需再训练即可提升推理准确率；（2）训练期重加权：将训练关注点转向低概率（即对应高 $\Delta\log p$）的令牌，显著提升多模型多基准的推理能力。我们的工作将更新方向确立为分析与优化 RLVR 的核心原则。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Search and Refine During Think: Facilitating Knowledge Refinement for Improved Retrieval-Augmented Reasoning
title_zh: 思考中进行搜索与精炼：促进知识精炼以改善检索增强推理
abstract:
Large language models have demonstrated impressive reasoning capabilities but are inherently limited by their knowledge reservoir.
Retrieval-augmented reasoning mitigates this limitation by allowing LLMs to query external resources, but existing methods often retrieve irrelevant or noisy information, hindering accurate reasoning.
In this paper, we propose **AutoRefine**, a reinforcement learning post-training framework that adopts a new "search-and-refine-during-think" paradigm.
AutoRefine introduces explicit knowledge refinement steps between successive search calls, enabling the model to iteratively filter, distill, and organize evidence before generating an answer.
Furthermore, we incorporate tailored retrieval-specific rewards alongside answer correctness rewards using group relative policy optimization.
Experiments on single-hop and multi-hop QA benchmarks demonstrate that AutoRefine significantly outperforms existing approaches, particularly in complex, multi-hop reasoning scenarios.
Detailed analysis shows that AutoRefine issues frequent, higher-quality searches and synthesizes evidence effectively.
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型展现了强大的推理能力，但其知识储备仍有限。为缓解这一限制，检索增强推理让模型查询外部资源，但现有方法常常检索到无关或噪音信息，影响准确推理。本文提出 **AutoRefine**，一种强化学习微调后的框架，引入“思考中搜索与精炼”的新范式。AutoRefine 在连续搜索调用之间加入明确的知识精炼步骤，使模型在生成答案前反复过滤、提炼并组织证据。此外，我们结合面向检索的定制奖励与答案正确性奖励，采用群体相对策略优化进行训练。单跳与多跳问答基准上的实验表明，AutoRefine 在特别是复杂多跳推理情景下显著优于现有方法。深入分析显示，AutoRefine 频繁发起高质量搜索并高效合成证据。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Credal Prediction based on Relative Likelihood
title_zh: 基于相对似然的可信预测
abstract:
Predictions in the form of sets of probability distributions, so-called credal sets, provide a suitable means to represent a learner's epistemic uncertainty. In this paper, we propose a theoretically grounded approach to credal prediction based on the statistical notion of relative likelihood: The target of prediction is the set of all (conditional) probability distributions produced by the collection of plausible models, namely those models whose relative likelihood exceeds a specified threshold. This threshold has an intuitive interpretation and allows for controlling the trade-off between correctness and precision of credal predictions. We tackle the problem of approximating credal sets defined in this way by means of suitably modified ensemble learning techniques. 
To validate our approach, we illustrate its effectiveness by experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrating superior uncertainty representation without compromising predictive performance. We also compare our method against several state-of-the-art baselines in credal prediction.
abstract_zh: 以概率分布集合的形式进行预测，即所谓的信念集，是表示学习者认知不确定性的合适方式。本文提出了一种基于相对似然这一统计学概念、具有理论依据的信念预测方法：预测目标是由一组合理模型所产生的所有（条件）概率分布构成的集合，其中合理模型是指其相对似然超过给定阈值的那些模型。该阈值具有直观的解释，并且能够控制信念预测在正确性与精确性之间的权衡。我们通过适当修改的集成学习技术来处理对以这种方式定义的信念集进行近似的问题。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: OrthAlign: Orthogonal Subspace Decomposition for Non-Interfering Multi-Objective Alignment
title_zh: OrthAlign：正交子空间分解用于无干扰多目标对齐
abstract: Large language model (LLM) alignment faces a critical dilemma when addressing multiple human preferences: improvements in one dimension frequently come at the expense of others, creating unavoidable trade-offs between competing objectives like helpfulness and harmlessness. While prior work mainly focuses on constraint-based optimization algorithms and data selection strategies to mitigate conflicts, these approaches overlook the fundamental issue of resolving conflicts directly at the parameter level. In this paper, we present OrthAlign, an innovative approach that pioneers a new paradigm by leveraging orthogonal subspace decomposition to fundamentally resolve gradient-level conflicts in multi-objective preference alignment. OrthAlign strategically decomposes parameter update spaces into orthogonal subspaces, ensuring that optimization toward different preferences occurs in mathematically non-interfering directions. Building upon this, we provide theoretical guarantees demonstrating that when parameter increments satisfy both orthogonal subspace constraints and spectral norm bounds, the resulting updates exhibit linear Lipschitz growth rather than exponential instability, ensuring stable convergence across all preference dimensions. Extensive experiments show that: I. OrthAlign achieves maximum single-preference improvements ranging from 34.61% to 50.89% after multiple-objective alignment across helpful, harmless, and truthful dimensions. II. With an average overall reward improvement of 13.96%. Our code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/OrthAlign.
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）在处理多种人类偏好时，其对齐面临一个关键困境：某一维度上的改进常常以牺牲其他维度为代价，从而在诸如有用性与无害性等相互竞争的目标之间形成不可避免的权衡。尽管以往工作主要关注基于约束的优化算法和数据选择策略来缓解冲突，但这些方法忽视了一个根本性问题，即直接在参数层面解决冲突。在本文中，我们提出了 OrthAlign，这是一种创新方法，通过利用正交子空间分解，从根本上解决多目标偏好对齐中的梯度级冲突，从而开创了一种新范式。OrthAlign 有策略地将参数更新空间分解为正交子空间，确保朝向不同偏好的优化发生在数学上互不干扰的方向上。在此基础上，我们给出理论保证，表明当参数增量同时满足正交子空间约束和谱范数界时，所得更新呈现线性 Lipschitz 增长而非指数不稳定性，从而确保在所有偏好维度上的稳定收敛。大量实验表明：I. OrthAlign 在有用性、无害性和真实性等维度上进行多目标对齐后，实现了 34.61% 到 50.89% 的最大单一偏好提升。II. 平均总体奖励提升达到 13.96%。我们的代码可在 https://anonymous.4open.science/r/OrthAlign 获取。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Supporting High-Stakes Decision Making Through Interactive Preference Elicitation in the Latent Space
title_zh: 通过潜在空间的交互式偏好获取支持高风险决策
abstract:
High-stakes, infrequent consumer decisions, such as housing selection, challenge conventional recommender systems due to sparse interaction signals, heterogeneous multi-criteria objectives, and high-dimensional feature spaces. 
This work presents an interactive preference elicitation framework that couples preferential Bayesian optimization (PBO) with two complementary components: (i) large language models (LLMs) that interpret natural language input to produce personalized probabilistic priors over feature utility weights to mitigate cold start, and (ii) an autoencoder (AE)-based latent representation that reduces effective dimensionality for sample-efficient exploration. The framework learns a latent utility function from user pairwise comparisons observed and integrated in real-time.
We evaluate the developed method on rental real estate datasets from two major European cities. The results show that executing PBO in an AE latent space improves final pairwise ranking accuracy by 12%. For LLM-based preference prior generation, we find that direct, LLM-driven weight specification is outperformed by a static prior, while probabilistic weight priors that use LLMs only to rank feature importance achieve 25% better pairwise accuracy on average than a direct approach.
abstract_zh: 本研究针对诸如住房选择等高风险、非频繁的消费者决策场景提出交互式偏好获取框架，以应对传统推荐系统在稀疏交互信号、多样化多准则目标与高维特征空间方面的挑战。框架将偏好贝叶斯优化（PBO）与两个互补模块耦合：（i）利用大语言模型（LLM）解释自然语言输入，生成关于特征效用权重的个性化概率先验以缓解冷启动问题；（ii）基于自编码器的潜在表达将有效维度降低，使探索更高效。该框架实时学习用户成对比较的潜在效用函数并及时整合反馈。我们在两座欧洲主要城市的租赁房地产数据集上评估所提方法。结果表明，在自编码器潜在空间执行PBO使最终成对排名准确性提升了12%。对于LLM驱动的偏好先验生成，我们发现直接由LLM指定权重不如静态先验，而仅让LLM对特征重要性排序的概率权重先验相比直接方法平均在成对准确性上提升了25%。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: More effort is needed to protect pedestrian privacy in the era of AI
title_zh: AI时代需要更多努力保护行人隐私
abstract: In the era of artificial intelligence (AI), pedestrian privacy is increasingly at risk. In research areas such as autonomous driving, computer vision, and surveillance, large datasets are often collected in public spaces, capturing pedestrians without consent or anonymization. These datasets are used to train systems that can identify, track, and analyze individuals, often without their knowledge. Although various technical methods and regional regulations have been proposed to address this issue, existing solutions are either insufficient to protect privacy or compromise data utility, thereby limiting their effectiveness for research. In this paper, we argue that more effort is needed to protect pedestrian privacy in the era of AI while maintaining data utility. We call on the AI and computer vision communities to take pedestrian privacy seriously and to rethink how pedestrian data are collected and anonymized. Collaboration with experts in law and ethics will also be essential for the responsible development of AI. Without stronger action, it will become increasingly difficult for individuals to protect their privacy, and public trust in AI may decline.
abstract_zh: 在人工智能（AI）时代，行人隐私正日益面临风险。在自动驾驶、计算机视觉和监控等研究领域，大型数据集通常在公共空间中采集，在未经行人同意或匿名化处理的情况下捕捉到他们的影像。这些数据集被用于训练能够识别、跟踪和分析个体的系统，而当事人往往并不知情。尽管已经提出了各种技术方法和区域性法规来应对这一问题，但现有解决方案要么不足以保护隐私，要么会损害数据效用，从而限制其在研究中的有效性。本文认为，在保持数据效用的同时，在人工智能时代保护行人隐私还需要付出更多努力。我们呼吁人工智能和计算机视觉社区认真对待行人隐私问题，并重新思考行人数据的采集与匿名化方式。与法律和伦理专家的合作对于人工智能的负责任发展也将至关重要。如果不采取更有力的行动，个人将越来越难以保护自己的隐私，公众对人工智能的信任也可能下降。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: SafeFlowMatcher: Safe and Fast Planning using Flow Matching with Control Barrier Functions
title_zh: SafeFlowMatcher：利用控制障碍函数的流匹配实现安全且快速的规划
abstract: Generative planners based on flow matching (FM) produce high-quality paths in a single or a few ODE steps, but their sampling dynamics offer no formal safety guarantees and can yield incomplete paths near constraints. We present SafeFlowMatcher, a planning framework that couples FM with control barrier functions (CBFs) to achieve both real-time efficiency and certified safety. SafeFlowMatcher uses a two-phase prediction-correction (PC) integrator: (i) a prediction phase integrates the learned FM once (or a few steps) to obtain a candidate path without intervention; (ii) a correction phase refines this path with a vanishing time‑scaled vector field and a CBF-based quadratic program that minimally perturbs the vector field. We prove a barrier certificate for the resulting flow system, establishing forward invariance of a robust safe set and finite-time convergence to the safe set. In addition, by enforcing safety only on the executed path—rather than all intermediate latent paths—SafeFlowMatcher avoids distributional drift and mitigates local trap problems. Moreover, SafeFlowMatcher attains faster, smoother, and safer paths than diffusion- and FM-based baselines on maze navigation, locomotion, and robot manipulation tasks. Extensive ablations corroborate the contributions of the PC integrator and the barrier certificate.
abstract_zh: 基于流匹配（FM）的生成式规划器能够在单步或少量 ODE 步内生成高质量路径，但其采样动力学无法提供形式化的安全保证，并且在约束附近可能产生不完整路径。我们提出 SafeFlowMatcher，这是一种将 FM 与控制屏障函数（CBF）耦合的规划框架，以同时实现实时效率和可认证安全性。SafeFlowMatcher 使用两阶段预测-校正（PC）积分器：（i）预测阶段在不进行干预的情况下，对学习得到的 FM 积分一次（或少量几步），以获得候选路径；（ii）校正阶段利用随时间缩放并渐近消失的向量场，以及基于 CBF 的二次规划，对该路径进行细化，并以最小方式扰动该向量场。我们为所得流系统证明了一个屏障证书，建立了鲁棒安全集的前向不变性以及对安全集的有限时间收敛性。此外，通过仅对实际执行的路径而非所有中间潜在路径施加安全约束，SafeFlowMatcher 避免了分布漂移，并缓解了局部陷阱问题。并且，在迷宫导航、运动控制和机器人操作任务中，SafeFlowMatcher 相比基于扩散和基于 FM 的基线方法，能够获得更快、更平滑且更安全的路径。大量消融实验进一步证实了 PC 积分器和屏障证书的贡献。
notes: 批量一键完成：采纳 AI 预标。

title: Can Compressed LLMs Truly Act? An Empirical Evaluation of Agentic Capabilities in LLM Compression
title_zh: 压缩的大型语言模型真的能执行代理行为吗：对LLM压缩中代理能力的实证评估
abstract: Post-training compression reduces the computational and memory costs of large language models (LLMs), enabling resource-efficient deployment.
